description: Foreign key linking to the F&B inventory item being supplied under this agreement
Unique identifier for this supply agreement record. Primary key.

Date when this supply agreement expires or was terminated. Null for ongoing agreements. Used for contract renewal planning and vendor relationship lifecycle management.

Current lifecycle status of this supply agreement. Active agreements are available for procurement. Inactive or suspended agreements are not used for new orders but retained for historical reference.

Actual unit cost from the most recent purchase order with this vendor for this item. Used for cost variance analysis and price trend tracking. Historical data point for this specific supply relationship.
Date of the most recent purchase order placed with this vendor for this item. Used for supplier performance tracking and relationship management. Specific to this vendor-item pair.
Number of days from purchase order submission to delivery for this vendor-item combination. Critical for inventory planning and reorder point calculations. Varies by vendor.
Minimum quantity that must be ordered from this vendor for this item. Vendor-specific constraint that affects procurement batch sizing and inventory holding costs.

Indicates whether this vendor is the preferred/primary supplier for this item among multiple sourcing options. Used to prioritize vendors in automated procurement workflows while maintaining multi-sourcing capability.
Negotiated unit cost for this item from this specific vendor. Used for cost comparison across vendors and procurement decision-making. Varies by vendor for the same item.
Supplier's unique identifier for this item. Used for purchase order line item matching and invoice reconciliation. This code is specific to the vendor-item combination.

The number of inventory items where the physical count differed from the system book quantity, requiring investigation.

Primary housekeeping role classification. Determines work assignment types, productivity benchmarks, and labor cost allocation. Room attendants clean guest rooms, turndown attendants provide evening service, house persons handle heavy cleaning and linen transport, supervisors oversee sections, inspectors perform quality checks, and public area cleaners maintain lobbies and common spaces.
Current section or floor assignment within the property. Sections are typically organized by floor, wing, or building. Section assignments may be permanent (based on seniority bidding) or rotational.

Date used for calculating seniority ranking within the housekeeping department. May differ from hire date due to transfers, breaks in service, or union contract provisions. Critical for section bidding rights and shift preference allocation.

Current shift assignment for the attendant. AM shift typically covers morning checkout cleaning, PM shift handles afternoon turnovers and turndown service, night shift performs deep cleaning and public area maintenance. Split shifts cover peak periods. On-call attendants fill in for absences.

Productivity benchmark expressed as the target number of room credits the attendant should complete per shift. Room credits vary by room type: standard rooms = 1.0 credit, suites = 1.5-2.0 credits, checkout rooms = 1.2 credits, stayover rooms = 0.7 credits. Used for performance evaluation and labor cost per occupied room (CPOR) calculation.
Date the attendant's employment ended. Null for active employees. Used for historical workforce analysis and rehire eligibility determination.
Uniform size for the attendant used for ordering and inventory management of housekeeping uniforms. Format varies by property (e.g., S/M/L/XL or numeric sizing).

Union job classification code as defined in the collective bargaining agreement. Determines pay grade, work rules, and assignment restrictions. Examples include classifications for different room types, shift differentials, and specialized duties.
Indicates whether the attendant is a member of a labor union. Determines applicability of collective bargaining agreement provisions, grievance procedures, and union-specific benefits.
Primary key for housekeeping_training_completion
Foreign key linking to the housekeeping attendant who enrolled in or completed the training course
Unique identifier for this training completion record. Primary key for the association.

Reference to the employee who served as instructor or facilitator for this specific course delivery. Null for self-paced or vendor-delivered courses. Used for instructor effectiveness tracking and quality assurance.
Foreign key linking to the learning course in which the attendant is enrolled or has completed

Sequential attempt number for this attendant-course combination. Increments with each enrollment if attendant fails and re-enrolls. Compared against learning_course.max_attempts to enforce attempt limits.

Date the attendant completed their most recent bloodborne pathogen safety certification. OSHA requires annual refresher training for all employees with potential occupational exposure. [Moved from attendant: This date represents the completion_date of the most recent bloodborne pathogen training completion record. It belongs in the association as it is specific to a particular course enrollment, not an inherent attribute of the attendant.]

Indicates whether the attendant has completed OSHA-required bloodborne pathogen training. Housekeeping staff may encounter blood, bodily fluids, or contaminated materials during room cleaning and must be trained in proper handling, disposal, and exposure response protocols. [Moved from attendant: This flag represents the CURRENT certification status derived from the most recent completed training_completion record for bloodborne pathogen courses. It should be computed from the association rather than stored redundantly on attendant.]
Indicates whether a completion certificate was issued to the attendant for this training. True for completed courses where learning_course.certification_issued_flag is true.
Unique certificate number issued for this training completion. Null if no certificate issued. Used for verification and audit purposes.

Date when the certification earned from this course completion expires and recertification is required. Calculated as completion_date plus learning_course.validity_period_days. Null for courses that do not expire. Critical for compliance alerting and scheduling qualified staff.

Date the attendant completed their most recent chemical handling safety certification. Certifications typically require annual renewal to maintain compliance with OSHA standards. [Moved from attendant: This date represents the completion_date of the most recent chemical handling training completion record. It belongs in the association as it is specific to a particular course enrollment, not an inherent attribute of the attendant.]

Indicates whether the attendant has completed required training for safe handling, storage, and use of cleaning chemicals. Certification includes OSHA Hazard Communication Standard (HCS) training and property-specific chemical safety protocols. [Moved from attendant: This flag represents the CURRENT certification status derived from the most recent completed training_completion record for chemical handling courses. It should be computed from the association rather than stored redundantly on attendant. The source of truth is the training completion record with its expiry date.]
Date when the attendant successfully completed the learning course. Null if course is in-progress or failed. Used to calculate certification validity periods.

Current status of the training enrollment. Enrolled indicates registration but not started; in_progress indicates active participation; completed indicates successful finish; failed indicates did not meet passing requirements; expired indicates certification period has lapsed; withdrawn indicates attendant dropped the course.
Timestamp when this training completion record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Date when the attendant was enrolled in the learning course. Marks the start of the training lifecycle for this attendant-course combination.
Timestamp when this training completion record was last modified. Updated when status changes, scores are recorded, or certification dates are set.

Percentage score achieved by the attendant on the course assessment or examination. Null if course does not require assessment. Compared against learning_course.passing_score to determine completion eligibility.

Indicates whether the attendant is certified to clean suite-level accommodations. Suite cleaning requires additional training for handling luxury amenities, multiple rooms, and higher guest expectations. Suite-qualified attendants typically receive higher credit values per room. [Moved from attendant: This flag represents certification status that is earned through completion of suite service training courses. The qualification is derived from training_completion records for suite-related courses, making the association the proper source of truth.]

Actual number of training hours the attendant completed for this course. May differ from learning_course.duration_hours for partial completions or extended sessions. Used for labor cost tracking and continuing education credit calculation.

Indicates whether the attendant is certified to service VIP guest rooms. VIP certification requires demonstrated excellence in cleaning standards, attention to detail, discretion, and ability to handle special requests. VIP rooms receive enhanced amenities and white-glove service. [Moved from attendant: This flag represents certification status that is earned through completion of VIP service training courses. The qualification is derived from training_completion records for VIP-related courses, making the association the proper source of truth.]

Current operational status of the housekeeping team in the workforce lifecycle.
Classification of the housekeeping team based on primary function and service area.
Unique identifier for the laundry order. Primary key for the laundry order transaction.

Identifier of the department requesting the laundry service (e.g., Housekeeping, Food and Beverage (F&B), Spa, Banquet).

Category of laundry items in this order: linen (sheets, pillowcases), terry (towels, robes), uniform (staff clothing), guest (guest laundry service), banquet (event linens), spa (spa towels and robes).

Free-text notes from quality inspection documenting any issues, defects, or observations about the returned laundry.

Free-text field for special handling instructions, stain treatment requests, or other processing notes for the laundry vendor.
Date and time when the laundry order was submitted to the laundry facility. Primary business event timestamp for the transaction.

Total cost for processing this laundry order. Calculated based on pricing_method, weight, and item count. Contributes to housekeeping OpEx (Operating Expenditure) and CPOR (Cost Per Occupied Room) metrics.
Total number of individual laundry items included in this order across all item types.
Total weight of laundry items in this order measured in pounds. Used for weight-based pricing calculations.

Actual turnaround time in hours from submission to return. Calculated as the difference between actual_return_timestamp and submission_timestamp. Used for vendor SLA (Service Level Agreement) performance tracking.

business_glossary_term: Block Wash Factor Application - Block Wash Factor Application Id
description: Primary key for the block_wash_factor_application association

business_glossary_term: Block Wash Factor Application - Room Block Id
description: Foreign key linking to the group room block to which the wash factor was applied

business_glossary_term: Block Wash Factor Application - Wash Factor Id
description: Foreign key linking to the wash factor configuration that was applied to the block
The actual attrition percentage observed after the block concluded, calculated as (contracted_room_nights - picked_up_room_nights) / contracted_room_nights. Used for post-event analysis.
Free-text explanation of why this specific wash factor was selected for this block (e.g., 'Corporate group, 90-day lead time, 50-room block matches historical profile').
Lifecycle status of this application record. 'active' = currently used for forecasting; 'superseded' = replaced by a newer application; 'final' = block concluded, actual wash recorded.
Name or user identifier of the revenue manager who applied or approved this wash factor application, or 'SYSTEM' if automatically applied by RMS.
The wash factor percentage that was applied to this specific block during forecasting. This is the predicted attrition rate used by the RMS.
Indicates whether the block size matched the wash factor's block_size_tier exactly, required interpolation between tiers, or used a default factor. Supports wash factor selection audit trail.
Timestamp when this application record was created in the system.

The date on which this wash factor was applied to the block for forecasting purposes. Supports tracking of multiple factor applications over the block lifecycle (e.g., initial forecast, revised forecast).
Identifier of the RMS forecast run during which this wash factor was applied. Links to the specific forecast cycle for audit and reproducibility.
Indicates whether the booking lead time matched the wash factor's booking_lead_time_bucket exactly, required interpolation, or used a default factor. Supports wash factor selection audit trail.
Timestamp when this application record was last updated (e.g., when actual wash was recorded post-event).

The difference between applied wash percentage and actual wash percentage (actual_wash_pct - applied_wash_pct). Positive variance indicates the block washed more than predicted; negative indicates better pickup than predicted.

Expected percentage of block rooms that will not be picked up, used for revenue forecasting and inventory planning. Expressed as percentage (e.g., 15.00 for 15%). [Moved from room_block: This attribute in room_block represents a specific wash factor percentage applied to the block. However, a block may have multiple wash factors applied over time (initial forecast, revised forecast, post-event analysis) or may be evaluated against multiple factor configurations. This attribute should move to the association to support tracking of multiple applications with their effective dates and contexts.]
Unique identifier for the room block record. Primary key.
Reference to the associated event or group booking that this room block supports.

Foreign key linking to revenue.group_evaluation. Business justification: Group evaluations assess displacement and revenue impact before accepting blocks. Linking blocks to originating evaluations enables post-booking performance review, wash factor validation, and revenue

Foreign key linking to guest.guest_group_block. Business justification: Room blocks represent inventory allocation for group bookings. Linking to guest_group_block enables pickup reconciliation, attrition calculation, billing reconciliation, and group contract compliance
Reference to the hotel sales manager responsible for managing and servicing this room block.
Reference to the hotel or resort property where the room block is allocated.
Reference to the system user who last modified the room block record.

Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Group room blocks often involve third-party intermediaries (DMCs, travel agencies, corporate travel management companies, wholesalers) who are tracked as vendors in procurement systems. Hotels need to
Monetary penalty amount charged to the group if pickup falls below the attrition threshold. Expressed in property base currency.
Contractual percentage threshold below which the group incurs attrition penalties for unmet room night commitments. Expressed as percentage (e.g., 20.00 for 20%).
Remaining number of room nights still available for pickup within the block.
Unique alphanumeric code identifying the room block, used for reservations and reporting. Externally-known identifier for group bookings.
Descriptive name of the room block, typically including the group or event name for easy identification.

Current lifecycle status of the room block: tentative (pending confirmation), definite (confirmed and contracted), waitlist (pending availability), cancelled (terminated), released (inventory returned), completed (event concluded).

Classification of the room block by purpose: group (general group travel), event (MICE events), corporate (business contracts), tour (tour operators), wedding (social events), conference (meeting-focused).

Channel or method through which the room block was originally booked: direct (property direct), GDS (Global Distribution System), OTA (Online Travel Agency), CRS (Central Reservation System), sales_team (hotel sales), third_party (intermediary).
Text description of the cancellation terms and penalties applicable to the room block.
Text description explaining the reason for room block cancellation, used for lost business analysis.
Date and time when the room block was cancelled, if applicable.
Commission rate percentage payable to intermediaries or group organizers for the block. Expressed as percentage (e.g., 10.00 for 10%).
Date and time when the room block status changed from tentative to definite (confirmed).
Total number of room nights contracted across all room types and dates in the block agreement.
Date and time when the room block record was first created in the system.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in the block (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
Date by which the group must pick up reserved rooms or release them back to general inventory. Also known as release date.
Monetary deposit amount required to secure the block, expressed in property base currency.
Date by which the deposit payment must be received to maintain the block reservation.
Indicates whether a deposit is required to secure the room block.
Last date of the room block period when rooms are reserved for the group or event.
Confidential internal notes and comments for hotel staff regarding the room block management and service delivery.
Indicates whether the block has Last Room Availability commitment, meaning the group rate applies even if only one room remains available.
Date and time when the room block record was last modified.
Contracted room rate amount per night for the block, expressed in property base currency.
Indicates whether the block allows overbooking beyond contracted room nights.
Primary email address of the block owner for communication and coordination.
Full name of the primary contact or decision-maker responsible for the room block on the client side.
Primary contact phone number of the block owner.
Cumulative number of room nights actually reserved and consumed from the block by guests.
Calculated percentage of contracted room nights that have been picked up (reserved). Expressed as percentage (e.g., 85.50 for 85.5%).
Special rate code assigned to the room block for pricing and revenue tracking purposes.
Free-text field capturing any special requirements, preferences, or requests associated with the room block (e.g., room location preferences, amenities, accessibility needs).
First date of the room block period when rooms are reserved for the group or event.
Unique identifier for the availability snapshot record. Primary key for this entity.

Foreign key linking to revenue.demand_forecast. Business justification: Availability snapshots capture actual rooms sold/available; demand forecasts predict future demand. Linking actuals to forecasts enables forecast accuracy measurement (MAPE calculation), model perform
Identifier of the hotel property for which this availability snapshot is recorded.
Identifier of the room type (e.g., Standard King, Deluxe Suite) for which availability is tracked.
Indicates whether new reservations are allowed to check in on this date. When true, no new arrivals are accepted, but existing multi-night stays can continue.
Indicates whether guests are allowed to check out on this date. When true, reservations must extend beyond this date to capture higher-value multi-night stays.
The timestamp when this availability snapshot record was first created in the data warehouse.
Free-text notes documenting any discrepancies found during reconciliation, including root cause and resolution actions.
Indicates whether Last Room Availability is enabled for this room type on this date. When true, the property commits to selling rooms at contracted rates even if only one room remains.
Maximum number of nights allowed for a reservation starting on this date. Used to prevent long stays during high-demand periods.
Minimum number of nights required for a reservation starting on this date. Used for demand management and revenue optimization.
Net number of rooms available to sell after accounting for all deductions (OOO, OOS, house use, blocks). Calculated as total physical rooms minus all unavailable and blocked rooms.
Occupancy rate percentage for this room type on this date, calculated as (rooms sold + complimentary rooms) / net available rooms * 100. Key performance metric for revenue management.
Maximum number of rooms that can be overbooked for this room type on this date, based on historical no-show and cancellation patterns. Used for yield optimization.
Status of the nightly reconciliation process for this snapshot. Indicates whether the snapshot has been validated against PMS folios and room status.
Number of rooms allocated to contracted allotments (OTA, wholesaler, corporate contracts) that are held but not yet sold.
Number of rooms blocked for group reservations (MICE, corporate blocks) that are not yet sold but are held off general inventory.
Number of rooms occupied by complimentary guests (e.g., loyalty rewards, service recovery, VIP comps). Counted in occupancy but not in revenue.
Number of rooms sold for day-use only (check-in and check-out on the same day), typically for meetings or short stays.
Number of rooms occupied by hotel staff, contractors, or used for operational purposes (e.g., training, storage). Not available for sale.
Number of rooms temporarily unavailable due to maintenance, repair, or refurbishment. These rooms are not available for sale.
Number of rooms permanently or long-term unavailable due to major renovation, structural issues, or strategic decisions. Not available for sale.
Actual number of rooms overbooked (sold beyond physical capacity) for this room type on this date.
Number of rooms sold (occupied by paying guests) for this room type on this date. Used in OCC (Occupancy Rate) calculation.
The business date for which this availability snapshot is recorded. Represents the stay date, not the snapshot creation date.
The exact date and time when this availability snapshot was captured. Typically recorded during the nightly audit process.
Indicates whether this room type is completely closed for sale on this date across all channels. When true, no new reservations are accepted.
Total number of physical rooms of this room type at the property, representing the maximum inventory capacity.
The timestamp when this availability snapshot record was last updated in the data warehouse.
Primary key for change_audit
System identifier of the user who authorized or executed the change.
Identifier of the property where the inventory change occurred.
Reference to the original audit log entry that this entry reverses, if this is a reversal transaction.
Level of authority that approved the change: system (automated), property manager, revenue manager, regional director, or corporate level.
Indicates whether this type of change required explicit management approval per SOX internal control policies.
Date and time when the change was formally approved by the authorizing authority, if approval was required.
Full name of the user who authorized or executed the change, for audit trail readability.
The hotel business date (night audit date) for which this inventory change is effective, distinct from the change timestamp.
Category of impact this change has on inventory: availability (room count changes), control (CTA/CTD/LOS/stop-sell), status (OOO/OOS), allocation (block/allotment), or pricing (rate-related).
Standardized code indicating the business reason for the change (e.g., REVENUE_OPTIMIZATION, MAINTENANCE, GROUP_BLOCK, SYSTEM_CORRECTION, NIGHT_AUDIT_ADJUSTMENT, EMERGENCY_OOO).
Free-text explanation providing additional context for the change, supporting dispute resolution and audit review.
Exact date and time when the inventory change was committed to the system. Primary business event timestamp for this audit log entry.
Unique identifier of the specific entity that was changed (e.g., room number, block ID, allotment ID, control rule ID).

Type of inventory entity that was modified: room inventory, room block, allotment, control (CTA/CTD/LOS/stop-sell/overbooking limit), availability override, out-of-order/out-of-service designation, or channel inventory mapping.
Distribution channel code affected by the change (e.g., OTA, GDS, DIRECT), if applicable to channel-specific inventory modifications.
Indicates whether this change was made to satisfy regulatory compliance requirements (USALI, SOX, or other).

business_glossary_term: Close to Arrival (CTA) / Close to Departure (CTD) Change Flag
description: Indicates whether this change modified CTA (Close to Arrival) or CTD (Close to Departure) restrictions, which control bookings based on arrival or departure dates.
Last date on which the inventory change remains effective, supporting date-range controls and LOS restrictions.
First date on which the inventory change becomes effective, supporting date-range controls and LOS (Length of Stay) restrictions.
Name of the specific field or attribute that was modified (e.g., availability_count, overbooking_limit, room_status, CTA_days, stop_sell_flag).

business_glossary_term: Length of Stay (LOS) Restriction Change Flag
description: Indicates whether this change modified LOS (Length of Stay) restrictions, including minimum or maximum stay requirements.
Indicates whether this change affected Last Room Availability (LRA) settings, which control whether the last available room can be sold at all rate levels.
The value of the field after the change was applied. Stored as string to accommodate various data types.
Indicates whether this change was made as part of the night audit process to reconcile inventory discrepancies.
Indicates whether this change modified overbooking limits, a critical control for revenue management and guest satisfaction.
The value of the field before the change was applied. Stored as string to accommodate various data types.
Code identifying the rate plan affected by the change (e.g., BAR, CORP, GOV), if applicable to the changed entity.
Timestamp when this audit log record was first created in the data warehouse, distinct from the business change timestamp.
Indicates whether this audit log entry represents a reversal or correction of a previous change.
Code identifying the room type affected by the change (e.g., KING, SUITE, DBL), if applicable to the changed entity.
Unique transaction or batch identifier from the source system that generated this change, enabling cross-system traceability.
Indicates whether this change involved stop-sell controls, which prevent bookings for specific room types, rate plans, or channels.
Primary key for out_of_order

Foreign key linking to compliance.health_safety_incident. Business justification: Rooms go out of order due to safety incidents (guest injury, hazardous condition, equipment failure). Links OOO record to triggering incident for root cause tracking, remediation workflow, and lost re

Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: OOO rooms generate lost revenue accruals and repair cost capitalization decisions requiring specific GL account posting. Finance teams need this link for CapEx vs OpEx classification, FFE reserve trac
Identifier of the staff member who reported or logged the out-of-order condition (typically housekeeping, front desk, or maintenance staff).
Identifier of the hotel property where the room is located.
Identifier of the specific physical room that is out-of-order or out-of-service.
Identifier of the staff member who closed the out-of-order record and returned the room to service (typically housekeeping or maintenance supervisor).

Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: When rooms go out-of-order for repairs (HVAC, plumbing, electrical, furniture replacement), hotels engage external vendors. Tracking which vendor performed the work is essential for cost allocation, w
Actual cost incurred for repairs, maintenance, or renovation work. Captured upon work order completion for financial reconciliation and FF&E tracking.
Actual date when the room was returned to sellable inventory and made available for guest occupancy. Null if still out-of-order.
Precise timestamp when the room was returned to sellable inventory, including time of day for accurate inventory restoration tracking.

business_glossary_term: Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) Compliance Flag
description: Indicates whether the out-of-order condition affects an ADA-compliant accessible room, requiring special handling for guest accommodation and compliance tracking.
Name of the manager or supervisor who approved the out-of-order designation for audit trail and accountability.
Name of the staff member who closed the out-of-order record and verified the room is ready for guest occupancy.
Timestamp when this out-of-order record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and operational reporting.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for cost amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
Total number of days the room was out-of-order or out-of-service, calculated from start date to actual return date. Used for RevPAR and occupancy impact analysis.
Estimated cost in local currency for repairs, maintenance, or renovation work required to return the room to service. Used for CapEx and OpEx planning.
Planned or estimated date when the room is expected to be returned to sellable inventory after repairs or maintenance are completed.
Indicates whether a guest reservation was impacted by this out-of-order designation (True if guest had to be relocated or reservation cancelled).
Percentage point impact on property occupancy rate due to this room being out-of-order. Used for STR reporting and competitive benchmarking.

business_glossary_term: Impact on Revenue Per Available Room (RevPAR)
description: Calculated impact on property RevPAR due to this room being out-of-order. Critical metric for revenue management and performance analysis.
Estimated revenue loss due to the room being unavailable for sale during the out-of-order period. Calculated using ADR and occupancy forecasts for RevPAR impact analysis.
Free-text field for additional notes, observations, or special instructions related to the out-of-order condition and repair work.
Standardized code representing the reason category for the room being out-of-order or out-of-service (e.g., MAINT, REPAIR, RENO, DAMAGE, INSPECT).
Detailed textual description of the specific reason the room is out-of-order or out-of-service (e.g., plumbing leak, HVAC failure, carpet replacement, pest control treatment).
Designation type: OOO (Out-of-Order - not counted in available inventory) or OOS (Out-of-Service - counted in inventory but not sold). Follows USALI standards for inventory accounting.
Urgency level assigned to the repair or maintenance work (Critical, High, Medium, Low). Critical priority indicates immediate revenue impact or safety concern.
Current lifecycle status of the out-of-order record (Open - newly reported, In Progress - work underway, Completed - room returned to service, Cancelled - designation reversed).
Indicates whether guest relocation to another room or property was required due to this out-of-order condition. Used for service recovery tracking.
Name of the staff member who reported the out-of-order condition for operational tracking and accountability.

Department responsible for addressing the issue and returning the room to service (e.g., Housekeeping, Maintenance, Engineering, Facilities, Renovation). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Housekeeping|Maintenance|Engineering|Facilities|Renovation|Pest Control|Safety|Quality Assurance — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Indicates whether the out-of-order condition involves a safety or health hazard requiring immediate attention per OSHA or local safety regulations.
Date when the room was first designated as out-of-order or out-of-service and removed from sellable inventory.
Precise timestamp when the room was designated as out-of-order or out-of-service, including time of day for accurate inventory impact tracking.
Timestamp when this out-of-order record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
Reference number of the maintenance or repair work order associated with this out-of-order designation. Links to work order management system.
Primary key for room_status
Identifier of the housekeeping staff member currently assigned to clean or service this room.

Foreign key linking to guest.guest_group_block. Business justification: Group room block management requires tracking which rooms are assigned to which blocks for rooming list fulfillment, pickup tracking, and block-specific housekeeping instructions. Front desk and house

Foreign key linking to compliance.health_safety_incident. Business justification: Health/safety incidents trigger room status changes (blocked, out of order, requires inspection). Links status to incident for incident response workflow, housekeeping coordination, and guest relocati
Identifier of the housekeeping supervisor or inspector who performed the quality inspection of the room.
Identifier of the guest currently occupying the room. Null if room is vacant.
Identifier of the hotel property where the room is located. Links to the property master data.
Identifier of the active reservation currently occupying the room. Null if room is vacant.
Identifier of the physical room. Links to the room inventory master data.
Explanation of why the room is administratively blocked from sale (e.g., VIP hold, renovation, inventory control, group block).

Type of cleaning service required or last performed. Checkout: full cleaning after guest departure; Stayover: daily service for occupied room; Deep Clean: comprehensive periodic cleaning; Turndown: evening service; Refresh: light touch-up.
Date and time when this room status record was first created in the system.
Boolean flag indicating a mismatch between front desk status and housekeeping status (e.g., front desk shows vacant but housekeeping shows occupied). Requires investigation.
Free-text notes describing the nature of any status discrepancy and actions taken to resolve it.
Boolean flag indicating whether the guest has activated the do not disturb indicator, preventing housekeeping and other staff from entering.
The scheduled check-in date for the next arriving guest. Used for due-in planning and housekeeping prioritization.
The scheduled checkout date for the current guest occupying the room. Used for due-out planning.

Current operational status from the front desk perspective. Vacant: unoccupied and available; Occupied: guest currently in residence; Reserved: booked for future arrival; Due Out: guest scheduled to check out today; Due In: guest scheduled to check in today; Out of Order: room unavailable for sale.

Current housekeeping state of the room indicating cleanliness and readiness. Dirty: needs full cleaning; Clean: cleaned but not inspected; Inspected: cleaned and quality-checked; Pickup: minor refresh needed; Out of Service: maintenance required; Do Not Disturb: guest requested no service.
Boolean flag indicating whether the room is administratively blocked from sale (e.g., for renovation, VIP hold, or inventory control).
Boolean flag indicating whether the room has been cleaned and is ready for occupancy. True if housekeeping status is clean or inspected.
Boolean flag indicating whether the room has passed housekeeping quality inspection after cleaning.
Boolean flag indicating whether the room is out of order and unavailable for sale due to maintenance or other operational issues.
Date and time when the room was last cleaned by housekeeping staff.
Date and time when the room last passed housekeeping quality inspection.
Date and time when this room status record was last modified in the system.
Date and time when the room was last occupied by a guest (most recent check-in or last known occupancy).
Date and time when the room was last vacated by a guest (most recent check-out).

Current maintenance condition of the room. Available: no maintenance issues; Minor Repair: small issues not blocking occupancy; Major Repair: significant issues requiring attention; Out of Order: room cannot be occupied; Scheduled Maintenance: planned preventive maintenance.
Simple binary occupancy indicator. Vacant: no guest assigned; Occupied: guest currently checked in; Reserved: future reservation exists.
The expected or actual date when the room will be or was returned to service after being out of order.
Detailed explanation of why the room is out of order (e.g., plumbing issue, HVAC failure, renovation, pest control, water damage).
The date when the room was first designated as out of order.

Housekeeping priority level for servicing this room. Urgent: immediate attention required (e.g., due-in within 1 hour); High: priority cleaning (e.g., VIP guest, early check-in); Normal: standard cleaning schedule; Low: can be deferred.
Free-text special instructions for housekeeping staff regarding this room (e.g., guest allergies, VIP preferences, extra amenities required).
Date and time when the room status was last updated in the system. Tracks the most recent status transition.

Status of evening turndown service for the room. Not Requested: no turndown service scheduled; Requested: guest or property standard requires turndown; Completed: turndown service has been performed; Declined: guest declined turndown service.

business_glossary_term: Rate Plan Room Type Assignment ID
description: Unique surrogate identifier for each rate plan room type assignment record. Primary key for the association.

business_glossary_term: Rate Plan Room Type Assignment - Channel Rate Plan Id
description: Foreign key linking to the channel rate plan configuration. Identifies which rate plan this room type is being sold under.

business_glossary_term: Rate Plan Room Type Assignment - Inventory Room Type Id
description: Foreign key linking to the room type master catalog. Identifies which room type this rate plan assignment applies to.
Timestamp when this rate plan room type assignment was created in the system.
Date when this room type is no longer available under this rate plan. Null indicates ongoing availability. Used to manage promotional periods and seasonal configurations.
Date when this room type becomes available under this rate plan. Used for seasonal rate plan configurations and promotional periods.
Timestamp when this rate plan room type assignment was last updated. Tracks when revenue managers made changes to rates, restrictions, or sell limits.
Maximum number of nights allowed for this specific room type under this rate plan. Room-type-specific override of the base rate plan max_los restriction.

Minimum number of nights required for this specific room type under this rate plan. Can differ from the base rate plan min_los to implement room-type-specific restrictions (e.g., suites require 3-night minimum while standard rooms require 2).
User ID or username of the revenue manager who last modified this assignment configuration.

Ranking used to determine which rate plan to display or apply when multiple rate plans are available for the same room type. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used in rate shopping and channel distribution logic.

The room-type-specific rate adjustment applied to this rate plan. Can be a percentage markup/markdown or flat amount depending on adjustment type. Revenue managers set different adjustments per room type to reflect demand and value differences.

Current availability status for this room type under this rate plan. OPEN = available for sale, CLOSED = not bookable, CLOSED_TO_ARRIVAL = no check-ins allowed, CLOSED_TO_DEPARTURE = no check-outs allowed. Revenue managers adjust this based on demand forecasts.
Maximum number of rooms of this type that can be sold under this rate plan. Used for inventory allocation and yield management to control how many rooms are available at promotional rates versus BAR.
Primary key for room_type_competitive_benchmark

business_glossary_term: Room Type Competitive Benchmark - Inventory Room Type Id
description: Foreign key linking to the room type being benchmarked against the competitive set

business_glossary_term: Room Type Competitive Benchmark - Revenue Competitive Set Id
description: Foreign key linking to the competitive set used for benchmarking this room type

The booking lead time window in days used for rate shopping comparisons for this specific room type and competitive set pairing. Allows different lead time analysis for different room type segments (e.g., suites may use 30-day window, standard rooms 7-day window).

The STR room type category classification used for this specific room type when benchmarking against this competitive set. Maps property-specific room types to standardized STR categories for apples-to-apples comparison. Examples: STANDARD, DELUXE, SUITE, PREMIUM.

The weighting percentage applied to this competitive set when calculating blended benchmark metrics for this room type. Used when a room type is benchmarked against multiple competitive sets with different importance levels. Sum of weights across all comp sets for a room type should equal 100.
System timestamp when this benchmark configuration record was created.

The date from which this room type to competitive set benchmark configuration becomes active. Allows revenue managers to schedule future changes to benchmark mappings aligned with property renovations, rebranding, or competitive strategy shifts.
The date on which this benchmark configuration expires and is no longer used for rate shopping or STR reporting. Null indicates an active, ongoing configuration.

Indicates whether this is the primary competitive set used for benchmarking this room type. A room type may be mapped to multiple competitive sets (primary luxury set, secondary airport set), but only one is designated as primary for executive reporting and KPI dashboards.
User ID or username of the revenue manager who last modified this benchmark configuration.
System timestamp when this benchmark configuration record was last modified.

The standard length of stay in nights used for rate shopping and benchmarking comparisons for this room type and competitive set combination. Different room types may use different LOS windows (e.g., suites benchmarked at 3-night stays, standard rooms at 1-night stays).

Override frequency for automated rate shopping for this specific room type and competitive set pairing. Allows revenue managers to configure more frequent rate checks for high-value room types or key competitive sets. Overrides the default rate_shop_frequency from the competitive_set master.

Current operational status of this benchmark configuration. ACTIVE configurations are used in live rate shopping and STR reporting. INACTIVE configurations are historical. SUSPENDED configurations are temporarily disabled but may be reactivated.
User ID or username of the revenue manager who created this benchmark configuration mapping.
Unique identifier for this room type promotion configuration record. Primary key.

business_glossary_term: Room Type Promotion - Inventory Room Type Id
description: Foreign key linking to the room type participating in this promotion
Foreign key linking to the loyalty promotion offering special terms for this room type

Current operational status of this room type promotion configuration. Values: active (available for booking), paused (temporarily disabled by revenue management), sold_out (inventory cap reached), expired (booking window closed).

Comma-separated list of date ranges (YYYY-MM-DD to YYYY-MM-DD) when this room type is excluded from the promotion due to high demand periods, holidays, or inventory constraints. Example: 2024-12-20 to 2024-12-31, 2024-07-04 to 2024-07-04.

Points earning multiplier specific to this room type and promotion combination. Example: 2.0 means double points, 3.0 means triple points. Overrides the promotion-level multiplier when room-type-specific earning rules apply.
Last date when members can book this room type under this promotion. May differ from promotion end_date to manage inventory availability or phase out specific room types from the offer.
First date when members can book this room type under this promotion. Used for early-access offers or phased promotional launches by room category.
Timestamp when this room type promotion configuration was created by the loyalty or revenue management team.
Maximum number of rooms of this type allocated to this promotion. Used by revenue management to control promotional inventory and protect higher-rated business. Null if no cap applies.
Minimum number of nights required to book this room type under this promotion. Overrides property-level or promotion-level minimum LOS when room-type-specific restrictions apply. Null if no override.
Special promotional rate (in property currency) offered for this room type under this promotion. Overrides standard BAR or member rates. Null if promotion does not include rate discount.
Cumulative count of rooms of this type booked under this promotion to date. Updated in real-time by reservation system. Used to enforce inventory_allocation_cap.
Username or employee ID of the loyalty or revenue management team member who configured this room type for this promotion.
Unique identifier for the physical room. Primary key. Authoritative SSOT for physical room identity in Oracle OPERA PMS.

Foreign key linking to compliance.ada_assessment. Business justification: ADA assessments evaluate individual rooms for accessibility compliance (barrier identification, remediation tracking). Links room to its most recent assessment for compliance status reporting and reme
Identifier of the adjacent room that connects to this room via connecting door. Null if no connecting room.

Foreign key linking to compliance.fire_safety_record. Business justification: Fire safety inspections evaluate individual rooms for suppression systems, alarms, extinguishers, evacuation routes. Links room to fire safety record for compliance certification, insurance requiremen
Identifier of the property (hotel, resort, or vacation property) where this room is located.
Identifier of the room type classification (e.g., Standard King, Deluxe Suite, Executive Double) assigned to this room. Determines rate category and inventory grouping.
Indicates whether the room meets ADA accessibility standards including wheelchair access, grab bars, visual alarms, and accessible bathroom fixtures.
Indicates whether the room has a private balcony or terrace. Premium feature used for rate differentiation.
Total number of beds in the room. Used for occupancy capacity and guest preferences.

Primary bed configuration in the room. Critical for guest preferences and room assignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: king|queen|double|twin|california_king|murphy|sofa_bed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Code identifying the building or tower within a multi-building property. Null for single-building properties.
Indicates whether this room has a connecting door to an adjacent room. Used for family bookings and group assignments.
Timestamp when this room record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.
Comma-separated list of additional room features and amenities (e.g., minibar, safe, coffee maker, smart TV, work desk). Used for guest preference matching and marketing.

business_glossary_term: FF&E (Furniture Fixtures and Equipment) Condition Score
description: Condition assessment score for room furniture, fixtures, and equipment on a scale of 0.00 to 5.00. Used for CapEx planning and PIP prioritization.
The floor level where the room is located within the building. Used for housekeeping routing and guest preferences.
Current front office occupancy state. Indicates whether the room is vacant, occupied by a guest, reserved for arrival, or blocked.
Current housekeeping state of the room. Updated by housekeeping staff and used for room assignment and availability.
Indicates whether the room includes a kitchenette with cooking facilities. Common in extended-stay properties.
Timestamp when this room record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data synchronization.
Date of the most recent renovation or major refurbishment. Used for PIP (Property Improvement Plan) tracking and FF&E (Furniture Fixtures and Equipment) lifecycle management.
Indicates whether this room is eligible for Last Room Availability distribution to OTA partners. Used for channel distribution controls.
Maximum number of guests allowed in the room per fire code and property policy. Used for reservation validation.
Current operational state of the room. Determines whether the room is available for sale and occupancy. Lifecycle status field.
Expected date when the room will return to service. Used for inventory forecasting and revenue management.
Reason code or description for why the room is out of order. Null when room is in service. Used for maintenance tracking and OCC impact analysis.
Date when the room was taken out of order. Used for revenue impact analysis and maintenance duration tracking.
Indicates whether this room can be included in overbooking calculations. Used by RMS for yield optimization.
The externally-known room number displayed on the door and used for guest communication. Business identifier for the room.
Indicates whether smoking is permitted in the room. Used for guest preference matching and inventory segmentation.
Total interior square footage of the room. Used for marketing, rate positioning, and guest selection criteria.

Type of view from the room windows. Impacts rate positioning and guest satisfaction. Used for upsell opportunities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ocean|city|mountain|garden|pool|courtyard|no_view — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Code identifying the wing or section within a building. Used for operational routing and guest location preferences.
Unique identifier for the room amenity record. Primary key for the room amenity catalog.

Foreign key linking to compliance.fire_safety_record. Business justification: Fire safety inspections evaluate amenities (smoke detectors, sprinklers, fire extinguishers) installed in rooms. Links amenity to fire safety record for inspection tracking, compliance certification,

Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Premium room amenities (TVs, furniture, artwork) meeting capitalization thresholds are tracked as fixed assets requiring depreciation schedules, useful life tracking, and CapEx budget management. Esse

Foreign key linking to compliance.health_safety_incident. Business justification: Amenity failures (broken furniture, malfunctioning appliances, electrical hazards) trigger safety incidents requiring investigation and remediation. Links amenity to incident for root cause analysis,
Self-referencing FK on room_amenity (parent_room_amenity_id)
Identifier of the system user who created this amenity record. Supports accountability and audit compliance.
Identifier of the property where this amenity is available. Links to the property master data.
Identifier of the specific physical room where this amenity is installed. Null if amenity is defined at room type level only.

Foreign key linking to inventory.room_type. Business justification: room_amenity product description states amenities are 'available by room type and individual room'. Currently only has FK to room (room_id). Many amenities are defined at room_type level (e.g., all De
Identifier of the vendor or supplier who provided the amenity. Links to procurement and vendor management systems.

High-level classification of the amenity type for inventory management and guest preference tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: technology|bathroom|bedding|minibar|furniture|climate_control|entertainment|connectivity — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Standardized code identifying the amenity type. Used for system integration and reporting consistency across properties.
Detailed description of the amenity including brand, model, features, and guest-facing benefits. Used for marketing and guest communication.
Business-friendly name of the amenity as displayed to guests and staff. Examples: Smart TV, Minibar, Espresso Machine, Premium Bedding.
Manufacturer or brand name of the amenity. Important for luxury properties where brand recognition drives guest satisfaction.
Price charged to guest if amenity is consumed or used. Null for complimentary amenities. Used for minibar items and premium services.
Current physical condition of the amenity as assessed during last inspection. Drives maintenance and replacement decisions.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the unit cost. Required when unit_cost is populated.
Date and time when this amenity record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails and data lineage.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the charge amount. Required when charge_amount is populated.
Sort order for displaying amenities in guest-facing interfaces and reports. Lower numbers appear first.
Date after which this amenity configuration is no longer active. Null indicates no planned end date.
Date from which this amenity configuration becomes active and available for guest use or booking display.
Indicates whether this amenity is displayed in guest-facing channels such as booking engines, mobile apps, and property websites. Used for marketing and distribution.
Date when the amenity was installed or placed in the room. Used for tracking asset age and planning replacement cycles.

business_glossary_term: Is ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act) Compliant
description: Indicates whether the amenity meets ADA accessibility requirements. Critical for compliance and guest accommodation matching.
Indicates whether the amenity is provided at no additional charge to the guest. False indicates a chargeable amenity such as minibar items.
Indicates whether the amenity meets environmental sustainability standards. Used for green certification programs and guest preference matching.
Indicates whether this amenity is considered a premium or luxury feature that differentiates the room type and justifies higher ADR (Average Daily Rate).
Date of the most recent inspection or condition assessment of the amenity. Critical for quality assurance and guest satisfaction.
Date and time when this amenity record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking and synchronization.
Number of days between scheduled maintenance or inspection cycles for this amenity. Used for preventive maintenance scheduling.
Manufacturer model number or SKU for the amenity. Used for procurement, warranty tracking, and replacement ordering.
Planned date for replacing or refreshing the amenity based on lifecycle policy. Supports proactive FF&E capital planning.
Current operational availability of the amenity. Inactive or out_of_service amenities are not available to guests.
Purchase order reference number for the amenity acquisition. Used for financial reconciliation and audit trails.
Number of units of this amenity present in the room or room type. Example: 2 for pillows, 1 for minibar.
Free-text field for operational notes, handling instructions, or guest communication guidelines related to the amenity.
Acquisition cost per unit of the amenity. Used for CapEx (Capital Expenditure) tracking and ROI (Return on Investment) analysis.
Unit in which the amenity quantity is measured. Used for inventory tracking and replenishment.
Date when manufacturer warranty coverage expires. Important for cost planning and vendor relationship management.
Unique identifier for this specific installation record. Primary key.

business_glossary_term: Room Material Installation - Material Master Id
description: Foreign key linking to the material master record for the installed item
Foreign key linking to the physical room where the material is installed
Current condition assessment of the installed material. Updated by housekeeping inspections and engineering assessments. Drives maintenance and replacement prioritization.
Timestamp when this installation record was created in the system. Audit trail field.
Date when the material was installed in the room. Used for age calculation, warranty tracking, and depreciation schedules.
Free-text notes about the installation including installer, special configuration, or issues encountered. Audit trail for property management.
Date when preventive maintenance was last performed on this material. Used to calculate next maintenance due date.
Date when this material was last replaced in the room. Tracks replacement history for lifecycle analysis and budgeting.
Number of days between scheduled preventive maintenance activities for this material in this room. Varies by material type and room usage intensity.
Planned date for next replacement based on lifecycle policy, condition assessments, and capital planning cycles. Drives CapEx budgeting and procurement forecasting.
Number of units of this material installed in the room (e.g., 2 nightstands, 1 desk, 4 pillows). Critical for inventory valuation and replacement planning.
Actual cost per unit paid at time of installation. Captured for asset valuation, depreciation calculation, and ROI analysis. May differ from current material_master.standard_price.
Timestamp when this installation record was last updated. Audit trail field.
Date when manufacturer or vendor warranty expires for this installation. Used to determine repair vs. replacement decisions and vendor claim eligibility.
Primary key for room_type
Reference to the property where this room type is defined. Room types are property-specific configurations.

Comma-separated list of accessibility features available in this room type. Examples: roll-in shower, grab bars, visual alarm, hearing kit, lowered fixtures. Used for guest matching and compliance tracking.

Current operational status of the room type. Inactive types are not available for new reservations. Seasonal types are active only during specific periods. Renovation types are temporarily unavailable.
Description of bathroom setup. Examples: single full bath, 1.5 bath, dual vanity, separate tub and shower. Used for guest expectations and competitive positioning.
Number of beds in the room type. Used for occupancy calculations and guest preference matching.

Primary bed configuration for the room type. Critical for guest preference matching and inventory allocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: king|queen|double|twin|california_king|murphy|sofa_bed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Timestamp when this room type record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Date when this room type configuration expires or is replaced. Null for currently active configurations. Used for historical tracking and renovation planning.
Date when this room type configuration becomes effective. Used for managing room type changes, renovations, and new inventory rollouts.
Typical floor range where this room type is located. Examples: 1-5, 6-10, 15-20. Used for guest preference and accessibility planning.

business_glossary_term: Is ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act) Compliant
description: Indicates whether this room type meets ADA accessibility requirements including mobility, hearing, and visual accommodations. Required for compliance reporting and guest accessibility requests.
Indicates whether this room type can be configured as a connecting room. Used for family and group booking allocation.
Indicates whether this room type is classified as a suite with separate living and sleeping areas. Used for inventory segmentation and revenue reporting.
Timestamp when this room type record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data synchronization across PMS, RMS, and CRS systems.
Indicates whether this room type participates in Last Room Availability agreements with OTA partners. Critical for channel distribution and rate parity management.
Maximum number of guests allowed in this room type per fire code and property standards. Used for reservation validation and yield optimization.
Indicates whether this room type can be overbooked as part of revenue management strategy. Used by RMS for yield optimization.

Indicates whether this is a pseudo room type used for inventory management purposes (e.g., house use, out of order, complimentary) rather than a sellable room type. Used for inventory controls and reporting exclusions.
Rate category classification used for revenue management and pricing strategy. Examples: BAR, Corporate, Government, Group, Promotional. Links to rate plan structures.
High-level classification of the room type by service tier. Used for segmentation in revenue management and guest experience programs.
Comma-separated list of key room features and amenities. Examples: balcony, kitchenette, fireplace, whirlpool, separate shower, walk-in closet. Used for guest communication and OTA distribution.

Short alphanumeric code used to identify the room type in PMS and RMS systems. Examples: KING, QQDBL, JSUITE, PRES. This is the operational identifier used across reservations, inventory, and revenue management.
Detailed marketing description of the room type including amenities, features, and guest experience highlights. Used for guest-facing channels and OTA distribution.
Full descriptive name of the room type as displayed to guests and staff. Examples: Deluxe King, Queen Queen Double, Junior Suite, Presidential Suite.
Property segment classification for this room type. Aligns with brand standards and service level expectations.
Indicates whether this room type is available for sale to guests. Non-sellable types include staff rooms, maintenance rooms, and owner units.
Smoking policy for this room type. Most properties are non-smoking; some maintain designated smoking inventory.
Display sequence for this room type in PMS screens, reports, and guest-facing channels. Lower numbers appear first.
Total floor area of the room type in square feet. Used for guest communication, competitive positioning, and property planning.
Standard number of guests for this room type used for base rate calculations. Additional guests may incur extra person charges.

Classification of the view from the room. Impacts pricing strategy and guest satisfaction. Used in revenue management for rate differentiation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ocean|city|mountain|garden|pool|courtyard|no_view|partial — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Unique identifier for this room type vendor supply relationship. Primary key.

business_glossary_term: Room Type Vendor Supply - Inventory Room Type Id
description: Foreign key linking to the room type that this vendor supplies goods and services for

business_glossary_term: Room Type Vendor Supply - Vendor Id
description: Foreign key linking to the vendor providing goods and services for this room type
Current status of this supply relationship. Inactive relationships are retained for historical spend analysis but not available for new procurement.
Date when the supply contract for this room type-vendor combination becomes effective. Used for contract lifecycle management.
Date when the supply contract for this room type-vendor combination expires. Triggers renewal workflows and vendor re-evaluation.
Timestamp when this supply relationship was established in the system.
Date of the most recent purchase order placed with this vendor for this room type category. Used for vendor activity tracking and relationship health monitoring.
Timestamp of the most recent update to this supply relationship record.
Standard lead time in days for this vendor to deliver goods for this room type category. Critical for inventory planning and renovation scheduling.
Minimum order quantity required by this vendor for this room type category. Used for procurement planning and order consolidation.
Percentage discount negotiated with this vendor for supplies related to this room type category. Applied at invoice time.
Indicates whether this vendor is the preferred supplier for this room type category. Used for procurement routing and approval workflows.
Category of goods or services this vendor provides for this room type. Examples: FF&E (furniture, fixtures, equipment), Amenities, Housekeeping Supplies, Linens, Technology.
Cumulative spend amount with this vendor for this room type category. Used for vendor tier classification, negotiation leverage, and spend analytics.
Unique identifier for the channel inventory mapping record. Primary key.
Identifier of the channel contract or agreement under which this inventory mapping is governed. Links to channel contract master data.
User identifier of the person or system account that created this channel inventory mapping record.
Identifier of the hotel property for which this channel inventory mapping applies.

Foreign key linking to inventory.room_type. Business justification: channel_inventory_map defines which room types are available to each distribution channel. Currently uses denormalized room_type_code (STRING). Adding FK to room_type.inventory_room_type_id normalizes
Maximum number of days in advance that a reservation can be made through this channel for this room type. Null indicates no maximum booking window restriction.
Minimum number of days in advance that a reservation must be made through this channel for this room type. Used for channel-specific booking window controls.
Method by which inventory is allocated to this channel: free_sell (real-time availability), allotment (fixed block), block (group block), or dynamic (RMS-driven allocation).
Code identifying the specific booking source within the channel (e.g., sub-channel, affiliate, or partner identifier). Provides granular attribution for channel performance analysis.
Unique code identifying the distribution channel (e.g., EXPEDIA, BOOKING_COM, DIRECT_WEB, GDS_SABRE, VOICE, MOBILE_APP). Standardized channel identifier used across CRS and PMS systems.
Numeric ranking indicating the priority of this channel for inventory allocation (lower number = higher priority). Used when multiple channels compete for limited inventory.
Maximum number of rooms of this type that can be sold through this specific channel on any given date. Used for channel-level inventory allocation and overbooking controls.
Boolean indicator of whether this room type is visible and bookable through this channel. False indicates the mapping exists but the room type is hidden from the channel.
Boolean indicator of whether bookings made through this channel for this room type are eligible for commission payments to the channel partner.

Technical method used to connect and synchronize inventory with this channel: API (Application Programming Interface), GDS (Global Distribution System), XML feed, manual update, or channel manager platform.
Timestamp when this channel inventory mapping record was first created in the system.
The date on which this channel inventory mapping expires or is no longer active. Null indicates open-ended availability.
The date from which this channel inventory mapping becomes active and the room type is available for sale through the specified channel.
Timestamp of the most recent successful inventory synchronization with this channel for this room type mapping.
Boolean indicator of whether Last Room Availability is enabled for this channel-room type combination, allowing the channel to sell inventory down to the last available room.
Current lifecycle status of the channel inventory mapping indicating whether the room type is currently being distributed through this channel.
Code identifying the market segment targeted by this channel distribution (e.g., LEISURE, CORPORATE, GROUP, GOVERNMENT). Used for revenue management segmentation.
Channel-specific override for maximum length of stay restriction in nights. Null indicates no channel-specific override; property-level LOS rules apply.
Channel-specific override for minimum length of stay requirement in nights. Null indicates no channel-specific override; property-level LOS rules apply.
Timestamp when this channel inventory mapping record was last modified or updated.
Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this channel inventory mapping configuration.
Channel-specific overbooking limit expressed as a percentage of physical inventory. Allows controlled overbooking to optimize occupancy while managing walk risk.
Boolean indicator of whether rate parity rules are enforced for this channel-room type mapping, ensuring consistent pricing across distribution channels.
Code identifying the rate plan associated with this channel inventory mapping. Defines pricing strategy and restrictions for this channel-room type combination.
Boolean indicator of whether a manual stop-sell override is in effect for this channel-room type combination, preventing new bookings regardless of availability.
Frequency in minutes at which inventory availability is synchronized with this channel. Lower values indicate more real-time synchronization.
Primary key for control

Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_audit. Business justification: Inventory control decisions (stop-sell, CTA/CTD, overbooking limits, LRA) are audited for brand standards compliance, revenue management policy adherence, and regulatory requirements. Links control to

Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Inventory controls (stop-sell, CTA/CTD, LOS restrictions) are often campaign-driven (flash sale requires 2-night minimum, member-exclusive rates with specific restrictions). Required for promotional r
Identifier of the distribution channel (OTA, GDS, Direct) to which this control applies. Null indicates control applies to all channels.

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Inventory controls are managed by revenue management departments that map to specific cost centers for labor allocation, system cost allocation, and P&L responsibility. Required for departmental budge
Identifier of the user who manually overrode RMS-generated controls. Null if control was not manually overridden.
Identifier of the property to which this inventory control applies.
Identifier of the rate plan to which this control applies. Null indicates control applies to all rate plans for the room type and date.
Identifier of the group block that this control is associated with. Null if control is not tied to a specific group reservation.
Identifier of the room type (e.g., Deluxe King, Standard Queen) to which this control applies.

Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: Inventory controls (sell limits, hurdles, stop-sell flags) are tier-differentiated in luxury hospitality. Properties set different availability rules for base vs platinum members. Revenue strategy req
Maximum number of days in advance that bookings can be made for this stay date. Null indicates no advance booking restriction.
Percentage of total sell limit allocated to this specific channel. Used for channel-level inventory gating. Sum across all channels for a date should not exceed 100%.
Average Daily Rate of the competitive set (STR comp set) for comparable room types on this stay date. Used for competitive benchmarking.
Current lifecycle status of this inventory control record. Active controls are enforced by PMS and CRS; inactive controls are ignored.
Date and time when this inventory control record was first created in the system.
Indicates whether new arrivals are prohibited on this date. True means guests cannot check in on this date, but existing reservations spanning this date are allowed.
Indicates whether departures are prohibited on this date. True means guests cannot check out on this date; used to enforce minimum stay through high-demand periods.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this record (hurdle_rate, forecast_adr, competitive_set_adr). Typically matches property base currency.
Date and time when this control becomes effective. Controls are typically pushed to distribution channels in advance of the stay date.
Date and time when this control expires and is no longer enforced. Null indicates control remains effective until explicitly superseded.
RMS-forecasted Average Daily Rate for this room type on this stay date. Expressed in property currency.
RMS-forecasted demand (number of rooms) for this room type on this stay date. Used to inform control decisions and diagnostic analysis.
RMS-forecasted occupancy percentage for this room type on this stay date. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 85.50 means 85.5% occupancy).
Minimum acceptable rate (ADR) below which the room type should not be sold for this date. Used by revenue management to protect yield. Expressed in property currency.
Indicates whether this room type participates in Last Room Availability programs with OTAs. True means OTAs can sell even when inventory is constrained, ensuring rate parity.
Maximum number of consecutive nights a guest can book when this date is the arrival date. Null indicates no maximum stay restriction.
Minimum number of days in advance that bookings must be made for this stay date. Used to prevent last-minute bookings during high-demand periods.
Minimum number of consecutive nights a guest must book when this date is the arrival date. Null or 1 indicates no minimum stay restriction.
Date and time when this inventory control record was last modified. Updated whenever any control parameter changes.
Absolute number of rooms above physical inventory that can be sold. Alternative to percentage-based overbooking. Null if percentage method is used.
Percentage above physical inventory that can be sold to account for expected cancellations and no-shows. For example, 10.00 means allow 110% of physical rooms to be sold.
Free-text explanation provided by the user when manually overriding RMS-generated controls. Required when override_user_id is populated.
Date and time when this control was last published to distribution channels (OTA, GDS, CRS). Null if control has not yet been distributed.
Business reason code explaining why this control was applied (e.g., HIGH_DEMAND, RENOVATION, GROUP_BLOCK, SEASONAL_CLOSURE). Used for diagnostic and reporting purposes.
Target Revenue Generation Index for this stay date. RGI measures property performance relative to competitive set. Value of 100 means at-market performance.
Maximum number of rooms of this type that can be sold for the stay date. Null indicates no limit. This is the primary inventory cap enforced by the PMS and CRS.
Origin of this control record. RMS indicates generated by IDeaS G3 RMS; manual indicates user override; API indicates external system integration.
The specific date for which this inventory control is effective. Controls are date-specific and define sell constraints for a single night of stay.
Indicates whether all sales are stopped for this room type on this date. True means no new bookings are accepted regardless of availability. Overrides all other controls.
Revenue management tolerance for walking guests (relocating to another property due to overbooking). Higher tolerance allows more aggressive overbooking.
Unique identifier for the overbooking policy configuration record.
Reference to the user who approved this overbooking policy for activation.
Reference to the hotel property to which this overbooking policy applies.
Reference to the specific room type (e.g., King Suite, Double Queen) governed by this overbooking policy.
Reference to the user who most recently modified this overbooking policy record.
The role or position authorized to approve and activate this overbooking policy, ensuring accountability for revenue and guest satisfaction impact.
The date and time when this overbooking policy was formally approved for use.
The expected cancellation rate (as a percentage) for this room type during the policy period, used to justify overbooking limits.
Defines which distribution channels (direct, OTA, GDS) are subject to this overbooking policy, enabling channel-specific inventory control.
The date and time when this overbooking policy record was first created in the system.
The date on which this overbooking policy expires or is no longer enforced. Null indicates an open-ended policy.
The date from which this overbooking policy becomes active and begins to govern inventory allocation.
The date and time when this overbooking policy record was most recently updated.
Maximum length of stay (in nights) allowed for reservations subject to this overbooking policy. Null indicates no maximum restriction.
Minimum length of stay (in nights) required for reservations subject to this overbooking policy. Null indicates no minimum restriction.
Indicates whether Last Room Availability is enabled for this overbooking policy, allowing distribution partners to sell inventory up to the overbooking limit.
The absolute maximum number of rooms that can be overbooked above physical capacity, calculated or manually set based on the overbooking limit configuration.
The expected no-show rate (as a percentage) for this room type during the policy period, factored into overbooking calculations.
Free-text notes providing additional context, rationale, or special instructions related to this overbooking policy.
Defines whether the overbooking limit is expressed as a percentage of physical capacity, an absolute room count, or dynamically calculated by the RMS.
The numeric value representing the maximum overbooking threshold. Interpretation depends on overbooking_limit_type (e.g., 10.00 for 10% or 5.00 for 5 rooms).
The actual number of physical rooms of this room type available at the property, used as the baseline for calculating overbooking limits.
Business identifier code for the overbooking policy, used for reference in revenue management and distribution systems.
Human-readable name describing the overbooking policy (e.g., 'High Season King Suite Overbooking', 'Standard Double Overbooking').
Current lifecycle status of the overbooking policy indicating whether it is actively enforced in reservation systems.
The priority level of this overbooking policy relative to other policies, used to resolve conflicts when multiple policies apply to the same inventory.
Number of higher-category rooms reserved as a buffer to accommodate overbooked guests through complimentary upgrades, reducing walk risk.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the walk cost estimate (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
Estimated average cost per walked guest (including relocation, compensation, and goodwill), used in revenue optimization calculations.
