The full legal name of the party as registered with governing authorities. For individuals, this is the full legal name; for organizations, this is the registered business name.
The date and time when this party record was last modified, used for audit trail and change tracking.
Free-form text field for capturing additional information, special instructions, or contextual details about the party that do not fit structured fields.
External business identifier or code assigned to the party for operational reference and integration with external systems.
Current lifecycle status of the party indicating whether the party is actively engaged in business, temporarily suspended, pending approval, or dissolved.
Classification of the party entity indicating whether it represents an individual person, corporate organization, government entity, vendor, partner, or franchise operator.
Standard number of days allowed for payment settlement with this party, used for accounts payable and receivable management.
The postal or ZIP code for the party's address, used for mail delivery and geographic segmentation.
Three-letter ISO currency code representing the preferred currency for financial transactions with this party.
Two-letter ISO language code representing the preferred language for communication with this party.
The name of the primary contact person at the party organization for business communications and relationship management.
The job title or role of the primary contact person at the party organization.
The primary email address for official communication with the party. This is the main contact channel for digital correspondence.
The primary telephone number for voice communication with the party. Includes country code and area code where applicable.

Official registration or incorporation number assigned by the jurisdiction where the party is registered. For organizations, this is the company registration number; for individuals operating as sole proprietors, this may be a business license number.
The nature of the business relationship between this party and the organization, such as property owner, franchise operator, vendor, strategic partner, contractor, or agent.
An alternate telephone number for the party, used as a backup contact method or for specific departments.
The state, province, or primary administrative subdivision where the party is located.
The stock exchange ticker symbol for the party if publicly traded, used for financial market identification.

Government-issued tax identification number for the party. For US entities, this may be an EIN (Employer Identification Number) or SSN (Social Security Number); for international entities, the equivalent national tax identifier.
The time zone in which the party primarily operates, using IANA time zone database naming convention (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London).
The commercial or trade name under which the party conducts business, if different from the legal name. Also known as DBA (Doing Business As) name.
The official website URL of the party, used for public information and digital presence.
Unique surrogate identifier for each individual line-item work scope record within a Property Improvement Plan (PIP). Primary key for this entity.

Foreign key linking to property.currency. Business justification: Each PIP item is costed in ONE currency. Normalize currency reference to master currency table. Remove currency_code string - will be retrieved via JOIN.

Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: PIP capital projects include F&B outlet renovations (kitchen equipment, dining room refresh, bar upgrades). Capital planning tracks outlet-specific PIP items for budget allocation and ROI analysis. St

Foreign key linking to property.meeting_space. Business justification: A PIP item CAN target a specific meeting space (e.g., ballroom renovation, AV equipment upgrade). This FK will be nullable - most PIP items target other areas. No columns to remove - property_area is
Reference to the parent Property Improvement Plan under which this line-item work scope belongs. Links the item to its governing PIP document and approved CapEx budget.

Foreign key linking to procurement.procurement_contract. Business justification: PIP capital improvement items frequently reference master procurement contracts for FF&E purchases. Enables contract compliance verification, negotiated pricing validation, and ensures PIP spending ad

Foreign key linking to property.facility. Business justification: A PIP item CAN target a specific facility (e.g., pool resurfacing, restaurant kitchen upgrade). This FK will be nullable - some PIP items are property-wide (e.g., roof replacement, HVAC system). No co
Reference to the hotel or resort property at which this PIP line-item work scope is being executed. Enables multi-property portfolio tracking of improvement spend.
Reference to the SAP S/4HANA vendor master record for the contractor responsible for this PIP item. Enables linkage to procurement contracts, insurance certificates, and payment terms.

The actual date on which this PIP line item was fully completed, inspected, and accepted. Used for brand compliance verification, final cost reconciliation, asset capitalization triggering in SAP, and PIP progress reporting to ownership and brand.

The actual invoiced and posted cost in the property's functional currency for this PIP line item upon completion or at current spend date. Compared against estimated_cost for budget variance reporting. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA AP invoice postings against the PIP work order.

The actual date on which work for this PIP line item commenced on-site. Compared against scheduled_start_date to identify schedule slippage and its downstream impact on brand compliance deadlines and hotel operations.

Name or identifier of the ownership, asset management, or corporate authority who approved the CapEx budget for this PIP item. Required for SOX financial controls documentation and CapEx authorization audit trail.

The date on which the CapEx budget for this PIP item was formally approved by the designated authority. Required for SOX financial controls audit trail and for establishing the authorized spend baseline.

The SAP S/4HANA fixed asset number assigned to the asset created or replaced by this PIP item upon capitalization. Links the PIP line item to the property's fixed asset register for depreciation scheduling and asset lifecycle management.

Indicates whether this PIP item is mandated by the brand franchisor as a condition of franchise agreement compliance (True) or is an ownership-initiated discretionary improvement (False). Brand-required items carry compliance deadlines and potential franchise termination risk if not completed.

The SAP S/4HANA internal order or WBS (Work Breakdown Structure) element code to which this PIP item's costs are allocated. Enables precise CapEx budget tracking, cost center assignment, and financial reporting per USALI and SOX requirements.

The date by which this PIP item must be completed to satisfy brand franchisor compliance requirements. Failure to meet this deadline may result in franchise agreement penalties, brand standard deficiency notices, or franchise termination proceedings.

The procurement contract or purchase order number in SAP S/4HANA governing the contractor's engagement for this PIP item. Used for invoice matching, lien waiver tracking, and contract compliance verification.

The name of the primary contractor or vendor responsible for executing the work scope for this PIP item. Used for contractor performance tracking, lien waiver management, and procurement compliance. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Vendor Master.
The timestamp when this PIP item record was first created in the data platform. Provides audit trail for record provenance and supports data lineage requirements in the Databricks Silver Layer.

Narrative explanation for why this PIP item has been deferred beyond its original scheduled completion date. Required for brand franchisor approval of deferrals and for ownership reporting on PIP execution delays. Populated only when item_status is 'deferred'.

The revised target completion date approved by the brand franchisor for a deferred PIP item. Replaces the original scheduled_completion_date as the binding compliance deadline when a deferral has been formally granted.

The budgeted or estimated cost in the property's functional currency for completing this PIP line item, as approved in the CapEx budget. Used for budget variance analysis, ownership reporting, and CapEx authorization tracking per USALI and SAP S/4HANA project accounting.

Classification of the PIP item into the FF&E (Furniture, Fixtures and Equipment) taxonomy per USALI and brand standards. Determines CapEx vs OpEx treatment, depreciation schedule, and procurement channel. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: furniture|fixtures|equipment|soft_goods|technology|signage|artwork|building_infrastructure|os_and_e — promote to reference product]

The SAP S/4HANA General Ledger account code for posting costs associated with this PIP item. Determines whether the expenditure is capitalized (balance sheet) or expensed (P&L) per USALI and IFRS/GAAP accounting treatment.

The date on which the brand quality assurance inspector conducted or is scheduled to conduct the formal inspection of this completed PIP item. Required for brand compliance certification and franchise agreement documentation.

The result of the brand quality assurance inspection for this PIP item. A failed or conditional pass outcome triggers a punch list of remediation items that must be resolved before the item can be closed for brand compliance purposes.

Indicates whether this PIP item requires a formal brand quality assurance inspection upon completion (True) before it can be marked as accepted and closed. Brand-mandated items typically require inspector sign-off as a condition of compliance certification.

Detailed narrative description of the specific work scope for this PIP line item, including materials, finishes, quantities, and brand standard requirements (e.g., 'Replace all guestroom soft goods including headboards, drapery, and carpet per brand standard BS-2024-GR-07').

Externally-known alphanumeric reference code assigned to this PIP line item, typically matching the numbering scheme in the brand-issued PIP document or SAP work order. Used for cross-referencing with brand inspectors, contractors, and CapEx approval documentation.

Current lifecycle status of this PIP line item within the execution workflow. Tracks progression from initial identification through approval, active construction, and final completion or deferral. Used for PIP compliance reporting to brand and ownership.

Indicates whether this PIP item addresses a life safety, fire code, ADA accessibility, or OSHA regulatory compliance requirement (True). Life safety items are treated as critical priority and may not be deferred without regulatory authority approval.

Brand-assigned or ownership-assigned priority ranking for this PIP item. Critical items typically represent brand standard violations or life-safety issues requiring immediate remediation. Drives sequencing of work execution and resource allocation.

The physical area or zone of the property affected by this PIP line item. Drives scheduling coordination with hotel operations to minimize guest impact. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: guestroom|lobby|corridor|exterior|food_and_beverage|meeting_space|fitness_center|pool|back_of_house|spa|parking|landscaping — promote to reference product]

Free-text notes documenting outstanding deficiencies, remediation requirements, or conditional items identified during brand inspection or internal quality review. Captures specific corrective actions required before final acceptance of this PIP item.

The quantity of units, rooms, fixtures, or items covered by this PIP line item's work scope (e.g., 120 guestrooms, 45 chairs, 3 elevators). Used for unit cost benchmarking, procurement quantity planning, and cost-per-unit analysis.

The number of guestrooms placed out of order (OOO) in the PMS as a direct result of this PIP item's work scope. Critical input for RevPAR impact modeling in IDeaS G3 RMS and for coordinating room inventory availability in Oracle OPERA PMS during renovation.

The planned date by which this PIP line item is expected to be fully completed and ready for inspection. Used for milestone tracking, brand compliance deadline management, and rooms-out-of-order (OOO) planning to minimize RevPAR impact.

The planned date on which work for this PIP line item is scheduled to commence. Used for construction phasing, contractor mobilization planning, and coordination with hotel operations to manage guest impact and room inventory availability.

Indicates whether this PIP item contributes to the property's sustainability or energy efficiency program (True), such as LED lighting upgrades, low-flow plumbing fixtures, or HVAC efficiency improvements. Supports ESG reporting and green certification tracking.
The unit of measure for the quantity field on this PIP item (e.g., each, room, sqft, sqm, linear_ft, lot). Required for accurate cost-per-unit benchmarking and procurement specification.

The timestamp when this PIP item record was most recently modified in the data platform. Used for incremental ETL processing, change data capture, and audit trail compliance in the Databricks Silver Layer.

Classification of the nature of work required for this PIP item. Distinguishes between full replacement, renovation/refresh, new installation, repair, technology upgrade, or regulatory compliance work. Drives cost estimation methodology and contractor selection.

Indicates whether this policy can be applied to group block reservations. Group policies often have different terms than individual FIT (Free Independent Traveler) bookings.

Indicates whether an advance deposit payment is required at time of booking under this policy. Common for non-refundable and group bookings.
Numeric sequence for ordering policies in user interfaces and booking engines. Lower numbers appear first. Used for presenting policies from most to least flexible.
Date after which this cancellation policy is no longer active for new reservations. Null indicates policy is currently active with no planned end date.
Date from which this cancellation policy becomes active and can be applied to new reservations. Part of policy lifecycle management.

Number of hours before scheduled arrival time during which a guest can cancel without penalty. Null indicates no free cancellation window.
Indicates whether a payment guarantee (credit card, deposit, or prepayment) is required to book under this cancellation policy. Typically true for stricter policies.
Concise, guest-friendly summary of the cancellation policy displayed during booking process. Typically 1-2 sentences highlighting key terms.
Internal staff notes and operational guidance for applying this cancellation policy. Not visible to guests. Used for training and exception handling.
Indicates whether this policy represents a non-refundable rate where no refund is provided under any circumstances. True for NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) policies.

Timestamp when this cancellation policy record was last updated. Tracks policy changes for revenue management analysis and compliance audit.

Percentage of total reservation value charged as penalty when penalty_type is percentage_of_stay. Value stored as decimal (e.g., 50.00 for 50%).

Detailed description of the cancellation policy terms and conditions, including guest-facing language explaining cancellation rules, deadlines, and penalties.
Human-readable name of the cancellation policy displayed to guests and staff. Examples: Flexible Cancellation, Moderate Cancellation, Strict Cancellation, Non-Refundable.
Current lifecycle status of the cancellation policy. Only active policies can be assigned to rate plans and reservations.
Classification tier of the cancellation policy indicating the level of flexibility offered to guests. Aligned with revenue management strategy and rate plan positioning.
Indicates whether this policy can be overridden by seasonal or event-specific cancellation rules during high-demand periods.
Unique identifier of this cancellation policy in the source operational system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation with PMS (Property Management System) or CRS (Central Reservation System).

Number of days before arrival when this rate plan is no longer available for booking. Used to close last-minute bookings for certain rate plans. Null indicates no end restriction.
Number of days before arrival when this rate plan becomes available for booking. Used to control early booking windows for promotional rates. Null indicates no start restriction.

Indicates whether this rate plan is available for booking through OTA channels (Expedia, Booking.com, etc.). Subject to rate parity agreements and channel management rules.
The percentage of the room rate paid as commission to the booking channel or agent. Typically 10-25% for OTAs, 10% for travel agents. Null if commissionable_flag is false.
Indicates whether bookings under this rate plan are eligible for travel agent or OTA commission. True for most OTA and GDS rates; false for net rates and direct promotional rates.
The date and time when this rate plan record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and lifecycle tracking.
The order in which this rate plan should be displayed to guests and agents in the booking interface. Lower numbers appear first. Used to prioritize high-value or strategic rate plans.

The method by which the reservation must be guaranteed. Credit card: hold on card; Deposit: partial payment required; Prepayment: full payment required; Direct bill: corporate account billing; None: no guarantee required.

Indicates whether this rate plan includes bundled amenities, services, or F&B components (e.g., breakfast included, spa credit, parking). True for package rates; false for room-only rates.

Date on which this booking source was first activated and made live for reservation acceptance in the CRS and PMS. Used for channel performance benchmarking (measuring ramp-up period) and historical channel mix analysis.

Technical connectivity method used to exchange availability, rates, and inventory (ARI) data between the hotel's CRS/PMS and this booking source. Determines integration complexity, latency, and update frequency for rate and inventory distribution.

Numeric level in the booking source attribution hierarchy used for revenue reporting roll-ups. Level 1 = top-level channel (e.g., OTA), Level 2 = sub-channel (e.g., OTA Desktop), Level 3 = specific source (e.g., Expedia Desktop US). Enables hierarchical channel mix analysis from property to brand level.

Current lifecycle status of the booking source record. 'active' = currently receiving reservations; 'inactive' = temporarily disabled; 'pending' = configured but not yet live; 'suspended' = paused due to contract or compliance issue; 'retired' = permanently decommissioned. Used for channel activation management in Sabre SynXis CRS.

Maximum advance booking window in days applicable to this booking source, as configured in the CRS. Defines the furthest point in advance that a reservation can be made through this channel. Used for long-range demand forecasting and group block management.

Minimum advance booking window in days applicable to this booking source, as configured in the CRS and RMS. Defines the earliest point before arrival that a reservation can be made through this channel. Used for demand forecasting and inventory control in IDeaS G3 RMS.

Code referencing the default cancellation policy applied to reservations booked through this source, as configured in Oracle OPERA PMS. Determines refundability rules (e.g., Non-Refundable Rate (NRR), flexible cancellation). Impacts revenue recognition and overbooking strategy.

High-level classification of the distribution channel type indicating whether the booking originates from a direct brand channel, an indirect intermediary (OTA/GDS/Wholesale), a voice channel (call center/reservation agent), or a group/MICE channel. Used for strategic channel mix reporting and direct booking ratio KPI tracking.

Defines the basis on which commission is calculated for this booking source: net_rate (commission on net room rate), gross_rate (commission on gross rate including taxes), flat_fee (fixed amount per booking), or per_segment (GDS segment fee per booking). Drives commission calculation logic in accounts payable processing.

Standard commission percentage payable to the booking source intermediary (OTA, travel agency, GDS) per confirmed reservation. Expressed as a percentage of the net room revenue. Used for cost-of-acquisition (COA) calculation, channel profitability analysis, and accounts payable commission processing in SAP S/4HANA. Null for direct/zero-commission channels.

Fixed monetary fee charged per booking or per GDS segment for this booking source, expressed in the property's base currency (USD). Applicable when connectivity_fee_applicable is True. Used alongside commission_rate to compute total cost of acquisition (COA) per channel.

Indicates whether a GDS or channel connectivity fee is charged per booking for this source, in addition to or instead of a commission. True for GDS sources with per-segment fees (Amadeus, Sabre, Travelport). Used for total cost-of-acquisition calculation and channel profitability reporting.

Expiration date of the distribution agreement or contract with this booking source partner. Null for open-ended agreements. Used for contract renewal alerts, commission rate expiry management, and channel deactivation planning.

Effective start date of the distribution agreement or contract with this booking source partner. Used for contract lifecycle management, commission rate validity tracking, and channel activation scheduling in SAP S/4HANA contract management.

Timestamp when this booking source record was first created in the data platform. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and reference data change management. Conforms to ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).

Source code as configured in Sabre SynXis CRS for this booking source. This is the operational identifier used during reservation creation and rate distribution. May differ from the internal source_code when CRS uses a legacy or partner-assigned code.

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which commissions and connectivity fees for this booking source are settled. Used for multi-currency commission processing in SAP S/4HANA accounts payable and foreign exchange reconciliation.

Date on which this booking source was deactivated or retired from the CRS and PMS. Null for currently active sources. Used for channel lifecycle management, historical reporting continuity, and audit trail maintenance.

Standard GDS identifier code for the booking source when the channel is a Global Distribution System participant. Applicable values include Amadeus (1A), Sabre (1S), Travelport Galileo (1G), Travelport Worldspan (1P). Null for non-GDS sources. Used for GDS connectivity reconciliation and GDS segment fee billing.

Defines the geographic reach of this booking source: global (worldwide distribution), regional (specific world region), domestic (single country), or property_specific (single property only). Used for channel distribution strategy and regional channel mix analysis.

business_glossary_term: International Air Transport Association (IATA) Agency Code
description: IATA-assigned numeric agency code for travel agency or OTA booking sources participating in IATA-governed distribution. Used for agency commission processing, GDS segment attribution, and travel agency performance reporting. Null for non-agency sources.

Frequency at which room inventory and rate availability are pushed or synchronized to this booking source channel. Real-time channels receive instant updates via API; others receive batch updates. Impacts overbooking risk management and rate parity compliance.

Indicates whether this booking source is a direct brand channel (True) or an indirect intermediary channel (False). Direct channels include brand website, brand app, call center, and walk-in. Used for direct booking ratio KPI tracking and cost-of-acquisition benchmarking against indirect channels.

Indicates whether this booking source has Last Room Availability (LRA) contractual rights, meaning the channel must be offered inventory even when only the last room is available. LRA is a common contractual obligation in corporate and GDS agreements. Impacts inventory control rules in IDeaS G3 RMS.

Defines the marketing attribution model applied to bookings from this source for campaign effectiveness measurement and marketing ROI (Return on Investment) reporting. Used by Salesforce CRM and marketing analytics to attribute revenue to marketing campaigns. 'none' for channels not subject to marketing attribution (e.g., GDS corporate).

Defines the payment collection model for this booking source: merchant (OTA collects payment from guest and remits net to hotel), agency (hotel collects and pays commission to channel), net_rate (channel marks up a net rate), or direct_bill (corporate direct billing). Impacts revenue recognition, accounts receivable, and cash flow management.

Source code as configured in Oracle OPERA PMS for this booking source. Used for front desk reservation attribution, daily operations reporting (DOR), and property-level channel mix analysis. May differ from CRS source code due to property-level configuration.

OTA or channel partner tier classification indicating the level of preferred partnership status. Preferred partners typically receive enhanced placement, lower commission rates, or co-marketing benefits. Used for OTA partnership management and channel strategy prioritization.

Indicates whether this booking source is subject to rate parity monitoring as part of the OTA contract or channel distribution agreement. When True, the Revenue Management System (RMS) and channel manager enforce rate parity rules to prevent undercutting on this channel. Critical for OTA contract compliance.

Specifies the rate parity obligation for this booking source: full_parity (rates must match all channels), narrow_parity (rates must match other OTAs but may differ from direct), or no_parity (no rate parity obligation). Drives rate distribution rules in Sabre SynXis CRS and IDeaS G3 RMS.

Numeric sort order for displaying booking source records in PMS, CRS, and reporting tool dropdown lists and channel mix reports. Lower values appear first. Used for UI configuration in Oracle OPERA PMS and Sabre SynXis CRS administration screens.

Standardized sub-channel category classifying the booking source by interface type and traveler segment. The scheduled guest departure date for the stay associated with this booking. Combined with check_in_date to derive length of stay (LOS) and average length of stay (ALOS) metrics.

The commission rate percentage applied by the distribution channel for this booking (e.g., 15.00 for 15%). Used for commission structure analysis, channel cost benchmarking, and contract compliance monitoring.

The per-booking connectivity or transaction fee charged by the channel technology provider (e.g., GDS segment fee, CRS transaction fee). Included in total cost-of-acquisition calculation alongside commission.

The corporate account or negotiated rate code associated with this booking, if applicable. Used for corporate channel performance analysis, negotiated rate compliance monitoring, and corporate account revenue attribution.

The timestamp when this channel booking transaction record was first created in the Silver layer data product. RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED per TRANSACTION_HEADER canonical role. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and ETL reconciliation.
The unique booking identifier assigned by the Central Reservation System (Sabre SynXis CRS). Used for cross-system reconciliation between CRS and PMS booking records.

The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the booking was transacted by the distribution channel (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency revenue reconciliation and FX conversion in financial reporting. Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET per TRANSACTION_HEADER canonical role.

The segment identifier assigned by the Global Distribution System (GDS) for bookings originating through GDS channels (e.g., Sabre, Amadeus, Travelport). Used for GDS billing reconciliation and travel agent attribution.

The total gross value of the booking as received from the distribution channel, inclusive of all room charges before deduction of channel commissions or fees. Expressed in the booking currency. Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET per TRANSACTION_HEADER canonical role.
Indicates whether this booking has been cancelled (True = cancelled, False = active). Used for channel-level cancellation rate analysis and net booking volume reporting.
Indicates whether this booking has been modified after initial confirmation (True = modified, False = original booking). Used to track modification rates by channel and assess channel data quality.

Indicates whether the booked rate is compliant with the rate parity agreement for this channel (True = compliant, False = parity violation detected). Critical for OTA contract compliance monitoring and channel rate parity management.

The timestamp when this channel booking transaction record was last updated in the Silver layer data product. RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED per TRANSACTION_HEADER canonical role. Used for change data capture, incremental load processing, and audit compliance.

The number of days between the booking timestamp and the check-in date. Stored as a business attribute to support lead time distribution analysis by channel, demand forecasting, and last-minute booking pattern identification.

The number of nights for the booked stay, derived from check-in and check-out dates. Stored as a business attribute to support LOS-based rate restriction analysis, minimum stay controls, and ALOS reporting without requiring date arithmetic at query time.

The market segment classification code assigned to this booking (e.g., TRANS, GROUP, CORP, LEISURE, GOVT). Used for segmented revenue analysis, channel-to-segment attribution, and STR benchmarking alignment.

The net revenue retained by the property after deduction of channel commission from the gross booking value. Primary metric for channel contribution analysis and cost-of-acquisition benchmarking. Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET per TRANSACTION_HEADER canonical role.
The total number of guests included in this booking. Used for occupancy analysis, per-guest revenue calculations, and channel demand pattern reporting.
The number of rooms included in this booking transaction. Typically 1 for FIT (Free Independent Traveler) bookings; may be greater for group or multi-room bookings received through a channel.
The booking confirmation number assigned by the Online Travel Agency (OTA) platform (e.g., Booking.com, Expedia). Used for reconciliation with OTA partner systems and commission invoice matching.

The externally-communicated confirmation number generated by the Property Management System (Oracle OPERA PMS) for this booking. Serves as the primary guest-facing booking reference. BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER per TRANSACTION_HEADER canonical role.

Classification of the rate type applied to this booking. BAR (Best Available Rate), LRA (Last Room Availability), NRR (Non-Refundable Rate), corporate, package, promotional, or group. The contractual commission rate applied to the gross booking value, expressed as a decimal fraction (e.g., 0.15 = 15%). Sourced from the applicable commission schedule. Used to calculate the commission amount and for rate parity and cost-of-acquisition analysis.

Classification of the commission arrangement: 'standard' = default contractual rate; 'override' = manually overridden rate approved by revenue management; 'promotional' = time-limited promotional rate; 'group' = group booking commission; 'negotiated' = bilaterally negotiated rate outside standard schedule.

The GDS or channel connectivity fee charged by the distribution technology provider (e.g., Sabre SynXis transaction fee) associated with this booking, in local currency. Included in total cost-of-acquisition (CoA) reporting alongside commission.

Timestamp when this commission accrual record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for audit trail and SOX financial controls compliance. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.

Free-text notes describing the nature of a commission dispute with the OTA or GDS partner. Populated when accrual_status is 'disputed'. Supports the channel dispute resolution workflow and AP reconciliation process.

The exchange rate applied to convert the local currency commission amount to the base currency at the time of accrual. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA treasury rates. Required for accurate multi-currency financial reporting and audit trail.

The SAP S/4HANA GL account code to which the commission expense is posted (e.g., USALI Schedule 6 - Sales and Marketing / Distribution Costs). Ensures correct financial statement classification per USALI and GAAP/IFRS.

The total gross booking value of the reservation on which commission is calculated, in the local transaction currency. Represents the full room revenue amount before any discounts or adjustments. Forms the base for commission rate application. Part of TRANSACTION_HEADER MONETARY_TRIPLET.
The date on the commission invoice received from the OTA or GDS partner. Used for AP aging analysis, payment due date calculation, and period-end accrual reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA.

The OTA or GDS partner's invoice reference number received for commission payment. Used to match accrued commission liabilities against received invoices in SAP S/4HANA AP for three-way reconciliation. Null until invoice is received.

Indicates whether the booking is eligible for commission payment (True) or is a non-commissionable booking (False), such as direct loyalty redemptions, staff rates, or complimentary stays. Drives commission accrual logic and AP posting.

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the transaction currency in which the booking was made and commission is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency commission reconciliation and FX conversion. Part of TRANSACTION_HEADER MONETARY_TRIPLET.

The market segment classification of the booking as defined in Oracle OPERA PMS (e.g., TRANSIENT, GROUP, CONTRACT, WHOLESALE). Used to analyze commission costs by market segment and align with USALI revenue reporting categories.
The actual date on which the commission payment was disbursed to the OTA or GDS partner. Populated when accrual_status transitions to 'paid'. Used for AP reconciliation and cash flow reporting.
The contractual due date by which the commission payment must be disbursed to the OTA or GDS partner per the commission schedule terms. Used for AP cash flow planning and SLA compliance monitoring.

The payment transaction reference or bank transfer reference number from SAP S/4HANA AP when the commission is disbursed. Enables end-to-end traceability from accrual to cash payment for audit and reconciliation purposes.

The reason code for reversing a previously accrued commission. Populated when accrual_status is 'reversed' or 'cancelled'. Supports AP reconciliation and channel dispute management. The number of room nights associated with the booking for which commission is being accrued. Used to calculate CPOR (Cost Per Occupied Room) and to normalize commission costs across bookings of varying LOS (Length of Stay).

The total distribution cost for this booking, combining commission_amount_local and connectivity_fee_amount. Represents the all-in cost of acquiring the booking through this channel. Used for channel profitability and net RevPAR analysis.

Timestamp of the most recent modification to this commission accrual record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports change tracking, reconciliation audit trails, and SOX financial controls. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category.
Unique surrogate identifier for each commission or fee schedule record in the channel distribution cost management system. Primary key for the commission_schedule data product.

Reference to the distribution channel partner (OTA, GDS, CRS, channel manager, direct booking engine) to which this commission or fee schedule applies. Links to the channel partner master record.

Reference to the specific property or hotel to which this commission schedule applies. Null or a portfolio-level sentinel value indicates the schedule applies across all properties (chain-wide agreement).

Indicates whether commission and fee accruals under this schedule are automatically calculated and posted to the GL upon booking confirmation (True) or require manual accrual processing (False). Supports automated AP accrual workflows in SAP S/4HANA.

Frequency at which the channel partner invoices the property for commissions or fees: monthly (most common for OTA commissions), weekly, per_booking (real-time deduction), quarterly, annual (common for connectivity subscription fees). Drives AP accrual scheduling and cash flow forecasting.

The unit basis on which the commission or fee is calculated and accrued: per_booking (one charge per reservation), per_room_night (charge per occupied room night), per_segment (GDS per-segment charge), per_transaction (CRS per-transaction charge), per_period (fixed charge per billing period regardless of volume). The calendar date on which this commission or fee schedule expires and is no longer applicable. Null indicates an open-ended agreement with no defined expiry. Used for schedule versioning and contract renewal tracking.

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the flat fee or tiered threshold amounts are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency AP reconciliation and cost-of-acquisition reporting.

Classification of the cost type captured by this schedule: commission (percentage of booking revenue paid to OTA/agent), per_segment_fee (GDS per-segment charge), per_transaction_fee (CRS per-booking transaction fee), connectivity_fee (channel manager or switch connectivity charge), subscription_fee (fixed periodic platform fee), override_commission (negotiated override above standard rate). Drives AP coding and cost-of-acquisition (COA) categorization.

Fixed monetary fee amount per booking, segment, transaction, or billing period, expressed in the schedule currency. Applicable when fee_structure is 'flat' or 'per_unit'. The General Ledger account code in SAP S/4HANA to which commission and fee expenses are posted for financial reporting under USALI. Ensures correct P&L classification of distribution costs (e.g., OTA commission expense, GDS fee expense) per USALI departmental accounting.
Minimum Length of Stay (LOS) in nights required for this commission schedule to apply. Null indicates no LOS restriction. Supports commission scoping for extended-stay or minimum-stay rate plans.

Market segment code (e.g., leisure transient, corporate, group, wholesale) to which this commission schedule is scoped. Null indicates the schedule applies across all market segments for the channel partner. Aligns with Oracle OPERA PMS market segment configuration.

Contractual cap on the commission or fee amount payable per booking or per billing period. Applicable for schedules with a commission ceiling clause. Expressed in fee_currency_code. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.

Contractual minimum commission or fee amount payable per booking or per billing period, regardless of the calculated percentage amount. Applicable for schedules with a commission floor clause. Expressed in fee_currency_code. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.

Name or identifier of the internal team or individual (e.g., Revenue Management, Channel Distribution, Procurement) responsible for negotiating this commission schedule. Used for accountability tracking and contract ownership.

Free-text field for capturing additional context, special contractual conditions, negotiation history notes, or exceptions associated with this commission schedule. Not used for structured data; intended for operational reference by Revenue Management and AP teams.

Number of days from invoice receipt within which payment to the channel partner is due (e.g., 30 = Net 30). Used in AP aging analysis and cash flow management per SAP S/4HANA payment terms configuration.

Indicates whether rate parity compliance is contractually required and actively monitored for bookings under this commission schedule (True = rate parity clause in effect). Supports rate parity monitoring workflows and OTA contract compliance management.

Defines the revenue component on which the commission percentage is calculated: room_revenue (room charges only), total_revenue (all charges including F&B and ancillaries), net_revenue (after discounts and taxes), gross_revenue (before deductions), f_and_b_revenue (food and beverage charges only). Critical for accurate commission accrual and invoice reconciliation.

Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this commission or fee schedule, used in AP reconciliation, contract references, and channel partner communications (e.g., OTA-EXP-COMM-2024, GDS-SAB-SEG-FEE).

Human-readable descriptive name for the commission or fee schedule, used in reporting dashboards, AP workflows, and channel profitability analysis (e.g., 'Expedia Standard OTA Commission FY2024', 'Sabre GDS Per-Segment Fee Q1 2024').

Current lifecycle state of the commission schedule: active (currently in force), inactive (temporarily suspended), pending (approved but not yet effective), expired (past effective_until date), terminated (cancelled before expiry), draft (under negotiation, not yet approved). Drives AP processing eligibility and accrual inclusion.
Commission rate percentage applicable when booking volume or revenue falls within the Tier 1 threshold band in a tiered fee structure. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.

Lower volume or revenue threshold (in booking count or currency amount) that triggers the Tier 1 commission or fee rate in a tiered fee structure. Applicable when fee_structure is 'tiered'. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.
Commission rate percentage applicable when booking volume or revenue falls within the Tier 2 threshold band in a tiered fee structure. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.

Upper volume or revenue threshold that triggers the Tier 2 commission or fee rate in a tiered fee structure. Applicable when fee_structure is 'tiered'. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.

The calendar date on which this connectivity fee schedule was formally approved by the authorized approver. Used for audit trail, financial controls compliance, and contract lifecycle management.

The frequency at which the connectivity fee is billed by the technology provider. Per-transaction and per-segment fees are charged at the time of each booking event. Monthly, quarterly, and annual fees are charged on a recurring fixed schedule regardless of transaction volume.

The calendar date on which this connectivity fee schedule expires and ceases to be applied. Null for open-ended fee schedules with no defined end date. Used for contract lifecycle management and fee schedule versioning.

The base monetary amount of the connectivity fee as specified in the fee schedule. For flat-rate and per-transaction fees, this is the fixed charge. For percentage-based fees, this field stores the rate value (e.g., 0.0350 for 3.50%). Currency is defined in the fee_currency field.

CREATE indicates a new allocation was established; UPDATE modifies an existing allocation; CLOSE applies a stop-sell or close-out; REOPEN lifts a restriction; RELEASE returns unsold inventory to the pool; OVERRIDE applies a manual revenue management override.
Current operational status of this channel inventory allocation. Active allocations are enforced by the channel management system.
The total number of room nights (units) assigned to this channel for the specified stay date range and room type. Represents the allotment block size or sell limit granted to the channel.

Externally-known alphanumeric reference number assigned by the Central Reservation System (CRS) or Property Management System (PMS) to uniquely identify this inventory allocation action. Used for cross-system reconciliation between Sabre SynXis CRS and Oracle OPERA PMS.

Current lifecycle state of the inventory allocation record. ACTIVE indicates the allocation is live and controlling channel availability; INACTIVE means it has been superseded; EXPIRED means the stay date range has passed; CANCELLED means it was manually revoked; PENDING means it is awaiting activation.
Method by which inventory is allocated to this channel for this room type. POOLED = shared inventory pool, ALLOCATED = fixed allocation, FREESALE = unlimited access, BLOCKED = no access.

Classification of the inventory allotment method applied to this channel. FREE_SELL allows the channel to sell without a fixed block; ALLOCATION assigns a fixed block of rooms; STOP_SELL halts sales on the channel; CLOSE_OUT removes all availability; ON_REQUEST requires manual confirmation before booking is confirmed.
The username or employee identifier of the revenue manager or system user who applied this inventory control action. Used for audit trail, accountability, and change management reporting.

The number of room nights currently available for sale on this channel, calculated as allocated_units minus consumed_units. Represents the real-time sellable inventory for the channel on the specified stay dates.
The number of bookings cancelled on this channel allocation since the last reporting snapshot. Used to track net pickup and assess channel-level cancellation patterns for demand forecasting.
Percentage of total room type inventory allocated to this channel. Used for allocation-based inventory management strategies.

The category of distribution channel for this allocation. OTA (Online Travel Agency), GDS (Global Distribution System), DIRECT_WEB (brand.com), VOICE (call center), GSO (Global Sales Office), WHOLESALE (tour operators/wholesalers), METASEARCH (Google, Trivago, etc.). Supports channel mix optimization analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ota|gds|direct_web|voice|gso|wholesale|metasearch — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

The commission rate (expressed as a decimal, e.g., 0.15 for 15%) payable to the distribution channel for bookings made under this allocation. Used for cost of acquisition analysis and channel profitability reporting.

The fixed or per-transaction connectivity fee charged by the GDS or CRS for distributing inventory through this channel. Expressed in the property's local currency. Used for total cost of distribution analysis.
The number of allocated room nights that have been booked or consumed by the channel as of the last system update. Used to calculate remaining availability and monitor channel pickup performance.
Timestamp when this allocation record was created in the system.
The date and time when this inventory allocation record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation timestamp for data lineage and compliance purposes.

The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to financial fields (connectivity_fee) in this allocation record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent multi-currency reporting across global properties.

The number of days between the allocation action date and the stay_date_from (booking window). Used in revenue management analysis to understand how far in advance inventory controls are being applied relative to the arrival date.
Date when this channel inventory allocation configuration expires. Null for currently active allocations.
Date when this channel inventory allocation configuration becomes effective.

The date and time when this inventory allocation action became effective and was applied to the channel. Represents the business event timestamp when the allocation, stop-sell, or restriction was activated in the CRS.

Indicates whether arrivals are blocked on the first date of the stay period for this channel and room type. When TRUE, guests cannot check in on the stay_date_from date, preventing new bookings that start on that date.
Indicates whether departures are blocked on the stay_date_to date for this channel and room type. When TRUE, guests cannot check out on that date, preventing bookings that end on that date.

Indicates whether this inventory allocation action was automatically generated by the Revenue Management System (IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS) as opposed to being manually entered by a revenue manager. Supports analysis of automation adoption and RMS recommendation acceptance rates.

Indicates whether a stop-sell control has been applied to this channel for the specified stay dates and room type. When TRUE, the channel is prevented from accepting new bookings regardless of remaining available units.
Timestamp when this allocation record was last updated.

Indicates whether Last Room Availability (LRA) is contractually required for this channel. When TRUE, the channel must be offered the last available room at the property, typically a contractual obligation with certain OTA or corporate accounts.
The date and time when this inventory allocation action was lifted, reversed, or superseded. Null if the allocation is still active. Used to track the full lifecycle of each inventory control action.
Indicates whether Last Room Availability is enabled for this room-type-channel combination, overriding property-level LRA settings.
The maximum number of nights a guest is permitted to stay under this allocation. Enforced when restriction_type is MAX_LOS. Null indicates no maximum stay restriction is applied.
Channel-specific maximum length of stay restriction for this room type, overriding property-level defaults.
The minimum number of nights a guest must stay to be eligible to book under this allocation. Enforced when restriction_type is MIN_LOS. A value of 1 means no effective minimum restriction.
Channel-specific minimum length of stay requirement for this room type, overriding property-level defaults.

The number of additional room nights above the physical inventory that this channel is permitted to sell as part of the property's overbooking strategy. A value of 0 means no overbooking is permitted on this channel.
Maximum overbooking percentage allowed for this room type on this channel. Used for revenue management optimization.
The number of new bookings received on this channel allocation since the last reporting snapshot. Supports pickup reporting and demand forecasting in the Revenue Management System (RMS).
Priority ranking for this channel when allocating inventory for this room type. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.
Indicates whether rate parity rules are actively enforced for this room type on this channel.

Indicates whether rate parity compliance is enforced for this channel allocation. When TRUE, the allocated inventory must be offered at rates consistent with other channels per rate parity agreements. Used for OTA contract compliance monitoring.

Standardized code identifying the business reason for the inventory control action (e.g., HIGH_DEMAND, GROUP_BLOCK, OVERBOOKING_PROTECTION, RATE_PARITY_VIOLATION, CHANNEL_PERFORMANCE, MAINTENANCE). Used for revenue management analysis and audit reporting. Free-text narrative providing additional context for the inventory control action beyond the standardized reason_code. Captures specific business justification entered by the revenue manager or system.

The date on which any unsold allocated inventory is released back to the general property inventory pool. After this date, the channel no longer holds exclusive rights to the allocated units, enabling the property to sell them through other channels.

The type of booking restriction applied to this inventory allocation. MIN_LOS enforces a minimum Length of Stay (LOS); MAX_LOS enforces a maximum LOS; CLOSED_TO_ARRIVAL (CTA) prevents new arrivals on the specified date; CLOSED_TO_DEPARTURE (CTD) prevents departures; FULL_PATTERN_LENGTH requires exact stay duration; NONE means no restriction is applied.

The maximum number of room nights the channel is permitted to sell regardless of total property availability. Acts as a cap to prevent overselling on a specific channel and supports channel mix optimization.
The first date of the stay period for which this inventory allocation is effective. Defines the start of the date range over which the allocated room nights apply.
The last date of the stay period for which this inventory allocation is effective. Defines the end of the date range over which the allocated room nights apply.
The date and time when this inventory allocation record was last modified. Tracks the most recent change to the allocation for audit trail and change management purposes.

Monotonically incrementing version counter for this inventory allocation record. Incremented each time the allocation is modified, enabling optimistic concurrency control and historical version tracking in the Silver layer.

Reference to the master wholesale contract agreement under which this allotment is governed, including commercial terms and SLA obligations.

Foreign key linking to channel.gds_connection. Business justification: Wholesale allotments may be distributed via GDS networks (e.g., tour operator allotments loaded into Sabre for travel agent booking). Many allotments per GDS connection. Removes redundant gds_code whi
Reference to the wholesale partner (tour operator, bed bank, or travel consortium) with whom this allotment is contracted.
Reference to the hotel or resort property to which this allotment block applies, as managed in Oracle OPERA PMS.
Reference to the specific room type (e.g., Deluxe King, Standard Double) included in this wholesale allotment block.

Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Wholesale allotments may be distributed through specific channels (e.g., a tour operator allotment sold via a specific distribution channel). Many allotments per channel. Removes redundant channel_cod
Externally-known alphanumeric reference code assigned to this allotment block, used in communications with the wholesale partner and in the Central Reservation System (CRS) via Sabre SynXis.

Current lifecycle state of the wholesale allotment block. 'active' indicates rooms are available for the wholesaler to sell; 'released' indicates the release period has passed and unsold rooms have been returned to general inventory; 'suspended' indicates temporarily blocked by revenue management. Classification of the allotment commitment model. 'hard_block' means rooms are guaranteed and held regardless of demand; 'soft_block' means rooms are held but can be reclaimed by the property; 'free_sale' means the wholesaler can sell without prior confirmation; 'on_request' means each booking requires property confirmation.
Last date by which the wholesale partner must confirm bookings against this allotment block. After this date, unconfirmed allotment rooms may be released or renegotiated.
Number of reservations cancelled against this allotment block by the wholesale partner. Used to monitor wholesaler booking behaviour and assess wash factor for future allotment sizing.

Commission percentage payable to the wholesale partner or intermediary on consumed allotment bookings, where applicable. Expressed as a percentage of the contracted net rate. Some wholesale models are net-rate only with no commission.
Technical connectivity method used by the wholesale partner to access and consume this allotment. Determines how allotment updates and booking confirmations are exchanged.
Number of room-nights from the allotment block that have been picked up (booked) by the wholesale partner to date. Updated in near-real-time from the CRS and PMS.
Date from which the wholesale allotment agreement becomes commercially binding. Aligns with the master contract effective date in SAP S/4HANA.
Date on which the wholesale allotment agreement expires and the wholesaler's right to sell rooms under this block terminates. Nullable for evergreen agreements.
Total number of room-nights contracted with the wholesale partner for the defined stay period and room type. This is the original block size as agreed in the wholesale contract.

The pre-agreed net rate (exclusive of markup) payable by the wholesale partner per room per night under this allotment contract. This is the wholesale net rate model — the wholesaler adds their own markup before selling to the end consumer. Expressed in the contract currency.
Timestamp when this wholesale allotment record was first created in the source system or lakehouse Silver layer. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the contracted net rate and any financial terms in this allotment (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Aligns with the contract currency in SAP S/4HANA.
Number of rooms allocated per night within the stay period. Used when the allotment is expressed as a per-night room count rather than a total room-night block.
Indicates whether arrivals are blocked on the stay_date_from under this allotment. A Closed to Arrival (CTA) restriction prevents new check-ins on specific dates while allowing through-stays.

Indicates whether the allotment has been closed to new bookings by the revenue management team, overriding remaining availability. When True, the wholesaler cannot make new reservations against this block regardless of remaining units.

Indicates whether this wholesale allotment is subject to Last Room Availability (LRA) obligations, meaning the wholesaler must be offered the last available room at the contracted rate. A key commercial term in premium wholesale contracts.
Timestamp of the most recent modification to this wholesale allotment record, whether from a source system update or a manual override by the revenue management team.
Revenue management market segment code assigned to bookings originating from this wholesale allotment (e.g., WHOLE, TOUR, CONSOR). Used for USALI-compliant revenue segmentation and STR benchmarking.
Maximum number of nights permitted for a single booking under this wholesale allotment. Used to prevent excessively long stays at contracted net rates during peak demand periods.

Minimum number of nights a guest must stay for a booking to be accepted under this wholesale allotment. A Length of Stay (LOS) restriction applied at the allotment level to protect revenue during high-demand periods.
Free-text operational notes entered by the revenue management or channel distribution team regarding special conditions, exceptions, or commercial context for this allotment block.

Number of individual reservations booked against this allotment block by the wholesale partner. Distinct from consumed_units (room-nights) — a single multi-night booking increments pickup_count by 1 but consumed_units by the number of nights.
Published Best Available Rate (BAR) or rack rate for the room type at the property, used as the reference rate for calculating the wholesale discount depth and monitoring rate parity obligations.

Indicates whether this allotment's contracted net rate (after wholesaler markup) is expected to comply with rate parity obligations. True means parity monitoring is active for this allotment. Used in channel distribution compliance reporting.

Calculated date on which unsold allotment rooms are automatically released back to general inventory, derived from stay_date_from minus release_period_days. Stored for operational scheduling and revenue management triggers.

Number of days prior to the arrival date by which unsold allotment rooms must be released back to the property's general inventory. A key commercial term in wholesale contracts — e.g., a 21-day release means rooms not sold 21 days before check-in revert to the hotel.

Number of room-nights still available for the wholesale partner to sell within this allotment block. Calculated as contracted_allotment_size minus consumed_units, maintained as a stored operational field for real-time availability checks.
First date of the stay period covered by this allotment block. Defines the start of the contracted inventory window during which the wholesaler may sell rooms.
Last date of the stay period covered by this allotment block. Defines the end of the contracted inventory window. Inclusive of this date.

Historical cancellation and no-show rate expressed as a percentage of contracted allotment, used to adjust effective allotment sizing in revenue forecasting. Derived from historical performance of this wholesaler-property-room type combination.

Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Distribution channels often have primary marketing campaigns driving bookings. This FK enables attribution analysis and campaign performance tracking by channel. Essential for marketing ROI measuremen

Date on which this distribution channel was first activated and began accepting live bookings. Distinct from contract_start_date which reflects the commercial agreement date. Used for channel onboarding tracking and performance benchmarking from go-live.

Indicates whether this channel is eligible to display and sell the Best Available Rate (BAR) — the lowest publicly available rate at the time of booking. True = BAR rates are distributed to this channel; False = channel receives only specific contracted or negotiated rates. Drives rate distribution strategy in IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis CRS.

Per-transaction booking fee charged by the channel in addition to or instead of a commission percentage, expressed in USD. Common in GDS channels (e.g., GDS segment fee per booking). Used in cost-of-acquisition and channel profitability analysis.

Code referencing the default cancellation policy applied to bookings made through this channel (e.g., '24H_FREE', '48H_PENALTY', 'NON_REFUNDABLE'). Cancellation policies may differ by channel based on contractual agreements. Managed in Oracle OPERA PMS and Sabre SynXis CRS.

High-level grouping of the channel by booking relationship: DIRECT = guest books directly with the property (web, mobile, voice, corporate direct); INDIRECT = third-party intermediary involved (OTA, GDS, wholesaler, metasearch); OPAQUE = rate and property identity concealed until after booking (e.g., Hotwire, Priceline opaque). Drives cost-of-acquisition analysis and channel mix strategy.

Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the distribution channel across enterprise systems including Sabre SynXis CRS, Oracle OPERA PMS, and IDeaS G3 RMS. Used for channel mapping and cross-system reconciliation.

Free-text description of the distribution channel, including its market positioning, target guest segment, special contractual terms, or operational notes. Used for channel management documentation and onboarding reference.

Human-readable name of the distribution channel (e.g., 'Expedia Partner Central', 'Booking.com Extranet', 'Sabre GDS', 'Direct Web', 'Voice Reservations'). Used in reporting, dashboards, and channel mix analysis.

Current operational lifecycle status of the distribution channel. active = channel is live and accepting bookings; inactive = channel is configured but not currently selling; suspended = channel temporarily halted (e.g., rate parity violation, connectivity issue); pending_activation = channel onboarded but not yet live; decommissioned = channel permanently retired.

Categorical classification of the distribution channel by its market segment and technology interface. OTA = Online Travel Agency (e.g., Expedia, Booking.com); GDS = Global Distribution System (e.g., Sabre, Amadeus, Galileo); DIRECT_WEB = brand.com web booking; DIRECT_MOBILE = brand mobile app; VOICE = call center reservations; CORPORATE_DIRECT = negotiated corporate accounts; WHOLESALER = tour operators and bed banks; METASEARCH = Google Hotel Ads, Trivago. The commercial model under which commission is calculated for this channel. NET_RATE = channel marks up a net rate provided by the property; GROSS_RATE = commission deducted from the gross rate charged to the guest; MARKUP = channel applies a markup over the property's net rate; FLAT_FEE = fixed fee per booking regardless of rate. Critical for accurate revenue and cost-of-acquisition reporting.

Standard commission percentage charged by this distribution channel on each booking, expressed as a percentage of the room revenue (e.g., 15.00 for 15%). Drives cost-of-acquisition calculations and net revenue reporting per USALI standards. Confidential as it reflects negotiated commercial terms.

Impacts SLA and rate update latency.

Indicates whether property content (room descriptions, images, amenities, policies) is actively distributed to this channel via a content API or content management system. True = content is pushed/synced to this channel; False = content is managed manually or not distributed. Relevant for OTA and metasearch channels.

Date on which the commercial agreement with this distribution channel expires or is scheduled to terminate. Null for evergreen/open-ended agreements. Used for contract renewal management and channel lifecycle planning. Stored in yyyy-MM-dd format.

Date on which the commercial agreement with this distribution channel became effective. Used for contract lifecycle management, commission audit trails, and regulatory compliance. Stored in yyyy-MM-dd format.

Timestamp when this distribution channel record was first created in the enterprise data platform. Stored in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and record lifecycle management.

Channel identifier as configured in the Sabre SynXis Central Reservation System (CRS). Used for cross-system reconciliation between the enterprise data lakehouse and the CRS source of record. Enables traceability of bookings back to the originating CRS channel configuration.

Date on which this distribution channel was deactivated or decommissioned. Null for currently active channels. Used for channel lifecycle management, historical reporting, and audit trails. Stored in yyyy-MM-dd format.

business_glossary_term: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Data Processor Flag
description: Indicates whether this distribution channel acts as a GDPR data processor for guest personal data collected during the booking process. True = a Data Processing Agreement (DPA) is in place with this channel; False = channel does not process personal data on behalf of the property. Required for GDPR compliance and data governance.

Two-letter GDS chain code assigned to the hotel brand or property group within Global Distribution Systems (Sabre, Amadeus, Galileo/Travelport, Worldspan). Required for GDS-type channels to route availability and rate requests correctly. Null for non-GDS channels.

Geographic reach of this distribution channel. GLOBAL = channel operates worldwide; REGIONAL = channel serves a specific geographic region (e.g., Asia-Pacific, Europe); DOMESTIC = channel serves a single country; LOCAL = channel serves a specific city or metro area. Used for channel mix strategy and market segmentation analysis.

Method by which room inventory is allocated to this distribution channel. POOLED = channel draws from the shared central inventory pool (real-time); ALLOTMENT = a fixed block of rooms is pre-allocated to the channel; FREE_SALE = channel can sell without prior allocation up to a defined limit; ON_REQUEST = bookings require manual confirmation. Managed in IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis CRS.

Indicates whether this channel has Last Room Availability (LRA) rights — meaning the property must make the last available room accessible through this channel at the contracted rate. LRA is a common contractual obligation in corporate and GDS agreements. True = LRA applies; False = LRA does not apply.

Timestamp when this distribution channel record was most recently modified in the enterprise data platform. Stored in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format. Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and audit compliance.

Indicates whether bookings made through this channel are eligible for loyalty program points accrual and redemption. True = loyalty points are awarded and redeemable; False = bookings through this channel do not qualify for loyalty benefits (common for opaque OTA channels). Managed in Salesforce CRM Loyalty Management.

business_glossary_term: Meetings Incentives Conferences Exhibitions (MICE) Bookings Supported Flag
description: Indicates whether this distribution channel supports group and MICE (Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, Exhibitions) bookings in addition to transient reservations. True = MICE/group bookings can be sourced through this channel; False = transient bookings only. Relevant for event sales and Delphi by Amadeus integration.

Indicates whether Non-Refundable Rate (NRR) products are distributed through this channel. True = NRR fences are available on this channel; False = only flexible/refundable rates are distributed. Impacts revenue management yield strategy and cancellation policy enforcement.

Defines who collects payment from the guest for bookings made through this channel. HOTEL_COLLECT = property collects payment directly at check-in/check-out; CHANNEL_COLLECT = the OTA or intermediary collects payment and remits net to the property; SPLIT = partial payment collected by each party. Impacts accounts receivable and revenue recognition per USALI and GAAP/IFRS.

Indicates whether this distribution channel is certified as compliant with PCI DSS (Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard) for handling cardholder data. True = channel holds current PCI DSS certification; False = channel is not PCI compliant or certification has lapsed. Required for risk management and payment security governance.

ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the primary market served by this distribution channel (e.g., 'USA', 'GBR', 'DEU'). Used for geographic channel mix analysis, market penetration reporting, and regulatory compliance (e.g., GDPR for EU-based channels).

Numeric rank indicating the strategic priority of this channel in the distribution mix, where lower numbers indicate higher priority (e.g., 1 = highest priority, typically direct web). Used by revenue management to determine inventory release sequencing and rate strategy. Configured in IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis CRS.

Indicates whether this distribution channel contractually requires rate parity — i.e., the property must offer the same or lower rates on this channel as on any other channel. True = rate parity clause is active in the channel agreement; False = no rate parity obligation. Critical for revenue management compliance and rate strategy.

Specifies the scope of the rate parity obligation for this channel. BROAD = property must match rates across all channels including direct; NARROW = property must match rates on other OTAs but may offer lower rates on direct channels (common post-EU regulatory changes); NONE = no rate parity obligation. Drives revenue management and direct booking strategy.

Frequency at which the channel remits collected payments to the property for CHANNEL_COLLECT payment model bookings. Applicable only when payment_model is CHANNEL_COLLECT or SPLIT. Drives accounts receivable forecasting and cash flow management in SAP S/4HANA.

Comma-separated list of ISO 4217 three-letter currency codes supported by this distribution channel for booking and payment (e.g., 'USD,EUR,GBP,JPY'). Relevant for multi-currency rate distribution and foreign exchange management. Managed in Sabre SynXis CRS.

Foreign key linking to procurement.employee. Business justification: Channel contracts require employee ownership for accountability in contract management, renewal tracking, and performance monitoring. Standard practice in hospitality to assign contract ownership to s
Reference to the property or portfolio of properties covered by this contract. Null if the contract applies at the brand or enterprise level.
Indicates whether the channel is contractually entitled to receive the Best Available Rate (BAR) as defined in the Revenue Management System. Drives rate loading rules in Sabre SynXis CRS.
Code referencing the contracted cancellation and no-show policy applicable to bookings made through this channel (e.g., '24H', '48H', 'NON_REF'). Loaded into Oracle OPERA PMS and Sabre SynXis CRS.
Defines the revenue base on which the commission rate is applied: net rate (after discounts), gross rate (before discounts), room revenue only, or total revenue including F&B and ancillaries.

Base commission rate (expressed as a decimal, e.g., 0.1500 = 15%) payable to the channel partner per confirmed booking under this contract. Distinct from the commission_schedule product which captures tiered or conditional rate structures.

Fixed or per-transaction fee charged by the channel or GDS for technical connectivity and distribution services. Distinct from commission which is percentage-based. Used in cost-of-acquisition (COA) analysis.
Specifies how the connectivity fee is structured: per booking transaction, per GDS segment, annual flat fee, or monthly flat fee.

Free-text description of contractual obligations regarding property content (images, descriptions, amenities) that must be maintained on the channel platform, including minimum image counts, refresh frequency, and accuracy standards.
Human-readable descriptive name for the contract (e.g., 'Expedia 2025 Master Distribution Agreement — Americas').
Externally-known legal contract reference number assigned by the contracting parties or the legal department. Used for correspondence, audits, and dispute resolution.
Current lifecycle state of the contract. Drives whether the channel is eligible to receive inventory and rates via the CRS.

Classification of the commercial agreement by channel category. Determines applicable commission structures, rate parity obligations, and connectivity requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ota_distribution|gds_participation|direct_connect|wholesale|bed_bank|metasearch|corporate_negotiated — promote to reference product]
Timestamp when this contract record was first created in the system of record. Used for audit trail and SOX compliance.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all monetary values in this contract are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
Indicates whether the contract includes a data sharing or data processing agreement governing the exchange of guest and booking data with the channel partner. Required for GDPR Article 28 compliance.
ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the legal jurisdiction governing dispute resolution and contract interpretation.
Contractually agreed mechanism for resolving commercial disputes between the hotel and the channel partner.
Date on which the contract becomes legally binding and the channel is authorized to distribute inventory. Aligns with USALI period reporting.
Date on which the contract ceases to be binding. Null for evergreen contracts. Used to trigger renewal workflows and rate parity audits.

business_glossary_term: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Data Processing Agreement (DPA) Reference
description: Reference number or document identifier for the GDPR Data Processing Agreement (DPA) executed with the channel partner. Mandatory for EU-based OTA and GDS partnerships under GDPR Article 28.
Frequency at which commission invoices or settlement statements are issued between the hotel and the channel partner.

Indicates whether the contract includes a Last Room Availability (LRA) obligation, requiring the hotel to make the last available room accessible to this channel even when inventory is restricted for other channels.

Annual or contract-period monetary commitment for co-operative marketing spend with the channel partner (e.g., sponsored listings, display advertising on OTA platform). Expressed in the contract currency.
Indicates whether the contract permits the hotel to offer Non-Refundable Rates (NRR) through this channel. Relevant for revenue management strategy and cancellation policy governance.

Commercial payment model governing how the channel collects and remits payment: merchant model (channel collects from guest and remits net), agency model (hotel collects and pays commission), net rate (channel marks up a net rate), or wholesale.

business_glossary_term: Payment Card Industry (PCI) Compliance Confirmed Indicator
description: Indicates whether the channel partner has confirmed PCI DSS compliance as required by the contract terms for handling payment card data.

Tier designation within the OTA or GDS preferred partner program (e.g., Expedia Preferred Plus, Booking.com Preferred). Higher tiers typically confer better placement, lower commission rates, or co-op marketing benefits.

Indicates whether the contract includes a rate parity obligation requiring the hotel to offer the channel the same or better rates as other distribution channels. Critical for revenue management compliance and OTA relationship governance.

Specifies the scope of the rate parity obligation: broad parity (match all channels including direct), narrow parity (match only other OTAs, not direct), or no parity obligation. Reflects EU regulatory distinctions post-2015 investigations.
Number of calendar days prior to expiration_date by which either party must provide written notice of intent to renew or terminate. Drives automated contract renewal alerts.
Specifies whether the contract auto-renews at expiration, requires manual renegotiation, or is a one-time non-renewable agreement.
Date on which all parties executed the contract. Used for legal audit trails and SOX compliance.
Actual date the contract was terminated early, if applicable. Distinct from expiration_date which is the scheduled end. Populated only when contract_status is 'terminated'.
Timestamp of the most recent modification to this contract record. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the lakehouse ETL pipeline and audit compliance.

business_glossary_term: Central Reservation System (CRS) Channel Mapping ID
description: Unique surrogate identifier for each CRS-to-channel mapping configuration record in the Sabre SynXis CRS. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE — represents a configuration resource defining how a property's rates, room types, and inventory are translated and published to a specific distribution channel.

Foreign key linking to channel.booking_source. Business justification: CRS channel mappings define which booking source codes are used for reservations from each channel. Many mappings per booking source. Removes redundant booking_source_code which can be retrieved via F
Reference to the distribution channel (OTA, GDS, direct, voice, etc.) to which this mapping applies. Identifies the connected channel partner in the channel master.

Foreign key linking to channel.channel_rate_plan. Business justification: CRS channel mappings define how rate plans map between Sabre SynXis CRS and distribution channels. The crs_channel_mapping table has crs_rate_plan_code and channel_rate_plan_code (STRING). Adding chan
