Foreign key linking to channel.gds_connection. Business justification: CRS channel mappings can be specific to GDS connections when the channel is a GDS. Adding nullable gds_connection_id FK allows linking CRS mappings to specific GDS connection configurations (Sabre, Am

Foreign key linking to inventory.room_type. Business justification: CRS channel mappings must link channel room type codes to physical inventory room types for rate loading, availability synchronization, and booking fulfillment. Essential for channel manager operation
Reference to the hotel or resort property for which this CRS channel mapping is configured. Links the mapping to the specific property in the property master.

Code identifying the cancellation policy applied to bookings made through this channel mapping (e.g., 'FLEX24H' for 24-hour free cancellation, 'NRR' for non-refundable). Determines the terms communicated to guests at the point of booking on the channel.

Reference number or identifier of the commercial contract governing the distribution relationship with this channel (e.g., OTA master agreement number, GDS connectivity contract ID). Used for contract management, commission dispute resolution, and audit compliance.

The property identifier code as recognized by the distribution channel (e.g., Booking.com hotel ID, Expedia property ID, GDS chain/property code). Used to match the CRS property to the channel's own property record during rate and availability pushes.

Categorical classification of the distribution channel type. Determines the connectivity protocol, commission structure, and rate parity rules applicable to this mapping. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ota|gds|direct_web|voice|mobile|wholesaler|corporate|metasearch — promote to reference product]

The commission percentage charged by the distribution channel on bookings made through this mapping (e.g., 15.00 for 15%). Used for cost-of-acquisition analysis, channel profitability reporting, and USALI-compliant distribution cost accounting. Classified as confidential due to contractual sensitivity.

Fixed or per-booking connectivity fee charged by the channel or GDS switch for maintaining the distribution connection, expressed in USD. Used in total cost-of-acquisition calculations and channel ROI analysis per USALI standards.

The technical messaging protocol used for rate and availability distribution between Sabre SynXis CRS and the channel (e.g., OTA XML, HTNG, OpenTravel Alliance ARI). Determines the message format and integration method. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OTA_XML|HTNG|OpenTravel|ARI|TravelPort|Amadeus|direct_API — promote to reference product]

Indicates whether property content (room descriptions, images, amenities) is synchronized to the channel through this mapping in addition to rates and availability. Relevant for OTA and metasearch channels that require content distribution alongside rate/inventory feeds.

Timestamp when this CRS channel mapping configuration record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage tracking, and compliance with GDPR data processing records requirements.

The property identifier code as registered in Sabre SynXis CRS. This is the CRS-side property reference used in all rate and availability distribution messages to channels. Distinct from the internal property_id.

business_glossary_term: Central Reservation System (CRS) Rate Plan Code
description: The rate plan code as defined in Sabre SynXis CRS (e.g., BAR, LRA, NRR, corporate rate codes). This is the source-side rate plan identifier that is translated and mapped to the channel-specific rate plan code for distribution.

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which rates are distributed to this channel (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Determines the currency of rate publication and commission calculation for this channel mapping.

The date from which this CRS channel mapping configuration becomes active and rates/availability begin distributing to the channel. Used to schedule future mapping activations and track the start of channel distribution periods.

The date on which this CRS channel mapping configuration expires and distribution to the channel ceases. Null indicates the mapping is open-ended with no scheduled end date. Used for contract-aligned channel distribution management.

Code identifying the payment guarantee policy required for bookings through this channel mapping (e.g., 'CC_REQUIRED' for credit card guarantee, 'DEPOSIT' for advance deposit). Ensures PCI DSS-compliant payment handling requirements are communicated to the channel.

Defines how room inventory is allocated to this channel. 'pooled' means the channel draws from the shared inventory pool; 'allotment' means a fixed block is reserved for the channel; 'free_sell' means unlimited sell without allocation; 'on_request' means availability must be confirmed manually.

Indicates whether this channel mapping is configured to distribute mobile-specific rates or content optimized for mobile booking platforms. Supports mobile channel performance analytics and targeted mobile rate strategy.

Timestamp of the most recent successful rate and availability synchronization push from Sabre SynXis CRS to the distribution channel for this mapping. Used to monitor channel connectivity health, detect stale distributions, and support SLA compliance reporting.

Indicates whether this channel mapping is configured with Last Room Availability (LRA), meaning the channel has access to the last available room in the property's inventory. LRA is typically granted to GDS corporate accounts and preferred OTA partners under contractual obligation.

Externally-known unique alphanumeric code identifying this CRS-to-channel mapping configuration within Sabre SynXis. Used as the business identifier for cross-system reference and audit. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.
Human-readable descriptive name for this CRS channel mapping configuration (e.g., 'Booking.com Standard Rate Mapping - NYC Midtown'). Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.

Current operational lifecycle status of this CRS channel mapping. 'active' means the mapping is live and distributing rates/availability; 'suspended' means temporarily halted; 'testing' means in QA validation. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.

Classification of what this mapping configuration governs — whether it maps rate plans, room types, inventory controls, packages, or promotional offers to the channel. Serves as the CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.

The market segment code assigned to bookings originating from this channel mapping (e.g., 'LEISURE', 'CORPORATE', 'WHOLESALE', 'OTA'). Used for USALI-compliant revenue segmentation, STR benchmarking, and RevPAR analysis by channel.

Maximum number of days in advance that a booking can be made through this channel mapping (booking window). Controls how far into the future availability is published to the channel. Null indicates no maximum booking window restriction.

Maximum number of nights allowed for a booking through this channel mapping. Null indicates no maximum stay restriction. Used to control long-stay bookings on specific channels as part of yield optimization strategy.

Minimum number of days in advance that a booking must be made through this channel mapping. Used to enforce advance purchase restrictions (e.g., NRR non-refundable rates requiring 7-day advance booking) as distributed via the CRS.

Minimum number of nights required for a booking to be accepted through this channel mapping. A value of 1 means no minimum stay restriction. Used to enforce LOS (Length of Stay) controls as part of revenue management strategy distributed via the CRS.

Classification of the rate type distributed through this channel mapping. Indicates whether the channel receives Best Available Rate (BAR), Last Room Availability (LRA) rates, Non-Refundable Rates (NRR), corporate negotiated rates, packages, promotional rates, or wholesale rates. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bar|lra|nrr|corporate|package|promotional|wholesale — promote to reference product]

Defines whether a rate adjustment (offset) is applied to the CRS rate before distribution to this channel. 'none' means the CRS rate is published as-is; 'flat_amount' means a fixed currency amount is added or subtracted; 'percentage' means a percentage markup or markdown is applied.

The numeric value of the rate offset applied to the CRS rate before distribution to this channel. Positive values indicate a markup; negative values indicate a markdown. Interpreted as a currency amount or percentage depending on rate_offset_type. Null when rate_offset_type is 'none'.

Indicates whether rate parity rules are enforced for this channel mapping, requiring that the rates distributed to this channel match the Best Available Rate (BAR) published on other channels. Critical for OTA contract compliance and rate integrity monitoring.

Maximum number of rooms that can be sold through this channel per night under the current allocation configuration. A null or zero value indicates no sell limit is applied (free sell). Used to control channel-level inventory exposure and prevent overbooking.

Configured frequency in minutes at which rate and availability updates are pushed from the CRS to this channel. Lower values indicate near-real-time distribution (e.g., 5 minutes for OTA partners); higher values indicate batch distribution schedules.

Timestamp when this CRS channel mapping configuration record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, incremental ETL processing, and audit compliance. Critical for detecting configuration drift in channel distribution settings.
Unique surrogate identifier for each GDS connection configuration record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this master configuration entity.

Foreign key linking to channel.booking_source. Business justification: GDS connections are associated with specific booking source codes for attribution when reservations come through GDS channels. Many GDS connections per booking source. Removes redundant booking_source
Reference to the parent distribution channel record associated with this GDS connection, enabling channel mix analysis and cost-of-acquisition reporting.

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: GDS connectivity fees (segment fees, monthly charges) are departmental operating expenses requiring cost center allocation for accurate distribution cost tracking and budget management per USALI stand

business_glossary_term: Gds Account Manager Employee Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to procurement.employee. Business justification: GDS account managers are employees managing GDS connectivity, troubleshooting technical issues, and optimizing GDS performance. Critical for operational accountability, issue escalation, and performan
Reference to the property for which this GDS connection is configured. Links the GDS connectivity record to the specific hotel or resort property.

business_glossary_term: Third Party Due Diligence Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to compliance.third_party_due_diligence. Business justification: GDS providers are third-party data processors handling guest PII and payment data, requiring due diligence for data security, sanctions compliance, and financial stability. Real business process: tech
Date on which this GDS connection was first activated and made live for bookings. Used for contract term tracking, connectivity health history, and channel distribution reporting.

Method by which room availability is synchronized between the property management system and the GDS. Real-time provides instant updates; batch updates on a scheduled interval; on_request queries availability at booking time; seamless provides live two-way availability.

Two- to four-character hotel chain code registered with the GDS network, identifying the hotel brand or management company within the GDS. Assigned by the GDS provider and used for inventory and rate loading.

Percentage commission payable to travel agents or GDS intermediaries on bookings made through this GDS connection. Used for commission accrual, accounts payable processing, and channel cost analysis per USALI standards.

Current operational lifecycle status of the GDS connection. Active indicates live booking capability; suspended indicates temporary halt; pending_activation indicates setup in progress; decommissioned indicates permanently retired.

Fixed monthly fee charged by the GDS provider or switch vendor for maintaining the technical connectivity of this GDS connection. Distinct from per-segment fees; used for OpEx budgeting and channel cost allocation.

Technical connectivity model used for this GDS connection. Direct connect provides real-time two-way communication; switch connect routes through a switch provider; seamless provides full availability and rate transparency; standard uses batch updates.
Expiration date of the GDS connectivity contract. Null for evergreen contracts. Used for renewal alerts, contract renegotiation planning, and channel distribution continuity management.
Effective start date of the GDS connectivity contract governing this connection. Used for contract lifecycle management, renewal planning, and financial obligation tracking.

Indicates whether negotiated corporate rates are loaded and accessible through this GDS connection. Corporate rates are a primary revenue stream for GDS channels and require specific rate plan configuration in the CRS.
Timestamp when this GDS connection configuration record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and record lifecycle tracking in the silver layer lakehouse.

Identifier for the CRS-to-GDS interface configuration within Sabre SynXis CRS. Links the GDS connection to the specific CRS interface instance managing rate distribution and availability synchronization.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which rates are loaded and displayed to travel agents in this GDS connection. Ensures rate accuracy and currency consistency for international GDS distribution.
Date on which this GDS connection was or is scheduled to be deactivated. Null for currently active connections. Used for contract lifecycle management and channel distribution planning.

Numeric priority ranking that determines the property's display order in GDS search results for travel agents. Lower values indicate higher priority. Influenced by GDS tier, participation level, and performance metrics.
Human-readable name of the GDS provider (Sabre, Amadeus, Galileo, Worldspan, Travelport). Used for reporting, dashboards, and channel mix analysis.
Industry-standard two-character GDS network identifier assigned by IATA/ARC. 1S=Sabre, 1A=Amadeus, 1G=Galileo/Travelport, 1P=Worldspan. Used for routing and segment fee attribution.

Participation tier level for this GDS connection, reflecting the level of GDS merchandising, display priority, and content features contracted. Preferred tier provides enhanced display and content capabilities; standard is base participation; basic is minimal listing.

Result of the most recent automated health check for this GDS connection. Healthy indicates full two-way connectivity; degraded indicates partial functionality; failed indicates connectivity loss; unknown indicates health check has not been performed.

business_glossary_term: Last GDS Connectivity Health Check Timestamp
description: Timestamp of the most recent automated connectivity health check performed for this GDS connection. Used for SLA monitoring, connectivity uptime reporting, and proactive issue detection.

Indicates whether Last Room Availability (LRA) is enabled for this GDS connection, requiring the property to make the last available room accessible to GDS bookers. LRA is a contractual obligation with some GDS providers and corporate accounts.

Market segment code assigned to bookings originating from this GDS connection in the PMS (e.g., TRAN for transient, CORP for corporate, LEIS for leisure). Used for USALI-compliant revenue segmentation and STR STAR Report benchmarking.

Maximum number of days in advance that reservations can be booked through this GDS connection. Controls the booking window loaded to the GDS and aligns with the Revenue Management System (RMS) inventory control horizon.

Minimum number of days in advance that reservations must be booked through this GDS connection. Supports Last Room Availability (LRA) and advance purchase rate controls managed by the Revenue Management System.

Free-text operational notes regarding this GDS connection, including special configuration requirements, known issues, contractual exceptions, or migration history. Used by channel distribution and revenue management teams.

Indicates whether Non-Refundable Rates (NRR) can be loaded and booked through this GDS connection. NRR support is a key revenue management capability enabling advance purchase rate strategies via GDS.
The unique property identifier as registered within the specific GDS network. May differ from the internal PMS property code. Used for inventory display and booking routing within the GDS.
GDS-assigned Pseudo City Code (PCC) that identifies the travel agency or booking office within the GDS network. Used for commission tracking, segment attribution, and agency relationship management.

The technical protocol or standard used to load and update rate plans into the GDS. HTNG and OTA are XML-based standards; DHISCO is a switch-based protocol; manual indicates rates are loaded via GDS terminal; API indicates direct programmatic loading.

Indicates whether rate parity is contractually enforced for this GDS connection, requiring that rates loaded to the GDS match the Best Available Rate (BAR) offered on direct channels. Critical for OTA and GDS contract compliance monitoring.
Number of distinct rate plans currently loaded and active for this GDS connection. Used to monitor rate plan coverage, identify gaps in GDS rate distribution, and support rate parity audits.

Frequency at which rate updates are pushed from the Revenue Management System (RMS) or CRS to this GDS connection. Real-time supports dynamic pricing; scheduled frequencies reflect batch update configurations.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the GDS segment fee. Enables multi-currency cost-of-acquisition reporting for properties operating in non-USD markets.

Per-booking segment fee charged by the GDS provider in US dollars for each reservation booked through this GDS connection. A GDS segment is one hotel booking transaction. Used for cost-of-acquisition analysis and channel profitability reporting.
Email address for the GDS provider's technical support team for this connection. Used for connectivity issue escalation, health check alerts, and SLA breach notifications.

Name of the third-party switch provider (e.g., DHISCO, TravelClick, Pegasus) used to route availability and rate data between the CRS and the GDS, when connectivity_type is switch_connect. Null for direct connect configurations.
Timestamp when this GDS connection configuration record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, incremental ETL processing, and audit compliance in the silver layer lakehouse.

business_glossary_term: GDS Connectivity Uptime Service Level Agreement (SLA) Percentage
description: Contractually agreed minimum uptime percentage for this GDS connection as specified in the GDS connectivity SLA. Used for vendor performance monitoring and contract compliance reporting.

Unique surrogate identifier for each metasearch listing record in the silver layer. Primary key for this data product. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE — represents a managed digital listing asset on a metasearch platform.

Foreign key linking to channel.booking_source. Business justification: Metasearch listings (Google Hotel Ads, TripAdvisor, Trivago) drive traffic that converts to bookings. Each metasearch platform should have an associated booking_source code for attribution tracking. T

Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Metasearch advertising (Google Hotel Ads, TripAdvisor) is campaign-driven; each listing/bid strategy belongs to a specific marketing campaign for budget tracking, performance attribution, and ROI meas
Reference to the distribution channel record representing the metasearch platform (e.g., Google Hotel Ads, TripAdvisor, Trivago, Kayak, Skyscanner) through which this listing is managed.

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Metasearch advertising spend (Google Hotel Ads, TripAdvisor) is a marketing expense requiring cost center allocation for budget tracking, ROI analysis, and departmental P&L reporting.

business_glossary_term: Last Bid Updated By Employee Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to procurement.employee. Business justification: Metasearch bid updates by employees require tracking for operational accountability and performance analysis. Essential for understanding who manages metasearch campaigns and optimizing bid strategies

The account or hotel ID assigned by the metasearch platform (e.g., Google Hotel Center property ID, TripAdvisor hotel ID) to identify this property on the platform. Used for API authentication, platform-side reporting reconciliation, and connectivity management.
Reference to the hotel or resort property associated with this metasearch listing. Links the listing to the property master record for performance attribution and bid management.

The monetary bid amount configured for this listing under the active bid strategy. For CPC, represents the maximum cost-per-click in the listing currency. For CPA, represents the target cost-per-acquisition. For commission-based, this field is null (see commission_rate_pct). Serves as the principal quantitative fact (MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE) for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.

A multiplier applied to the base bid amount for specific market or device segments (e.g., 1.20 = 20% bid increase for mobile). Enables granular bid adjustment strategies without changing the base bid. Used in conjunction with bid_amount for effective bid calculation.

The bidding model applied to this metasearch listing. CPC (Cost Per Click) charges per user click; CPA (Cost Per Acquisition) charges per completed booking; commission-based charges a percentage of booking revenue; target_ROAS optimizes for return on ad spend; manual allows direct bid amount control. Drives cost-of-acquisition modeling and channel investment decisions.

Total number of direct bookings attributed to this metasearch listing within the reporting period. Used to calculate conversion rate, cost-per-acquisition (CPA), and metasearch contribution to direct booking revenue. Attribution follows the platform's last-click or assisted-conversion model.

Total gross booking revenue (in listing currency) attributed to direct bookings generated by this metasearch listing within the reporting period. Used to calculate return on ad spend (ROAS), channel contribution to revenue, and cost-of-acquisition metrics. Aligns with USALI revenue reporting.

Total number of user clicks on this property listing that directed traffic to the hotel's direct booking engine or landing page within the reporting period. Used to calculate click-through rate (CTR) and cost-per-click (CPC) actuals.

The ratio of clicks to impressions for this listing within the reporting period, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0350 = 3.50% CTR). Sourced directly from platform reporting APIs to ensure consistency with platform-side optimization signals. Used to assess listing creative and bid competitiveness.

The commission percentage charged by the metasearch platform per completed booking when the bid strategy type is commission-based. Expressed as a percentage of gross booking value (e.g., 12.00 = 12%). Null for CPC and CPA bid strategies. Critical for cost-of-acquisition and channel contribution analysis.

The technical integration method used to feed rate and availability data to this metasearch listing. 'direct_api' = direct platform API integration; 'gds_feed' = rates sourced via GDS (Global Distribution System); 'crs_feed' = rates sourced via Sabre SynXis CRS; 'third_party_aggregator' = via a connectivity partner. Impacts data freshness and rate accuracy.

The ratio of bookings to clicks for this listing within the reporting period, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0250 = 2.50% conversion rate). Sourced from platform reporting. Measures the effectiveness of the direct booking engine in converting metasearch traffic. Key input for bid optimization and landing page performance analysis.

The actual average cost per acquisition (CPA) realized for this listing within the reporting period, in listing currency. Calculated as total_spend divided by booking_count. Stored as a raw field sourced from platform reporting. Null for non-CPA bid strategies. Key metric for channel cost-of-acquisition benchmarking.

The actual average cost per click (CPC) realized for this listing within the reporting period, in listing currency. Calculated as total_spend divided by click_count. Stored as a raw field sourced from platform reporting to avoid recalculation discrepancies. Null for non-CPC bid strategies.

The timestamp when this metasearch listing record was first created in the data platform. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. Used for data lineage, audit compliance, and tracking the onboarding of new metasearch platform listings.

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this listing record (bid_amount, daily_budget_cap, monthly_budget_cap, cpc_actual, cpa_actual). Ensures consistent financial reporting across multi-currency property portfolios.

Maximum daily spend limit configured for this metasearch listing in the listing currency. Controls total metasearch investment exposure per day. Null if no daily cap is set. Used for budget pacing and channel investment governance.

The device category for which this bid configuration applies. Metasearch platforms support device-level bid adjustments. 'all' indicates a unified bid across all devices. Enables device-level performance analysis and bid optimization for direct booking conversion.

The landing page URL to which users are directed when clicking on this metasearch listing. Points to the hotel's direct booking engine (typically Sabre SynXis CRS-powered). Used for link validation, UTM parameter tracking, and ensuring correct booking attribution.

The average position at which this property listing is displayed on the metasearch platform results page (e.g., rank 1 = top position). Lower rank numbers indicate higher visibility. Used to assess bid competitiveness and correlate rank with click-through and conversion performance.

The date from which this listing configuration (bid strategy, bid amount, budget caps, device targeting) became active on the metasearch platform. Supports historical bid strategy analysis and change management auditing. Serves as the EFFECTIVE_FROM category for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.

The date on which this listing configuration ceases to be active. Null for currently active configurations. Enables slowly changing dimension (SCD) tracking of bid strategy and configuration changes over time. Serves as the EFFECTIVE_UNTIL category for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.

Total number of times this property listing was displayed to users on the metasearch platform within the reporting period. Foundational metric for calculating click-through rate (CTR) and measuring listing visibility. Sourced from platform reporting APIs.

Indicates whether this listing is configured to drive direct bookings through the hotel's own booking engine (True) versus redirecting to an OTA or third-party booking path (False). Key flag for measuring direct booking channel contribution and loyalty program integration.

Indicates whether automated rate parity monitoring is enabled for this metasearch listing. When True, the system actively checks for rate disparities between this listing and the direct booking channel. Supports compliance with AHLA rate parity guidelines and OTA contract obligations.

The timestamp when the bid amount, bid strategy, or budget configuration for this listing was last modified. Enables audit trail of bid management changes and supports analysis of bid adjustment impact on performance metrics. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.

Externally-known unique code or identifier assigned to this property listing on the metasearch platform. Used for reconciliation with platform-side reporting APIs and bid management systems. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.

Current lifecycle state of the metasearch listing. Controls whether the property is actively bidding and displaying on the platform. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. 'active' = live and bidding; 'paused' = temporarily stopped; 'suspended' = platform-enforced stop; 'pending_review' = awaiting platform approval; 'inactive' = deactivated; 'archived' = historical record.

Classification of the metasearch listing placement type. 'standard' = organic/paid standard placement; 'sponsored' = premium paid placement; 'featured' = highlighted featured listing; 'free_booking_links' = Google's commission-free direct booking link program. Determines cost structure and visibility tier.

Maximum monthly spend limit configured for this metasearch listing in the listing currency. Governs total monthly metasearch investment for the property-platform combination. Null if no monthly cap is set. Supports channel mix optimization and CapEx/OpEx planning.

Name of the metasearch platform on which this listing is active. Identifies the specific metasearch engine for bid strategy configuration, performance benchmarking, and cost-of-acquisition analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Google Hotel Ads|TripAdvisor|Trivago|Kayak|Skyscanner|Wego|HotelsCombined — promote to reference product]

Indicates whether the rates displayed on this metasearch listing are in parity with the Best Available Rate (BAR) across all channels. 'parity' = rates match; 'disparity_detected' = rates differ from direct channel; 'under_review' = parity check in progress; 'exempt' = contractual exemption applies. Critical for OTA contract compliance and direct booking channel protection.

The end date of the performance reporting period for which metrics on this record apply. Used in conjunction with reporting_period_start to define the measurement window for all performance KPIs on this listing record.

The start date of the performance reporting period for which impression, click, booking, spend, and rate metrics on this record apply. Enables time-series analysis of metasearch performance and aligns with STR benchmarking reporting cycles.

The ratio of booking revenue to total spend for this listing within the reporting period (e.g., 8.50 = $8.50 revenue per $1.00 spent). Sourced from platform reporting or CRS attribution data. Primary KPI for metasearch investment optimization and bid strategy evaluation.

The geographic market or audience segment this listing bid configuration targets (e.g., US, UK, DE, APAC). Enables market-level bid differentiation and performance analysis. Aligns with Sabre SynXis CRS market segmentation and IDeaS G3 RMS demand forecasting by market.

Total monetary amount spent on this metasearch listing (in listing currency) within the reporting period, including all click charges, acquisition fees, or commission payments depending on bid strategy. Core input for cost-of-acquisition (COA) and channel ROI calculations.

The timestamp when this metasearch listing record was last updated in the data platform. Supports incremental ETL processing, data freshness monitoring, and audit trail requirements for channel distribution data governance.

The UTM campaign parameter appended to the direct booking URL for this listing, enabling attribution tracking in web analytics and CRM systems. Used to measure metasearch-sourced traffic and bookings in Salesforce CRM and digital analytics platforms.
Unique surrogate identifier for the OTA partner record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the ota_partner master data product.

Foreign key linking to procurement.employee. Business justification: OTA account managers are employees managing partner relationships, negotiating contracts, and resolving disputes. Essential for accountability in partnership management, performance tracking, and esca

Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: OTA partnerships are brand-specific in multi-brand hospitality companies; Marriott Bonvoy partners differ from Ritz-Carlton partners. Brand-level partnership management, commission structures, and con

business_glossary_term: Third Party Due Diligence Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to compliance.third_party_due_diligence. Business justification: OTA partners are third-party processors of guest PII and payment data, requiring due diligence screening for GDPR compliance, sanctions screening, cybersecurity assessment, and financial stability. Re

Indicates whether the OTA partner operates an affiliate or sub-distribution network through which third-party websites or agents can book on behalf of the OTA. True = affiliate network active; False = direct OTA platform only. Relevant for booking source attribution accuracy.

Standard commission percentage charged by the OTA partner on each confirmed booking, expressed as a percentage of the gross booking value (e.g., 15.00 = 15%). Foundational input for cost-of-acquisition (COA) and channel profitability analysis.

Default cancellation policy type applied to bookings made through this OTA channel. Flexible = free cancellation up to 24-48 hours; Moderate = partial penalty; Strict = high penalty; Non_Refundable = NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) — no refund on cancellation. Impacts revenue management and guest experience.

Name of the channel manager or switch platform used to manage the technical connectivity to this OTA partner (e.g., Sabre SynXis, Siteminder, Amadeus iHotelier, RateGain). Relevant when connectivity is not direct API-to-API.

Commercial model governing how the OTA partner earns revenue. Net Rate = hotel sells at net, OTA marks up; Merchant = OTA collects full payment and remits net; Agency = OTA collects commission on hotel-collected payment; Retail = standard published rate with commission; Opaque = rate hidden from consumer until post-booking (e.g., Hotwire).

Frequency at which the connectivity fee is billed by the OTA partner or channel manager. Annual = yearly flat fee; Monthly = recurring monthly; Per_Booking = transactional fee per confirmed booking; One_Time = setup/onboarding fee only.

Annual or per-transaction connectivity fee charged by the OTA partner or channel manager for maintaining the technical integration (API/XML/GDS switch). Expressed in USD. Used for total cost-of-acquisition (COA) calculation and channel profitability reporting per USALI.

Technical protocol used for two-way inventory, rate, and availability (IRA) connectivity between the hotel's CRS/PMS and the OTA partner. XML = legacy XML push/pull; REST_API = modern RESTful API; GDS = Global Distribution System switch; SFTP = file-based batch; OTA_XML = OpenTravel Alliance XML standard; HTNG = HTNG web services.

Numeric score (typically 0–100) representing the completeness and quality of the hotel's content (photos, descriptions, amenities) on the OTA partner's platform. Higher scores correlate with improved search ranking and conversion rates. Sourced from OTA partner reporting APIs.
Date on which the master distribution agreement with the OTA partner becomes legally binding and operational. Governs the start of commission obligations and rate parity clauses.

Date on which the master distribution agreement with the OTA partner expires or is scheduled for renewal. Null indicates an evergreen/open-ended contract. Used for contract renewal management and risk monitoring.

Unique reference number of the master distribution agreement or contract executed with the OTA partner. Links to the contract management repository in SAP S/4HANA for financial and legal audit purposes.
Timestamp when the OTA partner record was first created in the data platform. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and silver layer ingestion tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

Indicates whether a formal data sharing agreement (DSA) is in place with the OTA partner governing the exchange of guest booking data, anonymised analytics, and performance reporting. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance when guest PII is transmitted.

Indicates whether the OTA partner is formally designated as a GDPR Data Processor under Article 28, processing guest personal data on behalf of the hotel as Data Controller. True = Data Processing Agreement (DPA) executed; False = not designated as processor.

Two-letter chain code assigned by the Global Distribution System (GDS) to identify the hotel brand/chain within GDS networks (Sabre, Amadeus, Travelport). Applicable only when connectivity_protocol is GDS. Null for non-GDS OTA partners.

ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the OTA partner's legal headquarters is registered. Used for tax treaty application, withholding tax determination, and regulatory jurisdiction identification.

Model governing how room inventory is made available to the OTA partner. Free_Sell = real-time availability from CRS with no pre-allocated block; Allotment = pre-contracted room block allocated to the OTA; On_Request = bookings require manual confirmation. Managed in IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis.

Indicates whether the OTA partner contract includes a Last Room Availability (LRA) clause, requiring the hotel to make its last available room accessible to this channel. LRA clauses impact revenue management inventory controls and are tracked in IDeaS G3 RMS.

Full registered legal name of the OTA partner organisation as it appears on the master distribution agreement (e.g., Expedia, Inc.; Booking Holdings B.V.). Used for contract management, AP reconciliation, and regulatory filings.

Description of the primary geographic markets or regions where the OTA partner has significant consumer reach and booking volume (e.g., 'North America, Western Europe'; 'Asia-Pacific'; 'Global'). Used for channel mix optimisation and market-specific distribution strategy.

Indicates whether the OTA partner operates a dedicated mobile application channel through which bookings are made. True = mobile app channel is active and tracked separately for attribution; False = desktop/web only. Supports channel mix and mobile booking trend analysis.

Business email address of the OTA partner's designated account manager. Used for commercial communications, promotional campaign coordination, and escalation. Classified as confidential PII per GDPR Article 4.

Full name of the OTA partner's designated account manager or market manager assigned to the hotel account. Primary point of contact for commercial negotiations, promotional campaigns, and issue resolution.
Business phone number of the OTA partner's designated account manager. Used for urgent escalations and commercial negotiations. Classified as confidential PII per GDPR Article 4.

Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the OTA partner within the Central Reservation System (CRS) and Property Management System (PMS). Used for channel mapping, rate distribution, and booking source attribution in Sabre SynXis and Oracle OPERA.

Current lifecycle status of the OTA partner relationship. Active = live and distributing inventory; Pending = contract under negotiation or connectivity setup in progress; Suspended = temporarily halted (e.g., rate parity breach); Terminated = contract ended.

Classification of the distribution partner by channel category. OTA = Online Travel Agency (Expedia, Booking.com); GDS = Global Distribution System (Sabre, Amadeus); METASEARCH = price comparison engine (Google Hotel Ads, Trivago); DIRECT = brand.com or call centre; WHOLESALE = tour operator/bed bank; AFFILIATE = sub-affiliate network. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OTA|GDS|METASEARCH|DIRECT|WHOLESALE|AFFILIATE — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]

Identifies which party collects payment from the guest. Hotel = property collects at check-in/check-out (agency model); OTA = OTA collects full payment and remits net to hotel (merchant model); Split = partial payment collected by each party.

Negotiated commission rate applicable when the property holds preferred partner or sponsored placement status with the OTA. Typically lower than the base rate in exchange for enhanced visibility or placement. Null if preferred status is not applicable.

Indicates whether the hotel or brand holds preferred partner or sponsored placement status with this OTA, typically in exchange for a higher commission rate or marketing investment. Preferred partners receive enhanced search ranking and visibility on the OTA platform.

ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the OTA partner's primary consumer market or country of dominant booking volume (e.g., USA, GBR, SGP). Used for geographic channel attribution and market penetration analysis.

Indicates whether the OTA partner contract includes a rate parity clause requiring the hotel to offer rates no lower on other channels than those offered on this OTA. True = rate parity clause is active; False = no rate parity obligation. Critical for revenue management and legal compliance monitoring.

Specifies the scope of the rate parity obligation. Broad = hotel cannot offer lower rates on ANY channel including its own website; Narrow = hotel cannot offer lower rates on OTHER OTAs but may offer lower rates on its own direct channels; None = no parity obligation. Relevant for EU regulatory compliance following rate parity investigations.

Number of days after the guest check-out date within which the OTA partner remits commission payments or net booking proceeds to the hotel. Used for cash flow forecasting and AP/AR reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA (e.g., 30 = net-30 terms).

Total number of guest reviews published on the OTA partner's platform for the hotel. Used alongside review_score to assess statistical significance and platform engagement. Sourced from OTA partner reporting APIs.

Average guest review score for the hotel as published on the OTA partner's platform (e.g., Booking.com score out of 10, Expedia score out of 5). Used for reputation management, competitive benchmarking, and NPS/GSS correlation analysis via Medallia.
VAT, GST, or equivalent tax registration number of the OTA partner's legal entity. Required for tax invoice processing, withholding tax compliance, and AP reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA.

Consumer-facing brand name under which the OTA partner operates (e.g., Hotels.com, Agoda). May differ from the legal entity name when the OTA operates multiple consumer brands under one legal umbrella.

Timestamp when the OTA partner record was most recently modified in the data platform. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental ETL processing, and audit compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Primary key for negotiated_rate

Foreign key linking to channel.booking_source. Business justification: Corporate negotiated rates (LNR) are loaded for specific booking sources such as TMCs (Travel Management Companies) and consortia. The channel_negotiated_rate table has iata_number (STRING) which shou

Foreign key linking to channel.channel_contract. Business justification: Corporate negotiated rates are governed by channel contracts with TMCs, consortia, and corporate accounts. Adding nullable channel_contract_id FK links negotiated rates to their governing contract. Re
Reference to the corporate account entity that holds this negotiated rate agreement. Links to the CRM or PMS corporate account master.
Reference to the distribution channel (GDS, TMC, direct, OTA) through which this negotiated rate is loaded and distributed.
Reference to the Travel Management Company (TMC) associated with this negotiated rate agreement. TMCs manage corporate travel programs and book through GDS channels.

Foreign key linking to procurement.employee. Business justification: Corporate rate loading by employees requires tracking for operational accountability and audit trails. Essential for understanding who loaded negotiated rates into systems and ensuring data quality in
Reference to the property at which this negotiated rate is applicable. A single corporate agreement may have multiple records — one per property.
Reference to the rate plan master record associated with this negotiated rate agreement.
Minimum number of days in advance that a booking must be made to qualify for this negotiated rate. Zero indicates no advance purchase requirement.
Current lifecycle status of the negotiated rate agreement. Controls whether the rate is bookable through distribution channels.
The agreed nightly room rate (ADR equivalent) for this corporate negotiated rate agreement, expressed in the agreement currency. This is the principal monetary value of the rate record.
Username or employee identifier of the revenue management leader or director who approved this negotiated rate agreement for loading and distribution.

Comma-delimited list or date range expressions representing dates on which this negotiated rate is not available (e.g., peak demand periods, holidays, special events). Stored as a descriptive string; detailed blackout records managed in rate restriction tables.
Code referencing the cancellation policy applicable to bookings made under this negotiated rate (e.g., 24H, 48H, NON-REF). Governs penalty and refund rules.
Percentage commission payable to the TMC or travel agency on bookings made under this negotiated rate agreement. Expressed as a percentage of the gross booking value.

Identifier for the travel consortia (e.g., Virtuoso, Signature, Ensemble, Internova) through which this negotiated rate is distributed. Consortia rates are loaded separately from direct LNR agreements.
Full name of the travel consortia organization associated with this rate agreement (e.g., Virtuoso, Signature Travel Network).
Timestamp when this negotiated rate record was first created in the source system or ingested into the Silver Layer. Supports audit trail and data lineage.
Native rate identifier as assigned by the Sabre SynXis Central Reservation System (CRS). Used for reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the CRS source system.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the negotiated rate amount is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
Date from which the negotiated rate agreement becomes active and bookable through distribution channels. Aligns with the contract start date.
Date on which the negotiated rate agreement expires and is no longer bookable. Nullable for open-ended agreements.
Two-letter GDS chain code identifying the hotel chain under which this rate is loaded in the GDS (e.g., HH for Hilton, MC for Marriott). Required for GDS rate distribution.
Current status of the negotiated rate's loading into the GDS (e.g., Sabre, Amadeus, Travelport). Tracks whether the rate is live and bookable through GDS channels.

Indicates whether this negotiated rate carries Last Room Availability (LRA) status, meaning the rate must be offered as long as any room of the applicable type remains available, regardless of inventory controls.
Indicates whether rate parity obligations apply to this negotiated rate across all distribution channels. Relevant for OTA and GDS channel compliance monitoring.
Timestamp of the most recent modification to this negotiated rate record, including rate amount changes, status updates, or GDS loading status changes.
Market segment classification code assigned to bookings made under this negotiated rate (e.g., CORP, GOVT, CONS). Used for revenue management segmentation and USALI reporting.
Maximum number of nights permitted under this negotiated rate. Null indicates no upper LOS restriction.
Minimum number of nights required for a booking to qualify for this negotiated rate. LOS = Length of Stay restriction applied at the channel level.
Calendar year in which this negotiated rate agreement was contracted. Corporate LNR agreements are typically renegotiated annually; this field supports year-over-year production analysis.
Rate code as configured in Oracle OPERA PMS for this negotiated rate. Used for cross-system reconciliation between CRS-distributed rates and PMS-posted rates.
Hotel segment category under which this negotiated rate is classified, aligning with the property's service tier (luxury, premium, select-service, standard).
Date on which this negotiated rate was loaded into the GDS and/or CRS for distribution. Tracks operational readiness of the rate for booking.
Alphanumeric rate plan code as loaded in the CRS and GDS systems (e.g., CORP01, LNR2025). Used for rate identification across distribution channels and PMS.
Human-readable name of the negotiated rate plan as displayed in the CRS, GDS, and TMC booking tools (e.g., 'Acme Corp 2025 LNR').

Classification of the negotiated rate type. LNR = Local Negotiated Rate, LRA = Last Room Availability, BAR = Best Available Rate, NRR = Non-Refundable Rate, consortia = travel consortia rate, government = government/per-diem rate.
Comma-delimited list or description of room type codes included under this negotiated rate agreement (e.g., 'KING,DBLQ,SUITE'). Defines which room categories the corporate rate applies to.
Name of the Travel Management Company (TMC) managing the corporate travel program for this negotiated rate (e.g., American Express Global Business Travel, BCD Travel, CWT).
Contractually committed number of room nights the corporate account has agreed to consume during the agreement period. Used for production tracking and rate renegotiation.
Unique surrogate identifier for each channel-specific rate plan configuration record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.

Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign_offer. Business justification: Promotional rate plans operationalize marketing offers; a "Summer Sale" campaign offer manifests as specific rate plans loaded into distribution channels. Linking rate plans to offers enables offer pe

Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Each channel-specific rate plan configuration belongs to one distribution channel. Many rate plans per channel. Removes redundant channel_code, channel_name, and channel_type which can be retrieved vi
Identifier of the hotel property for which this channel rate plan configuration applies. Enables property-level rate plan and channel management across a multi-property portfolio.

Minimum number of days in advance of arrival that a booking must be made to qualify for this rate plan on this channel. For example, a value of 7 means the guest must book at least 7 days before check-in. Supports advance purchase rate strategies and demand management.

Name or identifier of the revenue management or distribution team member who approved this channel rate plan configuration for publication. Supports governance, approval workflow tracking, and audit compliance for rate distribution decisions.

The base room rate amount (in property currency) from which the channel-specific rate is derived. For BAR-derived plans, this is the current BAR value. For flat rates, this is the fixed rate. Expressed per room per night. Supports rate parity monitoring and channel pricing strategy analysis.

The last calendar date on which bookings can be made for stays under this channel rate plan. Defines the closing of the booking window. Nullable if the rate plan remains bookable indefinitely until the stay date.

The earliest calendar date on which bookings can be made for stays under this channel rate plan. Defines the opening of the booking window for this rate plan on this channel. Used for promotional and advance purchase rate plan management.

Code referencing the cancellation and penalty policy linked to this channel rate plan. Examples: FLEX24H (free cancellation up to 24 hours), NRR-NOCANCEL (non-refundable, no cancellation), SEMI-FLEX48H (partial refund if cancelled 48+ hours prior). Critical for NRR and advance purchase rate plans.

The final channel-specific rate amount (in property currency) published to this distribution channel after applying the rate derivation method and adjustment. This is the rate visible to consumers on the channel. Per room per night. Core metric for rate parity compliance monitoring.

Current lifecycle status of the channel rate plan configuration. Active = currently published and bookable on the channel; Inactive = deactivated and not available; Suspended = temporarily paused (e.g., rate parity violation); Pending = awaiting activation or approval; Expired = past effective end date; Draft = configured but not yet submitted for distribution.

Number of days before arrival after which this rate plan is closed out and no longer bookable on this channel. Complements advance_purchase_days to define the booking window. A value of 0 means the rate is bookable up to the day of arrival.

The commission percentage payable to the distribution channel (e.g., OTA, GDS, travel agent) for bookings made under this rate plan. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 15.0000 = 15%). Critical for cost of acquisition analysis and channel profitability reporting.

Fixed fee amount (in property currency) charged by the channel or connectivity provider per booking or per period for distributing this rate plan on the channel. Distinct from commission (which is percentage-based). Supports total cost of acquisition calculation.
The date and time when this channel rate plan configuration record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting requirements.

business_glossary_term: Central Reservation System (CRS) Rate Plan ID
description: The rate plan identifier as assigned in the Sabre SynXis Central Reservation System (CRS). Used for cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the operational CRS. Enables traceability of rate plan configurations back to the source system.

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the rate amounts on this channel rate plan. Examples: USD, EUR, GBP, AED. Supports multi-currency channel distribution and rate parity analysis across international markets.

A 7-character binary string indicating which days of the week (Mon-Sun) this rate plan is applicable on the channel. Each position represents a day: position 1=Monday through position 7=Sunday. '1' = applicable, '0' = not applicable. Example: '1111100' = Monday through Friday only. Supports weekday/weekend rate differentiation.

The date from which this channel rate plan configuration becomes active and the rate plan is published to the channel. Defines the start of the rate loading schedule for this channel-rate plan combination.

The date on which this channel rate plan configuration expires and the rate plan is no longer published to the channel. Nullable for open-ended configurations. Supports rate loading schedule management and seasonal rate plan publishing.

Indicates whether this channel rate plan is a package rate that bundles room accommodation with additional inclusions (e.g., breakfast, spa credit, parking, F&B voucher). True = package rate with inclusions; False = room-only rate. Supports package revenue attribution and channel mix analysis.

Indicates whether rate parity rules apply to this channel rate plan configuration. True = this rate plan is subject to rate parity monitoring and must match rates across applicable channels; False = exempt from parity (e.g., opaque rates, member-only rates, negotiated corporate rates). Supports rate parity compliance management.

Indicates whether bookings made under this channel rate plan are eligible for a refund upon cancellation. True = refundable (flexible rate); False = non-refundable (NRR). Key attribute for consumer transparency and rate plan classification.

The date and time when this rate plan was most recently successfully loaded or updated on the distribution channel. Supports rate loading schedule management, troubleshooting, and audit of channel rate distribution operations.

Maximum number of nights a guest may stay under this rate plan on this channel. Nullable if no maximum stay restriction applies. Used in conjunction with min_los to define the eligible stay window for this channel rate plan.

Code indicating the meal plan inclusion for this channel rate plan. RO = Room Only; BB = Bed and Breakfast; HB = Half Board (breakfast + dinner); FB = Full Board (all meals); AI = All Inclusive; EP = European Plan (no meals); CP = Continental Plan (continental breakfast). Relevant for package rate plans. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: RO|BB|HB|FB|AI|EP|CP — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Minimum number of nights a guest must stay to be eligible to book this rate plan on this channel. A value of 1 indicates no minimum stay restriction. Length of Stay (LOS) restrictions are a key Revenue Management System (RMS) tool for yield optimization.

Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or contextual information about this channel rate plan configuration. Examples: rate loading exceptions, channel-specific negotiation notes, promotional campaign references, or revenue strategy rationale.

business_glossary_term: Property Management System (PMS) Rate Plan Code
description: The rate plan code as configured in Oracle OPERA Property Management System (PMS). Used for reconciliation between CRS channel distribution and PMS rate plan setup. Ensures rate plan consistency between the CRS and PMS for accurate reservation processing.

Indicates whether the channel-specific pricing adjustment is a markup (rate higher than base, e.g., to cover OTA commission) or a markdown (rate lower than base, e.g., promotional discount). NONE indicates no adjustment is applied and the base rate is published as-is.

Specifies whether the rate_adjustment_value is expressed as a PERCENTAGE of the base rate or as a fixed AMOUNT in the property currency. Determines how the channel-specific rate is computed from the base rate and adjustment value.

The numeric value of the rate adjustment applied to derive the channel-specific rate from the base rate. Interpretation depends on rate_adjustment_unit: if PERCENTAGE, this is the percentage (e.g., 15.00 = 15%); if AMOUNT, this is the absolute currency amount. Used in conjunction with rate_adjustment_type to compute the final channel rate.

The method used to calculate the channel-specific rate from the base rate. FLAT = fixed absolute rate amount; PERCENTAGE = percentage markup or markdown applied to a base rate; BAR_DERIVED = derived as a percentage or offset from the Best Available Rate (BAR); YIELD_MANAGED = dynamically set by the Revenue Management System (RMS); NEGOTIATED_FIXED = fixed contracted rate for corporate or wholesale agreements.

Operational status of the rate plan loading process to the channel. Loaded = successfully transmitted and active on the channel; Pending Load = queued for transmission to the channel; Load Failed = transmission error requiring remediation; Not Loaded = not yet submitted; Deactivated = previously loaded but now removed from the channel.

Code identifying the rate parity group to which this channel rate plan belongs. Rate plans within the same parity group are monitored for price consistency across channels. Enables grouping of comparable rate plans for parity compliance reporting.

The externally-known alphanumeric code identifying the rate plan as configured in the Central Reservation System (CRS) and Property Management System (PMS). Examples: BAR, NRR, LRA, CORP001, PKG-BB. This is the business identifier used across Sabre SynXis CRS and Oracle OPERA PMS for rate loading and distribution.

Human-readable descriptive name of the rate plan as displayed in the CRS and channel interfaces. Examples: 'Best Available Rate', 'Non-Refundable Rate', 'Last Room Availability Corporate', 'Bed and Breakfast Package'.

Classification of the rate plan by its commercial structure. BAR = Best Available Rate (dynamic public rate); NRR = Non-Refundable Rate (discounted, no cancellation); LRA = Last Room Availability (guaranteed availability for corporate); CORPORATE = negotiated corporate rate; PACKAGE = bundled rate with inclusions; PROMOTIONAL = limited-time promotional rate; WHOLESALE = net rate for tour operators; NEGOTIATED = contracted rate; OPAQUE = channel-opaque pricing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BAR|NRR|LRA|CORPORATE|PACKAGE|PROMOTIONAL|WHOLESALE|NEGOTIATED|OPAQUE — promote to reference product]

Comma-separated list of room type codes to which this channel rate plan applies. If null or 'ALL', the rate plan applies to all room types at the property. Examples: 'STD,DLX,STE' or 'ALL'. Supports room-type-specific channel pricing strategies.

The latest arrival date for which this channel rate plan is valid. Bookings must have an arrival date on or before this date. Nullable for open-ended rate plans. Supports seasonal and promotional rate plan management.

The earliest arrival date for which this channel rate plan is valid. Bookings made under this rate plan must have an arrival date on or after this date. Supports seasonal rate plan publishing and blackout date management.
The date and time when this channel rate plan configuration record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data processing in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
Unique identifier for this channel-package rate configuration. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to channel.channel_rate_plan. Business justification: Package rates distributed through channels may be based on or derived from channel rate plans. Adding nullable channel_rate_plan_id FK allows linking package pricing to the underlying rate plan when a

business_glossary_term: Channel Package Rate - Distribution Channel Id
description: Foreign key linking to the distribution channel through which the spa package is offered

business_glossary_term: Channel Package Rate - Spa Package Id
description: Foreign key linking to the spa package being distributed through the channel

Channel-specific cancellation policy code that overrides the packages default cancellation policy. Used when channel agreements require different cancellation terms (e.g., OTAs may require more flexible cancellation than direct bookings).

Channel-specific price for this spa package. May differ from rack rate due to channel agreements, commissions, or promotional strategies. This is the price displayed and charged through this specific channel.
Commission percentage charged by this channel for bookings of this specific package. May override the channels default commission rate for strategic packages or promotional periods.
Timestamp when this channel-package rate configuration was first created in the system.
Start date from which this channel-package rate configuration is active and bookings can be accepted through this channel.
End date after which this channel-package rate configuration expires and the package is no longer bookable through this channel. Null indicates indefinite availability.

Indicates whether this channel-package combination is currently active and accepting bookings. Revenue management uses this to control package availability by channel without deleting historical rate configurations.
Timestamp of the most recent rate update pushed to this channel for this package. Used for rate synchronization monitoring and troubleshooting.
Channel-specific minimum advance booking requirement in hours. May override the packages default minimum advance booking to accommodate channel-specific operational constraints or SLAs.
Timestamp of the most recent modification to this channel-package rate configuration.

Current status of rate loading to this channel for this package. LOADED = rates successfully pushed to channel, PENDING = awaiting transmission, FAILED = transmission error, SUSPENDED = temporarily disabled, INACTIVE = not currently distributed through this channel.
Unique surrogate identifier for each rate parity audit record captured during channel monitoring. Primary key for this transactional audit entity.

Reference to the OTA or distribution channel contract under which parity obligations are defined. Links to the channel contract master record for commission rates, parity clauses, and dispute resolution terms.
Reference to the distribution channel (OTA, GDS, direct web, voice, etc.) on which the audited rate was observed. Links to the channel master record.
Reference to the rate plan (e.g., BAR, LRA, NRR) under which the audited rate was published. Links to the rate plan master in the Revenue Management domain.
Reference to the property for which the rate parity audit was conducted. Links to the property master record in the Property domain.
Reference to the specific room type (e.g., King Deluxe, Double Standard) for which the rate was observed during the parity audit.

The number of days in advance of the stay date that the rate requires booking (advance purchase restriction). Used to ensure parity comparisons account for advance purchase rate conditions that may legitimately differ across channels.
Externally referenceable business identifier for this audit record, used in OTA contract compliance disputes and parity violation correspondence. Format: RPA-YYYY-MM-DD-XXXXXXXX.

Current lifecycle state of the rate parity audit record. 'open' indicates a new observation; 'violation_confirmed' indicates a confirmed parity breach; 'dispute_raised' indicates a formal dispute filed with the OTA/channel; 'resolved' indicates the channel corrected the rate; 'closed' indicates no action required; 'waived' indicates the violation was contractually waived.
The precise date and time at which the rate observation was captured during the automated or manual parity monitoring scan. This is the principal business event timestamp for this audit record.

The cancellation policy associated with the observed rate (refundable, non_refundable, partially_refundable). Ensures parity comparisons are made on equivalent rate conditions, as NRR rates are expected to be lower than refundable rates.
The URL or deep link to the channel page where the rate was observed at the time of the audit scan. Used as evidence in OTA parity dispute resolution and contract compliance documentation.

Classification of the distribution channel type. OTA (Online Travel Agency) includes Booking.com and Expedia; GDS (Global Distribution System) includes Sabre and Amadeus; direct_web is the hotel's own booking engine; metasearch includes Google Hotel Ads and TripAdvisor. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OTA|GDS|direct_web|voice|metasearch|wholesaler|corporate|consortium|bedbank — promote to reference product]

The contractually agreed parity rate threshold for this channel, rate plan, and property combination as defined in the OTA or GDS distribution agreement. May differ from the direct rate if a contracted rate floor or ceiling applies.
The date and time at which this rate parity audit record was first created in the data platform. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the observed rate, direct rate, and contracted parity rate are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures accurate cross-currency parity comparisons for international properties.

The rate available on the hotel's direct booking channel (own website or CRS direct) for the same room type, rate plan, stay date, and LOS at the time of the audit. Used as the parity benchmark for comparison.
The date on which a formal parity dispute was raised with the OTA or distribution channel partner. Null if no dispute has been filed. Used to track SLA compliance for dispute resolution timelines.

The reference number assigned when a formal parity dispute is raised with the OTA or distribution channel. Populated when audit_status transitions to 'dispute_raised'. Used for tracking dispute resolution correspondence.

The date on which the parity dispute was resolved and the channel corrected the rate or the dispute was formally closed. Null if the dispute is still open. Used to measure dispute resolution cycle time.

Indicates whether this audit record represents a confirmed rate parity violation, where the observed channel rate is lower than the direct booking rate or contracted parity rate beyond the allowable tolerance threshold. True = violation detected.

business_glossary_term: Rate Inclusive of Taxes and Fees Flag
description: Indicates whether the observed channel rate is inclusive of taxes and fees (True) or is a net/exclusive rate (False). Critical for accurate parity comparison as some channels display gross rates while others display net rates.
The length of stay in nights for which the rate was observed. Rate parity monitoring may vary by LOS as some channels offer LOS-based discounts that can trigger parity violations.

The meal plan included in the observed rate (RO=Room Only, BB=Bed and Breakfast, HB=Half Board, FB=Full Board, AI=All Inclusive). Ensures parity comparisons are made on a like-for-like basis when channels bundle different meal inclusions.

Identifies how the rate observation was captured. 'automated_scraper' indicates a rate shopping tool or API; 'manual_review' indicates a revenue manager manually checked; 'ota_alert' indicates the OTA itself flagged the discrepancy; 'partner_report' indicates a channel partner reported it; 'revenue_manager' indicates direct CRS/PMS review.
The actual room rate (in the property's local currency) observed on the audited channel at the time of the audit scan. This is the raw rate as displayed to the consumer on the channel.

The alphanumeric code identifying the rate plan under which the audited rate was published (e.g., BAR, LRA, NRR, CORP). Denormalized from the rate plan master for audit trail completeness and dispute documentation.
Human-readable name of the rate plan under which the audited rate was published (e.g., Best Available Rate, Last Room Availability, Non-Refundable Rate). Denormalized for audit trail readability.

Name of the rate shopping or competitive intelligence tool used to capture the channel rate observation (e.g., OTA Insight, RateGain, Duetto, Infor EzRMS). Relevant when monitoring_source is 'automated_scraper'.

The absolute monetary difference between the observed channel rate and the direct booking rate (observed_rate minus direct_rate). A negative value indicates the channel is undercutting the direct rate, constituting a parity violation.

The percentage difference between the observed channel rate and the direct booking rate, calculated as (observed_rate - direct_rate) / direct_rate * 100. Used to assess the severity of a parity violation relative to the direct rate.

Free-text notes documenting the outcome of the parity dispute, including the channel's response, corrective action taken, or reason for waiving the violation. Supports audit trail and OTA contract compliance documentation.

The date and time at which the parity audit record was reviewed by a revenue manager or channel distribution analyst. Supports audit trail and SLA compliance tracking for parity violation response times.
The name or employee identifier of the revenue manager or channel distribution analyst who reviewed and actioned this parity audit record. Populated for manually reviewed or escalated records.
Reference identifier or storage path to the screenshot or screen capture taken of the channel rate at the time of the audit. Serves as evidentiary documentation for OTA parity dispute resolution.
The specific arrival or stay date for which the rate was observed on the channel. Rate parity is evaluated per stay date to detect date-specific pricing discrepancies.

The allowable percentage variance between the channel rate and the direct/contracted rate before a parity violation is flagged. Defined per channel contract or property policy. Violations are only flagged when rate_variance_pct exceeds this threshold.

The date and time at which this rate parity audit record was last modified, such as when the audit status changed, dispute details were added, or resolution notes were updated. Supports change tracking and audit trail.

Severity classification of the rate parity violation based on the magnitude of the rate variance percentage. 'critical' typically indicates >10% variance; 'high' 5-10%; 'medium' 2-5%; 'low' <2%; 'none' for no violation. Drives escalation and dispute prioritization.

Classifies the nature of the rate parity violation. 'below_direct' means the channel rate is lower than the direct booking rate; 'below_contracted' means the channel rate is below the contracted parity rate; 'below_both' means the channel rate violates both thresholds; 'no_violation' means parity is maintained.
Unique surrogate identifier for each channel stop-sell, close-out, or restriction control record in the Silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this transactional entity.

Identifier of the rate plan (BAR, NRR, LRA, package, etc.) to which this restriction is scoped. Null indicates the restriction applies across all rate plans for the channel and room type combination.

Identifier of the specific room type (e.g., King Deluxe, Double Standard) subject to the stop-sell restriction. Null indicates the restriction applies to all room types at the property.

Free-text narrative providing additional context or justification for the restriction beyond the standardized reason code. Entered by the revenue manager or channel manager at the time of action.

Externally-known business identifier for this stop-sell or restriction control action, as generated by the CRS (Sabre SynXis) or channel manager. Used for cross-system traceability and audit reconciliation.

Current lifecycle state of the stop-sell or restriction control record. active: restriction is currently enforced; lifted: restriction has been removed; pending: restriction is queued for activation; cancelled: restriction was voided before activation; expired: restriction passed its scheduled end without explicit lift.

Classification of the inventory restriction being applied to the channel. stop_sell: no new bookings accepted; closed_to_arrival (CTA): no check-ins on the stay date; closed_to_departure (CTD): no check-outs on the stay date; min_los: minimum Length of Stay (LOS) required; max_los: maximum LOS allowed; close_out: full inventory closure.

Last stay date (inclusive) in the date range to which this restriction applies. Represents the end of the affected inventory window in the property calendar.

Status of the restriction transmission to the distribution channel via the CRS or channel manager. pending: queued for transmission; transmitted: sent to channel; confirmed: acknowledged by channel; failed: transmission error; retrying: re-attempting after failure. Number of units ordered for this line item. May represent guest count, number of pieces, hours of service, or equipment units depending on the item type and unit of measure.
Revenue classification for financial reporting and USALI compliance. Determines which revenue account this item posts to in the general ledger.
Calculated service charge amount for this line item. Derived from extended amount and service charge rate.
Indicates whether a service charge or gratuity applies to this line item. Common for food, beverage, and labor items.

Scheduled time when this item should be served or delivered to the event. Drives kitchen timing, service staff deployment, and guest experience.
Scheduled time when this item should be set up or prepared. Critical for kitchen production scheduling, AV setup coordination, and labor planning.
Free-text field for additional instructions, presentation requirements, or service notes. Communicated to kitchen, service staff, and AV technicians.
Calculated tax amount for this line item. Derived from extended amount and tax rate.
Indicates whether this line item is subject to sales tax or VAT. Determines tax calculation during billing.
