Scheduled indicates planned future audit, in progress means audit is actively being conducted, completed means audit fieldwork is done, under review indicates findings are being validated, closed means all corrective actions are complete, and cancelled means audit was terminated before completion.

Classification of the quality audit indicating the auditing body or program. Forbes Travel Guide evaluates luxury service standards, AAA Diamond assesses overall quality, brand standard ensures compliance with corporate guidelines, mystery shop provides anonymous guest perspective, internal QA validates operational adherence, and health safety covers regulatory compliance inspections.
Professional certification or credential number of the auditor, validating their qualification to conduct quality audits in the hospitality industry.
Full name of the primary auditor or inspector who conducted the quality audit. May represent an individual evaluator or lead inspector for team audits.
Name of the organization or entity that the auditor represents, such as Forbes Travel Guide, AAA, brand quality assurance team, or third-party mystery shopping firm.

Indicates whether the property met all mandatory brand standards and compliance requirements during the audit. True means full compliance achieved, false indicates brand standard violations requiring remediation.

The quality classification or rating level awarded based on audit results, such as Forbes 5-Star, Forbes 4-Star, AAA 5-Diamond, AAA 4-Diamond, or brand-specific tier designations. Drives market positioning and guest expectations.
The actual date when all required corrective actions were completed and verified. Used to measure remediation cycle time and compliance with deadlines.

Current status of corrective action implementation. Not started means no work has begun, in progress indicates active remediation, completed means property reports actions done, verified means auditor confirmed completion, and overdue means deadline has passed without completion.
The date by which all required corrective actions must be completed and verified. Used to track remediation timelines and ensure accountability for quality improvement.

Indicates whether the audit findings require formal corrective action plans to be developed and implemented. True means corrective actions are mandatory, false means audit was clean or findings are informational only.
The date and time when this quality audit record was first created in the system. Represents the initial data capture event for audit trail purposes.
Number of critical or high-severity deficiencies identified that pose immediate risk to guest safety, brand reputation, or regulatory compliance. Requires urgent remediation.
Total number of deficiencies, non-conformances, or findings identified during the audit. Provides a quantitative measure of quality gaps requiring corrective action.
Category score assessing physical property condition, maintenance standards, cleanliness, and facility upkeep. Evaluates the tangible aspects of the guest experience.
Category score assessing food and beverage operations including restaurant service, menu quality, culinary execution, bar service, and dining experience standards.
Indicates whether the audit results are tied to General Manager performance evaluation and accountability metrics. True means GM is formally accountable for audit outcomes and corrective actions.
Category score assessing room cleanliness, housekeeping standards, turndown service, linen quality, and overall room presentation. Critical component for guest satisfaction.
The date and time when this quality audit record was most recently updated. Used to track data currency and audit record lifecycle changes.
Number of major or medium-severity deficiencies identified that significantly impact guest experience or operational standards but do not pose immediate critical risk.
Number of minor or low-severity deficiencies identified that represent opportunities for improvement but do not materially impact guest experience or compliance.

Composite quality score representing the overall audit result. Scale and interpretation vary by audit type (e.g., Forbes uses 0-100, AAA uses point system). This is the primary metric for property quality classification.

Final audit outcome indicating whether the property met the required quality standards. Pass means all standards met, fail indicates significant deficiencies, conditional pass requires minor corrective actions, and pending means determination is under review.
The date of the previous quality audit of the same type conducted at this property. Used to calculate audit frequency and track historical score trends.
The quality classification or rating level held by the property before this audit. Used to track certification progression or regression over time.
The overall quality score from the previous audit of the same type. Used to calculate score variance and track quality improvement or degradation trends over time.
The scheduled or actual date of the follow-up audit conducted to verify that corrective actions have been effectively implemented and quality standards are now met.

Indicates whether a follow-up audit or re-inspection is required to verify corrective action effectiveness and validate quality improvement. True means re-inspection is mandatory, false means no follow-up audit needed.
The planned date when the audit is scheduled to conclude. Used for audit planning and resource allocation.
The planned date when the audit is scheduled to begin. Used for audit planning and resource allocation.
The change in overall score compared to the prior audit, calculated as current score minus prior score. Positive values indicate quality improvement, negative values indicate degradation.
Category score evaluating service quality, staff professionalism, guest interaction, and service delivery standards. Key component of overall audit assessment.
Category score evaluating spa and wellness facilities, treatment quality, therapist professionalism, and spa service standards. Applicable only to properties with spa amenities.

Reference to the staff member (housekeeping, concierge, or room service) who delivered the amenity.

Foreign key linking to experience.experience_special_request. Business justification: Amenity fulfillments often originate from guest special requests (e.g., champagne on arrival, extra pillows, dietary accommodations). This FK links the fulfillment record to the originating request, e

Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: F&B amenities (welcome drinks, birthday cakes, fruit baskets, minibar items) are prepared/delivered by specific outlets. Required for cost allocation, outlet workload tracking, inventory reconciliatio

Foreign key linking to compliance.food_safety_cert. Business justification: Amenity fulfillment involving food/beverage items (welcome amenities, in-room dining, special dietary requests) must reference food safety certifications for compliance verification, allergen tracking

Foreign key linking to experience.guest_experience_enrollment. Business justification: Amenities can be delivered as part of experience program enrollment (e.g., SALT program includes welcome amenity package, spa program includes in-room amenities). This FK links amenity delivery to the
Reference to the guest profile receiving the amenity.

Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Amenity transactions require GL account posting for revenue recognition (charged amenities) or expense recognition (complimentary). Critical for daily revenue posting, financial statement accuracy, an

Foreign key linking to property.facility. Business justification: Amenities are delivered to or consumed at facilities (spa treatment, pool cabana reservation, restaurant booking). Fulfillment tracking requires facility location for logistics coordination and facili
Reference to the property where the amenity is being fulfilled.
Reference to the reservation associated with this amenity fulfillment.

Foreign key linking to inventory.room_amenity. Business justification: Links guest amenity consumption to inventory amenity records for tracking usage rates, replenishment scheduling, cost allocation, and identifying missing/damaged amenities. Essential for amenity inven

Foreign key linking to experience.service_recovery_action. Business justification: Amenity fulfillments are often part of service recovery actions (e.g., complimentary champagne after a complaint, upgraded amenities as compensation). This FK links the fulfillment to the service reco
Reference to the external vendor or supplier who provided the amenity item, if sourced externally.
The actual date and time when the amenity was delivered to the guest room.

Classification of the amenity by purpose or program type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: welcome_gift|vip_package|loyalty_benefit|celebration_package|special_occasion|room_upgrade_amenity|complimentary_service — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Standardized code identifying the type of amenity (e.g., WINE_CHEESE, FRUIT_BASKET, FLOWERS, CHAMPAGNE, CHOCOLATE, WELCOME_KIT).
Detailed description of the amenity contents and presentation.
Human-readable name of the amenity item or package.
Reason for cancellation if the amenity fulfillment was cancelled.
Name or identifier of the person who cancelled the amenity fulfillment.
The date and time when the amenity fulfillment was cancelled.
Indicates whether a charge for the amenity has been posted to the guest folio (True/False).
The date and time when the amenity fulfillment record was first created in the system.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost amounts.
Name of the staff member who delivered the amenity.
The method or service channel used to deliver the amenity to the guest.
Any dietary restrictions or allergies to consider for food and beverage amenities.
Reference to the guest folio where the amenity charge was posted, if applicable.
Current status of the amenity fulfillment workflow.
Indicates whether the guest acknowledged receipt of the amenity (True/False).
The date and time when the guest acknowledged receipt of the amenity.
Free-text feedback or comments provided by the guest regarding the amenity.
Numeric satisfaction score provided by the guest for the amenity experience, typically on a scale of 1-5 or 1-10.
Indicates whether the amenity was provided complimentary (True) or charged to the guest (False).
User or system identifier of the person or process that last modified the amenity fulfillment record.
The date and time when the amenity fulfillment record was last updated.
The guest's loyalty program tier at the time of amenity fulfillment (e.g., Silver, Gold, Platinum, Diamond).

The special occasion or reason for the amenity delivery, if applicable. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: birthday|anniversary|honeymoon|business_milestone|vip_visit|apology|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Number of units or servings of the amenity to be delivered.
The room number where the amenity is to be delivered.
The date on which the amenity is scheduled to be delivered.
The precise date and time when the amenity is scheduled to be delivered to the guest room.
Total cost of the amenity fulfillment (quantity × unit cost) in the property's local currency.
Cost per unit of the amenity item in the property's local currency.
User or system identifier of the person or process that created the amenity fulfillment record.
Unique identifier for each guest interaction touchpoint record. Primary key for the guest_interaction data product.

Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: Guest interactions at F&B venues (sommelier consultations, chef table visits, dietary consultations, service recovery conversations) occur at specific outlets. Essential for touchpoint mapping, outlet

Foreign key linking to inventory.room_type. Business justification: Interaction analysis by room type (suite guests require more concierge service, economy rooms have more maintenance calls) enables product-level service design, staffing allocation, and identifying ro

Foreign key linking to loyalty.member. Business justification: Front desk, concierge, and service staff interactions require real-time loyalty tier visibility for benefit fulfillment (upgrades, late checkout), personalized greetings, and service level differentia
Reference to the employee who conducted the interaction. Enables staff engagement scoring and service quality attribution.
Reference to the guest who participated in this interaction. Links to the guest master data product.

Foreign key linking to property.facility. Business justification: Guest interactions occur at specific facilities (concierge desk, restaurant host stand, spa reception). Interaction analytics require facility-level segmentation for staffing optimization and service
Reference to the hotel property where the interaction occurred.
Reference to the reservation associated with this interaction, if applicable. Null for non-stay interactions.

Foreign key linking to inventory.room. Business justification: Guest interactions (in-room service calls, facility inquiries, room issue reports) require room linkage for context documentation, issue pattern identification by unit, and tracking interaction freque
Reference to a formal service recovery case if this interaction escalated to case management. Null for interactions that did not escalate.
User identifier of the staff member or system user who last modified this interaction record.

Foreign key linking to experience.touchpoint. Business justification: Guest interactions occur at specific touchpoints in the guest journey (pre-arrival, check-in, in-stay, checkout, post-stay). Linking interaction to touchpoint enables journey analytics and touchpoint
Description of the amenity or gesture offered, if applicable. Null if no amenity was provided.
Boolean indicator of whether a complimentary amenity or service recovery gesture was offered during this interaction.
System timestamp when this interaction record was first created in the source system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Code identifying the hotel department responsible for the interaction (e.g., FD for Front Desk, CONC for Concierge, HSK for Housekeeping, FB for Food and Beverage).
Target date by which follow-up action should be completed. Null if no follow-up is required.
Boolean indicator of whether this interaction requires subsequent follow-up action by staff or management.
Boolean indicator of whether the interaction was initiated by the guest (true) or proactively by staff (false). Distinguishes reactive vs proactive service.
Immediate satisfaction rating provided by the guest at the conclusion of the interaction, typically on a 1-5 or 1-10 scale. Null if not collected.
Length of the interaction in minutes. Used for staff productivity analysis and service quality assessment.
Three-letter ISO 639-2 language code indicating the primary language used during the interaction.
Specific location within the property where the interaction occurred (e.g., Front Desk, Lobby, Guest Room 305, Pool Area).
Detailed narrative notes captured by staff describing the interaction content, guest requests, and any commitments made. Confidential business information.
Human-readable business identifier for the interaction, used for tracking and reference in service recovery workflows.
The result or disposition of the interaction at the time of logging. Indicates whether the guest need was addressed or requires follow-up.
The operational system that originally captured this interaction record. Used for data lineage and quality assessment.
Brief summary or subject line describing the interaction topic. Used for quick reference and categorization.
The date and time when the interaction occurred in the property local timezone. Primary business event timestamp for journey sequencing and touchpoint analysis.

Classification of the interaction based on its primary purpose and nature. Enables journey mapping and touchpoint analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: check_in_greeting|check_out_farewell|concierge_inquiry|complaint|compliment|service_request|loyalty_recognition|proactive_outreach|room_visit|amenity_delivery|special_occasion_recognition|upsell_offer|feedback_solicitation|emergency_response|vip_welcome|other — promote to reference product]
System timestamp when this interaction record was last updated in the source system. Used for change tracking and audit trail.
Boolean indicator of whether this interaction is eligible for inclusion in Net Promoter Score survey sampling.
Assessed sentiment or emotional tone of the interaction, either manually classified by staff or automatically derived from text analytics.
Numeric sentiment score ranging from -100 (very negative) to +100 (very positive). Derived from natural language processing or manual assessment.
Boolean indicator of whether a special request made during this interaction was successfully fulfilled.
Boolean indicator of whether the guest was flagged as VIP at the time of interaction. Used for personalized service tracking.
Unique identifier for the guest journey touchpoint. Primary key.
Reference to a parent touchpoint if this touchpoint is a sub-touchpoint or variant of a broader interaction (self-referential hierarchy).
Indicates whether this touchpoint meets Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) accessibility requirements.
The degree of automation at this touchpoint (fully automated, semi-automated, manual, hybrid).
The brand code if this touchpoint is brand-specific (e.g., luxury, premium, select-service brand identifiers).

Indicates whether this touchpoint requires compliance review for regulatory adherence (e.g., General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA), Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS)).
The currency code for cost per interaction using ISO 4217 three-letter codes (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
The estimated or actual cost per guest interaction at this touchpoint, used for operational efficiency analysis.
The user identifier or name of the person who created this touchpoint definition.
The timestamp when this touchpoint record was created in the system.
Indicates whether this touchpoint involves collection or processing of privacy-sensitive guest data requiring special handling.
The default language code for this touchpoint using ISO 639-1 two-letter codes (e.g., en, es, fr, de, zh).
The department or functional area responsible for managing and delivering this touchpoint (e.g., Front Office, Housekeeping, Food and Beverage (F&B), Concierge, Guest Services, Revenue Management).
The date when this touchpoint definition expires or is no longer effective (null for indefinite).
The date when this touchpoint definition became or will become effective.
The method used to collect guest feedback at this touchpoint (survey, direct feedback, observation, system log, social media, review platform).
The target Guest Effort Score for this touchpoint, representing the desired ease of interaction (lower scores indicate less effort required).

The guest segments to which this touchpoint applies (e.g., all guests, loyalty members only, VIP guests, business travelers, leisure travelers, group bookings). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: all_guests|loyalty_members|vip_guests|business_travelers|leisure_travelers|group_bookings|families|couples — promote to reference product]

The specific channel through which the touchpoint interaction occurs (e.g., email, mobile app, front desk, in-room tablet, phone, SMS, chatbot, kiosk). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: email|mobile_app|web_portal|front_desk|concierge|phone|sms|chatbot|kiosk|in_room_tablet|social_media — promote to reference product]
Indicates whether this touchpoint is eligible for Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) survey measurement.
Indicates whether this touchpoint is eligible for Guest Satisfaction Score (GSS) measurement.
Indicates whether this touchpoint is mandatory for all guests (e.g., check-in, check-out) or optional.
Indicates whether this touchpoint is eligible for Net Promoter Score (NPS) survey measurement.
The stage of the guest journey lifecycle where this touchpoint occurs (pre-arrival, arrival, in-stay, departure, post-stay).
The user identifier or name of the person who last modified this touchpoint definition.
The timestamp when this touchpoint record was last modified.
The corresponding touchpoint identifier in the Medallia experience analytics platform for integration and feedback mapping.
Indicates whether this touchpoint supports multiple languages for international guests.
The business priority level assigned to this touchpoint for guest experience management (critical, high, medium, low).
Defines whether this touchpoint applies to all properties, specific brands, individual properties, or regions.
The corresponding touchpoint identifier in Salesforce Customer Relationship Management (CRM) for case management and guest interaction tracking.

business_glossary_term: Satisfaction and Loyalty Tracking (SALT) Program Linked Flag
description: Indicates whether this touchpoint is linked to the Satisfaction and Loyalty Tracking (SALT) program for performance measurement.
The typical sequence order of this touchpoint within its journey stage, used for journey mapping and visualization.
Indicates whether negative feedback or issues at this touchpoint automatically trigger service recovery workflows.
Indicates whether staff training is required to deliver this touchpoint effectively.
The category or modality of the touchpoint (digital, physical, voice, self-service, staff-assisted, automated).
Unique business code identifying the touchpoint type (e.g., PRE_ARRIVAL_EMAIL, CHECK_IN_DESK, IN_ROOM_DINING, CHECKOUT_KIOSK).
Detailed description of the touchpoint, its purpose, and the guest interaction it represents.
Human-readable name of the touchpoint (e.g., Pre-Arrival Welcome Email, Front Desk Check-In, In-Room Dining Order).
Current operational status of the touchpoint (active, inactive, pilot, deprecated, seasonal).
The typical or expected duration of the guest interaction at this touchpoint, measured in minutes.
The version number of this touchpoint definition, incremented when the touchpoint configuration is updated.
Unique surrogate identifier for the corporate account record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.

Foreign key linking to event.event_account. Business justification: Corporate accounts often have both transient room business (guest domain) and MICE business (event domain). Account managers need unified view of total account revenue, consolidated billing, and integ
Internal email address of the hotel group's account manager assigned to this corporate account. Used for escalation routing, contract renewal notifications, and CRM workflow automation.
Full name of the hotel group's internal account manager or sales manager responsible for managing the corporate relationship, contract renewals, and production performance.

Current lifecycle status of the corporate account. Controls rate eligibility, direct billing access, and CRM engagement workflows. Active accounts are eligible for negotiated rates and direct billing.

Classification of the corporate account by the nature of the client relationship. Drives rate eligibility, billing rules, and reporting segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: corporate|government|consortium|travel_management_company|wholesale|other — promote to reference product if additional types are required]

Target total revenue (rooms, Food and Beverage (F&B), events) expected from this corporate account in the contract year. Used for account manager performance tracking and revenue budget planning per USALI standards.
Contracted or projected annual room night volume committed by the corporate client. Used for account tiering, rate negotiation leverage, and production performance tracking against actuals.
City component of the corporate client's billing address. Used for invoice generation, tax jurisdiction determination, and geographic segmentation reporting.
ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the corporate client's billing address. Drives tax treatment, currency defaults, and regulatory compliance requirements (GDPR for EU entities).
First line of the corporate client's official billing address used for invoice generation and accounts receivable correspondence in SAP S/4HANA.

Free-text billing instruction specifying which charges (room, tax, incidentals, F&B) are to be direct-billed to the corporate account versus charged to the individual guest. Applied automatically at check-in in OPERA PMS.

Description or reference code for blackout date restrictions applicable to this corporate account's negotiated rates. Specifies periods (e.g., peak demand, special events) when contracted rates are not available.
Full legal registered name of the corporate client organization as it appears on contracts, invoices, and billing documents.
Date on which the negotiated rate agreement and corporate account contract expires. Nullable for open-ended agreements. Rate eligibility ceases after this date unless renewed.
Date on which the negotiated rate agreement and corporate account contract becomes effective. Reservations made on or after this date are eligible for contracted rates.
Timestamp when the corporate account record was first created in the source system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the credit limit and billing amounts are denominated for this corporate account (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).

Maximum outstanding balance permitted on the corporate account's direct billing (city ledger) facility in the OPERA AR module. Enforced at check-in and during reservation creation to prevent credit overexposure.
External account identifier from Salesforce CRM, used to cross-reference the corporate account record with the CRM system of record for guest profiles and marketing campaigns.

Indicates whether the corporate contact has provided consent for processing of their personal data in accordance with GDPR and CCPA requirements. Required for marketing communications and data sharing with third-party partners.

Indicates whether this corporate account is approved for direct billing (city ledger) to the hotel group. When true, eligible charges are posted to the AR account rather than requiring individual guest payment at checkout.

Percentage discount off the Best Available Rate (BAR) or rack rate granted to this corporate account under the negotiated agreement. Used by the Revenue Management System (RMS) for displacement analysis and yield optimization.

business_glossary_term: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Data Subject Region
description: Regulatory jurisdiction applicable to the corporate contact's personal data. Determines which privacy regulations govern data handling, retention, and subject rights requests (GDPR for EU/UK, CCPA for California).

business_glossary_term: North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) Code
description: Six-digit NAICS code providing a more granular industry classification for the corporate client, used alongside SIC for regulatory reporting, procurement analysis, and revenue segmentation.

Four-digit Standard Industrial Classification (SIC) code identifying the primary industry sector of the corporate client. Used for market segmentation, demand analysis, and MICE opportunity targeting.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the corporate account record in the source system. Used for incremental ETL processing, change data capture, and audit trail maintenance in the Silver Layer.
Minimum Length of Stay (LOS) requirement in nights applicable to this corporate account's negotiated rate. A value of 1 indicates no minimum stay restriction.
Indicates whether individual travelers booking under this corporate account are eligible to earn loyalty program points. Some negotiated rate codes exclude loyalty accrual per program terms.

Market segment code assigned to this corporate account in the Revenue Management System (RMS) and PMS. Used for STR benchmarking, RevPAR analysis, and demand forecasting segmentation (e.g., CORP, GOVT, CONSOR).

business_glossary_term: Meetings Incentives Conferences Exhibitions (MICE) Eligible Flag
description: Indicates whether this corporate account is eligible for MICE (Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, Exhibitions) group rates and event space allocations managed through Delphi by Amadeus.

Primary negotiated rate code assigned to this corporate account in the Central Reservation System (CRS) and Property Management System (PMS). Used for Best Available Rate (BAR) eligibility verification and rate distribution across channels. Denormalized natural-key reference; non-authoritative copy resolved per v2 rebuild (authoritative source is the linked parent via FK).

business_glossary_term: Oracle OPERA Accounts Receivable (AR) Account Number
description: Accounts Receivable account number assigned in Oracle OPERA PMS AR module, used for direct billing, city ledger management, and credit limit enforcement at the property level.
Agreed payment terms for direct billing invoices issued to this corporate account. Determines invoice due dates and triggers accounts receivable aging in SAP S/4HANA.

Comma-separated list of hotel property codes (from OPERA PMS) designated as preferred properties for this corporate account. Drives rate availability, inventory allocation, and channel distribution priority.
Business email address of the primary corporate contact. Used for contract communications, rate confirmations, and program updates. Subject to GDPR and CCPA data subject rights.
Full name of the primary travel coordinator or procurement contact at the corporate client organization responsible for managing the hotel program and employee travel bookings.
Business phone number of the primary corporate contact. Used for urgent communications regarding reservations, billing disputes, and contract negotiations.

Type of negotiated rate program governing this corporate account. LRA (Last Room Availability) guarantees rate access even at high occupancy. NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) offers deeper discounts with cancellation restrictions. Drives Revenue Management System (RMS) displacement analysis.

Tax exemption classification for this corporate account. Government and qualifying non-profit accounts may be fully or partially exempt from occupancy and sales taxes. Drives tax posting rules in OPERA PMS and SAP S/4HANA.

Official tax exemption certificate number issued by the relevant tax authority for this corporate account. Required for audit compliance when applying tax-exempt billing. Stored for SOX and USALI audit trail purposes.
Doing-business-as (DBA) or brand name of the corporate client if different from the legal registered company name. Used for correspondence and guest-facing communications.

VIP tier classification assigned to this corporate account based on production volume, strategic importance, and revenue contribution. Drives service recovery priority, amenity entitlements, and account manager assignment level.

business_glossary_term: Corporate Property Contract - Corporate Property Contract Id
description: Primary key for the corporate_property_contract association

business_glossary_term: Corporate Property Contract - Corporate Account Id
description: Foreign key linking to the corporate account that negotiated this property-specific contract
Foreign key linking to the specific property covered by this contract
Property-specific total revenue target (rooms, F&B, events) expected from this corporate account at this property. Varies by property size and account usage patterns.
Property-specific annual room night volume commitment. Targets allocated across properties based on corporate client travel patterns and property capacity.
Property-specific blackout dates or periods when negotiated rates are not available (e.g., peak season, special events). Blackout policies vary by property based on local demand patterns.
Date on which this property-specific contract expires. Renewal cycles vary by property based on local market conditions and negotiation timing.
Date on which this property-specific contract becomes effective. Each property contract has independent validity periods.
Current lifecycle status of this property-specific contract. Contracts managed independently per property with distinct activation and termination cycles.
Timestamp when this contract record was created in the system.
Maximum outstanding balance permitted for this corporate account at this specific property. Credit limits allocated across properties based on usage forecasts and risk assessment.
Indicates whether direct billing is approved for this corporate account at this specific property. Billing privileges granted property-by-property based on credit approval and operational capability.
Percentage discount off BAR or rack rate granted at this specific property. Discount levels vary by property based on volume commitments and competitive dynamics.
Date of the most recent pickup review meeting for this property-specific contract. Revenue management teams review actual vs. target room nights quarterly or monthly per property.
Minimum length of stay restriction for negotiated rate usage at this property. LOS requirements vary by property based on demand calendar and rate protection strategies.
Property-specific rate code assigned in the Central Reservation System for this corporate account at this property. Rate codes vary by property based on local competitive positioning.
Property-specific payment terms for direct billing invoices. Terms may vary by property based on local AR policies and account credit standing.
Email of the property-level account manager. Property-level contacts manage day-to-day contract execution and pickup tracking.
Name of the property-level sales manager or DOS responsible for managing this corporate account relationship at this specific property.
Type of rate program governing this property-specific contract (LRA, FIXED, DISCOUNT_PERCENT, TIERED). Program type negotiated independently per property based on demand patterns.
Timestamp when this contract record was last modified.
Unique surrogate identifier for this group function space booking record. Primary key.

business_glossary_term: Group Function Space Booking - Guest Group Block Id
description: Foreign key linking to the guest group block that is booking this function space

business_glossary_term: Group Function Space Booking - Meeting Space Id
description: Foreign key linking to the meeting space being reserved for this group function
Actual number of attendees who participated in this function. Captured post-event for reconciliation against guaranteed attendance.
Audio-visual equipment and technical requirements specific to this group function (projector, microphones, video conferencing, lighting). Drives AV labor and rental charges.
Date when this function space was reserved for the group block. Tracks when the booking commitment was made.
Current lifecycle status of this function space booking (tentative, definite, cancelled, completed). Tracks progression from hold to confirmed to executed.
Indicates whether the contractual minimum catering spend for this meeting space was met by the group function. Null if no minimum applies.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for rental_amount and other monetary values on this booking record.
Scheduled end date and time for the group function or event in this meeting space. Used for space turnover planning and billing.
Scheduled start date and time for the group function or event in this meeting space. Critical for space scheduling and setup coordination.
Number of attendees the group has guaranteed for this function. Used for catering billing and minimum charges even if actual attendance is lower.
Meeting space rental charge for this specific function booking. May vary based on duration, setup complexity, and negotiated group rates.
Room setup configuration requested for this specific group function (theater, classroom, banquet, reception, u-shape, hollow square, boardroom, custom). Determines labor requirements and capacity.
Free-text notes capturing special setup instructions, décor requirements, accessibility needs, or other function-specific details for operations and banquet teams.

Unique surrogate identifier for the guest group block record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key. Entity role: MASTER_AGREEMENT — represents a contracted room block allocation binding the property to a group organizer for a defined stay window.

Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Group blocks are frequently generated by marketing campaigns (wedding packages, conference promotions, corporate incentive programs). Group sales teams track campaign attribution for ROI measurement,
Reference to the accounts receivable (AR) master account or corporate billing account to which the group master folio charges are posted. Sourced from OPERA PMS AR module.
Reference to the guest profile of the primary group leader or organizer responsible for the block. Sourced from OPERA PMS guest profile or Salesforce CRM contact.
Reference to the internal hotel employee or sales coordinator assigned to manage this group block from the property side.
Reference to the property at which the group room block is contracted. Links to the property master in the property domain.
Number of ADA-compliant accessible rooms requested within the group block. Tracked for ADA compliance and pre-assignment planning.
The first date of the contracted room block period — the date the group is scheduled to begin checking in. Serves as EFFECTIVE_FROM for this agreement.

Contractual minimum pickup percentage the group must achieve to avoid attrition penalties. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 80.00 means the group must pick up at least 80% of contracted rooms). Aligns with revenue.wash_factor domain.

Free-text instructions governing how charges are routed and settled for the group block master folio (e.g., 'Room and tax to master; incidentals to individual guest folio', 'All F&B charges to master account'). Sourced from OPERA PMS cashiering module.

Current lifecycle state of the group room block. 'Tentative' indicates a hold pending contract execution; 'Definite' indicates a signed/confirmed block; 'Cancelled' indicates the block was released; 'Closed' indicates the group has departed and the block is reconciled. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this MASTER_AGREEMENT entity.
The date on which the group block was cancelled, if applicable. Null for active or closed blocks. Used for attrition penalty calculation and revenue recovery tracking.
Code referencing the cancellation and penalty policy applicable to this group block contract. Governs financial penalties if the group cancels after the cutoff date.
Number of complimentary (comp) rooms included in the group block contract, typically awarded on a ratio basis (e.g., 1 comp per 40 paid rooms). Tracked separately for revenue accounting under USALI.
The date on which the group block contract was formally executed and the block status moved to 'Definite'. Represents the BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP for the agreement lifecycle.
Negotiated room rate per night per room agreed in the group block contract. Expressed in the property's operating currency. Confidential commercial term.
Total number of room nights committed in the group block contract across all room types and all nights of the block period. Primary quantitative measure of block size.
Timestamp when the group block record was first created in the source system (OPERA PMS or Delphi). Serves as RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED for this entity.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values on this group block record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).

The contractual deadline by which individual reservations must be made against the block at the contracted group rate. Rooms not picked up by this date are released back to general inventory per the attrition policy.
Source system identifier from Delphi by Amadeus event sales platform linking this group block to the originating sales opportunity or group proposal. Enables cross-system traceability.
The last date of the contracted room block period — the date the group is scheduled to check out. Serves as EFFECTIVE_UNTIL for this agreement.
Contractual deadline by which the group deposit must be received to maintain the block in 'Definite' status.
Actual deposit amount received from the group organizer to date. Compared against deposit_required_amount to determine outstanding deposit balance.
Contractual deposit amount required from the group to confirm the block. Expressed in the currency defined by currency_code.

Externally-known alphanumeric code used to identify the group block across systems (OPERA PMS, CRS, OTA channels). Used by guests and agents to associate individual reservations to the block. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this agreement.
Human-readable name of the group (e.g., 'Smith Wedding Party', 'Acme Corp Q3 Sales Conference', 'Chicago Bulls Away Team'). Displayed on folios, rooming lists, and internal reports.

Free-text operational notes and special instructions for the group block (e.g., VIP handling requirements, early check-in requests, amenity preferences, security arrangements). Sourced from OPERA PMS group block notes field.

Rate plan code applied to individual reservations made under this group block. Links to the negotiated group rate in the revenue rate plan. Sourced from OPERA PMS and distributed via SynXis CRS. Denormalized natural-key reference; non-authoritative copy resolved per v2 rebuild (authoritative source is the linked parent via FK).

Categorical classification of the group travel party type. Drives billing rules, rate eligibility, and operational handling. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tour|corporate|wedding|sports_team|airline_crew|government|other — promote to reference product]
Timestamp of the most recent modification to the group block record in the source system. Serves as RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED for this entity. Used for incremental ETL processing.
Revenue management market segment classification assigned to this group block (e.g., GRP-CORP, GRP-LEISURE, GRP-CREW). Used for USALI-compliant revenue reporting and STR benchmarking segmentation.
Native block identifier from Oracle OPERA PMS Group Blocks module. Used for reconciliation and lineage tracing between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational source system.
Maximum number of rooms blocked on any single night within the group block period. Used for capacity planning, displacement analysis, and revenue management evaluation.
Indicates whether this group has stayed at the property in a prior year or period. Used for loyalty recognition, preferential rate negotiation, and retention analytics.
Contractual deadline by which the group organizer must submit the complete rooming list with individual guest names and room type preferences.
Current status of the rooming list submission for this group block. Tracks whether the group organizer has provided individual guest names and room assignments required for pre-arrival processing.
Current count of individual reservations made against the group block (actual pickup). Compared against contracted_room_nights to calculate pickup rate and attrition exposure.
Code identifying the channel or origin through which the group business was sourced (e.g., direct sales, GDS, OTA, travel agent, repeat group). Used for channel contribution analysis.
Number of suite-category rooms included in the contracted block. Tracked separately from standard rooms for inventory management and upsell reporting.
Indicates whether this group block has been designated as VIP, triggering elevated service protocols, dedicated concierge assignment, and executive-level attention.

Estimated percentage of contracted rooms expected to be released back to general inventory based on historical pickup patterns for this group type. Used by revenue management for net block sizing and forecasting.
Unique identifier for the guest lifetime value record. Primary key.
Reference to the guest profile for whom this lifetime value is calculated. Links to the guest master record in OPERA PMS and Salesforce CRM.
Reference to the property where the guest has stayed most frequently. Used for targeted marketing and personalized offers.
The guest's average daily rate across all stays, calculated as total room revenue divided by total room nights. Key pricing metric for guest segmentation.
The guest's average Guest Satisfaction Score across all completed satisfaction surveys. Typically measured on a 1-10 scale. Key quality metric.
The guest's average length of stay in nights, calculated as total room nights divided by total stays. Indicates booking behavior pattern.
The guest's average Net Promoter Score across all completed NPS surveys. Ranges from -100.00 to 100.00. Measures guest satisfaction and advocacy.
The guest's average total revenue per stay, calculated as total revenue divided by total stays. Measures spend intensity per visit.
The date on which this lifetime value calculation was performed. Represents the as-of date for all cumulative metrics.
The methodology used to calculate this LTV record: historical (cumulative actuals only), predictive (forecast-based), or hybrid (actuals plus forecast).
Boolean indicator (True/False) signaling whether the guest is at risk of churn based on declining engagement, negative feedback, or extended time since last stay.
Numeric score (0.0000 to 1.0000) quantifying the probability of guest churn within the next 12 months. Higher scores indicate higher churn risk.
Timestamp when this LTV record was first created in the lakehouse. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all revenue amounts are denominated. Typically the reporting currency of the enterprise.
Score (0.00 to 100.00) representing the completeness and accuracy of the underlying guest data used in this LTV calculation. Higher scores indicate higher confidence.
Number of days elapsed between the most recent stay date and the LTV calculation date. Recency metric used in churn risk assessment.
The date of the guest's first recorded stay across all properties and brands. Marks the beginning of the guest relationship lifecycle.
Number of days between the first stay date and the LTV calculation date. Represents the total duration of the guest relationship.
Timestamp of the most recent update to this LTV record. Tracks data freshness and supports incremental refresh logic.
The guest's loyalty program membership number. Links LTV to loyalty tier and rewards fulfillment. Personally identifiable.
Current loyalty program tier of the guest (e.g., member, silver, gold, platinum, ambassador). Influenced by LTV tier and total stays.
Numeric score representing the guest's lifetime value, calculated using a proprietary algorithm combining historical revenue, recency, frequency, and predicted future value.
Classification tier assigned to the guest based on total revenue and engagement metrics. Drives VIP designation and loyalty program benefits.
Primary market segment classification for this guest based on booking patterns (FIT, corporate, group, leisure, business). Aligns with revenue management segmentation.
Predictive model version
The date of the guest's most recent completed stay across all properties and brands. Used to calculate recency metrics.
Predictive score (0.0000 to 1.0000) indicating the likelihood that the guest will book another stay within the next 12 months. Generated by CRM predictive analytics.
Code representing the brand (luxury, premium, select-service) where the guest has generated the most revenue or stays. Drives brand affinity analysis.
Forecasted revenue expected from this guest over the next 12 months, calculated using predictive models based on historical patterns and propensity scores.
Timestamp when predictive scores generated
Cumulative ancillary revenue from spa, golf, parking, resort fees, and other non-room, non-F&B services purchased by this guest.
Cumulative count of formal complaints or service recovery cases logged for this guest across all properties. Impacts churn risk and service recovery prioritization.
Cumulative food and beverage revenue generated by this guest across all properties, including restaurants, bars, room service, and banquets.
Cumulative total revenue generated by this guest across all revenue streams (rooms, F&B, ancillary). Sum of total_room_revenue, total_fb_revenue, and total_ancillary_revenue.
Cumulative count of room nights consumed by this guest across all properties and brands. Key volume metric for LTV calculation.
Cumulative room revenue generated by this guest across all properties and brands, excluding taxes and fees. Primary revenue component of LTV.
Cumulative count of completed stays by this guest across all properties and brands since first stay date.
Boolean indicator (True/False) designating whether the guest has been granted VIP status based on LTV tier, loyalty tier, or special designation.
Unique surrogate identifier for each guest note or alert record in the system. Primary key for the guest_note data product.

Foreign key linking to event.event_booking. Business justification: Guest notes frequently reference event-related special requests, VIP event attendee handling instructions, or event-specific service recovery issues. Front desk, concierge, and event coordinators need

Reference to the specific staff member assigned to action or follow up on this note. Null if assigned to a department rather than an individual.
Reference to the guest profile to which this note is attached. Links the note to the unified guest identity record.
Reference to the property at which this note was created. Used to determine property-specific vs. chain-wide scope of the note.
Reference to the staff member who marked the note as resolved. Enables accountability and service recovery performance measurement.

The specific hotel business date on which the note action or trace is scheduled to be executed (e.g., the date a pre-arrival amenity should be placed). Distinct from expiry_date which governs visibility.

The department responsible for actioning this note. May differ from the authoring department (e.g., Front Desk authors a note assigned to Housekeeping). Drives departmental task routing in OPERA. Exact date and time when the note record was first created in the system. Provides precise audit trail for note origination.

Classification governing how long this note record must be retained per data governance policy. Standard follows default retention schedules; extended applies to VIP or legal-relevant notes; legal_hold prevents deletion during litigation; purge_eligible marks records ready for deletion.

Indicates whether this note should be automatically surfaced to front desk staff during the guest check-in process. True = display at check-in; False = do not auto-display. Supports pre-arrival briefing and service personalization.

Indicates whether this note should be surfaced to front desk staff during the guest check-out process. True = display at check-out; False = do not auto-display. Useful for billing alerts or follow-up actions at departure.

Indicates whether this note should be surfaced to reservations agents when a new booking is made for this guest. True = display during reservation creation; False = do not auto-display. Supports proactive service planning.
The date after which the note is no longer considered active or actionable. Null indicates the note has no expiry and remains permanently active. Used to auto-expire time-bound traces and alerts.
NLP-extracted topics from note text

Indicates whether the guest has provided consent for this type of personal preference data to be stored and processed under GDPR Article 6. True = consent obtained; False = consent not obtained or withdrawn. Critical for EU/EEA guest data compliance.

The guest segment classification at the time the note was created. Free Independent Traveler (FIT), corporate, group, VIP, loyalty elite, or airline crew. Supports segment-specific service delivery and analytics.

Indicates whether the note content is restricted to management-level staff only and should not be visible to all front-line employees. True = management-only visibility; False = visible to all authorized staff.
Indicates whether this note is specifically flagged as a VIP protocol alert requiring elevated service standards. True = VIP alert; False = standard note. Drives VIP pre-arrival briefing workflows.
ISO 639-1 language code indicating the language in which the note text was authored (e.g., en, fr, de, zh-CN). Supports multilingual property operations and translation workflows.
Date and time when the note was most recently updated by any staff member. Supports audit trail and change detection in the Silver layer ETL pipeline.

The guest's loyalty program membership number at the time the note was created. Captured denormalized to support loyalty-aware pre-arrival briefing and cross-property service continuity without requiring a join to the loyalty profile.

Secondary classification providing finer-grained grouping within a note type (e.g., Room Preference, Dietary Restriction, Billing Alert, VIP Protocol, Housekeeping Instruction, Security Alert). The business date on which the note was created, aligned to the hotel operational date (which may differ from the calendar date for overnight shifts). Used for daily operations reporting (DOR) and pre-arrival briefing.

Current lifecycle state of the note. Active notes are visible and actionable; resolved notes have been actioned; expired notes have passed their expiry date; cancelled notes were voided by staff; pending notes are awaiting review or approval.

Classification of the note indicating its business purpose. Preference captures guest likes/dislikes; complaint records a service failure; compliment records positive feedback; alert flags an operational action required; trace is a time-bound task or follow-up; general is unclassified. business_glossary_term: Oracle OPERA PMS (Property Management System) Trace ID
description: Source system identifier for the trace or alert record as assigned by Oracle OPERA PMS. Used for reconciliation and lineage tracing back to the operational system of record.
Indicates whether this note is marked as high priority, requiring immediate staff attention or pre-arrival action. True = high priority; False = standard priority.

Graduated priority ranking of the note for staff triage and pre-arrival briefing. Critical notes require immediate escalation; high notes require same-day action; medium notes are addressed during the stay; low notes are informational.
Indicates whether this note should automatically recur on every future stay for this guest (True) or is a one-time note (False). Supports persistent preference management for repeat guests.

Defines the frequency at which a recurring note is re-activated. Every_stay applies on each new reservation; annual applies once per calendar year; seasonal applies during defined seasonal periods; one_time applies only once. Null if recurrence_flag is False.

Freeform text describing the action taken to resolve or address the note. Captures what was done, by whom, and any follow-up required. Classified as confidential as it may contain sensitive guest interaction details.
Date and time when the note was marked as resolved or actioned by staff. Null if the note has not yet been resolved. Supports service recovery tracking and staff performance analytics.

Defines whether the note is visible only at the originating property (property) or across all properties in the chain (chain). Chain-wide notes support cross-property service continuity for loyalty and VIP guests.
Sentiment score derived from note text

The channel or interface through which the note was originally captured (e.g., PMS front desk terminal, CRM, mobile app, web portal, voice call, email, in-person interaction). The specific stay date or arrival date to which this note is contextually relevant. Used for time-bound notes that apply only during a particular stay window rather than the guest's entire profile history.

Full freeform text body of the note or alert as entered by property staff. Contains the detailed narrative, instruction, or observation. Classified as confidential as it may contain sensitive guest behavioral or preference information.
Confidence of topic extraction
Unique surrogate identifier for a guest preference record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the preference data product within the guest domain.
Reference to the staff member who captured or last updated this preference record. Supports accountability tracking and training analytics for preference capture quality.
Reference to the guest profile to whom this preference record belongs. Links the preference to the unified guest identity in the guest domain.
Reference to the property at which this preference was captured or is applicable. A null value indicates the preference applies globally across all properties for the guest.

Reference to the specific stay or reservation during which this preference was captured or last confirmed. Enables preference-to-stay linkage for analytics on preference fulfillment rates and guest satisfaction correlation.

Specific description of the guest's food or environmental allergy (e.g., 'severe peanut allergy - anaphylactic', 'shellfish allergy', 'latex allergy'). Classified as restricted health information under GDPR and CCPA. Triggers mandatory F&B and housekeeping alerts. Only populated when is_allergy = True.

Guest's stated preferences for in-room amenities and welcome items (e.g., 'extra towels', 'foam bath products', 'specific brand toiletries', 'fruit basket', 'champagne on arrival'). Free-form text to accommodate the wide variety of amenity requests. Communicated to housekeeping and concierge for pre-arrival setup.

Guest's stated preference for bed configuration in their room. Sourced from OPERA PMS guest profile and used during room assignment to match available inventory. Supports pre-arrival preparation and upsell opportunity identification. Guest's preferred channel for receiving hotel communications including pre-arrival messages, in-stay notifications, and post-stay follow-up. Drives marketing and operational communication routing in Salesforce CRM. Supports GDPR and CCPA consent management.

Date on which the guest provided consent for collection and use of this preference data. Required for GDPR and CCPA audit trails. Null if consent_given is False or consent was not explicitly recorded.

Indicates whether the guest has provided explicit consent for the collection and use of this preference data (True). Required for GDPR Article 7 and CCPA compliance. Preferences captured without consent must not be used for marketing personalization.
Timestamp when this preference record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and GDPR Article 30 records of processing activities compliance.

Data governance classification level for this preference record, aligned with the enterprise data classification policy. 'restricted' for health/allergy data, 'confidential' for personal preferences, 'internal' for operational preferences. Drives access control and data handling policies.

Guest's stated dietary restriction or special dietary requirement (e.g., 'vegetarian', 'vegan', 'halal', 'kosher', 'gluten-free', 'lactose-free', 'diabetic'). Communicated to F&B operations via MICROS POS and Delphi for event catering. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: vegetarian|vegan|halal|kosher|gluten_free|lactose_free|diabetic|low_sodium|nut_free|shellfish_free — promote to reference product]

Free-text notes from operational staff regarding the fulfillment of this preference, including reasons for non-fulfillment, substitutions made, or special circumstances. Supports service recovery workflows and guest history documentation.

Operational status indicating whether this preference was fulfilled during the most recent stay. 'pending' = not yet actioned; 'fulfilled' = successfully delivered; 'partially_fulfilled' = partially met; 'not_available' = could not be accommodated (e.g., room type unavailable); 'waived' = guest waived the preference on arrival.

Guest's preferred timing and frequency for housekeeping service. Communicated to the housekeeping management system to schedule room servicing. 'do_not_disturb' and 'checkout_only' options support sustainability programs and guest privacy preferences.

business_glossary_term: Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) Requirement Flag
description: Indicates whether this preference represents an ADA-mandated accessibility accommodation (True), such as roll-in shower, hearing-accessible room, visual alert devices, or accessible room location. ADA requirements carry legal compliance obligations and must be fulfilled without substitution.

Indicates whether this preference record represents a food or environmental allergy (True). Allergy records trigger mandatory F&B and housekeeping alerts and are subject to heightened fulfillment controls per ISO 22000 food safety standards.

Indicates whether this preference is a hard requirement that must be fulfilled (True) or a soft preference that should be accommodated when possible (False). Mandatory preferences include ADA accessibility requirements, severe food allergies, and medically necessary accommodations.

Guest's preferred language for communications, in-room materials, and staff interactions. Stored as BCP 47 language tag (e.g., 'en', 'fr', 'es', 'zh-CN', 'de'). Used to personalize pre-arrival communications, in-room collateral, and staff greeting protocols.

Date on which the guest most recently confirmed this preference remains accurate and current. Used to identify stale preferences that may need re-validation during pre-arrival contact or check-in. Supports GDPR data accuracy principle.

The guest's loyalty program tier at the time this preference was captured (e.g., 'Silver', 'Gold', 'Platinum', 'Diamond'). Provides context for preference prioritization and fulfillment SLA — higher-tier guests may receive elevated preference fulfillment commitments.

Guest's preferred newspaper or publication for complimentary delivery (e.g., 'The Wall Street Journal', 'The New York Times', 'Financial Times', 'USA Today', 'local paper'). Used by front desk and concierge for pre-arrival preparation and morning delivery scheduling.

Guest's stated preference for pillow type and firmness. Sourced from OPERA PMS guest profile and communicated to housekeeping for pre-arrival room preparation. High-level category classifying the type of guest preference. Drives routing to the appropriate operational team (e.g., housekeeping, F&B, front desk). Standardized alphanumeric code representing the preference value as defined in the Property Management System (OPERA PMS) preference code table. Used for system-to-system integration and operational workflow routing (e.g., 'KGBED', 'NONSM', 'OCNVW', 'VEGAN').

Current lifecycle status of the preference record. 'active' indicates the preference is valid and should be honored; 'inactive' indicates it has been withdrawn or expired; 'pending_verification' indicates it requires staff confirmation; 'superseded' indicates it has been replaced by a newer preference record.

Specific type of preference within the category (e.g., 'floor_level', 'bed_type', 'smoking_status', 'view_type', 'pillow_firmness', 'dietary_restriction', 'allergy', 'newspaper_title', 'housekeeping_time', 'room_temperature'). Numeric rank indicating the relative importance of this preference when multiple preferences of the same type exist for a guest. Lower values indicate higher priority (1 = highest). Used by pre-arrival preparation workflows to resolve conflicts.

Guest's stated preference for the floor level of their room assignment. Used by front desk and room assignment workflows in OPERA PMS to optimize room allocation during check-in and pre-arrival room blocking.

Guest's preferred room temperature setting in degrees Celsius. Used for pre-arrival room preparation and smart room automation where available. Stored in Celsius for standardization; display conversion to Fahrenheit handled at the presentation layer.

Guest's preference for smoking or non-smoking room designation. Critical for room assignment compliance and property policy adherence. Most properties are entirely non-smoking; this field captures exceptions and guest-stated preferences for reporting.

Indicates how the preference was captured: 'stated' (guest explicitly requested), 'inferred' (derived from stay history patterns), 'observed' (noted by staff during stay), 'crm_imported' (sourced from Salesforce CRM), 'loyalty_profile' (from loyalty program record), 'survey' (from Medallia feedback), 'service_recovery' (captured during a service recovery interaction). The native identifier of this preference record in the originating source system (e.g., OPERA PMS preference record ID, Salesforce CRM preference object ID). Enables traceability back to the system of record for reconciliation and audit.
Timestamp when this preference record was most recently modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and audit compliance.

Date after which this preference record expires and should no longer be honored. Null indicates the preference is open-ended with no expiry. Supports GDPR data minimisation requirements by enabling automatic expiry of time-limited preferences.

The stated or inferred value of the preference (e.g., 'high floor', 'king bed', 'non-smoking', 'ocean view', 'firm pillow', 'vegetarian', 'gluten-free', 'The Wall Street Journal', 'do not disturb before 10am', '72°F'). Free-form string to accommodate the wide variety of preference types.

Guest's stated preference for the type of view from their room. Used during room assignment and pre-arrival blocking to enhance guest satisfaction. Supports upsell workflows when preferred view type is available at a premium. Date from which this preference record is effective and should be honored during guest stays. Supports time-bound preferences (e.g., a temporary dietary restriction during a medical recovery period) and preference versioning.
Unique surrogate identifier for each completed guest stay record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the stay_history data product. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS post-checkout records.

Reference to the corporate account associated with this stay, applicable when guest_type is 'corporate'. Links to negotiated rate agreements and corporate travel program tracking. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS accounts receivable module.

Reference to the group block associated with this stay, applicable when guest_type is 'group'. Links to group contract, pickup tracking, and group revenue attribution. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS group module.
Reference to the guest profile record representing the primary registered guest for this stay. Supports Lifetime Value (LTV) calculation, segmentation, and personalization across all properties.
Reference to the property where the stay occurred. Enables property-level performance analytics including ADR, RevPAR, and OCC reporting per USALI standards.
Reference to the originating reservation record from which this stay history was created upon checkout. Links stay history back to the reservation domain for full booking-to-stay traceability.

Average Daily Rate (ADR) for the stay, calculated as total room revenue divided by the number of occupied room nights. A primary KPI per USALI and STR STAR Report standards. Expressed in the property's local currency. Used for RevPAR computation and competitive benchmarking via STR.

Total ancillary charges posted to the guest folio during the stay, including spa, parking, laundry, telephone, and other non-room, non-F&B charges. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS. Contributes to TRevPAR and GOPPAR calculations per USALI.

Actual date the guest checked in to the property (yyyy-MM-dd). May differ from the originally reserved arrival date in cases of early arrival or late arrival. Used as the principal business event date for stay analytics and STR benchmarking.

Code identifying the distribution channel through which the reservation was originally made (e.g., direct_web, ota, gds, voice, walk_in, corporate_direct). Sourced from Sabre SynXis CRS channel mapping. Used for channel contribution and OTA partnership analysis.

Date on which the reservation was originally created (yyyy-MM-dd). Used to calculate booking lead time (days between booking_date and arrival_date), a key input for demand forecasting and pickup report analysis.

Exact date and time the guest completed the check-in process at the front desk or via mobile/digital check-in (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS front desk module. Used for operational SLA tracking and guest experience analytics.

Exact date and time the guest completed the checkout process (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS cashiering module. Marks the point at which the stay record is finalized and posted to the stay history.

Indicates whether the stay was provided on a complimentary (no-charge) basis (True = complimentary; False = revenue-generating stay). Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS. Used for USALI complimentary room reporting and GOP calculation.

Externally-known alphanumeric confirmation number assigned by the Central Reservation System (CRS) or Property Management System (PMS) at time of booking. Used by guests and agents to reference the stay. Sourced from Sabre SynXis CRS or Oracle OPERA PMS.

Timestamp when this stay history record was first created in the silver layer lakehouse, typically upon post-checkout processing from Oracle OPERA PMS (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for data lineage, audit trail, and ETL pipeline monitoring.

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all monetary amounts on this stay record are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency properties and international financial reporting under IFRS/GAAP.

Actual date the guest checked out of the property (yyyy-MM-dd). May differ from the originally reserved departure date in cases of early departure or extended stay. Used in conjunction with arrival_date to compute Length of Stay (LOS).

Indicates whether the guest activated Do Not Disturb (DND) status during the stay (True = DND activated at any point; False = DND not activated). Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS housekeeping module. Used for housekeeping scheduling and guest preference analytics.

Total Food and Beverage (F&B) charges posted to the guest folio during the stay, including restaurant, bar, room service, and minibar charges. Sourced from Oracle Hospitality MICROS POS. Contributes to TRevPAR calculation.
Topics extracted from review/feedback for the stay

Overall Guest Satisfaction Score (GSS) for the stay as captured by Medallia post-stay survey, typically on a scale of 1-10 or 1-100 depending on survey instrument. Distinct from NPS; measures overall satisfaction rather than likelihood to recommend. Used for Satisfaction and Loyalty Tracking (SALT) reporting.

Classification of the guest stay by traveler category. 'fit' (Free Independent Traveler) indicates leisure individual; 'corporate' indicates business traveler under a negotiated account; 'group' indicates part of a group block; 'crew' indicates airline or transport crew; 'complimentary' indicates no-charge stay. Drives segmentation and LTV modeling.

Actual number of nights the guest stayed at the property, computed as departure_date minus arrival_date. A core metric for Average Length of Stay (ALOS) analysis, revenue management, and demand forecasting in IDeaS G3 RMS.

Loyalty program membership number associated with the guest for this stay. Used to post loyalty points, track qualifying nights, and calculate tier status. Sourced from Salesforce CRM loyalty management module. Subject to GDPR and CCPA privacy regulations.

Number of loyalty program points earned by the guest for this stay, based on the applicable earning rate for the rate plan, room type, and member tier. Sourced from Salesforce CRM loyalty management. Used for loyalty program liability accrual and member engagement analytics.

The guest's loyalty program tier level at the time of the stay (e.g., Silver, Gold, Platinum, Diamond). Captured as a snapshot to preserve historical tier context for LTV analysis and personalization, independent of the guest's current tier. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product as tiers vary by brand]

Market segment classification assigned to the stay per USALI and STR reporting standards (e.g., transient, group, contract, wholesale). Used for Revenue Management System (RMS) demand forecasting and STR STAR Report benchmarking.

Net Promoter Score (NPS) provided by the guest for this stay, on a scale of 0-10. Sourced from Medallia post-stay survey. Used for Guest Satisfaction Score (GSS) tracking, service recovery identification, and experience analytics. Null if guest did not respond to survey.
Count of adult guests (18 years and older) registered for the stay as recorded in Oracle OPERA PMS. Used for occupancy reporting, F&B revenue forecasting, and capacity planning.
Count of child guests (under 18 years) registered for the stay as recorded in Oracle OPERA PMS. Used for family segment analytics, amenity planning, and F&B revenue forecasting.
The arrival date as originally reserved at time of booking, before any modifications. Used to measure early arrival or late arrival deviations and to support wash factor analysis in IDeaS G3 RMS.

The departure date as originally reserved at time of booking, before any modifications. Used to identify early departure and extended stay patterns for demand forecasting and wash factor analysis in IDeaS G3 RMS.

Primary payment instrument used to settle the guest folio at checkout. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS cashiering module. Used for payment mix analysis and PCI DSS compliance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|cash|direct_bill|loyalty_points|bank_transfer|voucher|cryptocurrency — promote to reference product]

Number of nights from this stay that count toward the guest's loyalty program tier qualification. May differ from los_nights if the rate plan is non-qualifying (e.g., wholesale, complimentary). Sourced from Salesforce CRM loyalty management.

Code identifying the rate plan under which the stay was booked (e.g., BAR, LRA, NRR, corporate negotiated, package). Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS and Sabre SynXis CRS. Critical for revenue management analysis and channel contribution reporting.
Physical room number assigned to the guest for the duration of the stay as recorded in Oracle OPERA PMS. Supports housekeeping scheduling, maintenance tracking, and room-level revenue attribution.

Total room charges posted to the guest folio for the entire stay, excluding taxes, fees, and ancillary charges. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS cashiering module. Core component of RevPAR and TRevPAR calculations per USALI.

Standardized code identifying the room type category occupied during the stay (e.g., KING, DBLQ, SUITE, DLXK). Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS room inventory. Used for room type mix analysis, upsell tracking, and RevPAR decomposition.

Indicates whether a service recovery action was initiated during or after this stay (True = service recovery case opened; False = no service recovery). Sourced from Medallia or Salesforce CRM case management. Used for Guest Recovery Rate (GRR) calculation and experience analytics.

Outcome status of the guest stay upon checkout processing. 'completed' indicates normal checkout on scheduled departure date; 'early_departure' indicates guest checked out before scheduled departure; 'extended' indicates stay was prolonged beyond original departure date; 'no_show' indicates reservation was not cancelled but guest never arrived; 'cancelled' indicates reservation was cancelled post-check-in processing.

Total tax charges applied to the guest folio for the stay, including occupancy tax, VAT, city tax, and other applicable levies. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS cashiering. Required for IFRS/GAAP financial reporting and SOX compliance.

Total amount charged to the guest folio for the entire stay, inclusive of room revenue, F&B revenue, ancillary revenue, and taxes. Represents the gross billing amount settled at checkout. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS cashiering module.

Timestamp when this stay history record was last modified in the silver layer lakehouse (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Captures post-checkout adjustments such as folio corrections, survey score updates, or loyalty point recalculations. Used for data lineage and audit compliance.

VIP designation code assigned to the guest for this stay in Oracle OPERA PMS (e.g., VIP1, VIP2, VVIP, CELEB). Drives special handling, amenity delivery, and management attention protocols. Null if guest has no VIP designation.

Unique surrogate identifier for each VIP designation record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the vip_designation data product. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS VIP code assignments and Salesforce CRM VIP profile records.

Reference to the corporate account record when the VIP designation is granted based on a corporate relationship (e.g., key account executive, contracted corporate partner). Null for individual leisure VIP guests. Sourced from Salesforce CRM account object.

Reference to the loyalty program membership record associated with this VIP guest. Links VIP designation entitlements with loyalty tier benefits to ensure non-duplication and correct benefit stacking. Sourced from Salesforce CRM loyalty management module.

Unique identifier for the communication consent record. Primary key.
Reference to the guest profile who provided or withdrew consent. Links to the guest master record in CRM.
