The date on which the consent expires and must be re-confirmed. Null for consents without expiration. Some jurisdictions and internal policies require periodic re-consent.
The date on which the guest provided explicit consent for the specified communication type and purpose. Critical for GDPR and CCPA compliance audit trails.
Precise timestamp when consent was granted, including time zone information. Provides granular audit trail for regulatory compliance.
Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code indicating the language in which the consent was presented and captured. Required for demonstrating informed consent in the guest native language.

The mechanism by which consent was captured. Distinguishes between explicit opt-in (GDPR-compliant), implicit opt-in, pre-checked box (non-compliant in many jurisdictions), verbal consent, written consent, or electronic signature.
Free-text notes providing additional context about the consent record. May include details about special circumstances, guest requests, or staff observations during consent capture.

The business purpose for which the communication consent applies. Examples include marketing campaigns, promotional offers, transactional notifications, service updates, loyalty program communications, and guest satisfaction surveys.

The channel or touchpoint through which the guest provided or withdrew consent. Current status of the guest consent. Indicates whether the guest has opted in, opted out, has a pending consent request, or if consent has expired or been revoked.
Version identifier of the consent text or privacy policy that was presented to the guest at the time of consent. Enables audit trail of what terms the guest agreed to.
The communication channel or method for which consent is being tracked. Distinguishes between marketing email, transactional email, SMS, push notifications, postal mail, and phone calls.
The date on which the guest withdrew or revoked their consent. Null if consent has not been withdrawn. Required for right-to-withdraw compliance under GDPR Article 7(3).
Precise timestamp when consent was withdrawn, including time zone information. Null if consent remains active. Critical for immediate suppression of communications.
Timestamp when this consent record was first created in the data platform. Part of standard audit trail for data lineage and compliance.
The unique consent record identifier from the source CRM system (Salesforce). Used for traceability and reconciliation with the operational system of record.
Timestamp when the guest confirmed their consent via double opt-in mechanism (e.g., clicked email confirmation link). Null if double opt-in was not used or not yet confirmed.

Indicates whether the consent was confirmed through a double opt-in process (e.g., email confirmation link). True if double opt-in was completed, False otherwise. Best practice for email marketing consent.
Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the guest primary country of residence at the time consent was captured. Used to determine applicable privacy regulations.
The IP address from which the consent was submitted. Used as part of the audit trail to demonstrate consent authenticity. May be considered PII in some jurisdictions.

The primary privacy regulation or jurisdiction governing this consent record. The lawful basis under GDPR Article 6 for processing the guest personal data for this communication purpose. Options include consent, legitimate interest, contract fulfillment, legal obligation, vital interest, or public task.

Indicates whether the guest has consented to automated profiling and decision-making for personalized marketing and offers. Required under GDPR Article 22.
Indicates whether this consent record is currently active and should be used for consent enforcement. False if the record has been superseded or logically deleted.

business_glossary_term: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Lawful Basis
description: The GDPR Article 6 lawful basis under which this contact record is processed. Required for all guest contact data belonging to EU/EEA residents. Consent is the most common basis for marketing; contract is the basis for transactional communications related to reservations.

Indicates whether this contact record is the designated primary contact for the guest within its channel type. Only one record per guest per channel should be flagged as primary. Used by CRS and PMS for reservation confirmations and pre-arrival communications.

Indicates whether this contact record has been verified through a confirmation process (e.g., email verification link, phone OTP, address validation). Verified contacts have higher data quality confidence and are preferred for critical communications such as reservation confirmations.

Date when the guest submitted the privacy request.
Precise timestamp when the privacy request was received by the system.
Comma-separated list of operational systems where guest data was accessed, modified, or deleted to fulfill the request (e.g., OPERA PMS, Salesforce CRM, Medallia).
Indicates whether third-party data processors or recipients must be notified of the privacy request action (e.g., erasure, rectification).
Status of notifications sent to third-party data processors or recipients regarding the privacy request.
Method used to verify the identity of the guest submitting the privacy request.
Status of guest identity verification process required before fulfilling the privacy request.
Timestamp when guest identity was successfully verified for the privacy request.

Unique surrogate identifier for the guest master profile record in the lakehouse silver layer. This is the primary key and golden record anchor for all guest identity resolution across properties and brands. Role: MASTER_PARTY.

When profile_status = 'merged', this field references the survivor profile_id into which this record was consolidated. Enables audit trail of merge lineage and supports identity resolution queries across the guest domain.

Free-text or coded description of the guest's accessibility requirements (e.g., wheelchair accessible room, roll-in shower, hearing loop, visual impairment aids). Drives ADA-compliant room assignment and service delivery. Treated as sensitive personal data.
The guest's date of birth in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-dd). Used for age verification, birthday recognition programs, loyalty tier benefits, and regulatory identity verification at check-in.

The name of the company or corporate account associated with this guest profile. Used for corporate rate eligibility, billing to master accounts, and corporate segment analytics. Applicable primarily to CORPORATE guest type.

business_glossary_term: Guest Country of Residence Code (ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3)
description: The guest's country of permanent residence as an ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 code. Distinct from nationality; used for geographic segmentation, GDPR/CCPA jurisdiction determination, and source market analytics.

The timestamp when the guest master profile record was first created in the source system of record (Oracle OPERA PMS or Salesforce CRM). Expressed in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Satisfies RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED requirement for MASTER_PARTY role.

The property code (hotel/resort identifier) at which this guest profile was originally created in the PMS. Used for cross-property profile governance, data stewardship assignment, and origin analytics.

The native contact identifier assigned by Salesforce CRM for this guest. Enables bi-directional synchronization between the PMS golden record and the CRM guest profile for marketing, loyalty, and case management workflows.
The date on which the guest provided or last renewed their data privacy consent. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance audit trails, consent expiry management, and regulatory reporting.

The primary email address for the guest used for reservation confirmations, pre-arrival communications, loyalty program notifications, and post-stay surveys via Medallia. Subject to GDPR and CCPA consent management.

Granular consent flag specifically for email marketing communications, separate from the overall marketing opt-in. Supports channel-specific consent management as required by GDPR, CCPA, and CAN-SPAM regulations.
The guest's legal family name (surname) as captured at check-in or profile creation. Used for identity verification, folio generation, and cross-property guest recognition.

business_glossary_term: GDPR Right to Erasure Request Flag
description: Indicates whether the guest has submitted a GDPR Article 17 Right to Erasure (Right to be Forgotten) request. True = erasure request received and pending or in-process. Triggers data suppression workflows and prevents further marketing processing.
The guest's self-identified gender code: M=Male, F=Female, X=Non-binary/Other, U=Undisclosed. Used for personalization, regulatory reporting in certain jurisdictions, and demographic analytics.
The guest's legal given name (first name) as captured at check-in or profile creation. Used for personalized guest recognition, folios, and correspondence across all properties.

Classification of the guest by travel purpose and booking relationship: FIT (Free Independent Traveler), CORPORATE (business traveler under corporate account), GROUP (group block participant), CREW (airline/transport crew), TRAVEL_AGENT, VIP. Drives rate eligibility, service protocols, and reporting segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FIT|CORPORATE|GROUP|CREW|TRAVEL_AGENT|VIP|WHOLESALE|GOVERNMENT — promote to reference product]

Indicates whether this profile record is the surviving (golden) record following a guest profile merge operation. True = this is the authoritative survivor record; False = this is a source or non-survivor record. Critical for identity resolution and deduplication governance.

The professional job title of the guest as provided during profile creation or CRM synchronization. Supports corporate account management, MICE event coordination, and personalized service delivery for business travelers.
The timestamp of the most recent modification to the guest profile record in the source system. Used for change data capture (CDC), ETL incremental loads, and audit trail compliance.
The date on which the guest enrolled in the loyalty program. Used to calculate member tenure, tier qualification windows, and anniversary recognition benefits.

The guest's loyalty program membership number assigned by the central loyalty platform (Salesforce CRM / loyalty engine). The primary identifier linking the guest profile to loyalty tier, points balance, and rewards history.

The guest's current loyalty program tier level, determining benefit eligibility, upgrade priority, and service recognition protocols. Tiers progress from NONE (non-member) through SILVER, GOLD, PLATINUM, to DIAMOND (highest).

Indicates whether the guest has provided explicit consent to receive marketing communications (email, SMS, direct mail). True = opted in; False = opted out or no consent given. Mandatory for GDPR and CCPA compliance in marketing campaign execution.
The guest's middle name or initial as provided. Supports full legal name matching for identity verification, passport validation, and regulatory compliance.
The guest's mobile/cell phone number in E.164 format. Used for SMS pre-arrival messaging, mobile check-in notifications, and digital key delivery.

Honorific or salutation prefix for the guest (e.g., Mr, Mrs, Dr). Used in personalized correspondence, folio headers, and VIP recognition communications. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Mr|Mrs|Ms|Miss|Dr|Prof|Sir|Rev — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Name suffix for the guest (e.g., Jr., Sr., III, PhD). Supports full legal name representation for identity documents and formal correspondence.
The guest's nationality expressed as an ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Required for regulatory reporting, visa compliance, and international guest analytics.

The native guest profile identifier assigned by Oracle OPERA Property Management System (PMS). Used for cross-system reconciliation and source-system traceability back to the PMS guest profile record.
The expiration date of the guest's passport document. Used to validate document currency at check-in and for regulatory compliance in jurisdictions requiring valid travel document verification.

business_glossary_term: Guest Passport Issuing Country Code (ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3)
description: The ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the authority that issued the guest's passport. Required for immigration compliance and international guest regulatory reporting.

The guest's passport document number as presented at check-in. Required for international guest identity verification, regulatory reporting to local authorities, and compliance with immigration laws in applicable jurisdictions.
The primary contact phone number for the guest in E.164 international format. Used for reservation confirmations, pre-arrival contact, and service recovery outreach.
The guest's preferred bed configuration for room assignment: KING, QUEEN, DOUBLE, TWIN, or SINGLE. Used during pre-arrival room blocking and at check-in to maximize guest satisfaction.

The guest's preferred floor level or floor range for room assignment (e.g., high floor, low floor, specific floor number). Captured from guest preferences in OPERA PMS and used during pre-arrival room blocking.

The guest's preferred communication language expressed as an ISO 639-1 two-letter language code (e.g., en, fr, de, zh). Drives language selection for folios, correspondence, digital communications, and on-property service delivery.

The guest's stated or historically derived preferred room type (e.g., King Deluxe, Suite, Double Queen). Stored as a room type code from the PMS room type configuration. Used by front desk for pre-arrival room assignment and upsell targeting.

Current lifecycle state of the guest master profile: active (in use), inactive (dormant, no recent stays), merged (absorbed into a survivor record), suspended (temporarily blocked), deceased (confirmed deceased, retained for audit). Governs record visibility and processing eligibility.

The guest's smoking preference for room assignment: NON_SMOKING, SMOKING (where available), or NO_PREFERENCE. Used during pre-arrival room blocking and check-in assignment to comply with guest preferences and property smoking policies.

Granular consent flag specifically for SMS/text message marketing communications. Supports channel-specific consent management as required by GDPR, CCPA, and TCPA (Telephone Consumer Protection Act) regulations.

The VIP designation level assigned to the guest in Oracle OPERA PMS, indicating the tier of elevated service protocols to apply at check-in and throughout the stay. NONE=standard guest, VIP1-VIP3=escalating VIP tiers, VVIP=highest recognition, BLACKLIST=do-not-accommodate flag.
Unique identifier for the profile merge event record. Primary key for the profile merge history audit trail.
The system user identifier or staff ID of the person who initiated or approved the merge operation. May be system account for automated merges.
The property identifier where the merge operation was initiated or where the profiles were primarily associated. May be null for enterprise-level merges.
The non-surviving guest profile identifier that was merged into the target profile. This profile is deactivated after the merge.
The surviving guest profile identifier that received all data from the source profile. This becomes the golden record after merge.
The total number of contact information records (email, phone, address) that were transferred from the source profile to the target profile.
The date and time when this merge history record was first created in the system. Typically matches merge_timestamp but may differ if recorded asynchronously.
The data retention classification assigned to this merge audit record, determining how long the record must be preserved per regulatory and business requirements.
Technical error message or exception details captured if the merge operation failed or encountered issues during execution.

business_glossary_term: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Consent Retained
description: Indicates whether marketing and data processing consent flags were preserved during the merge operation in compliance with GDPR requirements.
The date and time when this merge history record was last updated, such as when validation status or rollback status changed.
The total number of loyalty program points or rewards that were consolidated from the source profile into the target profile during the merge.
A numerical score (0.00 to 100.00) representing the algorithmic confidence level that the source and target profiles represent the same guest. Higher scores indicate stronger match certainty.
The specific data elements used to identify the profiles as duplicates, such as name+DOB, email+phone, loyalty ID, or combination of multiple attributes.
The batch identifier for bulk merge operations, grouping multiple profile merges that were executed together as part of a single data quality initiative.
Free-text comments or notes entered by the operator documenting the reason for the merge, special circumstances, or additional context.
The technical method or process used to execute the profile merge. Distinguishes between automated matching algorithms, manual operator review, and batch processing.
The business reason or trigger that initiated the profile merge operation. Indicates whether the merge was system-detected, guest-requested, or operationally driven.
The current status of the merge operation indicating whether it completed successfully, failed, is pending, or was subsequently rolled back.
The exact date and time when the profile merge operation was executed and committed to the system.
The total number of guest notes, traces, and alerts that were transferred from the source profile to the target profile during the merge operation.
The organizational department or functional area of the operator who performed the merge, such as Front Desk, CRM Team, IT Data Quality, or System Automation.
The full name of the staff member or system account that executed the merge operation for audit and accountability purposes.
The total number of guest preference records (room type, floor, amenities, etc.) that were transferred from the source profile to the target profile.
The total number of reservation records that were transferred from the source profile to the target profile during the merge operation.
Indicates whether this merge operation can be reversed or undone. True if rollback is technically possible, False if the merge is permanent or rollback window has expired.
The date after which the merge operation can no longer be reversed. Defines the time window during which rollback is permitted per data governance policy.
The total number of historical stay records that were transferred from the source profile to the target profile during the merge operation.
Free-text notes documenting the validation process, any issues identified, or confirmation of successful data integrity checks.
Indicates whether the merge operation has been validated for data integrity and business rule compliance after execution.
The date and time when the merge operation was validated for data integrity and completeness.
Unique identifier for the guest-to-guest relationship record. Primary key.
Identifier of the corporate account associated with this relationship. Used for corporate travel program management and booker-to-traveler attribution.
Identifier for the household or family unit to which both guests belong. Used for household-level Lifetime Value (LTV) aggregation and family recognition programs.
Identifier of the staff member who created this relationship record. Null if created through an automated process or API import.
Identifier of the primary guest in the relationship. The guest from whom the relationship originates.
Identifier of the staff member who last modified this relationship record. Null if modified through an automated process.
Indicates whether the related guest has authority to handle billing and payment matters for the primary guest.
Indicates whether the related guest has authority to make bookings on behalf of the primary guest. Used for assistant-mediated booking and corporate travel coordinator scenarios.
Date when consent was obtained from both guests for this relationship record. Required for data privacy compliance.

Indicates whether both guests have provided consent for this relationship to be recorded and used for service personalization. Required for General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) compliance.
Timestamp when this relationship record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Date when this relationship record is scheduled for deletion or archival per data retention policies. Required for GDPR right to erasure compliance.
Indicates whether the relationship is bidirectional (mutual, both guests recognize the relationship) or unidirectional (one-way, only the primary guest recognizes the relationship).
Indicates whether the related guest is designated as an emergency contact for the primary guest.
Date when the relationship ended or is scheduled to end. Null for ongoing relationships.

business_glossary_term: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Lawful Basis
description: The lawful basis under GDPR Article 6 for processing this relationship data. Typically consent or legitimate interest for guest relationship management.
Timestamp when this relationship record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
Free-text notes providing additional context about the relationship. Used for service personalization and special handling instructions.
Indicates whether the related guest is the primary contact for the primary guest. Used for assistant-mediated booking attribution and corporate travel coordinator visibility.

Numeric priority ranking of this relationship relative to other relationships for the same primary guest. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used when multiple relationships exist and one must be selected (e.g., primary emergency contact).
Current lifecycle status of the relationship. Active relationships are used for service personalization and household-level aggregation.
Type of relationship between the two guests. Defines the nature of the association (e.g., spouse/partner, parent/child, executive assistant, corporate booker-to-traveler, travel companion).
Indicates whether preferences should be inherited or shared between the two guests. Enables family recognition programs (e.g., greeting children by name based on parent preferences).
Unique identifier of this relationship record in the source system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.
Date when the relationship became effective. Used for historical tracking and lifecycle management.
Date when the relationship was verified. Null if not yet verified.
Indicates whether the relationship has been verified by staff or through a validation process. Unverified relationships may be self-reported or imported without confirmation.
Primary key for guest_enrollment
Foreign key linking to the guest profile who enrolled in the promotion
Foreign key linking to the loyalty promotion in which the member is enrolled
The date on which the bonus was posted to the member's loyalty account. Null if bonus_awarded_flag is false. Used to track fulfillment SLA compliance.
Indicates whether the promotion bonus (points, nights, or other reward) has been posted to the member's loyalty account. True once fulfillment is complete.
The channel through which the member enrolled in the promotion. Values: Email, Mobile App, Website, Property Front Desk, Call Center, Auto-Enrolled. Used for channel effectiveness analysis.
The date on which the member successfully met all qualifying criteria for this promotion. Null if not yet completed or if promotion expired before completion.
Unique surrogate identifier for this promotion enrollment record. Primary key.
The date on which the member enrolled or was auto-enrolled in this promotion. Used to determine eligibility window and track campaign velocity.

Current lifecycle state of this member's participation in the promotion. Values: enrolled (registered but no qualifying activity yet), in-progress (actively working toward criteria), completed (met all criteria, awaiting award), expired (promotion ended before completion), awarded (bonus posted to account), opted-out (member withdrew from promotion).
The date on which the member explicitly opted out or withdrew from this promotion. Null if member has not opted out. Used for preference management and campaign suppression.

Current progress toward the promotion's qualifying criteria. Interpretation depends on promotion type: for minimum_nights promotions, this is nights earned; for minimum_spend promotions, this is spend accumulated. Null if promotion does not track incremental progress.
Unique identifier for the guest market segment record. Primary key for both segment master definitions and individual guest-to-segment assignment records.

Foreign key linking to marketing.guest_segment. Business justification: Properties cross-reference booking market segments (transient, group, contract) with marketing behavioral segments (high-value leisure, business road warrior) for campaign eligibility rules, personali
Reference to the parent segment in the hierarchy for roll-up aggregation. Null for top-level segments.
Reference to the segment assignment record that supersedes this one. Used for tracking segment migration history and maintaining assignment lineage.
Reference to the individual guest profile for guest-to-segment assignment records. Null for segment master definition records.

business_glossary_term: ADR (Average Daily Rate) Index vs Property
description: Average Daily Rate index for this segment relative to property-wide ADR. 1.0 = at property average, >1.0 = premium segment, <1.0 = discount segment. Used for segment mix optimization.
Typical advance booking window in days for this segment. Used for demand forecasting and pickup analysis. Transient segments typically 0-30 days, Group segments 90-365 days.
Average ancillary revenue (F&B, spa, parking, resort fees, etc.) per stay for this segment. Used for total revenue forecasting and TRevPAR optimization.
Confidence score (0.000 to 1.000) for ML-inferred segment assignments. Higher scores indicate greater model certainty. Null for rule-based and manual assignments.
Date when the guest was assigned to this segment. Used for tracking segment migration and guest lifecycle analysis.
Date from which this segment assignment is effective for the guest. Used for time-bound segment membership (e.g., corporate contract validity period).
Date when this segment assignment expires for the guest. Null for indefinite assignments. Used for contract-based and time-limited segment memberships.

Method used to assign the guest to this segment. Rule-based uses business rules (e.g., booking channel, rate code), ML-inferred uses machine learning models for predictive segmentation, Manual-override represents analyst or sales manager assignment, Reservation-based inherits from reservation market segment code.
Free-text notes explaining the rationale for manual segment assignments or overrides. Used for audit trail and knowledge transfer.
Code indicating the business reason for this segment assignment (e.g., CORP_CONTRACT, BOOKING_CHANNEL, STAY_PATTERN, SALES_OVERRIDE, LTV_TIER). Used for assignment audit and quality control.
Historical average Length of Stay in days for guests in this segment. Used for demand forecasting and inventory optimization.
Code identifying the cancellation and modification policy applicable to this segment (e.g., 24HR, 72HR, NRR for Non-Refundable Rate, FLEX).
Indicates whether bookings in this segment are eligible for travel agent or OTA commission payments.
Standard commission percentage paid to intermediaries for bookings in this segment. Null if commission_eligible is False.
Timestamp when this segment record was first created in the lakehouse. Used for data lineage and audit trail.
Indicates whether group bookings in this segment should undergo displacement analysis to compare group revenue against potential transient revenue. Used for group evaluation and acceptance decisions.
Date when this segment definition was retired or superseded. Null for currently active segments.
Date when this segment definition became effective. Used for historical segment analysis and time-travel queries.

business_glossary_term: F&B (Food and Beverage) Attachment Rate Percentage
description: Percentage of guests in this segment who purchase F&B services during their stay. Used for TRevPAR analysis and ancillary revenue forecasting.

Numeric level in the segment hierarchy for roll-up reporting. Level 1 is top-level (e.g., Transient), Level 2 is sub-segment (e.g., Qualified Corporate), Level 3 is micro-segment (e.g., Tech Industry Corporate).
Timestamp when this segment record was last updated. Used for change tracking and incremental processing.
Indicates whether stays booked under this segment earn loyalty program points and elite night credits.
Multiplier applied to base points earning for this segment. Standard is 1.0, promotional segments may have higher multipliers (e.g., 1.5x, 2.0x).

Indicates whether this segment has Last Room Availability commitment, meaning inventory is available at the contracted rate even when the property is sold out. Typically True for top-tier corporate contracts and loyalty elite members.
Username or system identifier of the user or process that last modified this record. Used for audit trail and accountability.

Pricing strategy applicable to this segment. Dynamic uses RMS (Revenue Management System) optimization, Fixed uses BAR (Best Available Rate) or published rates, Negotiated uses contracted rates, Promotional uses discount codes, Opaque uses non-branded OTA channels.

business_glossary_term: RevPAR (Revenue Per Available Room) Contribution Percentage
description: Historical percentage contribution of this segment to total property RevPAR. Sum across all segments equals 100%. Used for strategic segment mix planning.
Broader business category for revenue and demand analysis. Used for RevPAR (Revenue Per Available Room) decomposition and TRevPAR (Total Revenue Per Available Room) attribution.
Short alphanumeric code identifying the market segment (e.g., TRANS, GRP, CORP, WHSL, COMP, CREW). Sourced from OPERA PMS Market Segment codes and IDeaS G3 RMS segment taxonomy.
Full descriptive name of the market segment (e.g., Transient, Group, Corporate Negotiated, Wholesale, Complimentary, Airline Crew).

Current lifecycle status of the segment definition. Active segments are available for assignment and reporting, Inactive segments are hidden from selection but retained for historical analysis, Deprecated segments are being phased out, Pending segments are awaiting approval.

High-level classification of the segment type. Transient represents FIT (Free Independent Traveler) guests, Group represents MICE (Meetings Incentives Conferences Exhibitions) and leisure groups, Contract represents negotiated corporate accounts, Wholesale represents OTA (Online Travel Agency) and tour operator bookings, Complimentary represents comp stays and employee rates, Crew represents airline and travel industry staff.
Unique identifier of this segment in the source system. Used for data reconciliation and lineage tracking.

business_glossary_term: USALI (Uniform System of Accounts for the Lodging Industry) Mapping Code
description: Standard USALI account code for financial reporting and GOP (Gross Operating Profit) attribution. Ensures consistent revenue classification across properties.
Indicates whether this segment is subject to dynamic yield optimization and inventory controls by the RMS. True for transient and qualified segments, False for contracted and complimentary segments.
Unique surrogate identifier for this guest-to-segment membership record. Primary key.
Foreign key linking to the marketing segment definition
Foreign key linking to the guest master profile record
Confidence score (0.0 to 1.0) indicating the strength of the segment assignment, particularly relevant for ML-model-based assignments. Captured from the detection phase as relationship data.
The date on which this guest was assigned to this segment. Captured from the detection phase as relationship data.

The date from which this segment membership became active and eligible for campaign targeting. Supports point-in-time segment membership queries. Captured from the detection phase as relationship data.

The date on which this segment membership expired or was removed. Null for currently active memberships. Supports historical segment membership tracking. Captured from the detection phase as relationship data.

Method by which this guest was assigned to this segment: rule-based (business rules engine), manual (marketing team override), or ml-model (machine learning prediction). Captured from the detection phase as relationship data.

Coded reason or business rule identifier explaining why this guest was assigned to this segment. Supports audit trail and segment definition refinement. Captured from the detection phase as relationship data.
Timestamp when this segment membership record was created in the system.
Date when this specific membership record was last evaluated and confirmed during segment refresh cycles. Enables tracking of stale memberships.
Current lifecycle status of this segment membership: active (in use for campaigns), inactive (temporarily disabled), expired (past expiry date), suspended (manually paused by marketing team).
Timestamp when this segment membership record was last updated.
User ID or system identifier that created this segment membership record. Supports audit trail for manual assignments.
Unique identifier for the benefit entitlement record. Primary key.
Reference to the loyalty program member who holds this benefit entitlement.
The system user ID or employee ID of the person who manually granted this benefit, if applicable. Null for system-generated entitlements.
The system user ID or employee ID of the person who last modified this benefit entitlement record.
Reference to the loyalty tier that grants this benefit as a standard entitlement. Null if the benefit is individually granted outside of tier benefits.
The minimum number of days in advance that a reservation must be made to redeem this benefit. Null if no advance booking requirement exists.
Indicates whether this benefit is automatically applied to eligible reservations or requires manual member selection. True if auto-applied, False if opt-in required.
Detailed description of the benefit, including terms, conditions, and any restrictions or exclusions.
Human-readable name of the benefit entitlement for display and communication purposes.
The category of benefit granted to the member. Examples include complimentary breakfast, room upgrade, late checkout, lounge access, welcome amenity, or bonus points multiplier.
Comma-separated list of date ranges during which this benefit cannot be redeemed, typically during peak demand periods or special events.
The brand code if this benefit is restricted to properties within a specific brand. Null if no brand restriction applies.
Indicates whether this benefit can be combined with other benefits or promotions during the same stay. True if combinable, False if mutually exclusive.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the monetary value of this benefit.
The date from which this benefit entitlement becomes valid and can be redeemed by the member.
Comma-separated list of property codes where this benefit can be redeemed. Populated only when property_restriction is SPECIFIC.

The origin or reason for granting this benefit. Tier standard benefits are automatic based on member tier; status match benefits are granted when matching status from another program; manual grants are discretionary; promotion benefits are campaign-driven; compensation benefits are service recovery; partner benefits come from co-branded or partner integrations.

Current lifecycle status of the benefit entitlement. Active entitlements can be redeemed; suspended entitlements are temporarily unavailable; expired entitlements have passed their validity period; revoked entitlements have been withdrawn; pending entitlements are awaiting activation.
The date on which this benefit entitlement expires and can no longer be redeemed. Null for benefits with no expiration.
The date and time when this benefit entitlement was created or granted to the member.
The date and time when this benefit entitlement record was last updated in the system.
The maximum length of stay (LOS) in nights for which this benefit can be applied. Null if no maximum stay restriction exists.
The minimum length of stay (LOS) in nights required to redeem this benefit. Null if no minimum stay requirement exists.
The estimated monetary value of this benefit in the property's base currency, used for program economics tracking and liability calculation.
Free-text field for additional comments, special instructions, or contextual information about this benefit entitlement.
The bonus points multiplier applied to eligible stays when this benefit is active. For example, 1.5 means the member earns 50% bonus points. Null if this benefit does not include a points multiplier.
The priority order for applying this benefit when multiple benefits are available. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used to resolve conflicts when benefits cannot be combined.
Indicates whether this benefit can be redeemed at all properties, only at specific properties, within a specific brand, or within a specific geographic region.
The geographic region code if this benefit is restricted to properties within a specific region. Null if no region restriction applies.
The number of redemptions remaining for this benefit. Calculated as usage_limit minus usage_count. Null if usage is unlimited.
The business reason or justification for revoking this benefit entitlement. Null if the benefit has not been revoked.
The date and time when this benefit entitlement was revoked. Null if the benefit has not been revoked.
The unique identifier of this benefit entitlement in the source operational system.
Indicates whether this benefit can be transferred to another loyalty member. True if transferable, False if non-transferable.
The number of times this benefit has been redeemed to date. Used to track remaining usage against the usage limit.
The maximum number of times this benefit can be redeemed during its validity period. Null indicates unlimited usage.
Unique identifier for the benefit redemption record. Primary key for tracking the complete lifecycle of a loyalty member benefit from entitlement through consumption or expiry.

Foreign key linking to loyalty.benefit_entitlement. Business justification: benefit_redemption tracks the consumption/usage of a benefit entitlement. Currently it duplicates benefit_type_code, benefit_name, benefit_description from the entitlement. Adding benefit_entitlement_
Reference to the staff member who fulfilled or delivered the benefit to the guest.
Reference to the loyalty program member who holds this benefit entitlement.
Reference to the hotel property where the benefit was delivered or redeemed.
Reference to the reservation during which the benefit was redeemed. Null if benefit was redeemed outside of a reservation context.

Foreign key linking to spa.appointment. Business justification: Loyalty elite benefits include complimentary spa treatments redeemed at specific appointments. Critical for benefit entitlement validation, spa treatment delivery tracking, and elite member spa benefi
Method by which the member acknowledged benefit receipt: digital signature, email confirmation, mobile app, verbal confirmation, or automatic system acknowledgment.
Date and time when the member acknowledged receipt of the benefit. Null if not acknowledged.
Actual time in minutes taken to deliver the benefit from the moment of request or check-in. Used for SLA compliance measurement.
Current lifecycle status of the benefit: active (available for use), redeemed (fully consumed), expired (past validity date), cancelled (revoked), or pending (awaiting activation).
Date when the benefit was cancelled or revoked. Null if benefit was not cancelled.
Explanation for why the benefit was cancelled (e.g., member downgrade, policy violation, program termination, member request).
Reservation confirmation number associated with the benefit redemption. Used for cross-reference with PMS (Property Management System) records.
Actual cost incurred by the property to deliver this benefit. Used for GOP (Gross Operating Profit) and program ROI (Return on Investment) analysis.
Date and time when this benefit redemption record was first created in the system.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the monetary value amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
Specific location within the property where the benefit was delivered (e.g., 'Front Desk', 'Executive Lounge', 'Room 1205', 'Restaurant - The Grill').
First date on which the benefit can be redeemed or used by the member.
Date when the benefit was granted or became available to the member.

Origin of the benefit entitlement: tier standard (automatic with membership level), status match (from competitor program), manual grant (by staff), promotion (campaign-driven), or compensation (service recovery).
Indicates whether an exception occurred during benefit fulfillment (True) or the benefit was delivered as standard (False).
Free-text notes explaining the exception circumstances, substitution details, or any special handling required during benefit delivery.
Standardized code indicating the type of exception: benefit unavailable, substitution provided, upgrade given, downgrade applied, partial fulfillment, or member declined benefit.
Last date on which the benefit can be redeemed. After this date, the benefit becomes void and cannot be used.
Identifier from the source system for cross-system reconciliation and audit trail purposes.
Date and time when this benefit redemption record was last updated or modified.
Indicates whether the member acknowledged receipt of the benefit (True) or not (False). Used for service quality tracking and dispute resolution.
Estimated monetary value of the benefit in the property's local currency. Used for program economics tracking and LTV (Lifetime Value) calculations.
Loyalty points value equivalent of this benefit. Used when members have the option to choose between benefits or points.
Date when the benefit was redeemed or consumed by the member. Null if not yet redeemed.
Precise date and time when the benefit was redeemed, including time zone. Used for operational tracking and SLA (Service Level Agreement) compliance.
Number of redemptions still available for this benefit. Decrements with each usage. Null indicates unlimited remaining usage.
Indicates whether the benefit was delivered within the program's SLA (Service Level Agreement) standards (True) or not (False). Used for elite service quality tracking.
Target time in minutes within which the benefit should be delivered according to program SLA standards.
Type of benefit provided as a substitute when the original benefit was unavailable. Null if no substitution occurred.
Maximum number of times this benefit can be redeemed during its validity period. Null indicates unlimited usage within the validity window.
Unique identifier for the fraud alert record. Primary key.
Reference identifier of the points ledger transaction that reversed fraudulent points. Links to points_ledger for audit trail. Null if no reversal occurred.

Foreign key linking to loyalty.points_ledger. Business justification: fraud_alert currently has triggering_transaction_id (STRING) and triggering_transaction_type (STRING). When the fraud is detected on a points transaction, adding triggering_points_ledger_id FK creates
Reference to the loyalty member account affected by the suspected or confirmed fraudulent activity.
Self-referencing FK on fraud_alert (related_fraud_alert_id)
Reference to the partner program if the fraud alert involves partner points transfer or partner-originated activity. Null if fraud is internal to hotel loyalty program.

Foreign key linking to compliance.privacy_incident. Business justification: Loyalty fraud investigations that uncover data breaches (unauthorized account access, PII exposure, credential stuffing) must trigger privacy incident records for GDPR/CCPA notification obligations. R

Foreign key linking to loyalty.points_transfer. Business justification: When fraud is detected on a points transfer transaction, this FK links to the specific transfer record. Nullable and mutually exclusive with other transaction FKs. Partner transfers are a common fraud

Foreign key linking to loyalty.redemption. Business justification: When fraud is detected on a redemption transaction, this FK links to the specific redemption record. Nullable and mutually exclusive with triggering_points_ledger_id. Enables direct navigation to the
Indicates whether the member account was frozen as a result of this fraud alert. True if account is currently frozen; false otherwise.
Date and time when the member account was frozen due to fraud alert. Null if account was not frozen.
Externally-visible unique business identifier for the fraud alert, formatted as FA- followed by 10 digits.

Severity level of the fraud alert based on potential financial impact, member risk, and program integrity threat. Critical indicates immediate action required with significant financial or reputational risk; high indicates substantial risk requiring urgent investigation; medium indicates moderate risk requiring timely review; low indicates minor risk for routine investigation.

Current status of the fraud alert in the investigation workflow. Open indicates alert created and awaiting assignment; investigating indicates active investigation in progress; confirmed_fraud indicates fraud validated and resolution actions taken; false_positive indicates alert determined to be legitimate activity; closed indicates investigation completed and case archived.

Classification of the suspected or confirmed fraudulent activity type. Account takeover indicates unauthorized access to member account; unauthorized redemption indicates points redeemed without member consent; points theft indicates fraudulent points accrual or transfer; manufactured spending indicates artificial transaction generation to earn points; velocity abuse indicates abnormal transaction frequency or volume; credential stuffing indicates automated login attempts using stolen credentials.
Date and time when the fraud alert record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the monetary value involved in the fraud alert.

Method by which the fraudulent activity was detected. Rule-based indicates detection by predefined business rules; ML model indicates detection by machine learning algorithm; manual report indicates detection by fraud analyst review; partner notification indicates alert received from partner program; member report indicates member-initiated fraud report.
Date and time when the fraudulent activity was first detected by the system or reported. Represents the business event time of fraud discovery.
Explanation of why the fraud alert was determined to be a false positive. Documents legitimate business activity that triggered the alert. Null if alert was not classified as false positive.
Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code derived from source IP address geolocation. Used to identify suspicious cross-border activity.
User identifier of the fraud analyst or investigator assigned to review and resolve the alert. Null if alert is unassigned.
Free-text notes documenting the investigation process, findings, evidence reviewed, and analyst observations. Confidential operational information.
Date and time when investigation of the fraud alert was initiated by an analyst. Null if investigation has not yet started.
Date and time when the fraud alert record was last updated. Audit trail for data lineage and change tracking.
Case or reference number provided by law enforcement agency if fraud was reported. Null if not reported to authorities.
Indicates whether law enforcement authorities were notified of the confirmed fraud. True if notification occurred; false otherwise.

Communication channel used to notify the member of the fraud alert. Email indicates electronic mail; SMS indicates text message; phone indicates voice call; mail indicates postal mail; in_app indicates mobile application notification.
Date and time when the loyalty member was notified of the fraud alert. Null if member was not notified.
Indicates whether the loyalty member was notified of the fraud alert and investigation. True if member was contacted; false otherwise.
Version identifier of the fraud detection model or rule set that generated the alert. Enables tracking of model performance and alert quality over time.
Estimated monetary value of the fraudulent activity in the program's base currency. Calculated based on points value, cash redemptions, or other financial impact.
Total number of loyalty points involved in the suspected or confirmed fraudulent activity. Null if fraud does not involve points movement.
Number of loyalty points reversed or clawed back from the member account as part of fraud resolution. Null if no points reversal occurred.
Date and time when regulatory report was submitted to authorities. Null if no regulatory reporting was required or report not yet submitted.
Indicates whether the fraud alert meets thresholds requiring regulatory reporting to authorities or industry bodies. True if reporting is required; false otherwise.

Action taken to resolve the fraud alert. Account freeze indicates temporary suspension of account activity; points reversal indicates fraudulent points removed from account; password reset indicates forced credential change; account closure indicates permanent account termination; no action indicates alert closed without intervention; member notification indicates member contacted regarding suspicious activity.
Free-text notes documenting the resolution decision, actions taken, and final outcome. Confidential operational information.
Date and time when the fraud alert was resolved and final action taken. Null if alert remains open or under investigation.
Numerical risk score assigned by fraud detection system, typically ranging from 0.00 to 100.00, with higher scores indicating greater fraud likelihood. Generated by rule-based engine or ML model.

Business channel through which the triggering transaction or activity occurred. Web indicates website; mobile_app indicates mobile application; call_center indicates phone reservation or service; property indicates on-property transaction; partner indicates partner program; API indicates system-to-system integration.

Type of device used for the triggering transaction or activity. Desktop indicates web browser on computer; mobile indicates smartphone; tablet indicates tablet device; API indicates programmatic access; unknown indicates device type could not be determined.
Internet Protocol address associated with the triggering transaction or suspicious activity. Used for fraud pattern analysis and geolocation. May be considered PII in some jurisdictions.
Type of loyalty program transaction or activity that triggered the fraud alert. Identifies the specific action that raised suspicion.

Reference to the loyalty tier required to participate in this promotion. Null if promotion is open to all tiers. Used to restrict offers to specific membership levels (e.g., Gold, Platinum, Diamond).
Date when this promotion was formally approved for launch. Null if promotion is still in draft or pending approval. Used for campaign governance and audit trail.
Username or employee ID of the manager or director who approved this promotion for launch. Null if promotion is still in draft status. Used for governance and approval workflow tracking.

Target date by which bonus points or nights will be posted to qualifying members' accounts after promotion completion. Used for member communication and fulfillment SLA (Service Level Agreement) tracking.

Maximum financial budget allocated for this promotion (total cost of all bonuses awarded). Promotion may be paused or closed early if budget is exhausted. Measured in base currency (typically USD).

Cumulative financial cost of bonuses awarded to date under this promotion. Updated as members complete qualifying activity and bonuses are posted. Used for budget tracking and ROI (Return on Investment) analysis.

Explanation for why the promotion was cancelled or terminated early. Null if promotion completed normally or is still active. Examples: 'Budget exhausted', 'Low engagement', 'Business priority change', 'Legal compliance issue'.
Timestamp when this promotion record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Date when the promotion expires. Qualifying activity after this date does not count toward the promotion. Null for open-ended promotions.
Comma-separated list of member IDs or segments explicitly excluded from this promotion (e.g., employees, test accounts, members who opted out). Null if no exclusions apply.

Primary marketing channel used to promote this offer. Examples: 'Email', 'Mobile App Push', 'Website Banner', 'Direct Mail', 'Social Media', 'In-Property Signage'. Used for channel attribution and ROI analysis.
Minimum number of qualifying nights required to earn the promotion bonus. Null if nights are not a qualifying criterion.
Minimum total spend (in base currency) required to qualify for the promotion. Null if spend is not a qualifying criterion. Typically measured in USD or property local currency.
Externally-known unique alphanumeric code for the promotion, used in marketing materials and member enrollment. Example: BONUS2024, WELCOME500.
Human-readable name of the promotion displayed to members and in marketing channels. Example: 'Double Points Summer Getaway', 'Elite Status Fast Track'.

Current lifecycle state of the promotion. Values: draft (being designed), scheduled (approved, not yet live), active (currently running), paused (temporarily suspended), completed (ended successfully), cancelled (terminated early).

Classification of the promotion mechanism. Values: bonus_points_multiplier (earn extra points per dollar), double_nights (accelerate tier qualification), targeted_offer (personalized to segment), welcome_back (re-engagement), birthday_bonus (member birthday reward), spend_challenge (gamified spending goal).

Detailed narrative of the requirements a member must meet to earn the promotion bonus. Examples: 'Complete 3 stays between June 1 and August 31', 'Spend minimum $500 on F&B (Food and Beverage) during stay', 'Book via mobile app using rate code MOBILE2024'.
Comma-separated list of property IDs or property codes where stays qualify for this promotion. Null or 'ALL' if promotion applies to all properties. Example: 'PROP001,PROP045,PROP102' or 'ALL'.

Comma-separated list of rate codes (BAR - Best Available Rate, corporate codes, promotional codes) that qualify for this promotion. Null or 'ALL' if all rate codes qualify. Example: 'BAR,CORP,PROMO2024'.

Indicates whether members must explicitly register or opt-in to participate in this promotion. True = registration required before qualifying activity counts; False = automatic enrollment for eligible members.

Indicates whether this promotion can be combined with other active promotions. True = member can earn bonuses from multiple promotions simultaneously; False = this promotion cannot be stacked with others.
Date when the promotion becomes active and members can begin earning toward the bonus. Qualifying activity before this date does not count.
Web link to the full legal terms and conditions document for this promotion. Displayed to members during enrollment and in promotional materials.
Count of members who have successfully completed all qualifying criteria and earned the promotion bonus. Used for conversion rate and campaign effectiveness analysis.
Count of unique members who have enrolled or been auto-enrolled in this promotion. Used for campaign reach and engagement metrics.
Timestamp when this promotion record was last modified. Updated whenever any attribute changes. Used for change tracking and audit trail.
