Primary key for competitive_set

Foreign key linking to procurement.employee. Business justification: Revenue managers define competitive sets; employee tracking required for strategic decision audit trail, comp set performance evaluation, and accountability for benchmarking configuration in revenue s

Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: Competitive benchmarking (RGI, MPI, ARI targets) informs budget planning and strategic financial targets for annual budget approval and strategic planning.

Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Competitive intelligence gathering must comply with antitrust and data privacy policies. Comp set approval validates legal compliance before rate shopping activation. Essential for competitive intelli
Identifier of the subject property (the 'home' property) that owns this competitive set definition. Links to the property master record in the Property domain.

The booking lead time window in days used for rate shopping comparisons within this competitive set (e.g., 0 = same-day, 7 = 7-day advance, 30 = 30-day advance). Ensures rate intelligence is captured at consistent booking horizons for fair ADR comparison.

The date on which the competitive set definition was formally approved by the revenue management authority. Supports audit trail requirements for comp set governance and STR STAR Report submission integrity.

Name or identifier of the revenue management leader or Director of Revenue who approved this competitive set definition. Required for governance and audit trail of comp set changes that affect STR STAR Report submissions.

The Average Rate Index (ARI) target threshold for this competitive set. ARI = Property ADR / Comp Set ADR. A value above 1.00 indicates the property targets a higher ADR than the competitive set average. Drives BAR pricing strategy in IDeaS G3 RMS.

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code used for normalizing ADR, RevPAR, and TRevPAR benchmarking metrics across all properties in the competitive set (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'GBP'). Ensures consistent monetary comparison in STR STAR Report analysis.

Human-readable name assigned to the competitive set (e.g., 'Downtown Luxury Primary Set', 'Airport Corridor Secondary Set'). Used in Infor EzRMS and STR STAR Report submissions to identify the grouping.

Current lifecycle status of the competitive set. 'active' sets are used in live RMS benchmarking and STR reporting; 'inactive' sets are suspended; 'pending_approval' awaits revenue management sign-off; 'archived' are historical sets retained for trend analysis.

The timestamp when this competitive set record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and change history tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

The primary guest demand segment this competitive set is designed to benchmark. 'transient' focuses on FIT (Free Independent Traveler) and leisure; 'group' focuses on MICE (Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, Exhibitions) and group blocks; 'contract' focuses on negotiated corporate rates; 'all_segments' covers the full mix.
The date from which this competitive set definition becomes active and is used for STR STAR Report benchmarking, RGI, MPI, and ARI calculations. Aligns with STR reporting period start.

The date on which this competitive set definition expires and is no longer used for active benchmarking. Null indicates an open-ended, currently active set. Supports historical trend analysis when comp set membership changes.

The geographic market or submarket in which the competitive set operates (e.g., 'Manhattan Midtown', 'Chicago O'Hare Corridor', 'Miami Beach'). Aligns with STR market definitions used for Market Penetration Index (MPI) calculations.

business_glossary_term: IDeaS G3 Revenue Management System (RMS) Competitive Set Code
description: The competitive set reference code as configured in IDeaS G3 RMS for pricing strategy alignment. Enables synchronization of comp set definitions between Infor EzRMS benchmarking and IDeaS G3 RMS dynamic pricing rules (DRR) and BAR optimization.

Indicates whether this competitive set has been formally submitted to STR (Smith Travel Research) for STAR Report benchmarking. True means the set is registered with STR and receives STAR Report data; False means it is an internal-only comp set.

The most recent date on which the competitive set membership and parameters were reviewed by the revenue management team. Best practice requires annual or semi-annual review to ensure comp set relevance as market conditions evolve.

The standard Length of Stay (LOS) in nights used as the basis for rate comparison within this competitive set. Typically set to 1 night for transient benchmarking. Used to normalize ADR comparisons across properties with different LOS mix.

ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the primary geographic market for this competitive set (e.g., 'USA', 'GBR', 'DEU'). Used for multi-national portfolio benchmarking and currency normalization in revenue performance reporting.

Hotel chain scale segment classification of the competitive set as defined by STR. Ensures that the comp set is composed of properties within the same or adjacent competitive tier. Used to validate comp set composition integrity for RGI and MPI calculations.

Maximum number of guestrooms a competitor property may have to qualify for inclusion in this competitive set. Used alongside min_room_count to define the acceptable size range for comp set membership.

Total number of competitor properties currently included in this competitive set. STR STAR Report typically requires a minimum of 3 and maximum of 10 competitor properties for statistically valid benchmarking. Derived from the comp set membership roster but stored here for quick reference.

Minimum number of guestrooms a competitor property must have to qualify for inclusion in this competitive set. Ensures size comparability for RevPAR and occupancy benchmarking. Typically aligned with the subject property's room count range.

The Market Penetration Index (MPI) target threshold for this competitive set. MPI = Property Occupancy Rate / Comp Set Occupancy Rate. A value above 1.00 indicates the property targets higher occupancy than the competitive set average. Used in revenue strategy planning.

Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this competitive set definition. Ensures comp set membership remains current and aligned with market dynamics. Triggers a review workflow in the Revenue Management System.

Free-text field for revenue management team annotations regarding this competitive set, such as rationale for competitor inclusion/exclusion, market context, or pending changes. Not used in automated benchmarking calculations.

The primary Key Performance Indicator (KPI) used to evaluate competitive performance for this comp set. Options include RevPAR (Revenue Per Available Room), ADR (Average Daily Rate), Occupancy Rate (OCC), TRevPAR (Total Revenue Per Available Room), or GOPPAR (Gross Operating Profit Per Available Room).

Filter defining the property service type composition of this competitive set. Ensures benchmarking is conducted against properties of comparable service level and amenity offering. Aligns with AHLA property classification standards.

Frequency at which competitor rates are retrieved from OTA and GDS channels for properties in this competitive set. Drives the cadence of dynamic pricing adjustments in IDeaS G3 RMS. Only applicable when rate_shopping_enabled is True.

Indicates whether automated rate shopping (competitive rate intelligence) is active for this competitive set. When True, the RMS retrieves competitor BAR rates from OTA channels and GDS for dynamic pricing decisions. When False, only STR STAR Report data is used.

The Revenue Generation Index (RGI) target threshold set by the revenue management team for this competitive set. RGI = Property RevPAR / Comp Set RevPAR. A target above 1.00 indicates the property aims to outperform the comp set. Used in IDeaS G3 RMS and Infor EzRMS for performance goal-setting.

business_glossary_term: Revenue Management System (RMS) Competitive Set ID
description: The native competitive set identifier as stored in the Revenue Management System (Infor EzRMS or IDeaS G3 RMS). Used for cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracing between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the operational RMS.

Classification of the competitive set indicating its strategic purpose. 'primary' is the main STR STAR Report comp set; 'secondary' is an alternate benchmarking group; 'custom' is an ad-hoc internal set; 'str_defined' is a set defined by STR directly; 'internal' is used for internal rate intelligence only.

business_glossary_term: STR (Smith Travel Research) Competitive Set Code
description: The unique alphanumeric code assigned by STR (Smith Travel Research) to identify this competitive set in the STAR Report system. Required for submitting and retrieving benchmarking data from STR. Maps directly to the STR comp set identifier in Infor EzRMS.

The STR-assigned market code identifying the geographic market in which this competitive set is benchmarked. Used to retrieve market-level aggregates for MPI calculation and to align with STR STAR Report market definitions.
Frequency at which STR STAR Report data is received and processed for this competitive set. Determines the cadence of RGI, MPI, and ARI metric refreshes in the Revenue Management System.

The STR-assigned submarket code for more granular geographic benchmarking within a broader STR market. Used when the competitive set operates in a distinct submarket (e.g., airport vs. downtown within the same city market).
The date on which this competitive set was submitted to STR for STAR Report registration. Relevant only when is_str_submitted is True. Used for audit and compliance tracking of STR data agreements.

The timestamp when this competitive set record was most recently modified. Used to detect changes in comp set definitions, track membership updates, and support incremental ETL processing in the Databricks Silver Layer. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Unique surrogate identifier for each competitor rate intelligence record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this data product.

Foreign key linking to revenue.competitive_set. Business justification: Competitor rate shopping is organized by competitive sets. competitor_rate currently has competitive_set_code (STRING) which should be normalized to FK. N competitor_rate records belong to 1 competiti
Reference to the competitor property record against which this rate was shopped. Links to the competitor property master.

Foreign key linking to procurement.employee. Business justification: Revenue analysts conduct competitor rate shopping; employee attribution required for competitive intelligence workflow tracking, data quality accountability, and performance evaluation of rate shoppin

Average Rate Index (ARI) calculated as Travel Hospitality's ADR divided by the competitive set's ADR for the same period. ARI > 1.0 indicates rate premium over the competitive set. Sourced from STR STAR Report or computed from shopped rates.

Availability status of the competitor room type and rate plan at the time of the shop. Indicates whether the rate was bookable (available), the room type was sold out, or the rate was closed to arrival. Used in demand and compression analysis.
Cancellation policy associated with the shopped competitor rate. Distinguishes refundable (flexible) rates from NRR (Non-Refundable Rates), which is a key dimension in rate plan competitive analysis.

Specific name of the booking channel or OTA platform where the rate was shopped (e.g., Expedia, Booking.com, Hotels.com, Marriott.com, Amadeus GDS). Provides granular channel-level competitive intelligence beyond the channel category.

The distribution channel through which the competitor rate was observed. OTA = Online Travel Agency (e.g., Expedia, Booking.com), GDS = Global Distribution System, direct = competitor brand website. Enables channel-level rate parity and competitive positioning analysis.
ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the country where the competitor property is located (e.g., USA, GBR, FRA). Supports geographic segmentation and multi-market competitive analysis.
Timestamp when this competitor rate record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and SLA monitoring for competitive intelligence data freshness.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the shopped rate is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency normalization in global competitive benchmarking.

Origin system or method by which the competitor rate data was obtained. Infor EzRMS automated shop, STR STAR Report feed, OTA scrape, GDS feed, manual entry, or third-party rate intelligence API. Affects data quality and freshness assessment.

Specific URL, feed identifier, or external reference from the data source where the competitor rate was retrieved (e.g., OTA listing URL, STR report batch ID, GDS transaction ID). Supports data lineage and auditability.

Day of the week corresponding to the stay date (MON through SUN). Pre-computed to support day-of-week rate pattern analysis and weekday vs. weekend competitive pricing strategies without requiring date function computation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MON|TUE|WED|THU|FRI|SAT|SUN — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Indicates whether mandatory fees (resort fees, destination fees, amenity fees) are included in the shopped rate (True) or excluded (False). Affects true cost comparison for rate parity and competitive positioning analysis.
Indicates whether the stay date falls on a weekend (Friday or Saturday night, True) or a weekday (False). Supports weekend vs. weekday rate positioning analysis and demand segmentation.
Number of days between the shop date and the stay date (stay_date minus shop_date). Key metric in revenue management for understanding booking window pricing behavior and demand curve positioning.
The number of nights for which the shopped rate applies, representing the Length of Stay (LOS) restriction or assumption used during the rate shop. Affects rate comparability across competitors.

STR-defined or internal market code representing the geographic market in which the competitor property operates (e.g., NYC, MIAMI, LONDON). Aligns with STR STAR Report market definitions for RGI and MPI benchmarking.
Meal plan included in the shopped rate. RO = Room Only, BB = Bed and Breakfast, HB = Half Board, FB = Full Board, AI = All Inclusive. Affects rate comparability and value positioning analysis.
Number of guests (occupancy) assumed when the rate was shopped (typically 1 or 2 adults). Affects rate comparability as some competitors price differently by occupancy.

Travel Hospitality's own Best Available Rate (BAR) for the equivalent room type, stay date, and channel at the time of the shop. Enables direct rate gap and rate index calculations without requiring a join to the rate plan table.

The competitor rate captured in the immediately preceding shop for the same property, stay date, room type, and channel. Enables rate change delta calculation and trend analysis without requiring a self-join.

Market segment classification of the competitor property (luxury, premium, select-service, extended stay, resort). Enables segment-level competitive benchmarking aligned with Travel Hospitality's own portfolio segmentation.

Indicates the direction of rate movement compared to the previous shop for the same competitor, stay date, room type, and channel. Supports rate velocity tracking and competitive pricing alerts in Infor EzRMS.

Absolute difference between the current shopped rate and the previous shopped rate (shopped_rate minus previous_shopped_rate). Positive value indicates a rate increase; negative indicates a decrease. Supports competitive pricing alert thresholds in RMS.

Difference between Travel Hospitality's own rate and the competitor's shopped rate (our_rate minus shopped_rate). Positive value indicates our rate is higher than the competitor; negative indicates we are priced below. Core metric for rate positioning decisions.

Competitor shopped rate converted to US Dollars (USD) using the exchange rate applicable at the time of the shop. Enables normalized cross-currency competitive benchmarking and global RevPAR index calculations.

Descriptive name of the competitor rate plan as displayed on the booking channel (e.g., 'Advance Purchase 21 Days', 'Flexible Rate', 'Member Exclusive'). Provides context for rate plan classification.
Classification of the competitor rate plan type shopped. BAR = Best Available Rate, NRR = Non-Refundable Rate. Enables comparison of like-for-like rate plan positioning across the competitive set.

Lifecycle status of this competitor rate record in the Silver Layer. Active = current valid record; superseded = replaced by a newer shop for the same key dimensions; invalid = data quality issue flagged; pending_review = awaiting analyst validation.

Standardized room type category used for cross-competitor comparison (e.g., Standard, Deluxe, Suite, Junior Suite). Normalized from competitor-specific room type codes to enable apples-to-apples benchmarking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|deluxe|superior|suite|junior_suite|executive|villa — promote to reference product]
The calendar date on which the competitor rate was shopped or retrieved. Used to calculate lead time and to track rate movement over time.
Reference to the rate shopping run or batch session that produced this record, enabling traceability back to the originating shop event.
Precise date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) at which the competitor rate was captured during the shopping session. Enables intra-day rate movement tracking.

The publicly available or shopped room rate for the competitor property for the specified stay date, room type, and rate plan. Represents the gross rate as displayed on the booking channel before taxes and fees. Core input for ADR and RevPAR competitive benchmarking.

Indicates whether the stay date coincides with a known special event, conference, or demand driver (True) that may explain elevated competitor rates. Supports demand-adjusted competitive rate analysis.
Official star rating of the competitor property (1.0 to 5.0) as recognized by the relevant hospitality rating authority. Used to filter and segment competitive benchmarking analysis.
The specific calendar date for which the competitor rate applies — i.e., the check-in date or the date of the night being priced. Core dimension for rate positioning analysis.

Indicates whether the shopped rate includes taxes and fees (True) or is a pre-tax rate (False). Critical for ensuring rate comparability across channels and geographies where tax display conventions differ.
Timestamp of the most recent update to this competitor rate record in the Silver Layer. Used for incremental processing, change data capture, and audit trail compliance.
Unique surrogate identifier for each demand forecast record generated by IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS. Primary key for the demand_forecast data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.

Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Demand forecasts incorporate expected lift from planned marketing campaigns. Forecasters link campaigns to forecast assumptions for post-campaign variance analysis and to refine future campaign lift m

Foreign key linking to procurement.employee. Business justification: Revenue analysts generate demand forecasts; employee attribution required for forecast accuracy tracking, performance evaluation, and accountability for override decisions in revenue management foreca

Foreign key linking to event.event_booking. Business justification: Demand forecasts incorporate known definite event bookings as constrained demand drivers. Critical for pickup forecasting, unconstrained vs constrained demand modeling, and event impact analysis in re

Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: Demand forecasts are reconciled against financial budgets for variance analysis, reforecasting cycles, and board reporting on revenue performance vs plan.

Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: Room demand forecasts drive outlet-level staffing and inventory planning (high occupancy forecast = increase outlet labor/stock). Required for integrated labor scheduling, procurement planning, outlet

Foreign key linking to experience.gss_score. Business justification: Demand forecasting models incorporate property GSS scores as a demand driver (higher satisfaction correlates with higher unconstrained demand). Revenue management systems use satisfaction metrics as r
Reference to the market segment (e.g., Transient, Group, Corporate, Leisure, OTA) for which the demand forecast is generated. Null when the forecast is at the property or room-type level only.
Reference to the hotel or resort property for which this demand forecast was generated. Aligns with the Oracle OPERA PMS property master.
Reference to the specific room type (e.g., King Deluxe, Double Standard, Suite) for which the demand forecast is generated. Null when the forecast is at the property or market-segment level only.

Forecasted Average Rate Index (ARI), representing the property's projected ADR relative to the competitive set's projected ADR. An ARI above 1.0 indicates the property is expected to achieve a rate premium over its competitive set.

Ratio of current bookings on-hand for the forecast date compared to the same point in time in the prior year or baseline period. A value above 1.0 indicates ahead-of-pace demand; below 1.0 indicates behind-pace. Used by revenue managers to assess demand trajectory.

Lower bound of the statistical confidence interval for the unconstrained demand forecast. Represents the pessimistic demand scenario used for risk-adjusted pricing and inventory control decisions in IDeaS G3 RMS.
Upper bound of the statistical confidence interval for the unconstrained demand forecast. Represents the optimistic demand scenario used for overbooking strategy and group evaluation in IDeaS G3 RMS.

Statistical confidence level (e.g., 90.00, 95.00) associated with the forecast confidence interval. Indicates the probability that actual demand will fall within the lower and upper confidence bounds.

The forecasted room demand (in room-nights) after applying available inventory capacity constraints. Represents the demand the property can actually accommodate. Used for occupancy projection and revenue optimization.
Timestamp when this demand forecast record was first captured and written to the Silver Layer data product. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary forecast values in this record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures multi-currency properties report revenue in the correct denomination.

Day of the week corresponding to the forecast date. Used for day-of-week demand pattern analysis, weekend vs. weekday segmentation, and pricing strategy differentiation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Monday|Tuesday|Wednesday|Thursday|Friday|Saturday|Sunday — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Forecasted percentage share of total demand attributed to the specific market segment in this record. For example, if the Transient segment is expected to represent 65% of total demand on the forecast date, this field holds 65.00. Populated only for market-segment-level forecasts.

business_glossary_term: Forecast Accuracy Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE)
description: Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) of the forecast model for this property-segment-horizon combination, based on historical back-testing. Lower values indicate higher model accuracy. Used to weight forecast confidence and flag low-accuracy forecasts for revenue manager review.
The stay date (arrival date) for which the demand forecast values apply. Represents the future date being predicted, formatted as yyyy-MM-dd.

The dimensional level at which this forecast record is generated. 'property' is the highest level; 'room_type_segment' is the most granular, combining room type and market segment dimensions as supported by IDeaS G3 RMS.

Number of days from the forecast run date to the forecast date, representing how far into the future this forecast projects. For example, a forecast run on 2024-01-01 for a stay date of 2024-04-01 has a horizon of 90 days. Used to assess forecast accuracy by horizon bucket.

Classification of the algorithmic approach used to generate the forecast. 'time_series' uses historical booking patterns; 'regression' uses demand drivers; 'machine_learning' uses ML-based models; 'hybrid' combines multiple approaches; 'manual_override' indicates a revenue manager manually adjusted the system forecast.

Version identifier of the demand forecasting model or algorithm used to generate this forecast record (e.g., 'IDeaS-G3-v8.2', 'EzRMS-v5.1'). Enables model performance tracking, A/B comparison, and audit of forecast methodology changes.

Externally-known unique identifier assigned by the Revenue Management System (RMS) to the specific forecast generation run that produced this record. Enables traceability back to the originating RMS batch or on-demand run.

The date and time when this forecast record was generated by the Revenue Management System. Represents the principal business event timestamp for this transaction, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

Current lifecycle state of this forecast record. 'active' indicates the current operative forecast; 'superseded' indicates a newer run has replaced it; 'draft' indicates a preliminary run not yet published to pricing decisions; 'published' indicates the forecast has been released to the Revenue Management System for pricing action; 'archived' indicates historical retention only.

Classification of the forecast methodology or purpose. 'unconstrained' reflects true market demand without inventory limits; 'constrained' reflects demand limited by available inventory; 'group_evaluation' is used for group block assessment in IDeaS G3 RMS; 'pickup' reflects incremental booking pace; 'budget' reflects the annual budget forecast baseline.
Flag indicating whether the forecast date falls on a public or national holiday in the property's market. Used to apply holiday demand adjustment factors in the forecasting model.

Flag indicating whether a revenue manager manually overrode the system-generated forecast values for this record. When true, the forecast reflects human judgment rather than pure algorithmic output. Used to track override frequency and assess model trust.

Flag indicating whether the forecast date coincides with a special event (e.g., conference, concert, sporting event, public holiday) that is expected to materially impact demand. When true, the forecast may incorporate event-specific demand uplift factors.

Forecasted Market Penetration Index (MPI), representing the property's projected occupancy share relative to the competitive set's projected occupancy. An MPI above 1.0 indicates the property is expected to capture more than its fair share of market demand.

Forecasted Average Daily Rate (ADR) in the property's local currency for the forecast date. Represents the expected average revenue per occupied room. Core revenue management KPI used to drive pricing strategy.
Forecasted number of room-nights expected to cancel from the current on-books position before the forecast date. Used to calculate net demand and inform overbooking strategy.
Forecasted number of room-nights expected to result in no-show arrivals on the forecast date. Used alongside cancellation forecasts to calibrate overbooking levels and protect revenue.

Forecasted occupancy rate expressed as a percentage of available rooms expected to be sold on the forecast date. Calculated as constrained demand divided by total available rooms. Key input to RevPAR and yield optimization decisions.

Forecasted incremental room-nights expected to be booked between the forecast run date and the forecast date. Represents the additional demand the RMS expects to materialize beyond rooms already on books.

Forecasted Revenue Per Available Room (RevPAR) in the property's local currency for the forecast date. Derived as projected ADR multiplied by projected occupancy rate. Primary yield optimization metric used by Revenue Management.

Forecasted total room revenue in the property's local currency for the forecast date. Calculated as projected rooms sold multiplied by projected ADR. Used for budget variance analysis and revenue strategy.
Forecasted number of rooms available for sale on the forecast date after accounting for out-of-order, out-of-service, and maintenance blocks. Denominator for occupancy and RevPAR calculations.
Forecasted number of rooms expected to be sold (occupied) on the forecast date. Used for housekeeping scheduling, staffing planning, and inventory control decisions.

Forecasted Revenue Generation Index (RGI), representing the property's projected RevPAR relative to the competitive set's projected RevPAR. An RGI above 1.0 indicates the property is expected to outperform its competitive set. Sourced from STR STAR Report benchmarking data integrated into Infor EzRMS.

Number of rooms already confirmed (on books) for the forecast date at the time the forecast was generated. Represents the definite demand component; the RMS adds pickup forecast to arrive at total projected demand.
Name or description of the special event influencing demand on the forecast date (e.g., 'Super Bowl LIX', 'New Year Eve', 'City Marathon'). Populated only when is_special_event is true.

The total forecasted room demand (in room-nights) without any inventory capacity constraints applied. Represents true market demand as estimated by the RMS model. Used to identify demand compression and inform inventory control decisions.
Timestamp when this demand forecast record was last modified in the Silver Layer, such as when a revised forecast supersedes a prior run for the same forecast date and segment.

Unique surrogate identifier for the market segment record. Primary key for the market_segment data product in the revenue domain. Role classification: MASTER_RESOURCE — this entity represents a classification resource used across revenue, reservation, and channel domains.

Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: Market segments map to typical loyalty tiers for segmentation alignment and forecasting. Business process: aligning revenue management segments with loyalty tier definitions for consistent guest class

Foreign key linking to loyalty.member_segment. Business justification: Revenue market segments correlate with loyalty member segments for unified guest profiling. Business process: cross-domain segmentation mapping enables consistent guest targeting across revenue optimi

Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: Market segments have outlet preferences/restrictions (corporate guests → business center cafe, leisure → poolside bar). Required for outlet positioning strategy, menu design, targeted marketing, guest

business_glossary_term: Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) Accommodation Typical
description: Indicates whether guests in this market segment typically require ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act) accessible accommodations. Relevant for government, medical, and certain group segments. Informs room inventory allocation and accessibility compliance reporting.

Average number of days between booking date and arrival date for this market segment. Key input for demand forecasting in IDeaS G3 RMS and Infor EzRMS. Segments with short lead times (e.g., OTA last-minute) require different yield strategies than long-lead corporate or group segments. Represents the MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE for this resource.

Average number of nights guests in this segment stay per booking (ALOS — Average Length of Stay). Used in revenue management for LOS (Length of Stay) restriction optimization, demand forecasting, and RevPAR (Revenue Per Available Room) modeling in IDeaS G3 RMS.

Qualitative classification of the historical cancellation rate tendency for this market segment. Informs overbooking strategy, no-show reserve calculations, and revenue protection policies in IDeaS G3 RMS. High-propensity segments (e.g., OTA flexible rate) require higher overbooking buffers.

Primary distribution channel through which this market segment predominantly books. Drives channel strategy, OTA (Online Travel Agency) commission management, and GDS (Global Distribution System) rate loading decisions in Sabre SynXis CRS. Used for channel mix analysis and cost-of-acquisition reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: direct|ota|gds|voice|group|wholesale|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Indicates whether bookings in this segment are subject to travel agent or OTA commission payments. Direct corporate and government segments are typically non-commissionable; OTA and GDS segments carry commission obligations. Drives accounts payable commission processing in SAP S/4HANA.

Standard commission rate percentage applicable to bookings in this segment when commission_eligible is true. Expressed as a percentage of room revenue (e.g., 10.00 for 10%). Used for cost-of-acquisition analysis, OTA contract management, and CPOR (Cost Per Occupied Room) calculations. Confidential as it reflects contracted commercial terms.

Classification of the segment's typical contribution margin profile relative to other segments. Reflects net revenue after channel commissions, OTA fees, GDS fees, and loyalty redemption costs. Premium tiers (e.g., direct corporate) yield higher GOP (Gross Operating Profit) than low-margin OTA or wholesale segments. Used in GOPPAR analysis.

Timestamp when this market segment record was first created in the enterprise data lakehouse. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and record provenance tracking. Aligns with RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED requirement for MASTER_RESOURCE entities.

The segment code as configured in Sabre SynXis CRS for central reservations and channel distribution. Used for rate plan distribution, availability controls, and channel-level segment reporting. Enables cross-system segment reconciliation.

Classification of the temporal demand pattern exhibited by this market segment. Corporate transient segments typically show weekday concentration; leisure segments show weekend and seasonal peaks; event-driven segments spike around MICE (Meetings Incentives Conferences Exhibitions) events. Used in Infor EzRMS demand forecasting.

Date from which this market segment definition becomes active and available for use in reservations, rate plans, and revenue reporting. Supports temporal validity management for segment taxonomy changes aligned with fiscal year or STR reporting period updates.

Date after which this market segment definition is no longer active. Null indicates an open-ended segment with no planned expiry. Supports segment lifecycle management and historical reporting continuity when segment taxonomies are revised.

business_glossary_term: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Data Subject Applicable
description: Indicates whether guest data associated with bookings in this market segment is subject to GDPR data subject rights (right to erasure, portability, access). Relevant for segments involving EU/EEA guests. Drives data retention and anonymization policies in the data lakehouse.

Indicates whether rates for this market segment are distributed through GDS (Global Distribution System) channels such as Amadeus, Sabre, and Travelport. Controls rate loading and availability display in Sabre SynXis CRS for GDS-connected travel agencies.

Indicates whether this market segment supports group room block reservations. Group-eligible segments (e.g., MICE, sports groups, tour groups) require group block management in Oracle OPERA PMS and Delphi by Amadeus for BEO (Banquet Event Order) coordination.

Numeric level of this segment within the market segment hierarchy. Level 1 represents top-level categories (e.g., Transient, Group); Level 2 represents sub-categories (e.g., Corporate, Leisure); Level 3 represents granular segments (e.g., Corporate Negotiated, Leisure OTA). Used for hierarchical roll-up in revenue reporting.

Indicates whether this market segment is for internal reporting and analytics purposes only and should not be exposed to guests, OTA partners, or external distribution channels. Internal segments (e.g., complimentary, house use, employee rate) are excluded from external rate distribution in Sabre SynXis CRS.

Indicates whether bookings under this market segment are eligible to earn loyalty program points and benefits. Wholesale, crew, and certain contracted segments may be excluded from loyalty accrual per program rules. Drives loyalty point calculation logic in Salesforce CRM loyalty management.

Detailed narrative description of the market segment, including its defining characteristics, typical guest profile, booking behavior, and applicable rate strategies. Used for training, documentation, and business glossary alignment across revenue management, reservations, and sales teams.

Current lifecycle status of the market segment record. Active segments are available for rate plan assignment, reservation coding, and revenue reporting. Inactive or deprecated segments are retained for historical analysis but excluded from new bookings. Aligns with MASTER_RESOURCE LIFECYCLE_STATUS requirement.

Indicates whether revenue managers are permitted to override the minimum rate floor for this segment. Restricting overrides for high-value segments (e.g., LRA corporate) protects rate integrity. Configurable in IDeaS G3 RMS inventory controls and Oracle OPERA PMS rate management.

Indicates whether bookings in this segment require a pre-negotiated rate agreement (e.g., corporate account rate, government per diem, airline crew contract). When true, a valid rate agreement must exist before reservations can be accepted at segment rates in Oracle OPERA PMS.

Qualitative classification of the historical no-show rate tendency for this market segment. Distinct from cancellation propensity — no-shows occur without prior cancellation and have different revenue recovery implications. Used in overbooking model calibration within IDeaS G3 RMS.

Indicates whether rates for this market segment are distributed through OTA (Online Travel Agency) channels such as Expedia, Booking.com, and Hotels.com. Controls channel availability in Sabre SynXis CRS and impacts OTA commission cost tracking.

Indicates whether payment card data associated with bookings in this segment falls within PCI DSS scope. Segments with direct card-present or card-not-present transactions require PCI-compliant data handling. Drives data masking and tokenization requirements in the lakehouse.

The segment code as configured in Oracle OPERA PMS. Used for reservation coding, folio routing, and rate plan assignment at the property level. Enables reconciliation between the enterprise data lakehouse and the operational PMS source system.

Classification of the segment's sensitivity to rate changes. Inelastic segments (e.g., airline crew, government contracted) book regardless of rate fluctuations. Classification of the rate plan type typically associated with this segment. BAR (Best Available Rate) for transient leisure, negotiated for corporate accounts, LRA (Last Room Availability) for top-tier corporate, NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) for advance purchase segments. The segment code as configured in IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS. Used for demand forecasting, pricing optimization, and inventory control at the segment level. May differ from PMS segment code due to system-specific taxonomy requirements.

High-level classification grouping the segment into a primary demand category. Drives pricing strategy, channel allocation, and revenue reporting roll-ups. Full descriptive name of the market segment as displayed in reporting, dashboards, and revenue management systems. Examples: Corporate Negotiated, Leisure Transient, Online Travel Agency, Government/Military, Group Meetings. Used as the IDENTITY_LABEL for this resource entity.

Secondary classification distinguishing whether the segment represents individual (FIT — Free Independent Traveler) bookings, group blocks, negotiated corporate accounts, wholesale/tour operator contracts, or consortia arrangements. Used in IDeaS G3 RMS for demand segmentation and pricing strategy.

Numeric value controlling the display sequence of market segments in reports, dashboards, and system configuration screens. Ensures consistent ordering in Oracle OPERA PMS segment lists, IDeaS G3 RMS segment configuration, and BI reporting tools.

The official STR (Smith Travel Research) segment code used for competitive benchmarking and STAR Report alignment. Enables mapping of internal segment definitions to the industry-standard STR taxonomy for RGI (Revenue Generation Index), MPI (Market Penetration Index), and ARI (Average Rate Index) calculations.

Timestamp when this market segment record was last modified. Tracks configuration changes to segment definitions, status updates, and taxonomy revisions. Essential for change data capture (CDC) in ETL pipelines feeding IDeaS G3 RMS and Infor EzRMS.

Ensures consistency in GOP (Gross Operating Profit), NOI (Net Operating Income), and EBITDA reporting across properties and for external financial audits under GAAP/IFRS.

description: Foreign key linking to the experience program for which this market segment is eligible

Date when this segment-program eligibility configuration becomes active. Allows for time-bound eligibility rules.

Reference to the subject property for which this STR benchmark or rate shop record applies. Links to the property master record in the Property domain.

The Average Rate Index (ARI) measures the subject property's ADR (Average Daily Rate) relative to its STR competitive set. An ARI above 1.0 (or 100) indicates the property is achieving a higher average rate than its comp set peers.
The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all STR STAR Report monetary benchmark values (ADR, RevPAR) are reported for this record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).

The current lifecycle status of this benchmark or rate shop record. 'active' = current valid record; 'superseded' = replaced by a revised STR submission; 'pending_review' = awaiting data quality validation; 'rejected' = failed data quality checks.

business_glossary_term: Competitive Set (Comp Set) Average Daily Rate (ADR)
description: The aggregated Average Daily Rate (ADR) of the STR competitive set for the benchmark report period. Used to compute the ARI and assess the property's rate positioning relative to the market.
The aggregated occupancy rate (OCC) of the STR competitive set for the benchmark report period. Used to compute the MPI and assess the property's demand capture relative to the market.

business_glossary_term: Competitive Set (Comp Set) Revenue Per Available Room (RevPAR)
description: The aggregated RevPAR of the STR competitive set for the benchmark report period. Used to compute the RGI and assess the property's overall revenue performance relative to the market.

The total number of rooms available (supply) across all properties in the STR competitive set during the benchmark report period. Used as the denominator for comp set RevPAR and occupancy calculations.

The unique identifier or code assigned to the competitor property within the rate shopping tool or STR system (e.g., STR property code, OTA Insight property ID). Used for consistent competitor tracking across rate shop cycles.

The name of the competitor hotel or resort property for which a rate shop observation was captured. Populated only for records with record_type = 'competitor_rate_shop'. Sourced from OTA Insight or RateGain rate shopping tools.

The originating data source or feed from which this benchmark or rate shop record was ingested. Identifies whether the record came from the STR STAR Report data feed, OTA Insight, RateGain rate shopping tool, Infor EzRMS, or manual entry.

The distribution channel through which the competitor rate was observed during rate shopping. Key channels include OTA (Online Travel Agency), direct (hotel website), GDS (Global Distribution System), brand website, and voice. Enables channel-level competitive rate analysis.

The timestamp when this STR benchmark or rate shop record was ingested into the silver layer lakehouse from the source data feed (STR, OTA Insight, RateGain, or Infor EzRMS). Used for data lineage, SLA monitoring, and ETL audit.

Indicates whether the shopped competitor rate is in compliance with rate parity agreements across distribution channels. True = rate parity maintained; False = rate disparity detected. Used for OTA contract compliance monitoring.

The number of days between the shop date and the stay date at the time the competitor rate was captured. A key dimension for rate shop analysis — rates typically vary by lead time (e.g., 0-day, 7-day, 30-day, 90-day windows).

The Length of Stay (LOS) in nights for which the competitor rate was shopped. Rate shopping tools capture rates for specific LOS windows (e.g., 1-night, 2-night, 7-night) as rates may vary by LOS due to minimum stay restrictions.

The STR-defined market location type for the subject property or competitor property (e.g., urban, suburban, airport, resort, small metro, interstate). Used to contextualize benchmark performance within the appropriate market geography.

The Market Penetration Index (MPI) measures the subject property's occupancy rate relative to its STR competitive set. An MPI above 1.0 (or 100) indicates the property is capturing more than its fair share of available demand.

The specific OTA platform name from which the competitor rate was shopped (e.g., Expedia, Booking.com, Hotels.com, Agoda). Populated when distribution_channel = 'OTA'. Enables OTA-level competitive rate parity analysis.

The subject property's Average Daily Rate (ADR) for the benchmark report period, expressed in the reporting currency. ADR is calculated as total room revenue divided by total rooms sold, per USALI standards.

The subject property's occupancy rate (OCC) for the benchmark report period, expressed as a decimal fraction (e.g., 0.8250 = 82.50%). Calculated as rooms sold divided by rooms available per USALI standards.
The subject property's Revenue Per Available Room (RevPAR) for the benchmark report period. RevPAR = ADR × Occupancy Rate. A primary KPI for hotel revenue performance per USALI and STR standards.
The total number of rooms available (supply) at the subject property during the benchmark report period. Used as the denominator for RevPAR and occupancy calculations per USALI standards.

The availability status of the competitor rate at the time of the rate shop observation. Indicates whether the rate was available for booking, sold out, restricted (e.g., minimum stay), on request, or not shopped for this combination.

The type of rate plan under which the competitor rate was shopped or the benchmark rate applies. Includes BAR (Best Available Rate), LRA (Last Room Availability), NRR (Non-Refundable Rate), package, corporate, group, and promotional rates.
