Discriminator indicating whether this record represents an STR STAR Report benchmark index record or a granular competitor rate shopping observation. Enables partitioned querying across both data types within this unified product.
The last date of the STR STAR Report benchmark period for which the benchmark indices and performance metrics are reported.
The first date of the STR STAR Report benchmark period (e.g., start of week, month, or year-to-date window) for which the benchmark indices and performance metrics are reported.
The temporal granularity of the STR STAR Report benchmark period. Determines whether the record represents a daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, annual, or year-to-date benchmark snapshot.

The Revenue Generation Index (RGI) measures the subject property's RevPAR performance relative to its STR competitive set. An RGI above 1.0 (or 100) indicates the property is outperforming its comp set on RevPAR. Sourced from STR STAR Report.

The standardized room type category for which the competitor rate was shopped (e.g., standard, deluxe, suite). Enables like-for-like rate comparison across competitor properties. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|deluxe|suite|junior_suite|executive|connecting|accessible|villa|other — promote to reference product]
The date on which the rate shopping observation was captured from the competitor channel. The difference between shop_date and stay_date defines the booking lead time for competitive rate analysis.

The publicly available rate amount captured for the competitor property during the rate shopping observation, expressed in the shopped rate currency. Represents the per-night room rate for the specified stay date, room type, and rate plan.

The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the shopped competitor rate amount is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). May differ from the benchmark currency if the competitor property operates in a different market.

The specific arrival or stay date for which the competitor rate was shopped. Represents the future date of intended guest stay, enabling forward-looking competitive rate analysis and demand forecasting.
The unique property identifier assigned by STR (Smith Travel Research) to the subject property. Used to match and reconcile STR STAR Report submissions and benchmark data feeds.

The version number of the STR STAR Report data file or submission from which this benchmark record was sourced. STR may issue revised reports for the same period; this field tracks which version of the report was ingested.
The date on which the property submitted its performance data to STR for inclusion in the STAR Report benchmark cycle. Used for data reconciliation and audit trail.
The timestamp when this record was last modified in the silver layer, such as when a revised STR STAR Report submission supersedes a prior record or when a data quality correction is applied.
Unique surrogate identifier for each displacement analysis record. Primary key for the displacement_analysis data product in the revenue domain Silver layer.

business_glossary_term: Related Banquet Event Order Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to fnb.banquet_event_order. Business justification: Displacement calculations include banquet F&B revenue to offset room revenue displacement from group blocks. Required for group profitability analysis, acceptance decisions, total property revenue opt

Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: When evaluating group blocks during high-demand periods driven by marketing campaigns, revenue managers need campaign context to assess true displacement cost and whether to accept group business.
Identifier of the demand forecast record used as the basis for transient displacement estimates in this analysis. Links to the revenue.demand_forecast product.

Foreign key linking to guest.guest_group_block. Business justification: Displacement analysis quantifies revenue impact of accepting specific guest group blocks. Revenue managers calculate transient displacement cost, net contribution, and RevPAR impact for each guest.gue

Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Displacement decisions impact revenue recognition and require GL posting for group vs transient revenue tracking, profitability analysis, and revenue mix reporting.
Identifier of the market segment associated with the group or contract business being evaluated (e.g., MICE, corporate group, leisure group). Drives segmentation in revenue reporting.
Identifier of the hotel or resort property for which the displacement analysis is being conducted. Links to the property master record.
Identifier of the proposed rate plan associated with the group or contract business being evaluated. Used to assess the proposed rate against transient BAR (Best Available Rate).
The calendar date on which the displacement analysis was performed by the Revenue Management System or revenue manager. Represents the principal business event date for this record.

Externally-known business identifier for the displacement analysis, typically generated by IDeaS G3 RMS group evaluation module (e.g., 'DISP-2024-00123'). Used for cross-system traceability and revenue manager communication.

Current workflow state of the displacement analysis record. 'draft' indicates in-progress; 'pending_review' awaits revenue manager sign-off; 'approved' or 'rejected' reflects final decision; 'superseded' indicates a newer analysis replaced this one.

Classification of the type of business being evaluated for displacement. Distinguishes between group room blocks, long-stay contracts, tour operator allotments, and wholesale agreements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: group_evaluation|contract_evaluation|tour_operator|wholesale|corporate_negotiated|government — promote to reference product]

Estimated ancillary revenue contribution from the group if accepted, including spa, parking, audio-visual, and other non-room, non-F&B spend. Supports total spend analysis for TRevPAR (Total Revenue Per Available Room) evaluation.

Distribution channel through which the group or contract inquiry originated (e.g., direct sales, GDS (Global Distribution System), OTA (Online Travel Agency), Delphi event sales). Informs channel cost considerations in the displacement calculation.
Timestamp when the displacement analysis record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to all monetary amounts in this displacement analysis record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).

Free-text narrative explaining the revenue manager's reasoning behind the recommendation outcome, including demand conditions, competitive context, or strategic considerations not captured in quantitative fields.

Categorical risk rating assigned to the displacement analysis indicating the severity of transient revenue displacement if the group is accepted. Derived from net_revenue_impact and forecast_occupancy_pct thresholds configured in IDeaS G3 RMS.

business_glossary_term: Estimated Transient Average Daily Rate (ADR) Displaced
description: Estimated average per-room per-night rate that transient guests would have paid for the same rooms and dates, as forecast by IDeaS G3 RMS. Core metric for measuring revenue opportunity cost of accepting group business.
Estimated number of transient room-nights that would be displaced (lost) if the group block is accepted. Derived from IDeaS G3 RMS demand forecast for the stay period.
Estimated Food and Beverage (F&B) revenue contribution from the group if accepted, including banquet, catering, and outlet spend. Part of the total spend analysis supporting TRevPAR-based evaluation.

Projected occupancy percentage (OCC) for the property during the stay period at the time of analysis, as sourced from the demand forecast. Contextualizes the displacement risk — high OCC increases displacement cost.
Total projected room revenue from the group block if accepted, calculated as proposed_group_rate × room_block_size × los_nights. Represents the revenue inflow side of the displacement equation.

Indicates whether the stay dates fall within a designated peak demand period (e.g., major events, holidays, high-season). Peak periods carry higher displacement risk and typically require higher minimum acceptable rates.
Indicates whether a special event (concert, convention, sporting event, etc.) is occurring in the market during the stay period, which may elevate transient demand and increase displacement cost.
Total number of nights in the proposed group stay period (LOS). Calculated as stay_date_to minus stay_date_from. Used in yield optimization and LOS restriction analysis.
The minimum per-room per-night rate at which accepting the group block would be revenue-neutral or accretive, as calculated by the displacement model. Used by sales teams as a negotiation floor.

Net revenue impact of accepting the group business, calculated as total_group_spend minus transient_room_revenue_displaced. A positive value indicates the group is accretive; negative indicates displacement loss exceeds group gain.
Additional free-text notes or commentary entered by the revenue manager regarding special circumstances, negotiation history, or strategic context not captured in structured fields.
The proposed per-room per-night rate offered to the group or contract client. Compared against estimated transient ADR (Average Daily Rate) to determine revenue opportunity cost.

Revenue management recommendation resulting from the displacement analysis. 'accept' indicates the group is revenue-positive; 'reject' indicates displacement loss exceeds group value; 'accept_with_conditions' requires rate or block size negotiation; 'defer' awaits further demand data; 'counter_offer' proposes alternative terms.
Name or employee identifier of the revenue manager or director who reviewed and acted upon the displacement analysis recommendation. Supports accountability and audit trail requirements.

Timestamp when the revenue manager completed their review and recorded the final recommendation outcome. Distinct from created_timestamp and updated_timestamp; represents the business decision event time.

Estimated change in RevPAR (Revenue Per Available Room) attributable to accepting the group block, expressed as a positive or negative dollar amount per available room. Key KPI for revenue management decision-making.
Native evaluation or job identifier assigned by IDeaS G3 RMS when the group evaluation was executed. Enables traceability back to the source system run for audit and re-evaluation purposes.
Total number of rooms in the proposed group or contract room block being evaluated for displacement impact. Core input to the displacement calculation.
Code identifying the room type or category requested in the group block (e.g., STD, DLX, STE). Used to assess displacement impact at the room-type level.
Name or description of the special event influencing demand during the stay period, when is_special_event is true. Provides context for the displacement risk assessment.
First night of the proposed group or contract stay period being evaluated. Defines the start of the displacement window for transient demand comparison.
Last night of the proposed group or contract stay period being evaluated. Defines the end of the displacement window for transient demand comparison.
Total estimated revenue from the group across all revenue streams (rooms + F&B + ancillary). Used as the numerator in the full displacement value comparison to support TRevPAR-based decision-making.

Total estimated transient room revenue that would be lost if the group block is accepted, calculated as estimated_transient_adr_displaced × estimated_transient_rooms_displaced × los_nights. Represents the revenue opportunity cost.
Timestamp when the displacement analysis record was last modified. Tracks revisions made by revenue managers or automated RMS re-evaluations.
Unique surrogate identifier for each group business evaluation record generated by IDeaS G3 RMS. Primary key for the group_evaluation data product in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
Identifier of the corporate account, travel agency, or group organizer account associated with this group inquiry, as maintained in Salesforce CRM and Oracle OPERA PMS AR module.

Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Group RFPs often originate from marketing campaigns targeting corporate/SMIC segments. Linking the campaign helps measure campaign effectiveness in driving qualified group leads and conversion rates.

Foreign key linking to loyalty.member. Business justification: Group bookings often involve loyalty members as decision-makers, influencing pricing decisions. Business process: tracking member-led group inquiries for loyalty credit attribution and personalized gr

Foreign key linking to guest.guest_group_block. Business justification: Revenue managers evaluate guest group blocks for displacement analysis and rate decisions. The group_evaluation process directly assesses guest.guest_group_block entities to determine whether to accep

Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Group booking decisions impact revenue recognition and require GL account mapping for group revenue tracking, displacement cost accounting, and profitability analysis.

business_glossary_term: Primary Banquet Event Order Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to fnb.banquet_event_order. Business justification: Group evaluations assess F&B revenue contribution from banquet events to offset room displacement. Required for displacement analysis, group acceptance decisions, total property profitability evaluati
Identifier of the hotel or resort property for which the group evaluation is being conducted. Links to the property master record in the Property domain.

Foreign key linking to revenue.revenue_rate_plan. Business justification: Group evaluations are performed for specific rate plans. group_evaluation has rate_plan_code (STRING) which should be normalized to FK. N group_evaluation records → 1 revenue_rate_plan. Removes redund

Foreign key linking to revenue.wash_factor. Business justification: Group evaluations apply wash factors from the wash_factor master table to estimate attrition. N group_evaluation records → 1 wash_factor. Note: wash_factor_pct on group_evaluation is the APPLIED value
Estimated ancillary revenue from the group including spa, parking, audio-visual, business center, and other non-room, non-F&B spend. Used in total spend calculation for net revenue impact assessment.
Proposed first night of the group room block. Used as the start of the displacement analysis window in IDeaS G3 RMS.

Expected attrition percentage for the group block by group type, representing the proportion of contracted rooms the group is expected not to use. Distinct from wash factor in that attrition is the contractual exposure, while wash is the statistical pickup adjustment.

The distribution channel through which the group inquiry was received (e.g., direct sales, GDS, OTA, travel agency). Used for channel contribution analysis and commission cost modeling in the net revenue impact calculation.

Alternative per-room per-night rate proposed by the revenue manager as a counter-offer to the group organizer when the proposed_group_rate is below the minimum_acceptable_rate. Populated only when revenue_manager_decision = 'counter_offer'.
Audit timestamp recording when this group evaluation record was first captured in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Used for data lineage and ETL audit trail.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this evaluation record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent financial reporting per USALI and IFRS/GAAP standards.

The date by which unbooked rooms in the group block are released back to general inventory. After this date, the property may sell unreserved block rooms to transient guests. Critical for inventory management and wash factor actualization.

Free-text justification provided by the revenue manager when their decision overrides the RMS recommendation (e.g., strategic account relationship, future business potential, competitive displacement risk). Required when revenue_manager_decision differs from rms_recommendation.
Timestamp when the revenue manager recorded their final accept, decline, or counter-offer decision. Used for SLA tracking and decision audit trail.

Forecasted transient Occupancy Rate (OCC) for the property during the group's proposed stay dates, as generated by IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS. High forecasted occupancy increases displacement cost and raises the minimum acceptable rate.
Proposed last night of the group room block (checkout date). Defines the end of the displacement analysis window.

Total estimated revenue lost from displaced transient business if the group block is accepted, calculated as transient_rooms_displaced multiplied by transient_adr_displaced across the stay period. Key component of the net revenue impact analysis.

Current lifecycle status of the group evaluation workflow. Tracks progression from initial inquiry receipt through revenue manager decision. 'pending' = awaiting RMS analysis; 'under_review' = revenue manager reviewing; 'recommended_accept' = RMS recommends acceptance; 'recommended_decline' = RMS recommends decline; 'accepted' = revenue manager approved; 'declined' = revenue manager rejected.
The principal real-world event timestamp when the group evaluation was initiated and processed by IDeaS G3 RMS. Represents the business event time, distinct from record audit timestamps.

Estimated Food and Beverage (F&B) revenue contribution from the group, including banquet, catering, and outlet spend. Sourced from Delphi by Amadeus event catering estimates and historical group F&B spend patterns.
Name of the group or event associated with the booking inquiry (e.g., 'Acme Corp Annual Sales Conference 2025'). Used for identification and reporting across Delphi by Amadeus and Oracle OPERA PMS.

Total projected room revenue from the group block at the proposed group rate, calculated as adjusted room block (post-wash) multiplied by proposed_group_rate across all nights. Gross revenue contribution from rooms only.

Classification of the group by business segment. Drives wash factor parameters and attrition modeling in IDeaS G3 RMS. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: corporate|association|government|leisure|sports|wedding|social|tour_operator|incentive — promote to reference product]

Historical average wash percentage for this group type and property, derived from past group performance data in IDeaS G3 RMS. Serves as the baseline input before adjustments for lead time and current demand conditions.

Externally-known alphanumeric reference number assigned to the group booking inquiry, as generated by the Central Reservation System (CRS) or Property Management System (PMS). Used to cross-reference the evaluation with the originating inquiry across Oracle OPERA PMS and Sabre SynXis CRS.

Indicates whether the group inquiry has converted to a definite (contracted) booking following a positive revenue manager decision. True = definite booking confirmed; False = still in tentative or evaluation stage.

Categorical bucket representing the number of days between the group inquiry date and the proposed arrival date. Used as a parameter in wash factor modeling, as groups booked further in advance typically exhibit higher wash rates.

Total number of nights in the proposed group stay, calculated as departure_date minus arrival_date. Represents the Length of Stay (LOS) for the group block used in displacement and revenue impact modeling.

The minimum per-room per-night rate recommended by IDeaS G3 RMS below which accepting the group would result in negative net revenue impact after accounting for displacement costs. This is the RMS-generated floor rate for negotiation.

Net revenue impact of accepting the group, calculated as total group spend (room + F&B + ancillary) minus displacement cost. Positive value indicates the group is accretive; negative value indicates the group destroys revenue versus transient alternative. Primary decision metric in the evaluation.
Maximum number of rooms occupied on any single night within the group block. Used for capacity planning and displacement analysis at the peak demand point.

The per-room per-night rate proposed by the group organizer or sales manager for the room block, expressed in the property's operating currency. This is the rate under evaluation against the displacement cost and minimum acceptable rate.

Final decision made by the revenue manager after reviewing the RMS recommendation and displacement analysis. May align with or override the RMS recommendation. 'pending' indicates no decision has been recorded yet.

Estimated change in Revenue Per Available Room (RevPAR) for the property during the group stay dates if the group is accepted, compared to the transient-only forecast. Negative RevPAR impact is a key indicator for decline recommendation.

The property's Revenue Generation Index (RGI) from the STR STAR Report for the competitive set during the evaluation period. Used as a competitive context benchmark to assess whether accepting the group at the proposed rate is competitively rational.

Automated accept, decline, or counter-offer recommendation generated by IDeaS G3 RMS based on displacement analysis, wash factor modeling, and net revenue impact. This is the system-generated recommendation prior to revenue manager override.
Total number of guest rooms requested in the group block across all nights of the stay. This is the peak or contracted room block size as submitted in the group inquiry.

The native record identifier from the originating operational system (IDeaS G3 RMS group evaluation ID or Delphi by Amadeus opportunity ID). Enables traceability back to the system of record for audit and reconciliation purposes.

The forecasted Average Daily Rate (ADR) of the transient business that would be displaced by the group block, as estimated by IDeaS G3 RMS. Used to quantify the revenue opportunity cost of accepting the group.

Estimated number of transient (individual) room nights that would be displaced if the group block is accepted, as calculated by IDeaS G3 RMS displacement analysis. Core input to the net revenue impact calculation.
Audit timestamp recording when this group evaluation record was last modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse, including revenue manager decision updates or status changes.

The adjusted wash factor percentage applied to the contracted room block to estimate actual pickup. Derived from historical wash percentage, group type attrition patterns, and booking lead time bucket. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 15.00 = 15% wash). Used to calculate the adjusted room block for revenue modeling.
Primary key for inventory_control

Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Inventory controls (sell limits, hurdles, stop-sells) vary by channel. Revenue managers allocate inventory differently across direct, OTA, GDS channels based on profitability and strategy. Fundamental

Reference to the Dynamic Rate Rule (DRR) applied to derive the current_bar for this room type and stay date. Links to the pricing strategy configuration governing automated rate adjustments in IDeaS G3 RMS.

Foreign key linking to event.event_booking. Business justification: Inventory controls (sell limits, hurdle rates, overbooking caps) are adjusted based on event booking displacement analysis. Essential for inventory optimization around events and group block allocatio
Reference to the property (hotel, resort, or vacation property) to which this inventory control record applies. Aligns with Oracle OPERA PMS property configuration and IDeaS G3 RMS property setup.
Reference to the rate plan (e.g., BAR, LRA, NRR, corporate, package) associated with this inventory control record. Determines the pricing tier and restrictions applied to the available inventory.

Reference to the specific room type (e.g., King Deluxe, Double Standard, Suite) for which this inventory control record is defined. Aligns with Oracle OPERA PMS room type configuration and IDeaS G3 RMS room class setup.

Foreign key linking to procurement.employee. Business justification: Revenue managers set inventory controls; tracking updater essential for override authority validation, compliance audit trails, and accountability for sell limits, hurdle rates, and overbooking decisi

Number of rooms of this room type on the stay date allocated to contracted allotments (OTA allotments, wholesale, tour operator contracts). Managed via Sabre SynXis CRS channel management and reduces the freely-sellable transient inventory.

Designates the level of authority required to approve the overbooking level for this inventory control record. Ensures governance and accountability for overbooking decisions per property SOPs and SOX internal control requirements.

Real-time count of rooms available for sale for this property, room type, stay date, and rate plan combination at the time of last update. Reflects physical inventory minus confirmed reservations, group blocks, and out-of-order rooms. Distributed via Sabre SynXis CRS for reservation fulfillment.
Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for this inventory control record as referenced in IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis CRS. Used for cross-system reconciliation and audit traceability.

Current lifecycle state of this inventory control record indicating whether the room type/rate plan combination is open for sale, closed, restricted, pending review, or subject to a manual override. This is the real-time availability state distributed via Sabre SynXis CRS.

Classifies the category of inventory control applied to this record. Determines how the control_value is interpreted and enforced by the Revenue Management System. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hurdle_rate|sell_limit|overbooking_level|los_restriction|cta_restriction|ctd_restriction|allotment|group_block — promote to reference product]

The operative value for this control record as applied by the Revenue Management System. Interpretation depends on control_type: for hurdle_rate it is the minimum acceptable rate in local currency; for sell_limit it is the maximum rooms to sell; for overbooking_level it is the number of rooms above physical capacity authorized to sell.

The date and time when this inventory control record was first created in the source Revenue Management System (IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS). Provides the audit trail creation anchor for data lineage and SOX compliance.

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary rate fields in this record (current_bar, min_rate, max_rate, hurdle_rate). Ensures multi-currency properties report rates in the correct denomination.

The current Best Available Rate (BAR) in local currency for this room type and stay date as published via Sabre SynXis CRS and channel distribution. Represents the lowest publicly available non-restricted rate and is the primary pricing signal for transient demand.

IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS demand forecast for unconstrained room demand for this room type on the stay date at the time this control record was generated. Used to contextualize the control_value against projected demand and support RevPAR optimization.

The date and time from which this inventory control record's control_value and restrictions became operative in the Revenue Management System. Supports temporal validity tracking and historical rate/availability grid reconstruction.

The date and time at which this inventory control record's control_value and restrictions ceased to be operative. Null for currently active records. Enables point-in-time historical analysis of the rate/availability grid.

Number of rooms of this room type on the stay date allocated to group blocks (MICE, tour groups, corporate groups). Reduces the transient sell limit and is managed via Delphi by Amadeus group block management and IDeaS G3 RMS group evaluation.

Minimum acceptable rate (in local currency) below which a reservation for this room type on the stay date should not be accepted, as determined by the Revenue Management System. Used in displacement analysis and group evaluation in IDeaS G3 RMS.

Indicates whether arrivals are blocked for this room type and rate plan on the stay date (Closed to Arrival / CTA restriction). When True, guests may not check in on this date but may stay through it as part of a longer booking.

Indicates whether departures are blocked for this room type and rate plan on the stay date (Closed to Departure / CTD restriction). When True, guests may not check out on this date, requiring stays to extend beyond it.

Indicates whether the applied control_value represents a manual override of the system_recommended_value. True when a revenue manager has manually intervened; False when the system recommendation was accepted as-is. Critical for RMS model performance auditing.

The date and time when this inventory control record's rate and availability state was last successfully distributed to Sabre SynXis CRS and downstream channels (OTA, GDS). Critical for SLA monitoring and channel distribution reconciliation.
Maximum number of nights a guest may stay when booking this room type at this rate plan on the stay date. Used to prevent long-stay displacement of higher-value transient demand during peak periods.

The ceiling rate in local currency above which no rate plan for this room type and stay date may be sold. Used to enforce rate integrity, prevent rate gouging, and comply with local pricing regulations during high-demand periods.

Minimum number of nights a guest must stay to be eligible to book this room type at this rate plan on the stay date. A Length of Stay (LOS) restriction used to optimize revenue during high-demand periods by requiring longer stays.

The absolute floor rate in local currency below which no rate plan for this room type and stay date may be sold, regardless of channel or negotiated agreement. Enforced as a hard constraint in the Revenue Management System and CRS.

Current confirmed occupancy rate (OCC) percentage for this room type on the stay date at the time of last update, calculated as confirmed reservations divided by physical_room_count. Key input to dynamic pricing and sell limit decisions in the Revenue Management System.

Number of rooms of this room type on the stay date designated as out of order (OOO) due to maintenance, renovation, or FF&E replacement. These rooms are excluded from sellable inventory and occupancy calculations per USALI standards.

Hard ceiling on the absolute number of rooms that may be overbooked above physical capacity for this room type on the stay date, regardless of the overbooking_pct calculation. Prevents excessive walk exposure during high-demand periods.

Maximum authorized overbooking level expressed as a percentage above physical room capacity for this room type on the stay date. Defined in the overbooking policy and used by IDeaS G3 RMS to set the sell limit above physical_room_count.

Free-text or coded justification provided by the revenue manager when is_override is True. Documents the business rationale for deviating from the system recommendation (e.g., local event, competitive action, group displacement). Required for audit and RMS calibration.

Total number of physical rooms of this room type at the property, representing the hard capacity ceiling before overbooking allowances are applied. Used as the denominator for occupancy rate (OCC) calculations.

Identifies the originating system or process that set the current rate and availability state for this control record. Supports data lineage, audit, and reconciliation between IDeaS G3 RMS, Sabre SynXis CRS, and Oracle OPERA PMS.

Maximum number of rooms authorized to be sold for this room type on the stay date, inclusive of overbooking allowance. Set by the Revenue Management System and may exceed physical_room_count when overbooking is authorized. Enforced at the CRS and PMS booking engine.

The specific calendar date (night of stay) for which this inventory control record applies. Represents the primary dimension of the rate/availability grid alongside property, room type, and rate plan.

The value recommended by the Revenue Management System (IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS) prior to any manual override. Enables comparison between algorithmic recommendation and the applied control_value for yield optimization analysis and RMS model performance tracking.

The date and time when this inventory control record was most recently modified in the source system. Used for incremental ETL processing, change detection, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.

Type of compensation offered to a walked guest when the walk_policy requires compensation. Governs the financial and loyalty implications of overbooking execution and is tracked for Guest Recovery Rate (GRR) and CPOR reporting.

Defines the operational procedure to follow when a guest with a confirmed reservation cannot be accommodated due to overbooking (a 'walk'). Determines whether the guest is walked to a partner hotel, upgraded, or compensated per AHLA guest relocation standards.
Unique surrogate identifier for the overbooking policy record. Primary key for the revenue_overbooking_policy data product in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
Identifier of the employee who formally approved this overbooking policy. Links to the workforce/employee master record. Required for SOX compliance and audit trail.

Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Overbooking practices must comply with consumer protection policies. Overbooking policy approval requires compliance review to ensure fair walk procedures and compensation. Essential for consumer prot
Identifier of the hotel or resort property to which this overbooking policy applies. Links to the property master record in the Property domain.

Foreign key linking to compliance.risk_register. Business justification: Overbooking carries operational and reputational risk requiring risk management oversight. Risk committee reviews overbooking policies and monitors walk incidents. Essential for enterprise risk manage
Indicates whether Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) accessible rooms are excluded from the overbooking pool under this policy. True = ADA rooms are never overbooked. Required for ADA compliance.

Organizational authority level required to approve and activate this overbooking policy. Defines the governance hierarchy for policy authorization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: revenue_manager|director_of_revenue|gm|corporate_rm|vp_revenue|cro — promote to reference product]
Date and time when this overbooking policy was formally approved by the designated authority. Recorded in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format. Required for audit trail and SOX compliance.

Indicates whether IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS is authorized to automatically adjust the overbooking percentage within the defined min/max bounds without manual revenue manager intervention. True = dynamic auto-adjustment enabled.

Historical or forecast cancellation rate percentage used as the actuarial basis for setting the overbooking level. Derived from IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS demand forecasting models and reviewed periodically by revenue management.

Distribution channel scope to which this overbooking policy applies. 'all' = all booking channels. 'direct' = brand.com and direct reservations. 'ota' = Online Travel Agency bookings. 'gds' = Global Distribution System bookings. 'voice' = call center. 'group' = group block reservations.
Date and time when this overbooking policy record was first created in the system, recorded in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.

Seven-character binary string representing the days of the week this policy applies to, ordered Monday through Sunday (e.g., '1111100' = Mon–Fri only, '1111111' = all days). Enables granular day-of-week yield optimization.

Demand classification tier from the Revenue Management System that this policy is calibrated for. Aligns with IDeaS G3 RMS and Infor EzRMS demand forecasting segments to enable yield optimization by demand level.
Calendar date from which this overbooking policy becomes active and enforceable in the Property Management System and Revenue Management System.
Calendar date on which this overbooking policy ceases to be active. Null indicates an open-ended policy with no defined end date.
Most recent date on which this overbooking policy was formally reviewed by the revenue management team. Used to track compliance with the defined review frequency.
Minimum Length of Stay (LOS) in nights required for this overbooking policy to apply. Supports LOS-based yield optimization strategies in IDeaS G3 RMS. Null indicates no minimum LOS restriction.

Number of loyalty program bonus points awarded to walked guests as additional compensation under this policy. Integrates with Salesforce CRM Loyalty Management and the Loyalty domain for points fulfillment.
Comma-separated list of loyalty program tier codes (e.g., 'PLATINUM,DIAMOND') whose members are exempt from being walked under this policy. Integrates with Salesforce CRM Loyalty Management.
Minimum overbooking level as a percentage below which the revenue management system should alert or auto-adjust. Supports dynamic overbooking optimization in IDeaS G3 RMS to prevent under-selling.
Scheduled date for the next formal review of this overbooking policy by the revenue management team. Drives workflow alerts in the Revenue Management System.
Historical or forecast no-show rate percentage used as the actuarial basis for setting the overbooking level. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS historical data and IDeaS G3 RMS forecasting models.

Maximum absolute number of rooms that may be overbooked above physical inventory, regardless of the overbooking percentage. Acts as a hard ceiling to limit walk exposure. Enforced in Oracle OPERA PMS inventory controls.

Maximum authorized overbooking level expressed as a percentage above the physical room inventory for the applicable room type (e.g., 5.00 = 5% above capacity). Core yield optimization parameter managed in IDeaS G3 RMS and enforced in OPERA PMS inventory controls.
Oracle OPERA PMS inventory control rule code corresponding to this overbooking policy. Used for reconciliation between the lakehouse and the operational PMS configuration.

Free-text field for revenue management team annotations, special conditions, or contextual notes about this overbooking policy (e.g., 'Applies during citywide convention periods only', 'Reviewed post-Q3 STR STAR Report').

Current lifecycle status of the overbooking policy. 'active' means the policy is enforced in OPERA PMS and IDeaS G3 RMS. 'draft' indicates pending approval. 'suspended' means temporarily disabled. 'archived' means retired from use.

Classification of the overbooking policy by reservation segment. 'standard' applies to general transient bookings. 'dynamic' indicates IDeaS G3 RMS auto-adjusts levels. 'group' applies to group blocks. 'guaranteed' applies to guaranteed reservation types only.

Comma-separated list of rate plan codes (BAR, NRR, LRA, corporate, etc.) to which this overbooking policy applies. Null indicates the policy applies to all rate plans. Aligns with Sabre SynXis CRS rate distribution configuration.
Scheduled frequency at which this overbooking policy is reviewed and potentially recalibrated by the revenue management team. Supports governance and continuous optimization of overbooking levels.
Native policy identifier or rule code as stored in IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS. Enables direct cross-system traceability between the lakehouse Silver Layer record and the source RMS configuration.
OPERA PMS room type code to which this overbooking policy applies (e.g., 'KING', 'DBLQ', 'SUITE'). A null or wildcard value indicates the policy applies to all room types at the property.
Revenue management season classification code (e.g., 'PEAK', 'SHOULDER', 'OFF') that scopes this overbooking policy. Aligns with IDeaS G3 RMS demand forecasting season definitions.

Date and time when this overbooking policy record was most recently modified, recorded in yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and Silver Layer incremental processing.

Indicates whether VIP-flagged reservations (loyalty elite, corporate VIP, celebrity) are excluded from walk eligibility under this policy. True = VIP reservations are protected from walk. Integrates with Salesforce CRM guest profiles.
Number of overbooked rooms at which the system triggers an alert to the front desk and revenue management team to initiate proactive walk management. Configured in Oracle OPERA PMS.

Monetary compensation amount authorized for walked guests under this policy, expressed in the property's operating currency. Applicable when walk_policy_type is 'cash_compensation' or 'voucher'. Governed by AHLA walk policy standards.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the walk compensation amount (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'GBP'). Required for multi-currency property portfolios.

Type of compensation offered to guests who are walked (relocated) due to overbooking. 'comp_room' = complimentary room at partner hotel. 'cash_compensation' = monetary payment. 'upgrade' = room upgrade if available. 'partner_hotel' = relocation to contracted partner property. 'voucher' = future stay voucher. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: comp_room|cash_compensation|upgrade|partner_hotel|voucher|loyalty_points|transport_voucher — promote to reference product]
Indicates whether complimentary transportation to the partner hotel is included in the walk compensation package under this policy. True = transportation is provided at property expense.
Unique surrogate identifier for each wash factor record in the revenue domain Silver layer. Primary key for the wash_factor data product.

Reference to the market segment associated with this wash factor record (e.g., corporate group, association, SMERF, leisure group). Supports segment-level attrition analysis and Revenue Management System (RMS) demand segmentation.
Reference to the property for which this wash factor record applies. Enables property-level group demand forecasting and displacement analysis within IDeaS G3 RMS.
Reference to the room type to which this wash factor applies. Allows wash factor differentiation by room category (e.g., standard, suite, accessible) for granular group block attrition modeling.

Revenue manager-adjusted wash factor applied to group block forecasting after applying business judgment, seasonal adjustments, or RMS recommendations on top of the historical wash percentage. This is the operative value consumed by IDeaS G3 RMS for net group pickup forecasting and transient displacement calculations.

Free-text explanation provided by the revenue manager for any manual adjustment applied to the historical wash percentage to derive the adjusted wash percentage. Documents the business rationale for overrides (e.g., 'Adjusted upward due to new corporate account with unproven pickup history').
Name or user identifier of the revenue manager or director who approved the adjusted wash percentage for use in RMS forecasting. Supports audit trail and governance requirements.
Date and time when the wash factor record was formally approved by the revenue manager for activation in the RMS. Provides a precise audit trail for governance and SOX compliance.
Maximum number of contracted group room nights that defines the upper bound of the block size tier. A null value indicates an open-ended upper bound (e.g., mega blocks with no ceiling).
Minimum number of contracted group room nights that defines the lower bound of the block size tier for this wash factor record.

Categorical tier representing the size of the group room block (e.g., small: 1–10 rooms, medium: 11–50 rooms, large: 51–200 rooms, mega: 200+ rooms). Block size is a significant predictor of wash behavior; larger blocks tend to exhibit higher attrition rates.

Categorical bucket representing the number of days between group block creation and the group arrival date. Lead time is a critical predictor of wash behavior; groups booked further in advance typically exhibit higher attrition rates. Used by IDeaS G3 RMS to apply the correct wash factor based on booking horizon.

Distribution channel code associated with this wash factor (e.g., GDS, direct, OTA, voice). Group blocks sourced through different channels may exhibit distinct attrition patterns, warranting channel-specific wash factors.

Statistical confidence level associated with the historical wash percentage estimate, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 95.00 = 95%). Derived from the sample size and variance of historical group block attrition data. Informs revenue managers of the reliability of the wash factor.
Date and time when this wash factor record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation anchor for the record lifecycle in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer.

Day-of-week pattern for which this wash factor applies. Group attrition rates can differ between weekday and weekend arrivals. Used to apply day-of-week-sensitive wash adjustments in the RMS forecast.

Estimated percentage of transient room revenue displaced by the group block after applying the wash factor. Quantifies the opportunity cost of accepting the group block versus holding inventory for transient demand. Used in group displacement analysis within IDeaS G3 RMS.
Date from which this wash factor record becomes active and is applied by the Revenue Management System (RMS) for group demand forecasting. Supports temporal versioning of wash factor configurations.

Date on which this wash factor record ceases to be active. A null value indicates the record is open-ended and remains in effect until superseded. Enables temporal management of wash factor versioning.

Current lifecycle status of the wash factor record. Controls whether the record is actively consumed by the RMS for forecasting. 'Superseded' indicates a newer version has replaced this record; 'pending_review' indicates the record requires revenue manager approval before activation.

Classification of the group business type to which this wash factor applies. Group type is a primary driver of attrition behavior; corporate groups typically wash differently than SMERF (Social, Military, Educational, Religious, Fraternal) or association groups. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: corporate|association|smerf|leisure|government|sports|tour_operator — promote to reference product]

End date of the historical data window used to calculate the historical wash percentage. Together with historical_observation_start_date, defines the precise lookback period for wash factor derivation.
Start date of the historical data window used to calculate the historical wash percentage. Defines the lookback period for the statistical derivation of the wash factor.

Observed historical attrition (wash) percentage derived from actual group block pickup data. Represents the proportion of contracted group room nights that were not consumed (i.e., rooms blocked but not picked up). Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 0.2500 = 25%). Source data from Oracle OPERA PMS group block history.

Indicates whether this wash factor record serves as the default fallback when no more specific wash factor matches a group block's attributes (group type, lead time, block size, season). True = default factor; False = specific factor.

business_glossary_term: Is Revenue Management System (RMS) Active Flag
description: Indicates whether this wash factor record is currently being consumed by the Revenue Management System (IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS) for live group demand forecasting and transient displacement calculations. True = actively used in RMS; False = not currently applied.
Date on which this wash factor record was most recently reviewed and validated by the revenue management team. Used to identify stale wash factors that may require recalibration.

Maximum number of days before arrival that defines the upper bound of the booking lead time bucket. A null value indicates an open-ended upper bound (e.g., 365+ days). Together with lead_time_min_days, precisely defines the lead time window.

Minimum number of days before arrival that defines the lower bound of the booking lead time bucket. Together with lead_time_max_days, precisely defines the lead time window for this wash factor record.
Scheduled date for the next review and recalibration of this wash factor record. Derived from last_reviewed_date and review_frequency. Supports proactive revenue management governance workflows.

Free-text field for additional revenue manager commentary, special conditions, or contextual information relevant to this wash factor record. May include references to specific group accounts, market events, or RMS configuration notes.

Prescribed frequency at which this wash factor record should be reviewed and recalibrated by the revenue management team. Ensures wash factors remain statistically current and reflective of evolving group booking behavior.

business_glossary_term: Revenue Management System (RMS) Wash Factor ID
description: Native identifier for this wash factor record as stored in the source Revenue Management System (IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS). Enables bidirectional traceability between the Silver layer data product and the operational RMS source system.

Number of historical group block observations used to calculate the historical wash percentage. Indicates statistical reliability of the wash factor; low sample sizes may warrant wider confidence intervals or manual override.

Seasonal classification for which this wash factor is applicable. Wash behavior varies significantly by season; peak season groups may exhibit lower attrition due to higher demand, while off-peak groups may wash more aggressively.
Date and time when this wash factor record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, incremental ETL processing, and audit trail requirements in the Silver layer.

Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this wash factor configuration within the Revenue Management System (RMS). Used as the business key for integration with IDeaS G3 RMS and Infor EzRMS.
Human-readable descriptive name for this wash factor configuration (e.g., 'Corporate Group 30-Day Lead Time — High Season'). Used by revenue managers to identify and manage wash factor rules.

Difference between the adjusted wash percentage and the historical wash percentage (adjusted_wash_pct minus historical_wash_pct). Quantifies the degree of manual override applied by the revenue manager relative to historical norms. Positive values indicate a more conservative (higher attrition) assumption.

Unique surrogate identifier for each channel contribution record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this transactional summary entity. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete revenue contribution measurement event per distribution channel, property, and reporting period.

Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Channel performance reports must attribute bookings/revenue to the marketing campaigns that drove traffic to each channel. This is core marketing ROI measurement and enables campaign-level profitabili

Reference to the distribution channel master record (e.g., OTA, GDS, direct web, voice, corporate, wholesale). Used to join channel-level attributes such as channel name, partner agreement, and commission tier.

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Channel revenue and distribution costs are tracked by cost center for departmental P&L, channel profitability analysis, and USALI departmental reporting.

Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Channel revenue and distribution costs post to specific GL accounts for financial statement preparation, commission expense tracking, and revenue net of distribution reporting.

Foreign key linking to loyalty.member_segment. Business justification: Channel performance analysis by loyalty segment reveals segment-specific booking behavior and channel preferences. Business process: segment-level channel effectiveness reporting for optimizing distri
Reference to the market segment associated with bookings through this channel (e.g., transient leisure, corporate, group, wholesale). Supports revenue management segmentation analysis.

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Channel performance rolls up to profit centers for consolidated reporting, ownership reporting, and portfolio-level channel strategy evaluation.
Reference to the hotel or resort property for which channel contribution is being measured. Enables property-level channel mix analysis and OTA partnership management decisions.

Foreign key linking to revenue.revenue_rate_plan. Business justification: Channel contribution analysis is segmented by rate plan. channel_contribution has rate_plan_code (STRING) which should be normalized to FK to revenue_rate_plan. N channel_contribution records → 1 reve

Average Daily Rate achieved through this distribution channel during the reporting period, calculated as gross room revenue divided by rooms sold. Industry-standard KPI per STR STAR Report methodology. Enables channel-level ADR benchmarking.

Average number of days in advance that reservations were made through this channel (booking lead time). Measures booking pace by channel and informs dynamic pricing and rate restriction strategies in the Revenue Management System.

Average Length of Stay (ALOS) in nights for bookings originating from this distribution channel during the reporting period. Calculated as total room nights divided by booking volume. Informs LOS (Length of Stay) restriction strategies in the RMS.
Total number of confirmed reservations received through this distribution channel during the reporting period. Core metric for channel productivity analysis and OTA partnership evaluation.

The principal operational business date on which this channel contribution record was generated or posted, as defined by the Property Management System (PMS) business date. Distinct from audit timestamps; used for daily operations reporting (DOR) and period-end reconciliation.

Total number of reservations cancelled through this distribution channel during the reporting period. Used to calculate channel-level cancellation rate and assess OTA cancellation policy impact on revenue.

Percentage of total bookings through this channel that were cancelled during the reporting period (cancellation_count / booking_volume * 100). High cancellation rates from OTA channels impact revenue predictability and yield optimization.

Percentage of total property booking volume or room nights attributable to this distribution channel during the reporting period. Measures channel contribution to overall distribution mix. Used for channel optimization strategy and OTA dependency analysis.

Classification of the distribution channel through which bookings were made. OTA (Online Travel Agency) includes Expedia, Booking.com; GDS (Global Distribution System) includes Sabre, Amadeus; direct_web is brand.com; voice is call center; corporate is negotiated accounts; wholesale is tour operators and bed banks. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OTA|GDS|direct_web|voice|corporate|wholesale|metasearch|loyalty_redemption — promote to reference product]

Total commission or distribution fee paid to the channel partner (OTA, GDS, travel agent) for bookings during the reporting period. Deducted from gross revenue to derive net revenue. Critical for channel cost analysis and OTA contract management.

Average commission rate (as a percentage of gross revenue) paid to the channel partner during the reporting period. Calculated as commission_amount / gross_revenue * 100. Used to benchmark OTA commission costs and negotiate channel agreements.

Lifecycle status of the channel contribution record. 'draft' indicates preliminary data; 'confirmed' indicates validated and finalized; 'adjusted' indicates post-close corrections applied; 'closed' indicates period locked; 'cancelled' indicates record voided.

Total distribution cost per confirmed booking through this channel, including commissions, GDS transaction fees, and incremental booking costs. Also known as Cost Per Acquisition (CPA). Key metric for channel ROI analysis and OTA partnership management.

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations across global properties and ensures financial reporting compliance.

Timestamp when data was extracted from the source operational system (OPERA PMS, SynXis CRS, or IDeaS G3 RMS) for loading into the Databricks Silver Layer. Used for data freshness monitoring and SLA compliance tracking.

Total GDS (Global Distribution System) per-transaction fees incurred for bookings processed through GDS channels (Sabre, Amadeus, Travelport) during the reporting period. Applicable only to GDS channel type; zero for non-GDS channels. Included in total distribution cost.

Total gross room revenue generated through this distribution channel before deducting commissions, OTA fees, or GDS transaction fees. Represents the full rack or negotiated rate charged to guests. Used in ADR and RevPAR calculations per USALI standards.

Boolean flag indicating whether this is a direct booking channel (brand.com, loyalty app, call center, walk-in). Direct channels carry zero or minimal commission cost. Used to track direct booking share as a KPI for reducing OTA dependency.

business_glossary_term: Is Online Travel Agency (OTA) Channel Flag
description: Boolean flag indicating whether this distribution channel is an Online Travel Agency (OTA) such as Expedia or Booking.com. Enables quick segmentation of OTA vs. non-OTA channels for commission cost analysis and direct booking strategy reporting.

Number of bookings through this channel made by loyalty program members during the reporting period. Tracks loyalty member channel preference and supports direct booking incentive strategies to reduce OTA dependency.

Net room revenue retained by the property after deducting all channel commissions and distribution fees from gross revenue. Primary metric for evaluating true channel profitability and informing channel mix optimization decisions.

Total number of reservations from this channel where the guest did not arrive and did not cancel. No-shows impact revenue recovery and are tracked per channel to assess booking quality and enforce no-show policies.

Number of bookings made at Non-Refundable Rate (NRR) through this channel during the reporting period. NRR bookings provide revenue certainty and reduce cancellation risk. Used to assess channel-level rate plan mix.

Occupancy rate (OCC) attributable to this distribution channel, expressed as a percentage of available rooms occupied by channel-sourced bookings. Supports channel-level yield analysis and demand forecasting integration with IDeaS G3 RMS.
Timestamp when this channel contribution record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements per SOX and GDPR compliance.
Timestamp when this channel contribution record was last modified. Tracks adjustments, restatements, or corrections to channel revenue data. Required for SOX financial controls.

Time granularity at which this channel contribution record is aggregated. 'daily' for day-level DOR reporting; 'weekly' for pace analysis; 'monthly' for USALI period reporting; 'quarterly' for STR STAR Report benchmarking.
The last date of the reporting period (stay date range) for which channel contribution metrics are aggregated. Together with reporting_period_start_date defines the measurement window.
The first date of the reporting period (stay date range) for which channel contribution metrics are aggregated. Used to define the measurement window for ADR, RevPAR, and channel mix analysis.

business_glossary_term: Revenue Net of Total Distribution Cost
description: Net revenue after deducting all distribution costs (commissions, GDS fees, OTA fees) from gross revenue. Represents the true retained revenue contribution of the channel. Supports channel profitability ranking and optimization decisions.

Revenue Per Available Room contributed by this channel, calculated as channel gross revenue divided by total available rooms for the reporting period. Core yield optimization metric per STR STAR Report and USALI standards.

Revenue Generation Index (RGI) attributable to this channel, measuring the property's RevPAR performance relative to the competitive set for channel-sourced bookings. RGI > 1.0 indicates above-market performance. Sourced from STR STAR Report and Infor EzRMS.

Total number of room nights generated by bookings through this channel during the reporting period. A room night equals one room occupied for one night. Fundamental input for occupancy rate (OCC) and RevPAR calculations.

Total all-in cost of distributing inventory through this channel, including commissions, GDS transaction fees, OTA merchant model costs, and any incremental booking fees. Used to calculate net channel profitability and CPOR (Cost Per Occupied Room) by channel.
Primary key for performance_actuals

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: RevPAR and room revenue actuals are tracked by cost center for departmental P&L, USALI reporting, and operational performance management.

Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: Actuals are compared against financial budgets for variance reporting, board presentations, and management performance evaluation against approved financial plans.
Reference to the fiscal calendar period (month/quarter/year) to which this performance date belongs, enabling USALI-aligned financial period reporting and variance analysis against budget.

Foreign key linking to experience.gss_score. Business justification: Revenue performance reporting includes GSS scores as a KPI alongside financial metrics for balanced scorecards (SALT program requirement). Daily flash reports show RevPAR and GSS together—mandatory fo

Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Actual revenue performance posts to specific GL accounts for financial statement preparation, revenue recognition, and external financial reporting compliance.

Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: Daily actuals track outlet-level F&B revenue for TRevPAR calculation and USALI reporting. Required for total property revenue reporting, GOP analysis, outlet performance attribution in consolidated fi

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Property performance actuals roll up to profit centers for consolidated financial reporting, ownership reporting, and portfolio-level performance analysis.
Reference to the property for which this daily revenue performance record is captured. Links to the property master entity in the Property domain.

Foreign key linking to procurement.employee. Business justification: Finance/revenue analysts reconcile daily actuals; employee attribution required for data quality accountability, reconciliation workflow tracking, and performance evaluation of financial reporting acc

Average Daily Rate in the property's operating currency, calculated as total room revenue divided by rooms sold (excluding complimentary). Core KPI for STR STAR Report benchmarking, RGI, ARI, and MPI calculations.
Total ancillary revenue on the performance date from upsells, packages, resort fees, and add-on services not captured in departmental revenue lines. Supports total revenue and TRevPAR reporting.

Average Rate Index representing the property's ADR performance relative to its competitive set, sourced from STR STAR Report. ARI > 1.0 indicates the property is achieving a rate premium over its comp set.
Budgeted Average Daily Rate for the performance date. Used for ADR variance analysis against actuals in the DOR, revenue management reporting, and STR STAR Report benchmarking.
Budgeted occupancy rate for the performance date as a decimal (e.g., 0.7500 = 75.00%). Used for occupancy variance analysis against actuals in the DOR and revenue management reporting.
Budgeted room revenue for the performance date as approved in the annual operating budget. Used for variance analysis against actual room revenue in the DOR and ownership reporting.
Budgeted total property revenue for the performance date as approved in the annual operating budget. Used for total revenue variance analysis in the DOR and ownership reporting.
Number of rooms sold through direct channels (brand website, call center, walk-in) on the performance date. Supports direct vs. indirect channel mix analysis and cost-of-acquisition reporting.

business_glossary_term: Online Travel Agency (OTA) Channel Rooms Sold
description: Number of rooms sold through Online Travel Agency (OTA) channels (e.g., Expedia, Booking.com) on the performance date. Supports channel distribution mix analysis and OTA commission cost management.
Cost Per Occupied Room on the performance date, calculated as total rooms department operating expenses divided by rooms sold. Measures operational efficiency of the rooms department per USALI.
Timestamp when this performance actual record was first created in the data lakehouse Silver layer. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this performance record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Enables multi-currency property portfolio reporting and consolidation.

Forecasted number of rooms to be sold on the performance date as generated by the Revenue Management System (IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS). Used for variance analysis between forecast and actual rooms sold.

business_glossary_term: Earnings Before Interest Taxes Depreciation and Amortization (EBITDA) Contribution
description: Property-level EBITDA contribution on the performance date, representing operating earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization. Used for ownership reporting, asset valuation, and management contract performance measurement.

Total Food and Beverage revenue generated on the performance date across all F&B outlets (restaurants, bars, banquets, in-room dining). Sourced from Oracle Hospitality MICROS POS and reconciled against SAP S/4HANA. Included in TRevPAR calculation per USALI.

Gross Operating Profit on the performance date, calculated as total operated department revenue minus total operated department expenses per USALI. Key profitability metric for ownership reporting and management contracts.

business_glossary_term: Gross Operating Profit Per Available Room (GOPPAR)
description: Gross Operating Profit Per Available Room in the property's operating currency, calculated as GOP divided by rooms available. Measures overall property profitability efficiency. Core KPI for ownership reporting and management fee calculations.

Indicates whether this daily performance actual record has been reconciled between Oracle OPERA PMS and SAP S/4HANA General Ledger. True = reconciled and approved for reporting; False = pending reconciliation. Supports financial close and SOX controls.
Total miscellaneous and rental income on the performance date including rentals, commissions, and other non-departmental income per USALI non-operating income classification.

Market Penetration Index representing the property's occupancy performance relative to its competitive set, sourced from STR STAR Report. MPI > 1.0 indicates the property is capturing more than its fair share of market occupancy.

Daily occupancy rate expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.8250 = 82.50%), calculated as rooms sold divided by rooms available. Core KPI for the STR STAR Report, DOR, and ownership reporting. Stored as a raw ratio; display layer applies percentage formatting.

Total revenue from all other operated departments on the performance date (e.g., golf, retail, recreation, business center) excluding rooms, F&B, spa, and parking. Classified per USALI other operated departments schedule.
Total parking revenue generated on the performance date from valet, self-park, and garage operations. Classified under other operated departments per USALI and included in TRevPAR.

The calendar date (business date) for which this revenue performance actual record applies. Represents the stay date or operating day used in the Daily Operations Report (DOR) and all KPI calculations.

business_glossary_term: Prior Year Revenue Per Available Room (RevPAR)
description: RevPAR for the same calendar date in the prior year. Used for year-over-year (YOY) variance analysis in the DOR, STR STAR Report benchmarking, and ownership reporting.

Lifecycle status of this performance actual record indicating whether figures are preliminary (intra-day), final (end-of-day close), adjusted (post-close correction), or closed (period-locked). Supports audit and reconciliation workflows.

Revenue Per Available Room in the property's operating currency, calculated as ADR multiplied by occupancy rate (or total room revenue divided by rooms available). Primary rooms revenue KPI for ownership reporting, STR STAR Report, and RGI benchmarking.

Revenue Generation Index representing the property's RevPAR performance relative to its competitive set, sourced from STR STAR Report. RGI > 1.0 indicates the property is outperforming its comp set. Core competitive benchmarking KPI.

Total number of rooms available for sale on the performance date after deducting out-of-order (OOO) and out-of-service (OOS) rooms. Denominator for Occupancy Rate (OCC) and RevPAR calculations per USALI and STR STAR Report standards.

Number of rooms occupied on a complimentary (no-charge) basis on the performance date. Tracked separately per USALI for accurate ADR and revenue reporting, as complimentary rooms are excluded from ADR calculation.
Number of rooms designated as out-of-order (OOO) on the performance date due to maintenance, renovation, or damage. Excluded from rooms available per USALI inventory standards.
Total number of rooms occupied (sold) on the performance date, including complimentary rooms. Used to calculate Occupancy Rate (OCC) and Average Daily Rate (ADR) per USALI standards.
Number of rooms sold under negotiated contract rates (airline crew, long-stay corporate contracts) on the performance date. Supports contract segment revenue analysis and channel mix reporting.
Number of rooms sold to group accounts (MICE, corporate groups, tour groups) on the performance date. Supports group vs. transient mix analysis and group revenue contribution reporting per USALI.
Number of rooms sold to transient (individual/FIT) guests on the performance date. Supports segment-level revenue analysis and channel mix reporting per USALI market segmentation standards.

Identifies the operational system of record from which this performance actual record was sourced or reconciled (e.g., Oracle OPERA PMS for rooms data, SAP S/4HANA for financial totals). Supports data lineage and reconciliation audits.
Total spa and wellness revenue generated on the performance date. Included in total property revenue and TRevPAR calculation per USALI other operated departments classification.

Total gross revenue across all operated departments and income streams on the performance date (rooms + F&B + spa + parking + other operated departments + miscellaneous + ancillary). Denominator basis for TRevPAR. Reconciled against SAP S/4HANA GL per USALI.

Total gross room revenue generated on the performance date across all rate plans and channels, before deductions. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS and reconciled against SAP S/4HANA GL. Numerator for ADR and RevPAR calculations per USALI.

Total Revenue Per Available Room in the property's operating currency, calculated as total property revenue divided by rooms available. Measures total revenue productivity of the property beyond rooms. Core KPI for ownership and management reporting.
Timestamp when this performance actual record was last modified, including post-close adjustments or reconciliation corrections. Supports audit trail and change tracking.

Reference to the revenue manager or analyst responsible for reviewing and acting on this pickup report. Links to the workforce/employee master. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER PARTY_REFERENCE requirement — the accountable internal party for this revenue management snapshot.

Foreign key linking to event.event_booking. Business justification: Pickup velocity reports track room block pickup rates for specific event bookings against cutoff dates. Essential for group pace reporting, attrition risk assessment, and event booking performance mon

Reference to the property (hotel, resort, or vacation property) for which this pickup report is generated. Links to the property master record in the Property domain.

Average Daily Rate (ADR) of all rooms currently on books for the stay_date as of the report_date. Calculated as total room revenue on books divided by rooms_on_books. Core revenue performance indicator alongside occupancy pace. Expressed in the property's operating currency.

business_glossary_term: Average Daily Rate (ADR) Variance to Forecast
description: Difference between adr_on_books and forecasted_adr (adr_on_books minus forecasted_adr). Positive value indicates rate is tracking above forecast; negative value indicates rate is below forecast. Used alongside rooms_variance_to_forecast to assess overall revenue pace.

Distribution channel through which the on-books reservations were sourced for this pickup report scope (e.g., DIRECT, OTA, GDS, VOICE, BRAND_WEB, WHOLESALE). When null, the report covers all channels. Enables channel-level pace analysis to assess OTA (Online Travel Agency) vs direct booking mix. Timestamp when this pickup report record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED requirement.

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values in this pickup report record (adr_on_books, forecasted_adr, revpar_on_books, forecasted_revpar, prior_year_adr, adr_variance_to_forecast). Typically the property's local operating currency (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).

Day of the week corresponding to the stay_date (e.g., Monday, Friday, Saturday).
