Revenue management system classification of expected demand intensity for the stay_date as of the report_date. Drives pricing strategy and inventory control decisions: 'distressed' triggers promotional actions; 'peak' triggers rate maximization and LOS (Length of Stay) restrictions.

Revenue management system forecast for the expected ADR (Average Daily Rate) on the stay_date, as generated by IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS. Baseline against which adr_on_books is compared to assess rate performance vs plan.

Revenue management system forecast for RevPAR (Revenue Per Available Room) on the stay_date. Baseline against which revpar_on_books is compared to assess overall revenue pace vs plan. Expressed in the property's operating currency.

Revenue management system forecast for total rooms expected to be occupied on the stay_date, as generated by IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS at the time of this report snapshot. Baseline against which rooms_on_books pace is measured to compute variance.

Indicates whether the stay_date coincides with a designated special event (conference, concert, sporting event, holiday, local festival) that materially impacts demand. When true, pace expectations and pricing strategies are adjusted relative to normal demand patterns.

Indicates whether the stay_date falls on a weekend (Saturday or Sunday). Boolean flag used to segment pickup reports into weekday vs weekend cohorts for pace analysis, as weekend demand patterns and pricing strategies differ materially from weekday.

Number of days between the report_date and the stay_date (stay_date minus report_date). Represents the booking window or days-out at the time of this snapshot. Critical for pace analysis — revenue managers compare pickup velocity across standard lead-time intervals (90, 60, 30, 14, 7, 1 days out).

business_glossary_term: Length of Stay (LOS) Restriction Active Flag
description: Indicates whether a LOS (Length of Stay) restriction (minimum stay requirement) is currently active for the stay_date. LOS restrictions are a key revenue management lever applied during high-demand periods to maximize total revenue across multiple nights.

Minimum number of nights a guest must stay to book the stay_date, when a LOS restriction is active. Null when los_restriction_active is false. Used to enforce stay-through requirements during high-demand periods to prevent short-stay displacement of longer, higher-value bookings.

Free-text annotations entered by the revenue manager to document observations, strategy decisions, or contextual factors affecting the pickup pace for this stay_date (e.g., 'Rate opened on OTA due to slow pace', 'Group block released — expect transient pickup'). Supports audit trail and strategy documentation.

Rate of new reservations being added per day for the stay_date, calculated as the incremental rooms picked up divided by the number of days elapsed in the measurement window. Expressed as rooms per day. Enables revenue managers to assess acceleration or deceleration of booking pace.

ADR (Average Daily Rate) on books for the equivalent stay_date in the prior year, at the same lead time. Enables year-over-year rate comparison to assess whether current pricing strategy is yielding rate improvement or erosion versus the prior period.

Number of rooms that were on books for the equivalent stay_date in the prior year, at the same lead time (days out). Enables year-over-year (YOY) pace comparison — a standard revenue management benchmarking technique to contextualize current booking pace against historical performance.

Rate plan identifier for which this pickup report is scoped (e.g., BAR, LRA, NRR, CORP, PKG). When null, the report covers all rate plans. Enables rate-plan-level pace analysis to assess BAR (Best Available Rate) and NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) booking velocity.

The calendar date on which this pickup snapshot was captured. Represents the 'as-of' date for all rooms-on-books and ADR figures in this record. The difference between stay_date and report_date defines the lead time (days out). Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP requirement (date-precision is standard for daily pickup reports).

Externally-known business identifier for this pickup report record, typically formatted as PKP-{PropertyCode}-{StayDate}-{SequenceNumber}. Used by revenue managers to reference specific pickup snapshots in IDeaS G3 RMS and Infor EzRMS reporting. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER requirement.

Current lifecycle state of this pickup report record. 'draft' indicates the snapshot is being assembled; 'published' indicates it is available for revenue manager consumption; 'superseded' indicates a later snapshot for the same stay_date/report_date exists; 'cancelled' indicates the report was voided. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER LIFECYCLE_STATUS requirement.

Classification of the pickup report generation cadence: 'daily' for standard automated daily snapshots, 'weekly' for weekly pace summaries, 'monthly' for month-end pace reviews, or 'adhoc' for analyst-triggered on-demand reports. Determines the reporting frequency context for pace trend analysis.

business_glossary_term: Revenue Per Available Room (RevPAR) on Books
description: Revenue Per Available Room (RevPAR) derived from current on-books position, calculated as (adr_on_books multiplied by rooms_on_books) divided by total_available_rooms. Composite pace metric combining rate and occupancy performance. Expressed in the property's operating currency.

Room type classification for which this pickup report is scoped (e.g., STD, DLX, STE, KNG, DBL). When null, the report covers all room types for the property. Enables room-type-level pace analysis to support inventory controls and upsell strategy.

Total number of rooms confirmed on books for the stay_date as of the report_date. This is the primary pace indicator — the current committed inventory position. Revenue managers compare this against forecast and prior-year actuals to assess demand trajectory.

business_glossary_term: Rooms on Books at 14 Days Out
description: Number of rooms that were on books for the stay_date when the lead time was 14 days (i.e., the snapshot captured 14 days prior to stay_date). Critical short-window pace indicator used to trigger last-minute pricing tactics and inventory controls.

business_glossary_term: Rooms on Books at 1 Day Out
description: Number of rooms that were on books for the stay_date when the lead time was 1 day (i.e., the snapshot captured 1 day prior to stay_date). Final pre-arrival pace indicator representing near-final committed occupancy before day-of walk-ins.

business_glossary_term: Rooms on Books at 30 Days Out
description: Number of rooms that were on books for the stay_date when the lead time was 30 days (i.e., the snapshot captured 30 days prior to stay_date). Key pace benchmark for near-term demand assessment and rate strategy adjustments.

business_glossary_term: Rooms on Books at 60 Days Out
description: Number of rooms that were on books for the stay_date when the lead time was 60 days (i.e., the snapshot captured 60 days prior to stay_date). Standard pace benchmark used by revenue managers to assess mid-range booking momentum.

business_glossary_term: Rooms on Books at 7 Days Out
description: Number of rooms that were on books for the stay_date when the lead time was 7 days (i.e., the snapshot captured 7 days prior to stay_date). Near-arrival pace indicator used to assess last-week demand and trigger promotional or restriction actions.

business_glossary_term: Rooms on Books at 90 Days Out
description: Number of rooms that were on books for the stay_date when the lead time was 90 days (i.e., the snapshot captured 90 days prior to stay_date). Used as a historical pace benchmark for comparison against current rooms_on_books to measure pickup velocity over the 90-day window.

Difference between rooms_on_books and forecasted_rooms (rooms_on_books minus forecasted_rooms). Positive value indicates ahead-of-pace (over-performing vs forecast); negative value indicates behind-pace (under-performing). Primary KPI for revenue manager intervention decisions.

Name or description of the special event associated with the stay_date when is_special_event is true (e.g., 'Super Bowl Weekend', 'Annual Tech Conference', 'New Year's Eve'). Provides context for anomalous pace patterns in revenue management reporting.

The specific calendar date for which booking pace and pickup velocity are being measured. This is the future arrival/occupancy date that revenue managers are monitoring. Central dimension of the pickup report — all rooms-on-books and ADR snapshots relate to this date.

Total number of rooms available for sale for the stay_date at the property (net of out-of-order and out-of-service rooms). Denominator for occupancy rate (OCC) calculation and RevPAR computation. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS inventory controls.
Timestamp when this pickup report record was last modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED requirement.
Unique surrogate identifier for each daily total revenue actuals record at the property level. Primary key for this Silver Layer data product.

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Total revenue actuals are tracked by cost center for USALI departmental reporting, manager accountability, and departmental P&L preparation.

Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: Total revenue actuals are compared against financial budgets for variance analysis, board reporting, and management performance evaluation against approved plans.
Reference to the fiscal calendar period (month/quarter/year) under which this stay date falls, enabling USALI-aligned financial reporting in SAP S/4HANA.

Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Total revenue actuals post to GL accounts for financial statement preparation, revenue recognition, and external financial reporting compliance.

Foreign key linking to fnb.fnb_outlet. Business justification: Total revenue actuals aggregate outlet-level F&B for TRevPAR calculation and USALI reporting. Required for financial consolidation, total property performance reporting, outlet contribution analysis t

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Total revenue actuals roll up to profit centers for consolidated financial statements, ownership reporting, and portfolio-level performance analysis.
Reference to the property for which total revenue actuals are recorded. Aligns with Oracle OPERA PMS property configuration.

Foreign key linking to procurement.employee. Business justification: Finance analysts reconcile total property revenue; employee attribution required for data quality accountability, GL posting workflow tracking, and performance evaluation of financial reporting accura

Indicates whether this revenue actuals record contains a post-close adjustment or restatement to previously posted figures. When true, the adjustment_reason field provides context. Supports SOX audit trail requirements.

Free-text description of the reason for any post-close adjustment or restatement applied to this revenue actuals record. Populated only when adjustment_flag is true. Required for SOX audit trail and USALI financial controls.

Average Daily Rate for the stay date, calculated as rooms revenue divided by rooms sold. Core KPI for revenue performance benchmarking against STR STAR Report and competitive set. Sourced from IDeaS G3 RMS daily actuals feed.

Average Rate Index for the stay date, representing the property's ADR divided by the competitive set's ADR. Measures the property's pricing performance relative to its competitive set. Sourced from STR STAR Report.
Number of rooms occupied on a complimentary basis (zero-rated) on the stay date. Tracked separately per USALI and STR reporting standards to distinguish from revenue-generating occupied rooms.

Total operating cost per occupied room for the stay date, calculated as total departmental operating expenses divided by rooms sold. Key efficiency metric per USALI and AHLA benchmarking standards. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA cost accounting.
Timestamp when this revenue actuals record was first created in the Silver Layer data product, typically following the nightly ETL load from Oracle OPERA PMS and SAP S/4HANA.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all monetary amounts on this record are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency property portfolios.

Day of the week corresponding to the stay date. Used for day-of-week revenue pattern analysis, demand segmentation, and yield optimization in IDeaS G3 RMS and Infor EzRMS. business_glossary_term: Earnings Before Interest Taxes Depreciation and Amortization (EBITDA) Contribution
description: EBITDA contribution for the stay date at the property level, representing GOP less management fees and other fixed charges but before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA. Required for SOX financial controls and IFRS/GAAP reporting.

Total net Food and Beverage revenue for the stay date, aggregated across all F&B outlets (restaurant, bar, banquet, in-room dining) sourced from Oracle Hospitality MICROS POS and Delphi by Amadeus. Aligns with USALI Food and Beverage Department.

Date on which the revenue figures were posted to the General Ledger in SAP S/4HANA. May differ from stay date due to night audit timing or period-end adjustments. Required for USALI and SOX financial controls.

Gross Operating Profit for the stay date, representing total revenue minus total departmental and undistributed operating expenses, before fixed charges. Core USALI profitability metric sourced from SAP S/4HANA GL.

business_glossary_term: Gross Operating Profit Per Available Room (GOPPAR)
description: Gross Operating Profit Per Available Room for the stay date, calculated as GOP divided by rooms available. Measures overall property profitability efficiency per available room. Key executive KPI per USALI and AHLA standards.
Number of rooms occupied for internal hotel use (staff, management) on the stay date. Excluded from revenue-generating occupancy per USALI and STR standards.
Indicates whether the stay date falls on a public or bank holiday in the property's market. Used for holiday demand uplift analysis and dynamic pricing strategy in IDeaS G3 RMS.

Indicates whether the stay date coincides with a special event (e.g., convention, concert, sporting event) that materially impacts demand and pricing. Used in IDeaS G3 RMS demand forecasting and revenue strategy.
Indicates whether the stay date falls on a weekend (Friday or Saturday night per hospitality convention). Used for weekend vs. weekday revenue segmentation and pricing strategy analysis.
Total net miscellaneous and non-operating income for the stay date, including rental income, cancellation fees, and other non-departmental revenue lines per USALI Non-Operating Income and Expenses.

Market Penetration Index for the stay date, representing the property's occupancy rate divided by the competitive set's occupancy rate. Measures the property's fair share of market demand. Sourced from STR STAR Report.

Occupancy percentage for the stay date, calculated as rooms sold divided by rooms available, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.8500 = 85%). Core KPI for yield optimization and STR STAR Report benchmarking.

Total net revenue from all other operated departments (e.g., golf, retail, recreation, business center) for the stay date, excluding rooms, F&B, spa, and parking. Aligns with USALI Other Operated Departments aggregate line.
Number of rooms designated as out-of-order (OOO) on the stay date due to maintenance, renovation, or damage. Deducted from available room count per STR and USALI standards.
Total net parking and valet revenue for the stay date, classified under USALI Other Operated Departments.

Lifecycle status of the revenue actuals record indicating whether figures are preliminary, posted to the general ledger, adjusted, period-closed, or voided. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA GL posting workflow.

Brand or service tier segment of the property for this revenue record (e.g., luxury, premium, select-service). Enables cross-segment revenue performance benchmarking and portfolio analytics aligned with AHLA and STR segmentation standards.

Revenue Per Available Room for the stay date, calculated as rooms revenue divided by rooms available (or ADR multiplied by occupancy rate). Primary yield optimization metric tracked in IDeaS G3 RMS and benchmarked via STR STAR Report.

Revenue Generation Index for the stay date, representing the property's RevPAR divided by the competitive set's RevPAR. An RGI above 1.0 indicates the property is outperforming its competitive set. Sourced from STR STAR Report and Infor EzRMS.

Total number of rooms available for sale on the stay date after deducting out-of-order and out-of-service rooms. Denominator for RevPAR and TRevPAR calculations. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS inventory.

Total net rooms revenue for the stay date at the property, sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS cashiering. Represents the primary revenue line under USALI Rooms Department. Used as the numerator in ADR and RevPAR calculations.

Budgeted rooms revenue for the stay date as set in the annual operating plan. Enables variance analysis between actual and budgeted rooms revenue performance. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA budgeting module.

Total number of rooms occupied (sold) on the stay date, excluding complimentary and house-use rooms unless otherwise configured. Used to compute ADR and Occupancy Rate (OCC). Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS.

Native record identifier from the originating source system (e.g., OPERA PMS daily revenue summary ID or SAP S/4HANA document number). Enables traceability and reconciliation back to the system of record.
Total net spa and wellness revenue for the stay date, classified under USALI Other Operated Departments. Includes treatments, retail, and membership fees.

The calendar date for which revenue actuals are reported. Represents the business date (night) in Oracle OPERA PMS, used as the primary time dimension for ADR, RevPAR, TRevPAR, and GOPPAR calculations.

Total departmental and undistributed operating expenses for the stay date, including labor, cost of goods sold, and other direct costs across all departments. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA cost accounting. Used to derive GOP.

Total net revenue across all revenue streams (rooms + F&B + spa + parking + other operated departments + miscellaneous income) for the stay date. Primary metric for TRevPAR calculation. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA daily revenue close.

Budgeted total revenue for the stay date across all departments as set in the annual operating plan. Enables variance analysis between actual and budgeted total revenue. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA budgeting module.

Total Revenue Per Available Room for the stay date, calculated as total revenue divided by rooms available. Captures full revenue contribution across all departments beyond rooms. Key metric for holistic revenue strategy.
Timestamp when this revenue actuals record was last modified in the Silver Layer, reflecting any adjustments, restatements, or corrections applied after initial load.
Unique surrogate identifier for a Dynamic Rate Rule (DRR) record in the revenue management system. Primary key for this entity.

Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Dynamic pricing rules are often triggered by marketing campaigns (e.g., increase rates during high-awareness periods). Revenue managers need campaign context to evaluate rule performance and campaign-

Identifier of the competitive set (comp set) used as the benchmark for competitive-based Dynamic Rate Rules. References the STR STAR Report comp set configuration in Infor EzRMS for RGI, MPI, and ARI-driven pricing decisions.

Foreign key linking to procurement.employee. Business justification: Revenue managers configure dynamic pricing rules; employee attribution required for approval workflow, rule performance evaluation, and audit trail of automated pricing strategy decisions in RMS opera
Identifier of the demand forecast record from IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS that this forecast-based Dynamic Rate Rule references for its trigger evaluation.

Foreign key linking to channel.channel. Business justification: Dynamic pricing rules target specific channels differently (e.g., OTA rates adjust based on commission costs, direct channel gets best rate). RMS systems apply channel-specific rate optimization strat

Identifier of the local demand event (e.g., MICE event, conference, festival, sporting event) that triggers this event-based Dynamic Rate Rule. Links to the event master record in Delphi by Amadeus or the events domain.

Foreign key linking to experience.gss_score. Business justification: Dynamic pricing rules incorporate guest satisfaction scores to adjust rates for properties with declining NPS/GSS (reputation-based yield management). Properties below GSS thresholds may trigger rate

Foreign key linking to guest.segment. Business justification: Dynamic pricing rules target specific guest segments for personalized rate adjustments. Revenue management systems apply segment-specific pricing logic (e.g., loyalty tier discounts, corporate negotia

Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: Dynamic pricing rules adjust rates based on member tier for personalized revenue optimization. Business process: applying tier-specific rate adjustments in revenue management systems to reward high-va

Foreign key linking to revenue.market_segment. Business justification: Dynamic rate rules can be scoped to specific market segments. dynamic_rate_rule has market_segment_code (STRING) which should be normalized to FK. N DRR records → 1 market_segment. Removes redundant m
Identifier of the hotel or resort property to which this Dynamic Rate Rule applies. Links to the property master record in Oracle OPERA PMS.
Identifier of the rate plan (e.g., BAR, LRA, NRR) to which this Dynamic Rate Rule applies. A null value indicates the rule applies across all eligible rate plans for the property.
Identifier of the specific room type (e.g., standard king, deluxe suite) to which this Dynamic Rate Rule applies. A null value indicates the rule applies across all room types at the property.

Foreign key linking to loyalty.member. Business justification: Dynamic rules can target specific high-value members for personalized pricing strategies. Business process: VIP member-specific rate rules for retention and revenue optimization, common in luxury hosp

Indicates whether the Dynamic Rate Rule increases or decreases the rate when triggered. Provides explicit directional intent separate from the sign of adjustment_value to prevent ambiguity in RMS configuration.

Method by which the Dynamic Rate Rule adjusts the published rate when triggered. Percentage applies a relative increase or decrease to the current rate; absolute_amount adds or subtracts a fixed currency value; bar_offset moves the rate a fixed amount above or below the Best Available Rate (BAR); bar_multiplier applies a multiplier to the BAR.

The magnitude of the rate adjustment applied when the Dynamic Rate Rule is triggered. Interpreted according to adjustment_type: a value of 0.10 with type 'percentage' means a 10% rate increase; a value of 25.00 with type 'absolute_amount' means a $25 rate increase; a value of -15.00 with type 'bar_offset' means $15 below BAR.
Maximum number of days in advance of the arrival date within which a booking must be made for this Dynamic Rate Rule to be eligible to apply. Null indicates no upper advance booking restriction.
Minimum number of days in advance of the arrival date that a booking must be made for this Dynamic Rate Rule to be eligible to apply. Supports advance purchase pricing strategies.
Workflow approval state of the Dynamic Rate Rule indicating whether it has been reviewed and authorized by a revenue manager before activation. Supports SOX-compliant pricing governance controls.
Name or employee identifier of the revenue manager or director who approved this Dynamic Rate Rule for activation. Required for SOX financial controls audit trail on pricing decisions.
Date and time when this Dynamic Rate Rule was approved for activation by the authorized revenue manager. Part of the pricing governance audit trail required under SOX financial controls.
Date and time when this Dynamic Rate Rule record was first created in the system. Part of the standard audit trail for pricing configuration governance.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the property in which rate floor, ceiling, and absolute adjustment values are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).

Seven-character binary string (positions 1–7 = Mon–Sun) indicating which days of the week this Dynamic Rate Rule is eligible to fire. A value of '1111100' means Monday through Friday only. Enables day-of-week yield optimization strategies.
The calendar date from which this Dynamic Rate Rule becomes active and eligible to trigger automated pricing adjustments in the RMS. Aligns with the stay date range for yield optimization.
The calendar date on which this Dynamic Rate Rule expires and ceases to trigger automated pricing adjustments. Null indicates an open-ended rule with no defined expiry.

Indicates whether this Dynamic Rate Rule can be combined (stacked) with other active rules for the same stay date, room type, and rate plan. When False, this rule takes exclusive precedence based on rule_priority and no other rules are applied concurrently.

Date and time when this Dynamic Rate Rule was most recently evaluated as true and triggered an automated pricing adjustment in the RMS. Used by revenue managers to monitor rule activity and effectiveness.
Maximum Length of Stay (LOS) restriction in nights beyond which this Dynamic Rate Rule does not apply. Null indicates no upper LOS restriction.
Minimum Length of Stay (LOS) restriction in nights that must be satisfied for this Dynamic Rate Rule to apply. Used in LOS-based yield optimization to drive longer stays during high-demand periods.

The highest permissible rate (in property currency) that this Dynamic Rate Rule may produce after adjustment. Acts as a pricing guardrail to prevent rates from exceeding the property's maximum acceptable rate cap. Sensitive pricing strategy data.

The lowest permissible rate (in property currency) that this Dynamic Rate Rule may produce after adjustment. Acts as a pricing guardrail to prevent rates from falling below the property's minimum acceptable ADR threshold. Sensitive pricing strategy data.
Number of days in the booking pickup window used to evaluate pickup velocity for pickup-based Dynamic Rate Rules. For example, a value of 7 means the rule evaluates rooms booked in the last 7 days.

business_glossary_term: Revenue Management System (RMS) Rule Reference ID
description: Native rule identifier assigned by the source Revenue Management System (IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS) for this Dynamic Rate Rule. Used for system-of-record traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver layer and the operational RMS.

Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the Dynamic Rate Rule within the Revenue Management System (RMS). Used for cross-system reference between IDeaS G3 RMS, Infor EzRMS, and Sabre SynXis CRS.
Detailed narrative description of the Dynamic Rate Rule's business purpose, pricing logic, and intended yield optimization outcome.
Human-readable name describing the Dynamic Rate Rule, used by revenue managers to identify and manage pricing strategies within the RMS dashboard.

Numeric priority ranking that determines the order of evaluation when multiple Dynamic Rate Rules are eligible to fire simultaneously for the same property, room type, and rate plan. Lower numeric values indicate higher priority (e.g., 1 = highest priority).

Current lifecycle state of the Dynamic Rate Rule governing whether it is eligible to trigger automated pricing adjustments. Active rules are evaluated by the RMS engine; draft rules are under configuration; suspended rules are temporarily disabled; archived rules are retained for historical audit.

Classification of the Dynamic Rate Rule by the trigger mechanism driving automated pricing adjustments. Occupancy-based rules fire on OCC thresholds; pickup-based on booking velocity; competitive-based on RGI/ARI/MPI benchmarks from STR STAR Report; event-based on MICE or local demand events; LOS-based on Length of Stay (LOS) restrictions; forecast-based on RMS demand forecasts. Cumulative number of times this Dynamic Rate Rule has been triggered and applied a pricing adjustment since its activation date. Used to assess rule utilization and yield optimization effectiveness.

Comparison operator applied to the trigger threshold value to determine whether the rule condition is met. For example, 'greater_than' with a threshold of 0.80 means the rule fires when OCC exceeds 80%.

Unit of measure for the trigger threshold value. Percentage applies to OCC-based rules; room_count applies to pickup-based rules; index_value applies to competitive benchmarking rules (RGI, MPI, ARI); currency_amount applies to ADR-based triggers; nights applies to LOS-based rules.

The numeric threshold at which this Dynamic Rate Rule fires. For occupancy-based rules, this is the OCC percentage (e.g., 0.80 = 80%). For pickup-based rules, this is the number of rooms picked up within the pickup window. For competitive-based rules, this is the RGI or ARI index value.
Date and time when this Dynamic Rate Rule record was most recently modified. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer ETL pipeline.
Incremental version counter tracking the number of times this Dynamic Rate Rule configuration has been modified. Supports audit trail and change management for pricing strategy governance.
Unique surrogate identifier for each manual pricing override record in the revenue management system. Primary key for the pricing_override data product.

Reference to the employee (typically a Director of Revenue Management or VP Revenue) who formally authorized the pricing override. May differ from revenue_manager_id when a second-level approval is required for large rate variances.

Reference to a specific event, conference, or MICE (Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, Exhibitions) event that triggered the pricing override when override_reason_code is 'event_demand'. Links to the event master record in Delphi by Amadeus.

Reference to the revenue manager employee who applied or approved the pricing override. Used for accountability tracking and override frequency analysis per individual manager.
Reference to the hotel or resort property for which the pricing override was applied. Links to the property master record in Oracle OPERA PMS.

Reference to the specific room type (e.g., King Deluxe, Suite, Standard Double) to which the pricing override applies. Sourced from Oracle OPERA PMS room type configuration.

Indicates whether this pricing override required formal approval before activation, based on the rate variance threshold or property-level governance policy. True = approval workflow triggered; False = auto-approved within tolerance.

The rate variance percentage threshold (as a decimal, e.g., 0.2000 = 20%) that triggered the approval requirement for this override. Captures the governance rule in effect at the time of submission for audit purposes.
The date and time when the pricing override was formally approved by the authorizing revenue manager or director. Null if override is still pending or was reversed without approval.

Specifies which distribution channels the overridden rate is published to. 'all_channels' = no restriction; 'direct_only' = hotel direct and brand website only; 'ota_excluded' = all channels except Online Travel Agencies (OTA); 'gds_only' = Global Distribution System (GDS) only; 'brand_website_only' = brand.com only.

Indicates whether the overridden rate includes a Closed to Arrival (CTA) restriction, preventing new reservations with an arrival date falling within the override effective window. True = CTA restriction active; False = arrivals permitted.

Indicates whether the overridden rate includes a Closed to Departure (CTD) restriction, preventing check-outs on specific dates within the override window. True = CTD restriction active; False = departures permitted.

The competitor or market reference rate (in property local currency) that informed the override decision, particularly when override_reason_code is 'competitive_pressure'. Sourced from STR STAR Report, OTA rate shopping, or RMS competitive benchmarking module.

The first calendar date on which the pricing override rate becomes active and is applied to reservations. Defines the start of the override window in the property's local time zone.

The last calendar date on which the pricing override rate remains active. After this date, the override status transitions to 'expired' and the RMS-recommended rate is reinstated. Nullable for open-ended overrides.
Number of room inventory units to which the pricing override applies. Null or zero indicates the override applies to all available inventory for the specified room type and date range.

Indicates whether this override specifically targets the Best Available Rate (BAR) — the lowest publicly available rate for a given date. True = BAR is being overridden; False = override applies to a non-BAR rate plan.
Maximum number of nights a guest may stay under the overridden rate. Null if no maximum LOS restriction is applied. Used in conjunction with los_restriction_min to define the eligible stay window.

The published full undiscounted rate (rack rate) for this room type and stay date. Serves as the reference rate for discount calculations, loyalty redemption valuations, and walk-in pricing. Typically the highest rate tier and the baseline for USALI revenue reporting.

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the base_rate_amount and all monetary fields on this rate plan are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency property portfolios.

Percentage discount applied to the BAR (Best Available Rate) or rack rate to derive this rate plan's selling price. Used for corporate negotiated rates, loyalty member rates, and promotional rates. A value of 0 indicates no discount from the reference rate.

Indicates whether this rate plan is subject to DRR (Dynamic Rate Rule) adjustments by the revenue management system. When true, IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS may automatically adjust the rate based on demand forecasts, occupancy levels, and competitive benchmarking.

The calendar date from which this rate plan becomes active and bookable. Used by IDeaS G3 RMS and Sabre SynXis CRS to control rate availability windows. Satisfies MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_FROM category.

The calendar date after which this rate plan is no longer available for new bookings. Null indicates an open-ended rate plan with no defined end date. Satisfies MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_UNTIL category.

Indicates whether this rate plan is distributed through GDS (Global Distribution System) channels such as Amadeus, Sabre, Travelport. Controls rate visibility to travel agents using GDS booking tools.
Indicates whether a travel agent or OTA (Online Travel Agency) commission is payable on bookings made under this rate plan. Drives commission accrual and payment processing in SAP S/4HANA AP.

Indicates whether this rate plan carries LRA (Last Room Availability) obligation, meaning the contracted rate must be honored even when only the last room in a category is available. Applicable to corporate negotiated rate contracts.

Indicates whether bookings under this rate plan are eligible for a full refund upon cancellation within the policy window. False for NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) plans; true for flexible BAR (Best Available Rate) plans.

The timestamp when this rate plan record was most recently updated in the source system. Used to detect changes for incremental ETL processing and audit compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Indicates whether stays booked under this rate plan qualify for loyalty program points accrual. Wholesale, opaque, and certain promotional rates are typically ineligible for points earning.

Minimum loyalty program membership tier required to access this rate plan. A value of 'none' indicates the rate is open to all guests regardless of loyalty status. Used to gate exclusive member rates.

Alphanumeric code identifying the market segment this rate plan targets (e.g., TRANSIENT, GROUP, CONTRACT, WHOLESALE). Aligns with STR STAR Report segmentation and USALI market segment definitions for revenue performance benchmarking.
Maximum number of nights permitted under this rate plan. Used to prevent extended stays at promotional or deeply discounted rates. A null value indicates no maximum stay restriction.

Indicates the F&B (Food and Beverage) inclusions bundled with this rate plan. Room only includes no meals; bed and breakfast includes morning meal; half board includes breakfast and dinner; full board includes all three meals; all-inclusive covers meals, beverages, and selected activities.

Minimum number of nights a guest must book to qualify for this rate plan. A LOS (Length of Stay) restriction used in yield optimization to drive longer stays during high-demand periods. A value of 1 indicates no minimum stay restriction.
Indicates whether this rate plan is distributed through OTA (Online Travel Agency) channels such as Booking.com, Expedia, or Agoda. Controls rate visibility on third-party booking platforms.

Secondary classification grouping rate plans by the market segment or distribution channel they serve. Public rates are available to all guests; negotiated rates are contracted with specific accounts; wholesale rates are sold through tour operators; group rates apply to block bookings; loyalty rates are exclusive to program members; employee rates are internal staff benefits.
The maximum rate above which this rate plan cannot be sold, used to cap pricing during peak demand periods for contracted or promotional rates. Null indicates no ceiling constraint.

The minimum rate below which this rate plan cannot be sold, regardless of dynamic pricing adjustments. Protects rate integrity and brand positioning. Used by IDeaS G3 RMS as a hard constraint in yield optimization algorithms.

Indicates the organizational scope at which this rate plan is defined and managed. Property-level plans apply to a single hotel; brand-level plans apply across a brand portfolio; chain-level plans apply across the entire company; global plans may span international markets.

Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the rate plan across systems (e.g., BAR, CORP001, PKG-SPA). Used as the business key in IDeaS G3 RMS, Sabre SynXis CRS, and Oracle OPERA PMS for rate plan lookup and distribution. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.

Free-text description of the rate plan's terms, inclusions, and conditions as displayed to guests and booking agents. Includes details on what is included in the rate, any special conditions, and marketing messaging.

Human-readable name of the rate plan displayed to guests and revenue managers (e.g., 'Best Available Rate', 'AAA Member Rate', 'Advance Purchase 21-Day'). Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE IDENTITY_LABEL category.

Classification of the rate plan by its commercial structure. BAR (Best Available Rate) is the publicly available flexible rate; LRA (Last Room Availability) guarantees availability to contracted accounts; NRR (Non-Refundable Rate) carries no cancellation flexibility; corporate rates are negotiated with companies; package rates bundle room with ancillary services; promotional rates are time-limited offers. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE category.

Current lifecycle state of the rate plan. Active plans are available for booking; inactive plans are temporarily disabled; draft plans are under configuration; suspended plans are paused due to yield or compliance review; archived plans are retired and no longer bookable. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.
Identifies the operational system of record from which this rate plan record was sourced. Enables data lineage tracking and conflict resolution when the same rate plan exists in multiple systems.

The native identifier of this rate plan in the originating operational system (IDeaS G3 RMS, Sabre SynXis CRS, or Oracle OPERA PMS). Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse Silver layer and the system of record.

Defines any day-of-week or date-specific restrictions on when stays under this rate plan are permitted. Weekday-only and weekend-only restrictions are common for corporate and leisure rates respectively; blackout dates exclude specific high-demand periods.

Indicates whether the base_rate_amount includes applicable taxes (VAT, occupancy tax, city tax). True for tax-inclusive markets; false for markets where taxes are added at checkout. Critical for accurate revenue recognition under USALI and IFRS/GAAP.
Unique surrogate identifier for each revenue budget record. Primary key for the revenue_budget data product in the Silver Layer lakehouse.

Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Annual revenue budgets allocate expected revenue by campaign (e.g., Q2 leisure campaign expected to drive $500K room revenue). Finance needs this link for budget-vs-actual reporting and campaign ROI v

Identifier for the STR (Smith Travel Research) competitive set used as the benchmarking reference for RGI, MPI, and ARI targets in this budget. Links to the STR STAR Report competitive set definition.

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Revenue budgets are allocated to cost centers for departmental planning, manager accountability, and USALI-compliant departmental budget reporting.
Reference to the revenue director or senior leader who formally approved this budget record. Supports governance, audit trail, and owner presentation accountability.

Foreign key linking to event.event_booking. Business justification: Budget planning incorporates known definite event bookings as committed revenue for fiscal period forecasting. Required for budget vs actual variance analysis with event segmentation and MICE revenue

Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: Revenue budgets are components of the overall financial budget and must reconcile for consolidated budget approval, owner reporting, and financial planning.

Foreign key linking to experience.gss_score. Business justification: Annual revenue budgets set GSS score targets alongside financial targets (SALT program requirements). Budget submissions must include target GSS by period—mandatory for brand compliance. Revenue and e
Reference to the market segment (e.g., transient leisure, transient corporate, group, wholesale, OTA) for which this budget is defined. Enables segment-level revenue planning and variance analysis.

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Revenue budgets roll up to profit centers for consolidated financial planning, ownership entity reporting, and portfolio-level budget consolidation.
Reference to the property for which this revenue budget applies. Links to the property master record in the Property domain.
The date on which the revenue director formally approved this budget version. Marks the transition from 'draft' to 'approved' strategy status.

Total number of room-nights planned to be available for sale during the planning period (capacity baseline). Used as the denominator for RevPAR, TRevPAR, and GOPPAR calculations and STR STAR Report benchmarking.

Budgeted Cost Per Occupied Room (CPOR) in the property's operating currency, representing the planned direct operating cost per occupied room night. Used for profitability planning and variance analysis against actuals.

Total budgeted revenue from Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, and Exhibitions (MICE) and event spaces for the planning period. Supports TRevPAR planning and event sales governance in Delphi by Amadeus.
Total budgeted Food and Beverage (F&B) revenue in the property's operating currency for the planning period. Supports TRevPAR planning and USALI departmental revenue reporting.

Budgeted Gross Operating Profit (GOP) in the property's operating currency for the planning period, per USALI departmental and undistributed expense structure. Used for GOPPAR derivation and owner NOI reporting.

Total number of room-nights planned to be occupied (sold) during the planning period. Derived from budgeted available rooms × target OCC, stored as a planning target for demand forecasting and group block management.

Total budgeted ancillary revenue (spa, parking, resort fees, retail, etc.) in the property's operating currency for the planning period. Completes the TRevPAR revenue stack beyond rooms, F&B, and events.

Total budgeted room revenue (in operating currency) for the planning period and market segment. The primary monetary commitment in the revenue budget, used for variance analysis against actuals in the DOR and management reporting. Classified confidential as a strategic financial target.

Aggregate budgeted total revenue across all revenue streams (rooms + F&B + events + other) in the property's operating currency for the planning period. The top-line financial commitment used in owner presentations and EBITDA planning.
Timestamp when this revenue budget record was first created in the lakehouse Silver Layer. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX financial controls compliance.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this budget record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent multi-currency reporting across global properties.

Identifies the system or method used to generate the demand forecast underpinning this budget: 'IDeaS_G3' (IDeaS G3 RMS automated forecast), 'Infor_EzRMS' (Infor EzRMS forecast), 'manual' (revenue manager manual input), or 'hybrid' (combination). Supports data lineage and forecast accuracy tracking.
The fiscal month or period number (1–12) within the fiscal year for monthly budget granularity. Supports period-level variance analysis in the DOR and management reporting.
The fiscal year to which this revenue budget belongs (e.g., 2025). Aligns with the property's USALI-compliant financial reporting calendar and SAP S/4HANA GL fiscal periods.

Indicates whether this budget has received formal approval from the property owner or ownership group (True = owner-approved; False = pending or not required). Relevant for managed properties where owner sign-off is contractually required.

Free-text description of the near-term tactical revenue actions planned for this budget period (e.g., BAR adjustments, LRA controls, OTA channel mix shifts, promotional rate activations). Captured from the revenue strategy narrative in IDeaS G3 RMS and Infor EzRMS.

Free-text field for additional context, assumptions, or commentary associated with this revenue budget record (e.g., macro-economic assumptions, renovation impact adjustments, force majeure notes). Supports management reporting and audit documentation.

Granularity of the planning horizon for this budget record: 'annual' (full-year plan), 'quarterly' (90-day rolling), 'monthly' (period-level), or 'weekly' (short-term tactical). Determines the reporting cadence for variance analysis.
The last date of the planning horizon covered by this revenue budget (e.g., 2025-12-31 for an annual plan). Defines the effective end of the budget period.
The first date of the planning horizon covered by this revenue budget (e.g., 2025-01-01 for an annual plan). Defines the effective start of the budget period for variance tracking against actuals.
Brand/service tier of the property for which this budget applies: 'luxury', 'premium', 'select_service', or 'extended_stay'. Enables cross-segment benchmarking and portfolio-level revenue governance.

Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for this budget record, used in management reports, owner presentations, and cross-system references (e.g., SAP S/4HANA GL budget entries). Format: RB-{PropertyCode}-{Year}.

Free-text description of longer-horizon strategic revenue initiatives included in this budget (e.g., loyalty program rate strategy, new OTA partnership, dynamic pricing rule (DRR) rollout, group business development targets). Supports owner presentations and management reporting.

Current lifecycle state of this revenue budget and strategy record. 'draft' = under preparation; 'approved' = signed off by revenue director; 'active' = in-period execution; 'superseded' = replaced by a newer version; 'archived' = closed period record.

Classifies the revenue strategy scope covered by this budget: 'transient' (FIT/BAR-driven room revenue), 'group' (MICE and group block revenue), 'total_revenue' (TRevPAR-level all-revenue planning), 'food_and_beverage' (F&B revenue plan), 'events' (event/conference revenue), or 'other'. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: transient|group|total_revenue|food_and_beverage|events|other — promote to reference product]
The date on which this budget version was formally submitted for review and approval. Used for governance tracking and audit trail in owner presentations and management reporting.

Budgeted Average Daily Rate (ADR) in the property's operating currency for the planning period. Represents the planned average revenue earned per occupied room per night. Core revenue KPI used in STR STAR Report, RGI, and MPI benchmarking.

Budgeted Average Length of Stay (ALOS) in nights for the planning period and market segment. Supports LOS-based pricing strategy, minimum stay restrictions, and demand pattern planning in IDeaS G3 RMS.

Budgeted Average Rate Index (ARI) target, representing the property's planned ADR performance relative to its STR competitive set (property ADR / comp set ADR). Supports rate strategy and competitive positioning planning.

business_glossary_term: Target Gross Operating Profit Per Available Room (GOPPAR)
description: Budgeted Gross Operating Profit Per Available Room (GOPPAR) in the property's operating currency. Represents the profitability target per available room after operating expenses, used in owner presentations and GOP governance.

Budgeted Market Penetration Index (MPI) target, representing the property's planned occupancy performance relative to its STR competitive set (property OCC / comp set OCC). Supports competitive benchmarking strategy.

Budgeted occupancy rate (OCC) expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00) for the planning period. Represents the planned proportion of available rooms expected to be sold. Core KPI input for RevPAR calculation and STR STAR Report benchmarking.

Budgeted Revenue Per Available Room (RevPAR) in the property's operating currency for the planning period. Calculated as ADR × OCC but stored as a strategic target for direct comparison against actuals and STR competitive set benchmarks (RGI).

Budgeted Revenue Generation Index (RGI) target, representing the property's planned RevPAR performance relative to its STR competitive set (property RevPAR / comp set RevPAR). RGI > 1.0 indicates planned market share gain.

business_glossary_term: Target Total Revenue Per Available Room (TRevPAR)
description: Budgeted Total Revenue Per Available Room (TRevPAR) in the property's operating currency, encompassing all revenue streams (rooms, F&B, events, spa, parking, etc.) per available room. Supports total revenue strategy planning beyond rooms-only RevPAR.
Timestamp when this revenue budget record was most recently modified. Tracks version changes, reforecast updates, and approval workflow transitions for audit and governance purposes.

Indicates the iteration of this budget record: 'original' (initial annual plan), 'revised' (mid-year adjustment), or 'reforecast' (rolling forecast update). Supports version-controlled variance analysis in the Daily Operations Report (DOR) and management reporting.
Primary key for strategy

Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Revenue strategies often incorporate specific marketing campaigns as tactical levers (e.g., deploy summer family campaign to drive weekend occupancy). Strategy owners need this link for execution trac

Reference to the competitive set definition used for benchmarking this strategy. The competitor set defines the peer properties against which RGI, MPI, and ARI targets are measured via STR STAR Report.

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Revenue strategies are implemented at cost center level for tactical execution, departmental accountability, and operational performance management.

Reference to the demand forecast record that informed and underpins this revenue strategy. Links to the revenue.demand_forecast product to provide the analytical basis for strategy targets and tactical decisions.

Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: Revenue strategies are aligned with financial budgets for strategic planning, ensuring revenue tactics support approved financial targets and ownership expectations.

Foreign key linking to experience.gss_score. Business justification: Revenue strategies explicitly target GSS improvement as a demand driver (experience-driven revenue strategy). Strategic plans link rate positioning to satisfaction goals—properties pursuing rate growt
Identifier of the hotel or resort property to which this revenue strategy applies. Links to the property master record in Oracle OPERA PMS.
Reference to the newer revenue strategy record that replaced this one when status is 'superseded'. Enables lineage tracking of strategy evolution and supports audit trail for revenue governance.

Date and time when the strategy status transitioned to 'active' and began governing live pricing and inventory decisions. Distinct from approval timestamp — a strategy may be approved in advance and activated at a later point.

Name or employee identifier of the Revenue Director or authorized revenue management leader who formally approved this strategy. Provides governance and audit trail for strategic revenue decisions per SOX internal controls requirements.

Date and time when the revenue strategy was formally approved by the authorizing Revenue Director. Establishes the official approval event in the strategy lifecycle for governance and audit trail purposes.

The primary distribution channel(s) this strategy targets for demand generation (e.g., 'Direct Web', 'GDS', 'OTA', 'Voice/CRS', 'Corporate Direct'). Informs channel mix optimization and OTA partnership management decisions. Aligns with Sabre SynXis CRS channel codes.
Date and time when this revenue strategy record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation anchor for the strategy lifecycle.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary target values in this strategy record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent financial reporting across multi-currency property portfolios.

Categorizes the temporal scope of the strategy planning horizon. 'weekly' for short-term tactical adjustments, 'monthly' for rolling monthly plans, 'quarterly' for seasonal strategies, 'annual' for full-year budget-aligned strategies, 'event_based' for strategies tied to specific demand events.

The approved inventory management methodology for this strategy period. 'open' allows all bookings, 'hurdle_rate' applies minimum rate thresholds, 'length_of_stay' (LOS) enforces minimum/maximum stay restrictions, 'nested' uses tiered availability controls, 'closed' restricts availability for specific segments or channels.

Free-text narrative documenting the specific tactical actions approved as part of this strategy (e.g., 'Open BAR for all channels on shoulder nights', 'Apply 5-night minimum LOS restriction over peak weekend', 'Close OTA channels when occupancy exceeds 85%'). Provides governance documentation for revenue decisions.

The scheduled date for the next formal performance review of this revenue strategy. Drives workflow triggers in the revenue management calendar and ensures timely strategy adjustments based on actual vs. target performance.

Additional free-text commentary, rationale, or contextual notes provided by the revenue management team regarding this strategy. May include market intelligence, competitive observations, or risk considerations not captured in structured fields.
The last date of the planning period covered by this revenue strategy. Together with planning_horizon_start_date, defines the full strategy window for yield optimization and performance tracking.
The first date of the planning period covered by this revenue strategy. Defines the beginning of the stay-date window over which the strategy governs pricing and inventory decisions.

The approved pricing methodology governing rate-setting for this strategy period. 'bar_based' uses Best Available Rate (BAR) as the anchor, 'dynamic' applies algorithmic Dynamic Rate Rules (DRR), 'fixed' uses static rate plans, 'hybrid' combines approaches, 'competitive_parity' aligns rates to competitive set benchmarks.

The primary guest market segment this strategy is designed to optimize (e.g., 'Corporate Transient', 'Leisure FIT', 'Group MICE', 'OTA Leisure', 'Government'). Aligns with market segment codes used in Oracle OPERA PMS and IDeaS G3 RMS segmentation.

The approved cadence at which this strategy is scheduled for performance review and potential revision by the revenue management team. Governs the operational rhythm of the revenue management process.

business_glossary_term: Revenue Management System (RMS) Strategy Reference ID
description: The native strategy or scenario identifier from the source Revenue Management System (IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS). Enables traceability back to the originating RMS record for reconciliation and system-of-record alignment.

Indicates whether this strategy was created in response to a specific demand-driving special event (e.g., major conference, sporting event, concert, holiday period). When True, the strategy may include event-specific pricing premiums and inventory controls.

Name of the demand-driving special event associated with this strategy when special_event_flag is True (e.g., 'Super Bowl LX', 'Annual Medical Conference', 'New Year Holiday Period'). Provides context for strategy rationale and post-event performance analysis.

Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this revenue strategy record within the Revenue Management System (RMS). Used for cross-system reference and audit trail. Sourced from IDeaS G3 RMS or Infor EzRMS strategy identifiers.
Human-readable name describing the revenue strategy (e.g., 'Q3 2025 Transient Yield Push', 'Holiday Season Total Revenue Strategy'). Used for identification in reporting and governance workflows.

Current lifecycle state of the revenue strategy record. 'draft' indicates work-in-progress, 'pending_approval' awaits revenue director sign-off, 'approved' is authorized but not yet in effect, 'active' is currently governing pricing decisions, 'superseded' has been replaced by a newer strategy, 'archived' is retained for historical audit.

Classification of the revenue strategy by business segment. 'transient' covers individual guest pricing, 'group' covers group block strategies, 'total_revenue' encompasses all revenue streams (TRevPAR focus), 'food_and_beverage' targets F&B revenue optimization, 'events' covers MICE and event revenue. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: transient|group|total_revenue|food_and_beverage|events|loyalty — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]

The approved target Average Daily Rate (ADR) in the property's operating currency for the strategy period. ADR is the primary pricing KPI representing average revenue earned per occupied room per night. Used to benchmark actual ADR performance against strategic intent.

The approved target Average Rate Index (ARI) for the strategy period. ARI = Property ADR / Competitive Set ADR. Measures the property's rate positioning relative to its competitive set. Used alongside MPI and RGI for comprehensive competitive benchmarking.

business_glossary_term: Target Gross Operating Profit Per Available Room (GOPPAR)
description: The approved target Gross Operating Profit Per Available Room (GOPPAR) for the strategy period. GOPPAR incorporates operating costs and provides a profitability-adjusted view of revenue performance, aligned with USALI GOP reporting standards.

The approved target Market Penetration Index (MPI) for the strategy period. MPI = Property Occupancy / Competitive Set Occupancy. Measures the property's occupancy share relative to its competitive set as defined in STR STAR Report.

The approved target Occupancy Rate (OCC) expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00) for the strategy period. Represents the proportion of available rooms expected to be sold. Core input to RevPAR calculation and yield optimization decisions.

The approved target Revenue Per Available Room (RevPAR) in the property's operating currency for the strategy period. RevPAR = ADR × Occupancy Rate. The primary composite KPI for hotel revenue performance and competitive benchmarking via STR STAR Report.

The approved target Revenue Generation Index (RGI) for the strategy period. RGI = Property RevPAR / Competitive Set RevPAR. An RGI above 1.0 indicates the property is outperforming its competitive set. Sourced from STR STAR Report benchmarking data.

business_glossary_term: Target Total Revenue Per Available Room (TRevPAR)
description: The approved target Total Revenue Per Available Room (TRevPAR) encompassing all revenue streams (rooms, F&B, spa, events, ancillary) per available room for the strategy period. Relevant for total revenue strategies beyond rooms-only yield optimization.
Name or employee identifier of the last person to modify this strategy record. Supports change management and audit trail for revenue governance.
Date and time when this revenue strategy record was last modified. Tracks the most recent change event for audit trail and data lineage purposes in the Silver Layer lakehouse.

Monotonically incrementing version number for this strategy record. Increments each time the strategy is revised and re-approved. Supports audit trail and change management for revenue governance workflows.
Name or employee identifier of the revenue management professional who created this strategy record. Supports governance, accountability, and audit trail requirements.
Unique identifier for the compliance audit record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to finance.ap_invoice. Business justification: External audit costs (audit_cost field) are paid via AP invoices. Linking audit records to invoices enables cost center allocation, budget variance analysis, and SOX audit trail for compliance expendi
Self-referencing FK on audit (follow_up_audit_id)
Identifier of the property where the audit was conducted.
The date on which the audit fieldwork was completed and exit conference conducted.
The date on which the audit fieldwork actually commenced. May differ from scheduled date due to operational constraints or rescheduling.

Current lifecycle status of the audit. Scheduled indicates the audit is planned but not yet started, in-progress indicates fieldwork is underway, completed indicates the audit has finished and report is finalized, cancelled indicates the audit was terminated before completion, failed indicates the property did not meet minimum compliance standards, and passed with conditions indicates the property met standards but has minor findings requiring corrective action.

Classification of the audit based on its purpose and conducting authority. Internal audits are conducted by company staff, brand standard audits verify adherence to brand requirements, regulatory audits are mandated by government agencies (health department, fire marshal, liquor control board), third-party audits are conducted by external certification bodies (Forbes Travel Guide, AAA), ADA accessibility audits verify compliance with Americans with Disabilities Act, and fire safety audits assess fire code compliance.
Professional certification or license number of the auditor, if applicable. Required for certain regulatory audits (e.g., health inspector license, fire marshal certification).
Email address of the lead auditor for follow-up questions and corrective action verification.
Phone number of the lead auditor for urgent communications regarding audit findings or corrective actions.
Full name of the lead auditor or inspector who conducted the audit.

Name of the organization or agency that the auditor represents. May be internal department name, regulatory agency (e.g., County Health Department, State Fire Marshal), or third-party certification body (e.g., Forbes Travel Guide, AAA, ISO certification body).

Indicates whether a formal certification, accreditation, or rating was awarded as a result of this audit. True for third-party certification audits (Forbes Travel Guide star rating, AAA Diamond rating, ISO certification) where the audit resulted in certification issuance or renewal.
Date on which the awarded certification expires and requires renewal. Used for tracking certification lifecycle and scheduling re-audits.

The level or grade of certification awarded, if applicable. Examples include Forbes Travel Guide star ratings (4-star, 5-star), AAA Diamond ratings (3-Diamond, 4-Diamond, 5-Diamond), or ISO certification levels. Null if no certification was awarded or audit type does not involve certification.
End date of the compliance period being audited. Used for periodic compliance audits that assess adherence over a defined time window.
Start date of the compliance period being audited. Used for periodic compliance audits that assess adherence over a defined time window.
Date by which all corrective actions must be completed and verified. Set by auditor or regulatory body based on finding severity. Null if no corrective action is required.
Indicates whether the audit identified findings that require formal corrective action plans. True when property must submit remediation plans and evidence of correction.

Total cost incurred for the audit, including auditor fees, travel expenses, and administrative costs. Used for budgeting and cost analysis of compliance programs. Null for internal audits where costs are not separately tracked.
Timestamp when this audit record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Number of critical non-compliance findings identified during the audit. Critical findings represent immediate health, safety, or legal risks requiring urgent remediation.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the audit cost amount. Required when audit_cost is populated.
High-level summary of audit findings, conclusions, and recommendations for executive reporting. Provides context for overall result and key takeaways.
Scheduled date for the follow-up audit or re-inspection. Null if no follow-up is required.
Indicates whether a follow-up audit or re-inspection is required to verify corrective actions have been implemented. True for audits with critical findings or regulatory enforcement actions.

Geographic or legal jurisdiction under which the audit was conducted. Identifies the country, state, province, county, or municipality whose regulations apply. Critical for multi-jurisdictional compliance tracking.
Timestamp when this audit record was last updated. Used for tracking changes and data currency.
Number of major non-compliance findings identified during the audit. Major findings represent significant deviations from standards that require corrective action but do not pose immediate risk.
Number of minor non-compliance findings or observations identified during the audit. Minor findings represent opportunities for improvement that do not constitute non-compliance.
Additional notes, observations, or context regarding the audit that do not fit into structured fields. May include special circumstances, property responses, or auditor commentary.

Final outcome determination of the audit. Pass indicates full compliance with all standards, fail indicates critical non-compliance requiring immediate remediation, conditional pass indicates compliance achieved with minor corrective actions required, pending review indicates the audit report is under final review before result determination, and not applicable is used when the audit was cancelled or scope changed.

Numeric score assigned to the audit, typically on a scale of 0-100 or as defined by the auditing body. Used for brand standard audits and third-party certifications. Null for pass/fail regulatory audits that do not use scoring.
Email address of the property contact for audit-related communications and corrective action tracking.
Name of the property representative who served as primary contact during the audit. Typically the General Manager, Director of Operations, or Compliance Manager.
External reference number assigned to the audit for tracking and reporting purposes. Unique business identifier.

The specific regulatory framework, standard, or code against which the audit was conducted. Examples include USALI financial reporting standards, PCI DSS payment security standards, GDPR or CCPA data privacy regulations, ISO 22000 food safety standards, OSHA workplace safety regulations, ADA accessibility standards, or local fire and building codes.
Reference identifier or document management system location of the formal audit report. May be a document ID, file path, or URL to the report repository.

Overall risk rating assigned based on audit findings. Critical indicates immediate threat to guest safety, legal compliance, or brand reputation requiring executive escalation. High indicates significant risk requiring urgent management attention. Medium indicates moderate risk requiring corrective action within normal timeframes. Low indicates minimal risk with opportunities for improvement.
The date on which the audit was originally scheduled to be conducted. Used for planning and resource allocation.

Detailed description of the areas, departments, processes, or compliance domains covered by the audit. May include specific operational areas such as front desk operations, housekeeping standards, food and beverage safety, fire safety systems, accessibility features, or financial controls.
Unique identifier for the audit finding record. Primary key for the audit finding entity.
Reference to the parent compliance audit or regulatory inspection during which this finding was identified.

Reference to the department responsible for addressing this finding (e.g., Housekeeping, Food and Beverage, Front Desk, Engineering).
Self-referencing FK on audit_finding (related_audit_finding_id)
